outcomes.text: The hearing convenes with dignity and immediately drowns in procedure, because the Admiral has read the draft articles closely and moves to adjourn under a clause you wrote. Losing lunch by your own paperwork has a flavor biscuit cannot cover. The crew eats late, cold, and quietly furious, though every one of them now reads the articles nightly, hunting the clause that got them. The parrot has the decency to object to the adjournment, and someday, everyone feels, that one will land.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Break the chain off the wheel yourself

success.text: You wait for the plaque's polish inspection, then part the chain with a capstan bar in one heave that the topmen score generously. The wheel spins free, the helmsman catches it like a returning dog, and the ship settles a point closer to her true course as if she had opinions all along. The Admiral calls it destruction of property, and the quartermaster, without looking up, prices the chain at three coins and pays it into his hand from the kitty. The assembly notes, for the record, that it would have preferred a hearing. The parrot, robbed of its link, transfers the objection to the Admiral personally.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The chain wins. The capstan bar skips, your ribs meet the wheel, and the marines are on you before the third swing, with the Admiral observing that violence is a language he happens to be fluent in. Three days of pump duty for assaulting furniture. The assembly minutes call your effort premature, sincere, and instructive, and the parrot roosts on the chain each night, working the small link, refusing on the record to consider the matter closed.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_coalition A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Word travels below the waterline: the gunners want in, the topmen want in, and the officers' steward has been reading the articles by lantern with the door bolted. The Admiral responds by offering the gunners a private deal, double shares for loyalty, terms available in his cabin, singly. The parrot has taken to riding the steward's shoulder through the gun deck, announcing OBJECTION at the cabin door on every pass, like a lighthouse warning shipping off a particular man.

choices.text: Read the gunners the deal's small print aloud

outcomes.text: You perform the Admiral's offer on the gun deck in his own cadence: double shares, payable at voyage's end, from a prize fund he alone audits, forfeit on any act of disloyalty, disloyalty to be defined later. The gunners listen the way men listen to a leak. Then the master gunner asks the only question that matters, defined by whom, and signs the articles with powder still on his hand. The parrot's objection at the cabin door is sustained by acclamation, its third, and it begins holding its head like a bird with a docket.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: Your performance goes long. Somewhere in the second reading you improvise a clause the offer does not actually contain, and the steward, of all people, corrects you from the hatchway with the original. Accuracy wins the room and costs you the stage. The gunners sign anyway, on the steward's word, not yours, and the parrot pointedly withholds comment, which stings worse than the objection would have.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Broker the Admiral's bribe for a cut

outcomes.text: You carry the offer to the topmen with a straight face and a commission, fourteen coins for honest courier work, and honest is doing a labor in that sentence. Three topmen take the deal, and the Admiral gets his wedge, thin, but a wedge. The assembly learns who carried it the same evening, because a ship keeps no secrets, only schedules them. The parrot delivers its objection to your hammock personally and roosts there, sustained by everyone who walks past.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You carry the offer to the topmen with a straight face and a commission, fourteen coins for honest courier work, and honest is doing a labor in that sentence. Three topmen take the deal, and the Admiral gets his wedge, thin, but a wedge. The assembly learns who carried it the same evening, because a ship keeps no secrets, only schedules them. The parrot delivers its objection to your hammock personally and roosts there, sustained by everyone who walks past.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Row the articles to the becalmed brig to leeward

success.text: You take the jolly boat across a mile of oily calm with a fair copy of the articles wrapped in oilcloth. The brig's crew reads it on their capstan by lantern, and their bosun asks, very quietly, whether the lunch clause is real or a lure. You confirm it is bedrock, and they copy the whole document overnight, longhand, every hand taking a paragraph, so that two ships now sail on the same words. The parrot, who insisted on coming, objects to the brig's parrot on jurisdictional grounds, and the two birds sustain each other into the small hours.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The calm ends while you are mid-channel, and the jolly boat and a rising sea have a short argument you lose. You arrive soaked, the oilcloth arrives worse, and the brig's crew reconstructs the smeared articles from your memory, which improves the wage clause and loses a comma the quartermaster will mourn for weeks. It still gathers a second ship's worth of names. Rowing back against weather takes your last strength and one fingernail, and the parrot bails with a tin cup, objecting to the ocean itself, which is sustained on the grounds that everyone aboard agrees.
effects: schedule 2 r18_pirate_constitution_crisis A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

The storm arrives owing nobody an apology, and the Admiral takes the wheel alone, citing ownership of the particular horizon the ship is currently being thrown at. She broaches a point, staggers, and every line aboard hums the same low note of disagreement. The parrot clamps onto the binnacle, soaked to a furious quarter of its size, and screams OBJECTION into the wind with total procedural confidence.

choices.text: Invoke salvage rights on whatever breaks loose

outcomes.text: While the ship fights for her life you fight for her inventory, netting a drifting sea chest, two casks, and an officer's writing desk that turns out to hold the marines' pay. Salvage law is generous to whoever is holding the rope, and you are holding all of them. Fourteen coins and a desk, against a crew who saw you coiling line while they were reefing canvas. The parrot's objection is delivered later, dry, from the galley stove, and nobody in the mess moves to overrule it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Relieve the wheel by rota, over his objection

success.text: The helmsman of the watch simply steps up beside the Admiral and takes spokes, as the rota provides, and the Admiral discovers you cannot court martial a man mid-gust without letting go. Hands aloft, hands at the pumps, the cook wedged in the galley making something hot out of loyalty and bones. The ship comes through it like an argument won on the merits. At the worst of it the parrot's objection to sole helm is sustained by the sea itself, which nearly takes the Admiral overboard and is talked out of it by two deckhands he suspended from lunch.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The relief works until a sheet parts and takes you across the deck into the pinrail, where you leave some blood and most of your wind. The rota holds anyway, hand after hand, each one stepping up as the last one tires, which is all the articles ever claimed to be. By dawn the ship is exhausted, floating, and theirs. The Admiral speaks of his steady hand at the wheel to anyone available, and the parrot follows him down the deck croaking OBJECTION at each retelling, sustained by witnesses every time.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Declare the weather emergency article in force

outcomes.text: You read article nine into the gale: in weather, the wheel belongs to whoever the crew calls to it, and the crew is calling. The quartermaster countersigns with a nod, the helmsman takes over, and the Admiral is escorted below on the legally magnificent grounds that his safety is now a matter of record. The ship answers her own crew like she has been waiting to. When the sun comes up the parrot presents itself at the wheel and says OBJECTION once, softly, at nothing in particular, and the helmsman says sustained, and gives it the windward spoke to stand on.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You read article nine and the wind eats every word past the title, so witnesses will later disagree about what was invoked and on whose authority. The helm changes hands anyway, in the practical way ships decide things, but the record is a smear of salt and shouting. The Admiral survives to claim the articles failed their first storm. The quartermaster starts drafting the amendment before her hands are dry, and the parrot dictates, or at least supervises.
effects: schedule 3 r18_pirate_constitution_compromise A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Under a rinsed blue sky the Admiral proposes terms in the great cabin: he retains the captaincy, the crew receives consultation rights, and everyone stops nailing things. The word consultation sits on the table like a fish sold twice. The parrot walks the length of the charts, stops on the word, and taps it with one claw, objecting, in what the steward swears is its most sustained performance to date.

choices.text: Refuse terms the mess hasn't heard read aloud

outcomes.text: You table nothing, sign nothing, and carry the paper to the deck, where the quartermaster reads it out to the whole ship's company at noon. Read in daylight, consultation rights turn out to mean the right to be present while ignored, and the crew declines them with a courtesy that borders on violence. The counteroffer goes back upstairs with the lunch clause underlined twice. The parrot's objection to the word consultation is sustained unanimously, and the word is struck out with a knife.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The public reading is the right idea at the wrong hour: half the watch is aloft, the wind shreds the sentences, and a rumor spreads that the crew rejected wages, which nobody proposed. Sorting the rumor costs a day and a split lip when the master gunner's mate takes offense on behalf of a clause that does not exist. Still, sunlight has done its work, and nobody aboard now believes the cabin makes better bargains than the deck. The parrot spends the day repeating the actual text verbatim, the only reliable transcript afloat.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Grant him every horizon, keep the ship

success.text: You draft the counterproposal with a straight face: the captaincy retains sole and eternal title to every horizon, sighted or unsighted, and the crew retains the vessel, stores, wheel, and lunch. He reads it three times looking for the trap, and the trap is the trade, and the trade is his own doctrine at face value. He signs, magnificent, above a line the quartermaster has left generously wide. The parrot objects that no man may own what recedes when approached, and you sustain it, quietly, into your sleeve, because that is precisely the clause doing the work.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: He is vainer than you budgeted but not stupider: he reads the draft twice and asks, silkily, which of the two of you plans to sail to a horizon and check. The cabin laughs, and legitimacy takes on water in front of the steward. But the draft exists now, on the table, in writing, and by supper the whole ship has heard its shape and started finishing the joke for you. The parrot memorizes the operative sentence off the page, uninvited, which is how the crew learns the wording survives even when the meeting does not.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Audit the Admiral's prize ledger uninvited

success.text: The ledger comes off the shelf while the cabin argues, and its arithmetic is a confession: crew shares rounded down for years, the difference logged under upkeep of dignity. You copy the worst page, return the book, and read the figures to the mess, keeping the four coins the steward presses on you as, in his words, an auditing fee. The assembly thanks you for the arithmetic and fines you one measure of goodwill for freelancing, both sincerely, and starts a fund for shorted hands that same night. The parrot reviews the copied page and objects to the handwriting, which everyone agrees is technically its weakest work, and sustains it anyway out of loyalty.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

fail.text: The ledger is a confession, and instead of reading it out you price it, and the Admiral, to his credit as a businessman, pays eight coins for its quiet return. The mess never sees the arithmetic. You have the distinct sensation of having audited yourself and found the books cooked. The parrot watched the whole transaction from the transom, and its objection, though never spoken aloud, follows you around the ship for days like weather.
effects: schedule 4 r18_pirate_constitution_reversal A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

Refitting the binnacle after the storm, the carpenter's mate finds a false bottom and a document older than the mast: the ship's original articles, first crew, faded but legal. Third signature down, in a hungrier hand, Sole Share, able seaman. He signed for shares by work, lunch by right, and the wheel by rota, then spent thirty years climbing out of his own handwriting. The parrot examines the signature for a long moment and does not object, which around here has come to mean the evidence is airtight.

choices.text: Enter his old signature into the record

outcomes.text: The quartermaster reads the original articles beside the new ones at a full muster, and the two documents agree on everything but the dates. You put the question formally: is the Admiral in breach of articles he himself signed, and the vote is not close. His authority is reduced overnight from divine to contractual, the largest demotion aboard since the plaque. He takes it strangely quietly, like a man shown a portrait from before he was rich. The parrot's silence on the signature is entered into the record as its most damning finding to date.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: You enter the signature at muster and the Admiral counters with the one move nobody priced: he agrees. He signed those articles, he says, and rose from them, proof the system rewards merit, his, specifically. It is nonsense, but it is confident nonsense in a good coat, and it buys him a faction of exactly four. The record still holds his name against his conduct, in his own hand, and the quartermaster files the page where the whole ship can visit it. The parrot takes up perch above the frame like a museum guard.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

choices.text: Sell him back his own signature, priced kindly

outcomes.text: He pays fourteen coins for the page without haggling, which tells you what it was worth, and burns it in the stern lantern with ceremony. What he cannot burn is the carpenter's mate, the quartermaster, the steward, and you, all of whom read it before it reached him. Still, coin is coin, and evidence was heavy anyway. The parrot perches on the stern rail while the page burns, objecting steadily into the smoke, and the smoke, everyone later agrees, sustained it.

choices.text: Buy out his captaincy in horizons, at his valuation

outcomes.text: The crew makes a formal offer, drafted at your expense in fair round hand, three coins to the scrivener: the Admiral's share of the ship, purchased in full, price one horizon, the good one to windward, delivery upon arrival. It is his own doctrine at settlement value, and he cannot refuse it without admitting horizons cannot be owned, and he cannot accept it without ever being paid. He signs, because the alternative is arithmetic in public. The ship passes to her crew for a sum that recedes when approached, and the parrot objects that the price is too high, which is sustained as the finest joke ever entered in a ledger.
effects: schedule 5 r18_pirate_constitution_legacy A parrot has raised a constitutional objection

outcomes.text: The offer is drafted, beautiful, and the Admiral throws the inkwell, which catches you above the eye and ends the negotiation as a negotiation. But thrown ink is a signature of its own: the whole ship hears their offer was met with furniture. By evening the marines have stopped saluting him in any direction, and the deckhands hold the offer open, patient as tide. The parrot wears a single drop of ink on its chest like a decoration and objects to nothing all night, saving itself, everyone senses, for the settlement.

text:
PIRATE CONSTITUTION

The new articles hang in a frame by the wheel, weatherproofed in wax, signed by every hand aboard and one claw. The Admiral keeps a cabin, a title, and unchallenged legal ownership of every horizon, none of which have arrived. Course is set at the noon muster now, argued openly, entered in the log as seamanship, because that is what it is: more eyes on the water, fewer surprises in it. The parrot has been offered the post of Keeper of the Articles, and has objected to its own promotion.

choices.text: Sell fair copies of the articles portside

outcomes.text: You have the quartermaster's clauses copied fine and sell them in port at a coin a page to crews with admirals of their own, plus a licensing fee you invented on the spot for the lunch clause. Fourteen coins, and the words travel farther than you ever will, which you tell yourself is a public service with a margin. The assembly notes you are charging thirsty men for water that fell on all of you, and adjusts your share of goodwill accordingly. On the plus side, four harbors over, there is now a parrot being taught the word OBJECTION, secondhand, at full price.

choices.text: Take your turn at the wheel under article twelve

outcomes.text: Your trick at the helm comes up on the rota like anyone's, and you stand it at dusk with the frame creaking gently beside you. The course you keep is the crew's, argued at noon, trimmed by the lookout, seasoned by the cook, and it is better sailing than any owned horizon ever produced. The ship answers small and sure, the way she has since the wheel went common. Below, the parrot resolves its own case at last: promotion declined, objection sustained, and it signs on instead as crew, one full share, payable in biscuit, the first bird on the books.

Your trick at the helm meets a squall with no manners, and you hold the course at the price of two fingers sprained inside the spokes. The rota sends relief before you ask, which is the whole document working in one gesture. The log records weather met and course kept, which is all the poetry a ship requires. The parrot rides out the blow inside your coat, objecting to each individual gust, every one sustained by you personally, through your teeth, until the sea gets bored.

choices.text: Rule on who may call a terrible plan navigation

success.text: The last open question comes to you at muster, and you deliver the finding the frame has been waiting for: a terrible plan may be called navigation by whoever will be standing on it when it fails. Ownership of the plan follows the feet, not the hat. The crew adopts it by roar, the steward embroiders it, and even the Admiral is heard applying it, wincing, to himself. The parrot, now crew, raises the final objection of the affair, that the ruling contains no lunch provision, and it is sustained, amended, and eaten.

fail.text: Your ruling comes out lopsided, the crew debates it through two watches, and the log for that day reads course made good: none, visibility: excellent, everyone talking. Rival wordings are nailed up side by side, which at least honors the founding tradition of nailing. Legitimacy limps until the quartermaster schedules the question for next muster, where it will lose to lunch on the order paper, as is constitutional. The parrot observes the whole tangle from the frame and pronounces, with the weary authority of a bird who has never been overruled, OBJECTION, and everyone says sustained, and goes to eat, which is somehow the system functioning.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

Tuesday, before dawn, a man with excellent luggage unpacks a butterfly net at the naturalists' hide. Professor Capture First means to net, pin, and publish the last moon-winged witness, and has brought a frame for the citation. The net carries an ethics appendix, three stapled pages he calls a formality. The old warden pours him tea and does not offer sugar.

choices.text: Take the retainer and mark two roosts on his map

outcomes.text: The retainer is generous and the second envelope is quieter and thicker. You mark the roosts, and the Professor calls you a pragmatist, which he means as praise and records as an expense. On the table the appendix rustles from three pages to five without anyone touching it. The old warden refills every cup except yours.
effects: schedule 4 r18_cryptid_consent_assembly A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

On your first shift the moon-winged crosses the moor once, unhurried, the way a person walks past a window they trust. The appendix, unprovoked, grows a preface.

The heron files an incident report in one long unblinking look. You are on the rota now, bruised and official.

The Professor demands a correction and is handed the correction request form, which asks for the witness's signature. The appendix turns one of its own pages, apparently to enjoy this.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

The long watch begins at dusk with soup, folding stools, and a strict seating plan. Word is the moon-winged sings to a quiet hide and to nothing else, and half the moor's naturalists have turned out to be quiet together. The Professor has requested a demonstration slot for the net, whose appendix reached eleven pages after one practice swing. The apprentice with the wet boots is in charge of hushing.

choices.text: Sit the whole watch and let it sing first

success.text: It sings a little after two, three notes and a long silence shaped exactly like a question. Nobody answers it, which turns out to be the right answer. The apprentice transcribes by invitation, at a distance, in pencil, and the warden shares out the last of the soup like a payment everyone earned. The appendix adds a chapter titled On Being Sung To, unassisted.
effects: schedule 5 r18_cryptid_consent_retaliation A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

fail.text: You last until one, then your stool folds without consulting you. The clatter empties the hide of song and dignity in the same second, and your elbow finds the soup pot on the way down. The moor hears about it by morning, the way moors do. The warden marks the rota: attended, enthusiastic, landed badly.
effects: schedule 5 r18_cryptid_consent_retaliation A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

choices.text: Book the Professor's demonstration for noon sharp

The swing costs the net nine new pages, including a schedule of approved hours it did not have yesterday. He bills the day to the grant as preliminary results.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

A clerk arrives at breakfast holding a seizure order for the observation notebooks. Professor Capture First has classified sixty years of the commons' careful evenings as field data of an active acquisition, his acquisition, pending. The warden puts down the toast. The appendix, riding in the net's basket, has grown a table of contents overnight.

choices.text: Argue the notebooks are diaries, not data

success.text: The hearing takes an hour and turns on one page: the entry where the warden wrote it looked tired tonight, and I wished it well. Wishing well is not data collection, the clerk rules, it is correspondence, and correspondence belongs to the writers. The notebooks go home in a procession, one volume per person, escorted. The seizure order is filed under attempted.
effects: schedule 2 r18_cryptid_consent_coalition A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

fail.text: The clerk agrees the notebooks are personal, then impounds them anyway, pending a definition of personal. The definition is expected within the quarter, which quarter being the open question. The warden visits the notebooks on Tuesdays and reads to them. The Professor cites their seizure as progress in his interim report.
effects: schedule 2 r18_cryptid_consent_coalition A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

choices.text: Write up the growing appendix for a journal

success.text: Your note, Observations on a Net That Objects, is accepted in a week, which is fast for a journal and instant for this one. The fee is respectable and the letters column erupts pleasantly. The commons is glad of the attention and cool about the byline, since sixty years of watching appears in your piece as the setting. The warden congratulates you by not mentioning it.

outcomes.text: Sixty years takes more candles than the hide owns. Around four you fall asleep mid-sentence and wake wearing the inkwell, having copied the same October twice. The clerk arrives to find evidence of hurry, which clerks read as guilt. The copies survive; your shirt and the schedule do not.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

By Thursday the net needs two carriers, one for the hoop and one for the appendix, which now travels in its own strap. The commons has called for testimony on the moon-winged's behalf, and nature is answering: the bog, the hawthorn, and an owl of few words have all confirmed attendance. The hearing is set for the hide at dusk. The Professor has hired an assistant whose entire job is holding the net upright.

choices.text: Charge admission to view the famous net

outcomes.text: You rope off the net and letter a sign, The Apparatus, As Featured. The moor queues, coins drop, and the Professor approves, mistaking a queue for a following. The appendix signs two autographs, which should worry him more than it does. Takings are excellent; the bog, waiting to testify, watches you make change and remembers it.

choices.text: Buy lanterns for the witnesses coming in

outcomes.text: Three lanterns, a soup, and a plank bridge for the bog, who travels slowly and arrives entire. The hawthorn comes potted, the owl by air, and the procession into the hide looks like a small stubborn constellation. Their testimony is unanimous: it says no, and it has said no for sixty years, politely, to everyone. The clerk writes politely in the margin and underlines it.

You arrive punctured and the hawthorn arrives apologetic, which from a hawthorn is a great honor and no help to your hands. The hearing starts late and scratchy. Your testimony is mostly hissed.

outcomes.text: The appendix takes the stand, or rather is placed on it, and the clerk swears it in on itself. Read aloud it runs four hours, during which the Professor hears his equipment's opinion of him at dictation speed. Section twelve, Consent of the Sought, is ruled binding on whoever holds the handle. He is holding the handle at the time.

The Professor calls the delay due diligence and orders supper. The appendix sulks and grows an index nobody asked for.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

Midnight at the roost, and the Professor is done with hearings. He advances up the moor with the net at port arms, the assistant trailing with the strap, the commons hurrying behind with lanterns and no plan. At the top of the swing the appendix unfurls to forty pages and the mesh is suddenly mostly margins. The moon-winged lands on the hoop itself, folds its wings, and says, clearly, no.

choices.text: Put yourself between the swing and the roost

outcomes.text: You stand where the net would land, which makes the net a legal question and you a signatory. The Professor lowers it by inches, appendix flapping like a caught gull. The moon-winged lifts off past your ear, slow, deliberate, close enough to teach, and for three seconds you understand flight the way you understand your own name. It shows the ones who stood; that is the fee.
effects: schedule 4 r18_cryptid_consent_compromise A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

outcomes.text: You step in a beat late and take the hoop across the ear, which the appendix immediately logs as improper deployment, page forty one. Sitting in the heather with your ears ringing, you watch the moon-winged circle once above you like a signature. The Professor claims interference. The moor's frost claims his tent ropes that same night.
effects: schedule 4 r18_cryptid_consent_compromise A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

choices.text: Recite his appendix at him, section nine first

success.text: Section nine, Consent of the Netted Party, requires a signature before the swing, and you read it out in the voice clerks use for weather. The Professor checks the page, and the page checks back. It has grown a signature line since morning, blank, waiting, notarized by damp. He stands in the heather at midnight asking a moth for its autograph and does not get it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_cryptid_consent_compromise A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

fail.text: He produces a waiver, self signed, dated tomorrow. The clerk on call rules that waiving another party's consent is a novelty and adjourns to consult someone senior, which at midnight means the owl. The owl says who, once. The swing is postponed rather than stopped, and everyone walks down the moor knowing the difference.
effects: schedule 4 r18_cryptid_consent_compromise A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

choices.text: Sell standing room to watch the attempt

outcomes.text: You paper the tavern with handbills, The Netting, One Night Only, and the moor turns out with flasks. He swings, the appendix blooms mid-arc like an accusation, and the crowd gets a better show than advertised: a moth declining a scientist at close range. You keep the gate money either way. The Professor keeps the strap and a new page titled Audiences, Ethics Of.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

Negotiations convene at the hide over scones and mutual exhaustion. The moon-winged, through the medium of the warden's careful listening, has offered terms: it will sing on Thursdays, tolerate sketches at forty paces, and be touched by nobody, ever, for any reason including science. The net is now more appendix than mesh, and the apprentice has begun indexing it. The Professor wants the terms renamed a research protocol so that he can sign one.

choices.text: Draft the terms as a permit only it can revoke

outcomes.text: You write the permit so the granting authority is the moon-winged itself, revocable by one shake of the head, no appeals form attached because none exists. The Professor signs a permit for the first time in his life instead of issuing one, and the novelty visibly costs him. Thursdays are lodged with the System as protected singing hours. The appendix copies the permit in as its final section and adds, in small type, at last.
effects: schedule 5 r18_cryptid_consent_reversal A moth says no and the net grows footnotes

outcomes.text: Your draft leaves a gap and he finds it, a clause reading observation incidental to passage, under which he plans a great many passages. The warden catches it at the reading, too late for grace. The permit goes back for a week of repairs, and the moor spends the week muttering. The Professor books passages.

choices.text: Take what is left of the net off him

success.text: It comes away easier than expected, one thread of mesh and forty pages of opinion, and he lets go rather than tear it. You hand it to the apprentice, who has the index ready, which is its own kind of victory. The commons thanks you and then, gently, over tea, explains that taking things off people is his method. You are asked to bring rather than seize in future.

choices.text: Go to the first Thursday and just listen

outcomes.text: The hide fills at dusk and holds its breath without being asked. The moon-winged sings the full song for the first time in sixty years, and the warden cries quietly into a handkerchief embroidered by someone long dead who would have loved this. Nobody transcribes; it was not that kind of invitation. The soup afterward tastes like the end of a war nobody declared.

Afterward the apprentice pats your arm without making eye contact. The latch is oiled by Friday, by you.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

Sixty years of notes land on the warden's table with a soft, decisive thump. They are not the commons' notebooks; they are the moon-winged's own, kept in dust and wing script, and they are about the naturalists. It has been studying them all along: their soups, their rotas, their errors in the dark. It intends to publish, and it is asking each of them, one by one, for consent to appear.

choices.text: Sell your consent to appear, cash up front

outcomes.text: You name a price and it pays without haggling, which stings more than haggling would. The chapter about you is accurate, which is the expensive part: reliable in soup, brave at midnight, sells things. The warden reads it and says nothing with tremendous fluency. The coins are good coins and they sit in your pocket like a citation.

choices.text: Consent freely and make sure refusals count

success.text: You sign, then walk the table asking each naturalist in turn, no hurrying, no reading over shoulders. The apprentice with the wet boots goes pink and says no, actually, and the moon-winged inks declined, respectfully in the margin and moves to the next name as if nothing has happened, because nothing has. The no lands like proof the whole book can be trusted. The warden signs last, twice, once for the entry and once for joy.

The moon-winged waits out the scuffle, then starts the round again from the top at its own pace. Everyone gets their yes or no said properly. Your wrist gets a bandage and a lesson.

choices.text: Check the appendix's authorship page, all of it

outcomes.text: The apprentice's index has reached the front matter, and there it is: the original three pages, drafted decades ago by a student with careful handwriting and large hopes, one P. Capture First. He wrote the ethics himself, before the citations got hungry. He reads his own young sentences standing very still, then asks, in a smaller voice than usual, whether the Thursday singing has a waiting list. It does; he is on it; the appendix leaves the record uncorrected.

outcomes.text: He denies the handwriting to the room, to the clerk, and finally to a graphologist he hires himself, who confirms it in front of everyone, itemized. Denial invoiced is denial doubled. He storms out past the net he can no longer lift alone. The pressure on the moor rises like weather.

text:
CRYPTID CONSENT

The field guide comes back from the binder in green cloth, one entry per resident, every entry by invitation. The moon-winged's page is blank except for two words in wing script: declined, respectfully. The net's hoop, empty of mesh entirely, hangs above the stove framing that page, and the appendix, bound as a companion volume, is consulted like a relative. Professor Capture First's lifetime citation count stands at one, a footnote in it, marked cautionary.

choices.text: Take a turn keeping the blank page dusted

success.text: The rota for the guide gains a new line, keeper of the page, and your name goes on it in the warden's steadiest hand. Dusting a refusal turns out to be pleasant work; visitors ask what the page says, and you get to answer, everything it wants to. On Thursdays the singing carries down to the shelf. The hoop gleams; retirement suits it.

fail.text: The shelf is high, the stool is the folding one, and history repeats as furniture. You come down with the duster in hand and the guide lands on you, open, unharmed, to the blank page, which at close range is still blank and still right. The tavern spends a loud week relitigating stool safety on your behalf. The warden logs a workplace incident and makes you soup.

choices.text: Grant him his one citation, footnote sized

outcomes.text: The commons votes him exactly one citation, in the appendix, in the section on how not to begin. He attends Thursdays now, forty paces back, sketching at the lawful distance, and his notebook has stopped saying specimen anywhere. Scholars visit the moor to study the net that refused; they cite the appendix constantly and him precisely once. He has been heard, at the tavern, calling this fair.

Scholars visit the moor to study the net that refused; they cite the appendix constantly and him precisely once. He has been heard, at the tavern, calling this fair.

He reads the footnote as vindication and tours with it, Capture First, As Cited, a lecture series. Attendance is strong for exactly one night per town, until audiences learn the citation is a warning label. The tour limps home, and the moor endures a season of his forwarding address. The blank page, consulted, offers no comment.

choices.text: License prints of the famous blank page

outcomes.text: The prints sell astonishingly, a blank rectangle and two words, and by spring every tavern on three moors hangs one. You take a royalty on each; the moon-winged, asked for comment through the warden, declines, respectfully, which you cannot print again because you already did. The commons notes that its refusal is now your revenue and adjusts your standing accordingly. The money is real; so is the look the heron gives you.

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The night clinic is quiet until the door opens on a young demon with a fever, led by the hand like any other sick child, except the hand has too many knuckles and the temperature is measured in kiln. The volunteers reach for the intake form and the intake form fights back: the species field accepts fourteen categories of person and the demon is not among them; it is listed two pages later, under environmental hazards, between damp and bees. Without a person-number the ledger cannot admit a patient. The child coughs, and the waiting room curtains catch fire briefly, in sympathy.

choices.text: Treat the child now, on your own name and number

outcomes.text: You sign the intake as guarantor and the ledger, finding a person-number in the right box, asks no further questions, because forms have never cared who suffers, only who signs. The volunteers move like weather: compresses, quench-tea, a cot by the window. The fever holds through the night and breaks toward dawn, and the child sleeps, smoking gently, under a blanket that will never be entirely rectangular again.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_assembly the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: You sign, and the treatment starts, and midway through the night shift a clerk from the day roster notices the species field and files a variance. Your name is now attached to the variance. The child sleeps on, unaware of the paperwork gathering above it like weather off the coast. You would sign again. You settle in a chair where you can watch the cot, and the form, in that order.

choices.text: Demand the registry issue a person-number tonight

outcomes.text: You get the registrar out of bed, which is a right everyone technically has and no one had ever tested. Confronted with a feverish child and a form that classifies it alongside damp, the registrar issues a provisional number on the spot, with a haste that resembles conscience. Provisional, the stamp says. So is everyone, you point out. The registrar does not write that down, but visibly stores it.

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The volunteers meet in the corridor at shift change, which is where the clinic keeps its parliament, to decide the question the intake form has forced: treat first and file later, or file first and hope the fevers agree to queue. Mid-debate the door opens and the demon's mother comes in with a covered pot, and the argument stops, because she has the bearing of someone who has raised children through worse than committees. She has brought soup. She has also brought corrections. The soup, she says, is for everyone. The corrections, her expression adds, are also for everyone.

choices.text: Ask the mother to review the intake form herself

success.text: She reads the form the way mothers read report cards: completely, twice, and aloud at the bad parts. Species: see hazards. Next of kin: not applicable. Emergency contact: authorities. By the end the corridor is silent and the form is on the counter looking guilty of everything. She takes out a pencil. The clinic's first demon-readable intake form is drafted before the soup is cool, and it is shorter, kinder, and asks one question the old form never thought of: who loves this patient, and how do we reach them fast.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_retaliation the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: She declines to fix the form, on the grounds that it is not hers, and hands it back with the pencil balanced on top like a dare. The point lands harder than any redraft: the form belongs to the clinic, and so does the shame of it. The volunteers begin the rewrite that night themselves, arguing over every field, which she observes from the soup station with the satisfaction of a teacher whose class has finally opened the book.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_retaliation the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Cater the meeting: sell bowls alongside the free soup

outcomes.text: You set up a modest concession beside her pot: bread, spoons, second helpings at a price. She watches this the way lighthouses watch small boats. The take is decent. At close she fills your bowl herself, without charge, and says nothing at all, and you eat the entire margin of your evening under a gaze that converts profit into homework.

The policy fits on a card above the intake desk. The mother reads it, nods once, the nod of an inspector passing a bridge, and ladles soup with slightly more ceremony, which in her grammar is a medal.

The mother serves the dissenters first and largest, because, she says, the ones who are afraid are the ones who have been here longest, and fear like that is a scar, not a side.

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The Order of Correctly Feared Creatures answers the clinic's new policy by morning: a warding cordon around the block, chalk sigils at carriage height, and a proclamation nailed to the door explaining that the perimeter exists for everyone's protection, including yours, statistically. Patients with the wrong silhouette now cannot reach the steps. The proclamation is signed by the Grand Warden, whose seal depicts a lantern held against the dark, and whose cordon is currently keeping a child with a lung complaint out in the actual cold.

choices.text: Scrub the sigils off the cobbles yourself, tonight

success.text: Chalk is chalk. You go out with a bucket and a stiff brush and erase the perimeter sigil by sigil, working inward, so that the ward's own geometry funnels its failure toward the door it was guarding. By dawn the cordon is a smear and the lung complaint is asleep in the second cot. The Order's response will come, but tonight the only barrier around the clinic is the queue for soup.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: The fourth sigil objects. The flash puts you on your back in the gutter with your eyebrows in some doubt, and the cordon reknits itself behind your brush like water. The mother retrieves you, applies the compress herself, and remarks that in her family, walls are dismantled starting from the person who built them, not the bricks. It is medical advice. It is also tactical.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Carry the clinic to the patients: rounds outside the cordon

outcomes.text: If the sick cannot cross the chalk, the clinic can. Volunteers pack satchels and walk rounds through the streets beyond the perimeter: compresses in doorways, quench-tea on steps, the intake card filled out on knees. The Order's cordon now encloses an empty waiting room and excludes the entire practice of medicine, a result its geometry never considered. Statistically, the proclamation begins to look very alone.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: Rounds work until the weather turns and satchel medicine meets its limits: there are treatments that need the cabinet, the cot, the roof. A rasping stone-child worsens in a doorway that is no substitute for a ward. The volunteers carry on, jaw-set, but everyone walking rounds now knows the cordon is not an insult. It is a tourniquet on the whole block, and it is tightening.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Challenge the cordon's permit: wards require a hazard finding

outcomes.text: Wards, it turns out, are municipal works, and municipal works require a documented hazard. The Order's filing, when you force it into daylight, cites no incident, no complaint, no injury: the sole evidence of danger is the intake form's own hazards page, which the clinic has since repealed. The permit dissolves. The Order has warded the block against a typo, and now the whole coast knows it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: The hearing accepts the Order's substitute evidence: fear, attested by three respectable households at a respectable distance. The cordon stands. But the transcript now contains the Grand Warden explaining under oath that no demon has ever harmed anyone at this clinic, which he offered as proof the wards are working, and which reads, in print, like something else entirely.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_coalition the night clinic volunteers will need you again

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The new intake form has been posted a week when the waiting room begins to fill with patients the old form never imagined. A mimic with a toothache confesses it has been the waiting room bench for eleven years, which explains a great deal about the bench. A ghost presents with anxiety, mostly about presenting. A golem arrives holding a warranty card and the dawning suspicion that what it actually needs is a physician. None of them have person-numbers. All of them have been sick alone for a very long time, and the corridor must now decide what the clinic is: an exception it keeps making, or a door that is simply open.

choices.text: Register them all: the clinic keeps its own roll now

success.text: The clinic opens its own patient roll, sovereign as any registry: name, ailment, who loves you, how do we reach them fast. The mimic signs as Bench, provisionally, then crosses it out and writes a name nobody has called it in a century. The ghost signs with intent. The golem's warranty card is stapled to a real chart. The Order's registry has fourteen categories of person; the clinic's has one, sick, and it turns out one is enough.
effects: schedule 4 r18_demon_clinic_crisis the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: The roll opens and immediately jams: the ghost cannot hold the pen, the mimic panics and becomes a coat rack mid-signature, and the golem's maker's mark raises questions of guardianship no one can answer at this hour. Progress by dawn: three half-entries and a corridor full of patience. The roll stays open. Some doors, the mother observes from the soup station, are opened the way soup is made, slowly, and while doing other things.
effects: schedule 4 r18_demon_clinic_crisis the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Bill the Order for eleven years of the mimic's bench service

outcomes.text: The invoice is a masterpiece: one waiting room bench, self-maintaining, load-bearing, on continuous duty eleven years, at the municipal furniture rate, plus interest, payable to the patient formerly known as furniture. The Order cannot dispute it without conceding the mimic performed labor, and cannot concede labor without conceding laborer, and cannot concede laborer without the entire hazards page folding in on itself. The mimic frames the first payment above its cot: proof, it says, that it was never just sitting there.
effects: schedule 4 r18_demon_clinic_crisis the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: The Order returns the invoice unpaid with a notation that furniture cannot invoice, and the mimic takes the notation badly, spending two full days as a hat stand of visible dejection. But the invoice circulates, as invoices do, and by week's end three taverns and a courthouse are auditing their own benches with new suspicion. Somewhere in the walls of the coast, a great deal of furniture is smiling.
effects: schedule 4 r18_demon_clinic_crisis the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Offer premium queue positions for paying patients

outcomes.text: The priority list earns steadily until the night the fever cart arrives and the queue reorders itself around severity without consulting the list, because triage, it turns out, is the one hierarchy this waiting room believes in. Refunds are issued by the mother, from your float, at a rate she considers fair. Her rates are final. The golem watches the arithmetic and files the evening under education.
effects: schedule 4 r18_demon_clinic_crisis the night clinic volunteers will need you again

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The fever returns to the demon child in the smallest hours, worse, with the dry-storm smell the old texts warn about, and the treatment is behind one door: the medicine cabinet, licensed to dispense to persons only, its lock enchanted to check the ledger before it turns. Using it off-registry voids the clinic's dispensary license entirely: every future patient, spent on this one. The child's mother stands very still by the cot, being calm the way sea walls are calm, and asks nobody in particular how much a license weighs.

choices.text: Invoke emergency doctrine: necessity outranks the ledger

outcomes.text: There is, buried in the dispensary charter, a necessity clause older than the Order itself, from a plague year when nobody checked anything but pulses: in peril of life, the cabinet serves the patient before the paperwork. You read it aloud to the lock. The lock, which has waited decades for someone to cite the good law, turns. The child gets the draught; the license survives on the oldest words in its own charter; the Order discovers its safeguard was younger than its conscience.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_compromise the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: The necessity clause exists but was amended, two reforms ago, to require pre-approval of the emergency, in writing, in triplicate, and the amendment holds at every level you can reach before dawn. The clause fails. The night does not: the volunteers break the fever the pre-cabinet way, with quench-tea and cold cloth and shifts of held hands, and by morning the child is asleep and the charter's margin holds a new note in the mother's hand about what, precisely, the law was for.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_compromise the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Sell your own black-market antipyretics at cost plus courage

outcomes.text: You know a supplier, because you are you, and the vials arrive by the back stair at a price that acknowledges the risk without celebrating it. The draught works. The mother pays, notes the markup, and says only that she will remember who came when it was expensive to come, which is either a blessing or a ledger entry, and in her voice there is no difference.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_compromise the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Put every volunteer's name on the variance and open the cabinet

success.text: The roster signs as one: if using the cabinet for a dying child voids the license, the license was void where it mattered, and the Order can revoke all of them or none. The cabinet opens. The child sleeps. The revocation notice arrives by second post and meets a wall of forty names and, behind them, a coast that has been reading transcripts. The Order stays its own hand and calls it discretion. The clinic calls it arithmetic.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_compromise the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: The cabinet opens, the child sleeps, and the license falls: the Order's clerks are braver against paper than against fevers. The dispensary closes and reopens by morning as a soup kitchen that happens to smell of medicine, staffed by the same forty names. Licenses, the mother says, ladling, are how they count you. Come and be counted here.
effects: schedule 5 r18_demon_clinic_compromise the night clinic volunteers will need you again

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The Order sends terms under the lantern seal: a provisional creature pass, entitling the bearer to treatment at any licensed clinic, subject to registration, inspection, and the classification of the bearer as livestock, care of. The pass is a small green card. It would work. It would open the cabinet, cross the cordon, quiet the clerks. The mother holds it at arm's length, reads it twice, and asks the room what her child would learn about itself, at ten years old, from a card that calls it a well-kept animal. Nobody answers. The card is very green. The fever, everyone remembers, comes back.

choices.text: Ask the Order's courier what pass he carries

success.text: The courier, cornered politely by soup, produces his own credential, and the room reads it in silence: species field, blank. Not filled in, blank, waived, by a scribbled exemption three wardens old. The Order's own staff do not survive their paperwork. You copy the exemption's wording onto the clinic's roll before the courier finishes his bowl, and the precedent walks out the door unaware it has defected.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_reversal the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: The courier will not show his papers, which is its own answer, and leaves the soup unfinished, which in this room is the gravest confession available. What remains is the question, now loose on the coast: what do the Correctly Feared carry in their own pockets? Questions like that do not need the clinic's help to breed.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_reversal the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Refuse the pass in the mother's words, as a body

outcomes.text: The clinic's answer is drafted at the soup station and signed by every name on the roll, patients included, Bench included: no card that a child cannot read without flinching will be accepted at this door. The refusal is not defiance, it explains, but diagnosis. The Order, which has procedures for enemies and none whatsoever for being accurately described, does not reply for eleven days, which the ghost says is the longest silence it has ever been in the room for.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_reversal the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: The refusal goes out and the cost arrives with it: two allied clinics up the coast, offered the same green cards, take them, because their fevers are just as real and their courage is spent elsewhere. The passes work there. Children carry them. The clinic does not judge, aloud, but the roll gains a new column that week, in the mother's hand: transfers in.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_reversal the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Accept the pass, for the clinic's cat

success.text: The application is flawless: species, cat; temperament, correctly feared; dependents, everyone. The Order processes it, because processing is what the Order is, and somewhere in the registry a mouser named Ledger becomes the first officially livestocked patient of the clinic. The absurdity is now a matter of record with a case number, and every future creature pass must survive comparison to the cat. The cat, for its part, presents its card at the soup station and is honored in full.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_reversal the night clinic volunteers will need you again

fail.text: The registry rejects the cat on the grounds that cats are already correctly classified, which means the Order has, in writing, ranked a mouser's paperwork above a child's. The rejection letter is pinned to the clinic door beside the proclamation, unannotated, because some documents satirize themselves. Pilgrims come to read it. The cat receives visitors beneath it with the gravity of a sitting judge.
effects: schedule 2 r18_demon_clinic_reversal the night clinic volunteers will need you again

text:
DEMON CLINIC

The Grand Warden comes in person, at night, without the seal, and the volunteers brace for escalation until they see how he is standing: like a patient. Behind the lantern and the cordon and the fourteen categories, the Order's founder is not, it emerges, entirely on the registry himself. He is a hearth-spirit, four centuries old, filed under an assumed species since before the Order was the Order, wardening the world against creatures so that no one would ever check the warden. He has a cough. He has had it for sixty years. He would like, he says, looking at the soup pot and not at anyone's eyes, to be seen by a doctor, and he does not mean examined.

choices.text: Take him in: intake form, cot, soup, the whole roll

outcomes.text: He is processed like anyone, which is the entire point and he knows it: name, ailment, who loves you, and at that field the pen stops for a long time before he writes, finally, the Order, I think, once. The cough is treatable; four centuries of hiding it are not, but the roll has a column for that too now. He sleeps in the fourth cot, between the golem and the bench, correctly feared by no one, and the cordon outside dies of neglect within the month.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_legacy the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: He submits to the intake and flinches at the roll's openness: sixty years of concealment do not undo in an evening, and when the mimic cheerfully recognizes a fellow long-term impersonator, he leaves before the compress cools, seal restored, spine relocked. But he leaves his cough's history on the chart, in his own hand. Patients who bolt, the mother says, folding the blanket for next time, are still patients. The chart stays open.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_legacy the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Make his registration public: the Order must audit itself

outcomes.text: You persuade him that the safe way out of a lie this old is daylight administered by his own hand: he files his true registration and simultaneously orders the audit of every waiver in the Order's vaults. The vaults, it turns out, are full: wardens, clerks, two respectable households from the cordon hearing, all exempted, all hiding. The Order does not fall. It does something stranger: it becomes, by audit, the largest registry of creatures on the coast, and therefore, by its own charter, their advocate.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_legacy the night clinic volunteers will need you again

outcomes.text: The registration leaks before he can file it: a clerk, a copyist, nobody is ever sure, and the coast learns the Grand Warden's species from a broadsheet instead of a confession. The Order convulses; the lantern seal changes hands twice in a season. He weathers it in the fourth cot, technically a fugitive, actually asleep. The clinic never says who it shelters. The intake form, notably, has no field for it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_legacy the night clinic volunteers will need you again

choices.text: Name a price for the clinic's silence

outcomes.text: He pays it, which is the worst part: four centuries of a hoarded warden's stipend, transferred without a haggle, because silence is the one commodity he has always known the price of. The clinic is funded for a decade. He still comes in for the cough, and you still hold the ladle, and between you the transaction sits at the table like a guest nobody introduces. The mother serves you both. Her silence is not for sale, and costs you more.
effects: schedule 3 r18_demon_clinic_legacy the night clinic volunteers will need you again

text:
DEMON CLINIC

A year on, the corridor gathers to ratify what has already happened, which is how the best laws are made. The intake form goes to the printer in its final revision, and the volunteers gather around the proof sheet like family around a crib. Species: the field is gone entirely. In its place, one checkbox, drafted by the mother, argued over for a season, and adopted word for word: Other, please exist. The Order has requested forty copies, officially for reference. The child, fever a year gone, has asked to sign the proof sheet as a witness, and there is no rule against it, because the parliament checked.

choices.text: Send the Order a framed copy, invoice enclosed: consulting

success.text: The invoice, one line, reads: form redesign, tested under fire, one child's dignity, priceless, administrative fee attached. The Order pays it in full and without comment, which is how bureaucracies apologize, and hangs the framed form in its own intake hall, where wardens process creatures beneath a checkbox that outranks their categories. The fee funds the soup pot for a year. The frame, the courier reports, is straightened daily by persons unknown.

fail.text: The Order returns the invoice marked gratis declined, accepts the frame, and files the fee request as an attachment to history. The mother rules the outcome a victory on inspection: they kept the form. Institutions, she says, pay in the currency they have, and the Order has never had money, only walls, and there is now a door in one.

choices.text: License the form to every clinic on the coast, per copy

outcomes.text: The licensing income is real and the mother's look is realer, so the schedule quietly includes a clause you do not advertise: free to any clinic that treats first and files later. Within a year the paid tier has three customers and the free tier has the entire coast, which was, you tell yourself most mornings, the plan. The golem, now the clinic's bookkeeper, records the arrangement under a heading of its own invention: profitable, barely, on purpose.

Within a year the paid tier has three customers and the free tier has the entire coast, which was, you tell yourself most mornings, the plan. The golem, now the clinic's bookkeeper, records the arrangement under a heading of its own invention: profitable, barely, on purpose.

choices.text: Ratify the roll as the region's open registry of care

outcomes.text: The clinic's patient roll, born the night a bench confessed, is adopted region-wide: any door of care may copy it, add to it, and honor its entries, and no entry may be struck for species, number, or fear. The Order co-signs, lantern seal and all, its first charter written by other hands. At the bottom of the founding page, above the wardens and the volunteers and the parliament, the first witness signature is small, careful, and slightly scorched, and the registry keeps it under glass: proof that the whole edifice rests, as it should, on a child who got better.

The Order co-signs, lantern seal and all, its first charter written by other hands. At the bottom of the founding page, above the wardens and the volunteers and the parliament, the first witness signature is small, careful, and slightly scorched, and the registry keeps it under glass: proof that the whole edifice rests, as it should, on a child who got better.

Ratification passes; funding does not, and the open registry begins life as it will continue it, broke, staffed by volunteers, held together by soup and spite. The mother is unbothered. Institutions with money, she says, hanging the founding page by the door, spend their courage keeping it. Institutions with none have nothing to lose but their patients, and here, she taps the checkbox, we have decided not to. The clinic opens at dusk, as usual. The queue is already forming, and no two silhouettes in it match.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Tuesday's briefing opens with the air visibly disagreeing. The Ministry of Clear Skies has classified the mill smoke as enthusiasm, and Spokesman Brill, gripping the podium with both hands, informs the room that there is also no podium. Across town the old typesetter who prints the weather on page four has been charged with sedition over the word smoky. An intern wearing three lanyards checks your credentials twice, apologizing both times.

choices.text: File a formal correction with the Standards Desk

outcomes.text: The Standards Desk clerk reads your correction, glances once at the window, and stamps it received against instructions. It enters the record as Query 1, the first document in Ministry history to mention weather by name, and the printers reprint it as a keepsake. Brill calls it an isolated draft. The podium creaks under him as he says so, in what witnesses describe as agreement.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_assembly The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_assembly The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: Your correction is reviewed, reclassified as weather, and therefore denied. The clerk explains, kindly, that the Ministry cannot accept documents about things that are not occurring. On the way out you hear Brill deny the corridor you are standing in. He gives you a small hopeful smile as you leave it anyway.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_assembly The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Take the envelope and ask the prepared question

outcomes.text: The question arrives typed on Ministry letterhead: would the Spokesman say the air has never been better. You ask it beautifully, with a concerned expression, and Brill lights up like a man briefly loved. The envelope holds a per diem, and inside it a second, thicker envelope marked for tone. From the dock, the typesetter watches you and prints nothing about it, which is worse.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_assembly The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Put your name under the weather report

success.text: You add your name beneath the word smoky, which under current law makes you an accessory to the sky. The printers hand you an apron with movable type in the pockets, their entire induction ceremony. Sedition, the typesetter observes, is easier to set in twelve point. Behind the briefing room curtain, Brill rehearses denials to a mirror that is officially not there either.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_assembly The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

fail.text: You add your name, and a constable adds his elbow, catching you just under the byline. The printers pull you inside and ink your bruise by accident, so you are technically published. Membership, the typesetter says, is mostly this. Outside, Brill assures a crowd that nobody has been struck, least of all by anyone.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_assembly The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Midnight in the print shop, where the presses idle under a Corrective Bulletin taped to the beam: all weather must now be filed as opinion. At the morning briefing the podium creaked during a denial, and Brill patted it, off the record, the way you would soothe a horse. The printers stand around a barometer and a decision.

choices.text: Sell tomorrow's denial to the grain merchants

outcomes.text: Merchants have worked out that Ministry denials are the only reliable forecast, since whatever is denied is certainly happening. You supply tomorrow's text, they short the harvest, and everyone in the back room becomes richer and slightly worse. A second payment arrives for the denial after that, in advance, on subscription. The typesetter raises the price of page four by exactly what you were paid, because now that is what truth costs.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_retaliation The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Run page four all night by hand

success.text: The press runs until dawn on shifts of two, and page four goes out folded inside sermons, recipes, and one knitting pattern. By noon the district knows the barometer reading the way it knows a neighbor's cough. The intern with three lanyards buys a copy with her own money, looks both ways, and reads it twice. Brill denies a rise in circulation from behind the podium, which creaks twice, and he thanks it before catching himself.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_retaliation The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

fail.text: The press runs hot and takes your sleeve, and briefly some of you, into the rollers. You are pulled free inked to the shoulder, a limited edition of one. The run finishes late and short, and constables collect the bundles that were slowest to leave. Wrapping your arm, the typesetter observes that freedom of the press assumes the press is on your side.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_retaliation The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Print the Bulletin beside the barometer, no comment

outcomes.text: You set the Corrective Bulletin and the barometer reading in identical type, side by side, and let them fight. No commentary is added, so no law is broken, and the town supplies the commentary internally at breakfast. The Standards Desk requests forty copies, officially for review, unofficially for the break room. When a reporter asks Brill which column is the opinion, the podium creaks before he does.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_retaliation The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: The columns run side by side, and the Ministry responds by outlawing adjacency. Juxtaposition becomes a licensed activity, and your license is refused before it exists. The Bulletin's next edition is printed on a page by itself, for its own protection. Brill announces the rule standing slightly to one side of the podium, having stopped trusting furniture.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_retaliation The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

The Notice of Voluntary Silence arrives by intern, who cannot meet anyone's eye over the topmost lanyard. A ribbon marked CEREMONIAL seals the press, and the typesetter's trial is set for the first clear day, which the Ministry insists is soon. A leaked memo reclassifies the podium as an alleged surface, pending review. Brill has begun standing beside it at briefings, loyal but careful.

choices.text: Scatter the type trays through the neighborhood

outcomes.text: The alphabet leaves the shop in aprons and market baskets, a drawer of vowels to the baker, the consonants to the laundress. By evening the district is one large press that smells of bread and starch. The Ministry seals an empty room and declares victory over furniture, its specialty. The typesetter keeps only the letter Y, for questions, and the intern looks carefully away while the semicolons go.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

The typesetter keeps only the letter Y, for questions, and the intern looks carefully away while the semicolons go.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: The baker's boy drops the tray of capitals on your foot, and the alphabet bolts down an alley with you hopping after it. A constable helps gather the letters, sorting them kindly, arresting nobody, embarrassed for everyone, while a crowd collects to supervise. The E's are never fully recovered, and page four runs slightly breathless for a week. Your foot files no grievance, having seen what happens to those who do.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Demand the Ministry define enthusiasm in writing

success.text: The definitions office, cornered, produces a committee, and the committee produces a draft: enthusiasm, a rising quality of air, particulate. The word particulate escapes into the record before anyone can deny it, and the record, unlike the sky, is admissible. The trial date develops a wobble. Behind Brill, the alleged surface creaks in the old way, like a colleague clearing its throat before a correction.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

fail.text: The Ministry defines enthusiasm as whatever the Ministry is currently standing behind, a definition with troubling implications for the podium. Your demand is filed under hostile curiosity, and your name is spelled correctly, which from them is a threat. The trial date moves closer, as dates do when pushed. Brill reads the definition aloud and, for one syllable, sounds unconvinced by his own air.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Follow the Ministry's laundry cart to the archives

success.text: In the sub-basement you find the Ledger of Operational Skies, the Ministry's private weather record, accurate to the hour, kept so the official laundry is never rained on. You copy a month of it and sell the copies at cost, plus a modest fee for nerve. The town learns the Ministry has always known, which is somehow both better and worse. The printers are glad of the proof and cool about the fee, and the typesetter charges you for her opinion of it, to make a point.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

fail.text: You find the Ledger of Operational Skies, and you find a buyer for it before you find anything else. A single merchant now owns the only honest weather in the region and rents it out by the hour, and the queue outside his office sours gently in the rain he saw coming. The printers hear about it, as printers do. At the next briefing you cannot look at the podium, which has never once charged anyone to be stood behind.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_coalition The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Unexpected professionals fill the printers' yard: the laundry guild, two almanac men, a bell ringer, and several fishermen, all of whom require the sky to be a fact. Nobody names the arrangement; it is only people who hang washing agreeing with people who read barometers. At the Ministry, the podium now stands wedged level on folded Corrective Bulletins, the first work those have ever done. Brill has requested, in writing, that the press corps like him more.

choices.text: Ask Brill to lean his weight on the podium

outcomes.text: Your question is procedural, polite, and physically unanswerable: if the surface is alleged, would the Spokesman kindly demonstrate by leaning somewhere else. Brill looks at the podium, the podium waits in its way, and he grips it like a railing in weather, live, in front of everyone. The room does not laugh at him exactly, it laughs near him, and the guards along the wall find themselves standing easy. By evening the Ministry announces a review of all load-bearing allegations.
effects: schedule 5 r18_ministry_denial_crisis The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: Brill takes the question well, which is the problem, because his superiors are watching him take it. Your credential is revoked mid-sentence by the intern, who mouths an apology over lanyard number two. The briefing ends early, the room scolded like a classroom in the rain. From the corridor you hear the podium creak alone, denied even an audience.
effects: schedule 5 r18_ministry_denial_crisis The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Accept the Clear Skies Gazette correspondent post

outcomes.text: The Gazette pays in advance and edits in retrospect, and your byline now floats over sentences you would not carry in a bucket. Your first assignment is a warm profile of the podium's planned replacement, which does not exist yet and therefore cannot be denied. The laundry guild takes your washing off the shared line without a word. At the Ministry canteen the pudding is excellent, the new door guard learns your name first, and everyone likes you, on instruction.
effects: schedule 5 r18_ministry_denial_crisis The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Put coin in for the joint almanac's paper

outcomes.text: Everyone gives what they can, and your three coins join the pile for paper that will carry tide tables, feast days, and the truth about the sky in one binding. The joint almanac is planned in an afternoon by people who believe in different gods and the same barometer. The fishermen promise distribution by boat, beyond the reach of ribbons marked CEREMONIAL. The typesetter lays out the title page and leaves a gap in it shaped like whatever the Ministry does next.

outcomes.text: Constables raid the meeting on grounds of unlicensed adjacency, professionals having gathered too near other professionals. In the shuffle a crate of tide tables finds your shin, and the yard's mood follows the constables down the lane, growing. The almanac is agreed anyway, in doorways, quietly. Passing the Ministry on the way home, you hear Brill through a window, rehearsing the word friends to the alleged surface.
