text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Every seat is taken for the grand briefing announcing the typesetter's trial, and the Ministry has built a stage for the occasion. Halfway through the third denial the podium gives out under Brill entirely, wood and allegation together, and he continues from the floor, denying first the collapse and then the floor. The room watches a man refuse gravity out of loyalty. The trial opens on what the Ministry is calling the next clear day, which is to say immediately.

choices.text: Read the Ministry's statement into the trial record

outcomes.text: You take the stand, take the statement, and take the fee, in that order, reading aloud that the air has been consistently celebratory. Your voice is steady and the money is steadier, a retainer now, recurring, like weather. From the dock the typesetter corrects your pronunciation of particulate, gently, and the gallery loves her for it. Outside, the price of an honest almanac rises again, and you would know, having helped set it.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_compromise The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Fill the gallery with people who saw the sky

outcomes.text: The courtroom fills with the laundress, the bell ringer, four fishermen, and sixty others, each sworn in, each asked what the weather was, each answering smoky. It is the dullest testimony ever given, and the gallery treats it like opera. The prosecutor objects to the sample size, and the sample size keeps arriving. By afternoon the clerk has stopped writing enthusiasm and started writing ditto.

outcomes.text: Sixty witnesses arrive at a courtroom built for forty, and the argument about the difference is conducted partly on your ribs. The bailiffs clear the steps with professional regret, and the crowd outside stays, humming. Testimony proceeds without you while you count stars that are officially also enthusiasm. Inside, the typesetter tells the court the weather anyway, alone, which was always plan B, and also plan A.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_compromise The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Argue sedition needs a fact to bend

success.text: Your motion is simple: sedition is the distortion of true conditions, and the Ministry certifies there are none, so the charge has nothing to stand on. The judge takes a recess, looks out a window for a long moment, and returns older. The charge is reduced to unlicensed accuracy, a fine, the judge remarking that a lie about nothing is at worst poetry. In the corridor the Ministry's lawyers quietly ask one another what else has nothing to stand on, and everyone thinks of the podium.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_compromise The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

fail.text: The motion is heard, admired, and denied, the judge noting that your logic is persuasive but the Ministry is adjacent. Your argument enters the record as a curiosity, like a fish with two tails. The trial resumes at increased speed, as if embarrassed. That evening the Ministry states that the law has never been better, from a temporary podium it is careful not to touch.
effects: schedule 2 r18_ministry_denial_compromise The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Terms arrive in a conference room with water nobody drinks. Whatever remains of the matter against the typesetter will be settled for a fine, and the fine waived, if the printers publish all weather under the heading MOODS OF THE REGION. The collapsed podium lies in a storeroom, unrepaired, because no Ministry carpenter will sign for work on an alleged object. Brill delivers the offer himself and then stays at the edge of the room, hoping somebody will tell him he is doing well.

choices.text: Refuse the heading, pay her fine together

outcomes.text: The fine is covered inside an hour by a hat passed through three trades, mostly small coins and one pearl button nobody claims. The heading is refused in a letter so polite the Ministry reads it twice to find the no. Brill receives the letter personally and keeps, for reasons he does not examine, the envelope. That night the printers' carpenter is seen measuring the storeroom window, for purposes he declines to file.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_reversal The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: The hat goes around and comes back light, and the argument over who gave what is the first the printers have had in weeks, which may have been the offer's whole design. Somebody's elbow finds you in the crush by the door, and the crush moves outside, and stays there muttering. The fine is covered by nightfall anyway, ungracefully. In its storeroom the podium waits, unfixed another day, listening to the Ministry deny it needs repair.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_reversal The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

choices.text: Accept MOODS, but print the millibars under it

outcomes.text: The printers accept the heading with a courtesy that should have worried someone, and beneath MOODS OF THE REGION they run the barometer's number, which has no synonyms. Today's mood: 941 and falling, bring in the washing. The Ministry cannot object to arithmetic without opening the question of what numbers are, a door even they will not touch. Circulation doubles among citizens who officially only wish to know how the region feels.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_reversal The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: The heading runs, the number runs, and the Ministry classifies the millibar as a rumor of foreign origin. Barometers must now be registered as opinions with moving parts. The almanac men take theirs into the cellar with the type trays, where the instruments carry on being right in the dark. Brill thanks the press for its moods, and nobody laughs, and he minds that more than the smoke.

choices.text: Break the seal and repossess the press

success.text: The ribbon marked CEREMONIAL turns out to be precisely as strong as ribbon. The press leaves on six shoulders through a crowd with opinions about seals, and the crowd's opinion carries the day, and also the press. Several printers do not love the method, and the typesetter says so with her whole face while helping lift. In the confusion someone, unrecorded, wheels the broken podium out too, on the principle that nothing should be left behind enemy allegations.

By morning the press has a guard, the guard has a chair, and the chair has a requisition approved faster than any weather report in Ministry history. The typesetter visits your bedside and does not say she told you so, out loud, in words.
effects: schedule 3 r18_ministry_denial_reversal The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Late light finds the podium in the print shop, fetched during the week and mended without paperwork, standing level on a new leg with a small brass barometer set into its top, since a carpenter will fix anything an employer has dropped. Then the intern arrives, out of uniform, down to a single lanyard, and unfolds a Ministry invoice dated last spring: french polishing, one podium, existing, approved and signed by Spokesman Brill. It is the first Ministry document known to admit that anything exists. She says she has relaminated her last denial.

choices.text: Publish a notarized facsimile of the invoice

outcomes.text: The facsimile runs on page one, sworn before a notary who reads it twice for pleasure. Existing sits there in column two, between quantity and cost, in the Ministry's own hand. Within the week the record against the typesetter is struck, and the fine, where one was paid, comes back in an envelope marked miscellaneous. An institution may deny a podium or polish it, the notary observes, but invoicing both was the error.
effects: schedule 4 r18_ministry_denial_legacy The Ministry denies this reminder exists.

outcomes.text: The facsimile runs, and the Ministry calls the invoice a forgery, then an authentic document concerning hypothetical furniture, then a test of public loyalty, each statement issued from a new temporary podium that nobody touches. A review of whether documents exist is announced, no completion date given. Arguing with the Ministry resumes as the regional pastime. Brill reads the denials at the briefing, and his voice, witnesses agree, has started doing the creaking.

choices.text: Auction the invoice back to the Ministry

outcomes.text: The Ministry bids against two merchants and a museum for its own admission, wins, and pays you in a room without windows. The invoice goes into a drawer that legally contains nothing. Honest weather in the region is now a commodity with exactly one previous owner, you, and the price climbs weekly. The intern watches the folder vanish and leaves without any lanyard at all, which from her is shouting.

choices.text: Hide the intern among the printers

success.text: She is issued an apron, a bunk above the paper store, and the title keeper of the brass barometer, which she takes seriously twice a day. By week's end she sets nine words a minute and has stopped apologizing at the front of sentences. The Ministry lists her as resigned for reasons of enthusiasm. Each evening she checks the podium's level, out of habit, the way you keep checking on anything that has been denied.

The typesetter sets your bandage as a headline joke, thinks better of it, then runs it anyway on the proof copy only.

text:
MINISTRY DENIAL

Weeks later the Ministry of Clear Skies rebrands as the Bureau of Atmospheric Feelings, and the briefing room reopens under new management and old habits. The podium, attending on loan at its own insistence, has filed Form D-1, a Grievance of an Existing Object, the first ever accepted, citing one season of denial and one collapse. Brill, no longer a spokesman, reads the barometer aloud in the market now, where his voice is admired and the numbers he announces are, for the first time, true. Page one carries the grievance in full; the weather stays on page four, as is proper.

choices.text: Sell the Bureau its new crisis manual

outcomes.text: You write the Bureau a manual on message discipline, chapter one being never sign for furniture. They pay in advance and post two new guards outside the briefing room to protect the replacement podium from grievances, a precaution taken directly from your appendix. The manual is excellent, which is the trouble with it. In the market Brill announces rain with your old confidence, and you find you cannot listen all the way to the end.

The manual is excellent, which is the trouble with it. In the market Brill announces rain with your old confidence, and you find you cannot listen all the way to the end.

choices.text: Install the podium as the shop's reading stand

success.text: Six of you carry it through the double doors, and the typesetter reads the day's weather from behind it to a shop that goes quiet on its own. It stands there with a brass barometer, a new leg, and a grievance number, more documentation than most citizens achieve. You are entered in the shop's book as a printer of observable facts, apron and all. As you sign, the podium creaks once, in what is now legally recognized as agreement.

fail.text: Podiums are heavier than allegations, and the doorway collects its toll from your knuckles and one hinge. You are entered in the shop's book anyway, under printer, with a note reading see also, door. The stand is up by supper and the shop gathers around it without being asked. Your hand will heal; the hinge is filing separately.

choices.text: Publish the podium's grievance as a broadside

outcomes.text: The grievance sells out three printings and is quoted in kitchens as a caution against standing behind things. Asked to respond, the Bureau declines to deny anything, citing a new policy of admitting whatever is load-bearing. The briefing room guards are reassigned to the market, where they mostly listen to Brill announce the pressure and nod, off duty in their hearts. The podium waives royalties, asking only that the record show it was there the whole time.

The broadside sells, but the Bureau buys the fourth printing entire and reissues it stapled to a rebuttal, which is longer, and free. The rebuttal's central claim is that furniture cannot lodge grievances, a sentence contradicted by the Bureau's own accepted Form D-1, and the town square divides over it audibly. Arguing with the Bureau resumes as the seasonal sport. The podium takes it well, having learned from the best that you survive nearly anything by continuing to stand there.

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

Dawn on the west jetty, and the river has two owners since Thursday. The Border Investment Authority redrew the banks into profitable jurisdictions and left forty families on the gravel bar in between, which is profitable to nobody and therefore does not exist. The volunteer ferry crews will run them across, but the checkpoint is held by the Authority's one incorruptible officer: a customs goose that refuses all coin and accepts only three identical bread forms per crossing. The Pilot puts it plainly: she can beat the current, the fog, and the Deputy Comptroller of Moving Persons, but nobody has ever beaten the goose.

choices.text: Sell the Authority your survey of the gravel bar

outcomes.text: The Deputy Comptroller pays in fresh notes and files your survey under Assets, Unrealized. By evening the gravel bar has an appraisal, a docking fee, and a waiting list to leave it. The families now owe rent on the place they are stranded, which even the goose appears to find vulgar. You are richer, and you tell yourself somebody would have measured the river eventually, somebody with a worse conscience, or a better one.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_assembly Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Sign on with a ferry crew for no pay

outcomes.text: You buy your own oar, which the Pilot says is the entire interview. By noon you know the whistle code, the rota, and the names the families use for each other, which is how you learn that the grandmother who counts every head twice is called Vess, and that she counts you now too. The crews do not thank you. They just stop explaining things, which among boat people is a promotion.

outcomes.text: You catch a gunwale with your ribs on the first practice run and the river takes your hat as a filing fee. The Pilot fishes you out by the collar and remarks that drowning requires a permit you do not hold. You are in, bruised, and the whistle code now contains one note that means specifically you. Vess counts you twice anyway, to be safe.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_assembly Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Demand the checkpoint produce its own charter

outcomes.text: The clerk on duty goes looking for the founding documents and comes back pale: the charter authorizes inspection of goods, and people are not goods, a point the Authority has spent years insisting on for tax purposes. Pending review, the families may receive visitors, blankets, and soup. The goose observes the whole exchange like a judge who already knows the verdict and is waiting for the humans to catch up.

outcomes.text: The clerk finds the charter, and appended to it a rider empowering the Authority to define goods at its discretion. By close of business the forty families are reclassified as unaccompanied freight, which comes with storage fees. You have made the paperwork worse, which on this river is the only weather that matters. The goose eats your copy of the complaint, which is the nearest thing to sympathy the checkpoint offers.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_assembly Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

Midnight in the boathouse, which smells of tar and proofing dough. The Crumb Clerk, formerly the Authority's own forms baker until she defected with the recipe, lays out the problem: the goose requires three bread forms per crossing, identical in weight, crumb, and crust, and it can detect a forged loaf at forty paces. Forty families means one hundred and twenty perfect loaves, plus decoys, plus a schedule. The Pilot chalks the river on the wall and asks who here can bake, row, or lie, ideally all three.

choices.text: Take a shift at the ovens and the oars

outcomes.text: You bake badly and row worse, but you show up for both, which is the whole religion of the rota. The Crumb Clerk promotes your sixth loaf to decoy grade and your ninth to actual bread. Vess sends soup over for the night shift with instructions that the goose is not to have any, on principle. By dawn there are forty perfect loaves and a crew that argues like family, which is to say constantly and about nothing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_retaliation Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: The oven and you disagree about the meaning of moderate heat, and the oven wins the argument on your forearm. The Crumb Clerk wraps it, notes that scars are the only certificate the Authority cannot revoke, and moves you to kneading. The loaves get made, mostly around you. You have learned the standard the hard way, which is how the standard prefers to be learned.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_retaliation Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Argue the goose's standard into a loophole

success.text: You read the checkpoint's bread specification aloud until you find it: three identical forms are required, but no clause says they must arrive with the same person. Split across three ferries, one loaf each, a crossing becomes a single application arriving in installments. The Crumb Clerk calls it the most beautiful misreading she has heard since she left the civil service. Even the goose, consulted at a distance, does not immediately object, which from the goose is a standing ovation.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_retaliation Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

fail.text: You present your loophole at the checkpoint and the goose listens with the patience of a very old empire. Then it eats the specification, which turns out to be the only copy, making the standard whatever the goose remembers it to be. The Deputy Comptroller thanks you personally for simplifying enforcement. The Pilot asks you, kindly, to do your reading further from the water.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_retaliation Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Corner the market on regulation flour

outcomes.text: You buy every sack of checkpoint grade flour on both banks and resell it at a price you describe as reflecting conditions. The Authority buys most of it back at a markup, because a shortage looks bad in the quarterly figures, and shortages are always somebody else's crime. The crews bake on with gray market rye and do not look at you. The goose accepts rye, as it happens, but Vess's opinion of you is now also identical in weight, crumb, and crust.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_retaliation Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

Three barges arrive flying the Authority's pennant, and by lunch the river has a new rule: every vessel must carry a stamped manifest, the stamp is kept in a locked office, and the office is only open while the office is closed. The Deputy Comptroller of Moving Persons calls this streamlining. Two ferries are impounded on a sandbank pending compliance, with the Pilot's boat next on the list. Out on the gravel bar, Vess has started rationing the soup, which she does by lying about how much she eats.

choices.text: Appeal the impound to the river magistrate

outcomes.text: The magistrate reads the new rule twice, asks when the stamp office opens, receives the answer, and rules that a requirement which cannot be met is a fee, and fees on stranded persons require a schedule the Authority never filed. The ferries are released with the Authority's apologies, drafted by the Authority, apologizing mostly to itself. The families cheer the boats home from the gravel bar. The goose attends the hearing uninvited and is entered into the record as a friend of the court.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: The magistrate reads the new rule twice, asks when the stamp office opens, receives the answer, and rules that a requirement which cannot be met is a fee, and fees on stranded persons require a schedule the Authority never filed. The goose attends the hearing uninvited and is entered into the record as a friend of the court.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: The magistrate turns out to hold an advisory seat on the Border Investment Authority's oversight board, which oversees itself. Your appeal is accepted, commended, and denied in a single sentence, which you are invited to admire as craftsmanship. The impound stands. The goose at least bites the magistrate on the way out, an opinion the record declines to preserve.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Cut the impounded ferries loose at night

success.text: Fog, a hacksaw, and the Pilot humming to keep time: by three in the morning both ferries are drifting home under borrowed stars. The Authority finds a sandbank holding nothing but its own signage and impounds the signage to save face. The crews repaint the boats by dawn, slightly different colors, slightly different names. The river, asked to identify the vessels, declines.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

fail.text: The night watchman turns out to be conscientious, which nobody had budgeted for, and his truncheon finds your shoulder twice before the fog takes sides. You limp home with rope burns and one liberated rowlock. The Authority doubles the guard and bills the crews for the watchman's overtime. The Pilot pours you something brown and says the plan was perfect except for containing you.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Run the soup line out to the gravel bar

outcomes.text: You row out with soup, blankets, and the boy who wants his chess set back, it having been impounded inside a ferry as suspected cargo. Vess feeds you first and argues about it afterward, her tradition. Word moves along both banks that the Authority is starving people over a stamp, and both banks, whatever their jurisdiction, remember owning spoons. By week's end the checkpoint smells of donated bread, which makes the goose thoughtful.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: Halfway out the current shrugs and you swim the last stretch towing the soup, which arrives as broth. Vess wraps you in the blanket you brought her and will not hear otherwise. The families eat, thinly. On the way back you notice the Authority's barge crews watching in silence, and one of them, when the Deputy Comptroller is not looking, points you toward the safer channel.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_coalition Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

The back room of a bakery on the east bank, flour on every lung. The Crumb Clerk has brought in half of bread row, the millers are in for the cost of the sacks, and one of the Authority's own barge stokers has arrived with a gift: blank manifest paper, liberated a sheet at a time in his boots. The plan on the table is logistics with a candle on it: decoy manifests for boats that do not exist, real crossings hidden in the gaps, everything timed to the one hour the checkpoint softens. The goose takes lunch at noon precisely, and during lunch, the Crumb Clerk swears, it reviews bread forms with a measurably open mind.

choices.text: License the decoy manifest trick to smugglers

outcomes.text: The brandy trade pays handsomely for your paper ghosts, and inside a week the river carries more fictional boats than real ones. The Authority commissions a census of vessels and gets a number that ends in a fraction. You are paid twice, once for the trick and once to forget who bought it. The crossing plan still works, but the Pilot now checks your pockets before planning sessions, publicly, as a joke, except it is not a joke.
effects: schedule 2 r18_refugee_ferry_crisis Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Knit the bakers and boat crews into one rota

success.text: You build the rota like a tide table: who bakes, who rows, who stands at the checkpoint asking the Deputy Comptroller long questions about his career. Bread row and the boats have hated each other since a flour barge sank in somebody's grandfather's day, and you get them to agree that the grandfather was wrong, both grandfathers, simultaneously. The Crumb Clerk says the schedule is the finest document she has seen since defecting. Vess is issued a copy, for counting purposes.
effects: schedule 2 r18_refugee_ferry_crisis Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

fail.text: The first joint meeting reopens the flour barge question of some grandfathers ago, and a proofing paddle catches you above the eye when you step between history and itself. Peace is restored over your swelling. The rota gets made, largely out of embarrassment. The Pilot says every good crew is founded on exactly one apology, and now they have theirs.
effects: schedule 2 r18_refugee_ferry_crisis Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Draft decoy manifests too boring to inspect

outcomes.text: You write cargo lists of such crushing dullness that inspection becomes a punishment: gravel, ballast, further gravel, a small quantity of damp rope. The checkpoint clerks begin waving your boats through to protect their own afternoons. The Deputy Comptroller institutes random checks and randomly checks nothing, twice. Meanwhile the real manifests, the ones with people on them, travel folded inside loaves the goose has already approved, which is the one drawer of the checkpoint nobody audits.
effects: schedule 2 r18_refugee_ferry_crisis Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: You get artistic, and one manifest lists forty gross of decorative hinges, which turns out to be a smuggling code already in use by the brandy trade. Two of your paper boats are ambushed by excise men and are not there, which is awkward for everyone's reports. The checkpoint tightens. The Pilot bans the word decorative from the boathouse.
effects: schedule 2 r18_refugee_ferry_crisis Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

Noon, and the goose is not eating. The Deputy Comptroller, acting on a tip about lunchtime crossings, has abolished lunch by decree: Directive 40 declares the checkpoint continuously vigilant, and a junior clerk now stands holding the goose's midday bread just out of reach. Three ferries are already midriver with every family aboard, Vess in the bow of the lead boat counting heads against the wind. The goose stares at its withheld bread with an expression the Pilot, through the glass, identifies as constitutional.

choices.text: Bring all three boats in together as one

success.text: The Pilot signals and the ferries close up until they land as a single hull: one crossing, one application, forty families and nine loaves presented in a block too coherent to sort. Procedure requires the checkpoint to process the queue in order, and the queue is everyone. Somewhere in minute twenty the junior clerk's arm gives out, the bread sags into reach, and the goose declares lunch unilaterally. By the time the Deputy Comptroller restores vigilance, the gravel bar is a place people used to be.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_compromise Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

fail.text: The current has views about formation rowing, and you take an oar handle to the jaw holding station. But the boats stay in a block, and a block is a queue, and a queue must be processed. The families land bruised and counted. Vess makes the tally twice, arrives at everyone, and starts on the soup before the checkpoint finishes its paperwork on the first loaf.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_compromise Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Read Directive 40 aloud at the checkpoint

outcomes.text: Directive 40 declares the checkpoint continuously vigilant, and you keep reading, because the annex defines vigilance as the full attention of the certified inspecting officer. The certified inspecting officer is the goose. The goose's full attention is on its withheld bread, therefore the checkpoint is out of compliance with its own decree, therefore closed. The clerks are still diagramming this when the ferries touch gravel on the profitable side.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_compromise Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: You read Directive 40 aloud, and the Deputy Comptroller listens with the smile of a man who wrote the annex. Vigilance, he notes, is defined as whatever survives review, and the boats are held at the buoy line while the checkpoint reviews, which takes exactly as long as it wants to. It is the goose that finally breaks: it bites the junior clerk, takes its bread, and while the checkpoint tends its wounded, the Pilot finds the current and does not waste it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_compromise Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Sell the Deputy the tip about a decoy hour

outcomes.text: You inform, but curated: the Deputy Comptroller buys the schedule of a crossing that does not exist and masses his barges around an empty hour. He pays in advance, which tells you everything about how often he is right. The real boats land in the shadow of his own ambush, and the families walk ashore behind a wall of Authority uniforms facing the wrong way. You are paid, the crossing succeeds, and nobody on the water knows how close you came to being worse. The goose knows, but the goose is not talking.
effects: schedule 3 r18_refugee_ferry_compromise Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

A week later the river has three owners, the Authority having discovered that redrawing lines is cheaper than defending them, and a fresh cluster of families is stranded on the newest margin. The Deputy Comptroller proposes terms in a tent with sandwiches: licensed humanitarian crossings, four a month, at a fee per head he calls a contribution. The Pilot wants to hear him out, the Crumb Clerk wants to bake him into something, and the goose has been seated at the table as a neutral party, being the only officer both sides trust. Its bread, everyone notices, is served first.

choices.text: Negotiate the fee into the Authority's column

success.text: You accept the fee per head in principle, then table the counting question: heads must be certified, certification is inspection, and inspection fees fall to the requesting party, which is the Authority. By the third pot of tea the Deputy Comptroller has agreed to pay the crews four crossings a month of what he still insists is a contribution. He signs proudly. The goose stamps the treaty with a webbed foot, which the clerks decide is easier to notarize than to explain.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_reversal Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

fail.text: You get the fee down, but the definitions get away from you, and the final text defines a crossing as one vessel, one direction, one calendar day, which makes the return trips contraband. The crews spend a demoralizing week rowing home empty by way of a neutral sandbar. The families still cross, but the arithmetic now leans on everyone. The goose refuses to stamp the treaty, which both sides agree is the most substantive legal review it received.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_reversal Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Pull the goose's personnel file from the archive

success.text: The archive clerk trades you the file for your silence about the state of the archive. Inside: the goose predates the Authority, was inherited from the old ferry toll, and its bread standard is the only continuously enforced regulation on the river, which by the doctrine of longest custom makes it the checkpoint's senior officer. You sell a copy to a law scrivener who nearly weeps at it. The crews are annoyed you charged money for the miracle, but the miracle is now circulating, stamped and paid for.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_reversal Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

fail.text: You find the file, and beside it the Authority's standing finder's fee for exactly this document, which they have wanted suppressed for years. You take the fee. The file goes into a locked drawer, and you tell yourself copies exist, which is true, because you kept one, which you tell nobody. The Crumb Clerk asks what you found, you say mostly feathers, and she looks at you the way the goose looks at unsatisfactory bread.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_reversal Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Feed both delegations from one pot

outcomes.text: You cook for the whole tent, families' recipes, crews' portions, and seat the junior clerks among the people they have been counting. It is harder to price a head that has passed you the salt. The fee survives on paper but dies in practice, waived clerk by clerk at the checkpoint's discretion, which turns out to be the goose's discretion, which was always bread. Vess supervises the pot and pronounces the Deputy Comptroller too thin to be evil at scale, which he visibly does not know what to do with.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_reversal Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: The pot scalds you when the tent's leg gives out, and lunch becomes triage. But the delegations end up rinsing burns in the same river, jackets off, ranks illegible. Something in the negotiation never quite recovers its formality after that. The terms stay bad, yet the clerks now say good morning to the boats they are supposed to be counting.
effects: schedule 4 r18_refugee_ferry_reversal Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

The Authority's records barge arrives to formalize the river once and for all: every manifest, directive, and appeal, bound and aboard, for a consolidated filing that will define who may move and what they owe for the privilege. It draws too much water for the channel, and everyone qualified to say so is stranded on a margin somewhere, uncounted. Then the goose's personnel file surfaces, in the way of all true documents, at the worst possible moment for the people who filed it: the goose predates the Authority, and by longest custom its bread standard outranks every regulation on the barge. The Deputy Comptroller calls an emergency hearing aboard the barge itself, which is the last place anyone should hold anything.

choices.text: Buy the salvage rights before the verdict

outcomes.text: You have read the channel depths and the barge's waterline, and you quietly buy the salvage concession for the reach below the checkpoint at a price reflecting its worthlessness. At the hearing's climax the barge finds the sandbar, settles, and begins feeding the river forty years of filings. Everything the Authority ever wrote about who may move becomes, legally, your soggy property, and you charge them by the crate to get it back, which they must, because it is evidence. The Pilot calls it the first honest fortune ever made on this river, and does not mean it as a compliment.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_legacy Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Take the ferries out to the sinking barge

outcomes.text: The barge finds the sandbar in the middle of the hearing and settles with a sound like a verdict, and the ferries are alongside before the Authority finishes deciding whose fault it is. Every clerk, stoker, and comptroller comes off dry, handed down into boats crewed by people they spent a season impounding, and the snapped oars come out of your own pocket without a vote. The filings stay aboard, dissolving in an orderly queue. Vess counts the rescued twice, because that is what you do with people, and paper does not get counted.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_legacy Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: You go into the water twice hauling clerks over gunwales, and the second time a crate of directives lands on you, briefly attempting enforcement. Everyone lives, including you, on the Pilot's insistence. The river accepts the paperwork in lieu of everything else. By dusk the checkpoint has no regulations left to consult except the goose, which has them all memorized in bread.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_legacy Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

choices.text: Move that the goose chair the hearing

outcomes.text: By longest custom the senior officer presides, and the senior officer is already at the table, eating an exhibit. The motion carries because nobody can locate a rule against it in time, the rules being aboard a barge taking water. The goose's first ruling from the chair is delivered by stamping every transit application on the table, wet foot, in ink, approved. Its second ruling is lunch, there is no third, and the hearing stands adjourned on the best jurisprudence the river has ever had.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_legacy Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

outcomes.text: The Deputy Comptroller objects that a goose cannot hold office, and produces from the hold the one box of precedents that proves it. The effort of retrieving the box is what shifts the cargo, and the barge goes down with the precedents held triumphantly aloft. Your motion is denied by a drowning archive. The goose watches its rival paperwork sink with an expression you would call promotion, if you had to notarize it.
effects: schedule 5 r18_refugee_ferry_legacy Three breads, one goose, forty families waiting

text:
REFUGEE FERRY

Spring, and the checkpoint is under new management, in that the management is the goose and nobody can produce a document saying otherwise, the documents having drowned. It presides from a straw lined crate, stamping transit applications wet footed at a rate the clerks describe as frankly unsustainable and the families describe as Tuesday. The rule of the river is now short enough to teach a child: anyone may cross, and everyone leaves with three breads for the road, baked on a rota the Crumb Clerk refuses to write down on the grounds that paper has a poor safety record here. The Deputy Comptroller of Moving Persons has been reassigned to inland weights and measures, where, reports say, he is being outmaneuvered by a set of scales.

choices.text: Stay on the boats for the new season

outcomes.text: You stay, and the ferry stops being a caper and becomes a timetable, which is the quietest victory available on water. Families cross on schedule, name their arrivals after the boats, and one baby is called after the Pilot's vessel, which the Pilot pretends to mind. Vess retires from counting heads, the count being reliably everyone, and takes up counting loaves instead, to keep her eye in. When new margins get drawn somewhere upstream, the crews are asked for by name, yours included.

You stay, and the ferry stops being a caper and becomes a timetable, which is the quietest victory available on water. Families cross on schedule, name their arrivals after the boats, and one baby is called after the Pilot's vessel, which the Pilot pretends to mind. Vess retires from counting heads, the count being reliably everyone, and takes up counting loaves instead, to keep her eye in. When new margins get drawn somewhere upstream, the crews are asked for by name, yours included.

You stay, and the first spring flood tries the timetable and finds it sincere: you pull a mooring cable with your back and feel the exact price of it. The boats hold, the crossings hold, and you walk crooked for a fortnight in a place where everybody knows why. Soup arrives at your door on a rota nobody admits to writing. The goose visits once, inspects your convalescence, and stamps the doorstep on the way out.

choices.text: Write the goose's rulings into a river code

success.text: You draft the whole jurisprudence in one afternoon: crossings are approved, bread is the fee, the fee is refunded as lunch, and appeals may be lodged with the goose during, and only during, the eating of the appeal. Scriveners from three jurisdictions copy it out, because it is the only code on the river that fits on one page and has never lost on review. The Crumb Clerk allows that some paper can be trusted, in small amounts, if a goose has walked on it first. The stamped page hangs in the boathouse, slightly webbed, entirely binding.

fail.text: You draft the code but cannot resist a preamble, the preamble uses the word heretofore, and the goose eats it, correctly. The clerks take this as precedent that all preambles are edible, which becomes the river's first constitutional doctrine and also its last. The code survives as spoken custom, which was the goose's ruling all along, you just failed to read the eating. Your surviving draft is used in the boathouse to wrap bread, an honor you are assured is significant.

choices.text: Franchise the crossing as a scenic tour

outcomes.text: You sell comfortable passage to travelers who want to see the famous goose, at famous goose prices, and the money is honestly ridiculous. The tours run in the slack hours, and the tourists are made to carry sacks of flour ashore, which they pay extra for, believing it authentic, and it is. The crews take your money for the kitty and keep a professional distance from your methods. The goose stamps the tourists through with everyone else, three breads for the road, having never once in its long career been able to tell an important person from a person.

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Eleven at night, and the fuse-crimping station wears a tarpaulin like a shroud. The Toolsetter lifts it to show you a pump valve seated in the crimper's bed, machined to fit within a thousandth. Peace Through Quarterly Growth has ordered nine hundred kinetic deliverables by quarter's end, and the order board says so in a font that has never been near a funeral. At the end of the line sits the first finished unit, a bomb stamped HANDLE WITH DIPLOMACY, waiting on a gift lot for somebody's treaty. The plan is one station a week, quiet as inventory, until the audit finds plowshares it cannot prove were ever anything else.

choices.text: Take the night wrench and retool station one

success.text: The crimper comes apart like it has been wanting to, and by four it presses pump valves with the same stroke that used to seat fuses. The Toolsetter initials your work and adds you to the rota under a job title that does not exist yet. The bomb stamped HANDLE WITH DIPLOMACY gets wheeled to the side aisle and marked PENDING DIPLOMACY, which nobody is authorized to schedule.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_assembly The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The die slips at half past two and takes a knuckle with it, and the crash brings a watchman who has to be shown an extremely boring clipboard until he leaves. Word gets round the district that something happens at the shell works after dark, which is true, which is the problem. The Toolsetter wraps your hand and adds you to the rota anyway, on the grounds that you bled on the machine and it is therefore partly yours.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_assembly The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: File a maintenance variance covering the whole line

outcomes.text: You draft Form M-11, Scheduled Recalibration, in prose so grey that the clerk who receives it develops a headache and stamps it just to make it stop. The line is now legally permitted to be full of unfamiliar machines for a quarter. In the side aisle, the stamped bomb acquires a variance number of its own, which makes it, administratively, a maintenance issue.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_assembly The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: Your variance uses the word substantial, which triggers a review, which triggers a supervisor, which triggers a memo asking why the night shift is recalibrating anything. The shift buries the memo under six routine ones, but upstairs now has a folder with the factory's name on it. Folders, once opened, do not stay thin.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_assembly The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Sell the displaced fuse tooling to a quiet broker

outcomes.text: The broker examines the fuse dies by lantern, pays in two envelopes, and asks no questions except when there will be more. The dies leave in a crate marked SEWING MACHINES, headed somewhere that wants them for exactly what they are. The shift watches you count the second envelope, and the watching has a temperature.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_assembly The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Shift change is four minutes long, and tonight the tail-fin jig must become a hinge bender inside them. The Manifest Clerk stands ready with two ledgers, one true, one beautiful, and a watch she taps like a conductor. In the paint locker, the stamped bomb now wears a coat of grey primer and a sheet, and the shift has started calling it Gerald. Peace Through Quarterly Growth has sent a circular praising everyone's throughput, which is one way to describe it.

choices.text: Explain the new machine in fluent head office

outcomes.text: You tell the day foreman the hinge bender is a pilot throughput initiative from the strategy floor, and his eyes glaze at the second syllable of initiative. He signs the handover without looking at the machine, which is now, officially, head office's idea. Gerald's sheet gets a chalk note reading STRATEGIC ASSET, DO NOT DUST, and nobody in management dares touch him.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_retaliation The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: You improvise the phrase vertical hinge synergy, and the day foreman, moved, writes it down and forwards it to the strategy floor for recognition. The strategy floor loves it and requests a presentation with projections, attendance mandatory. Attention is now pointed at the one station you needed it pointed away from, and Gerald's sheet twitches in the draft like it knows.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_retaliation The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Quote the broker a price for the tail-fin jig

outcomes.text: The jig goes out the back on a handcart at three in the morning, and the broker's envelopes have grown thicker, which means his buyer is getting closer to a war. You tell yourself tooling is only tooling, and the handcart squeaks all the way to the gate like it disagrees. In the paint locker, Gerald says nothing, which from a bomb is technically a courtesy.

choices.text: Move half a ton of bender in the four minutes

success.text: Eight of you take the weight in silence, walking the bender across the floor in a rehearsed shuffle timed to the whistle, and the day shift arrives to find furniture where a weapon jig stood. The Toolsetter calls it the finest shift change since the flood year and buys the tea. Gerald gets promoted to doorstop for the tooling cage, his first job with responsibilities.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_retaliation The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The bender comes down on the last yard and finds your foot under it, and the clang travels three streets, which at four in the morning is a form of publicity. A dockhand asks next day what the shell works drops at night, and the answer going round is anvils, which nobody believes. Your foot turns an honest purple, and the Toolsetter enters it in the true ledger as plant damage, sentimental.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_retaliation The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

A junior auditor arrives with a clipboard, a laminated authority, and a face that has never needed shaving. Peace Through Quarterly Growth wants every kinetic deliverable counted by dawn, and the shift has taken to calling him the Compliance Cherub before he reaches the door. Gerald stands primed and sheeted among forty crates, wearing a stencil that says PUMP, MISC. The count starts at midnight, which is your territory, not his.

choices.text: Read the Cherub's count sheet before he can

success.text: While the Cherub warms his hands, you read his master sheet upside down and learn something interesting: head office lists this factory's entire output as durable goods, miscellaneous. You correct two of his arithmetic errors, collect the audit assistance stipend for cooperation, and file the phrase away where you keep loaded things. The crew notes how fluently you read company paper and decides to keep half an eye on you, which is fair.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: You sell the Cherub a reconciled count that flatters head office and yourself, and he pays the discrepancy bounty out of a petty cash tin the size of a coffin. The reconciliation shaves the night shift's logged hours to make the totals sing, and somebody's overtime quietly dies for it. The envelope is heavy, and the canteen gets quieter when you enter it, and those two facts are related.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Run the pallet shuffle while he counts

outcomes.text: The same forty crates pass the Cherub four times in four positions, moved between aisles by a crew that has rehearsed boredom to a professional standard. He counts one hundred sixty deliverables, which is the exact number on his sheet, and goes home proud. Gerald crosses the floor twice in the rotation, sheeted, counted both times as PUMP, MISC, and thereby earns his first two official existences.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: A pallet corner takes you across the ribs on the third rotation, and your gasp is loud enough that the Cherub looks up and starts a fresh page. He finds nothing, because there is nothing findable, but he writes ATMOSPHERE: EVASIVE in his notes and underlines it. The district hears the shell works got audited at midnight, and every retelling adds a zero.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Demand his Form 77-K before a single crate opens

success.text: You inform the Cherub that enumeration without a Form 77-K, Authority to Count, is itself a countable offense, and watch him search a briefcase that contains only a 77-J. The audit adjourns until head office can post the correct form, which gives the line nine days and the paint locker a holiday. Gerald spends them under his sheet, legally uncountable, the happiest he has ever been.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The Cherub produces the 77-K, laminated, signed in three inks, and thanks you for your diligence in a voice that files things. Your objection goes into his notes as obstruction, adjacent, and the count proceeds with him watching you specifically. It could be worse, and the beautiful ledger will need to be more beautiful.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_coalition The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

The day shift eats lunch in the same canteen, and their senior fitter has noticed that station two now bends hinges. She sits down opposite you, sets her sandwich aside, and says her brother's town took a delivery of this factory's produce two winters back. She does not say it like a joke, and nobody at the table treats it like one. She wants in, and she wants to know the rota.

choices.text: Cut the day shift in and split the tooling bill

outcomes.text: The kitty opens, three coins lighter, and the senior fitter's crew takes the daylight half of the work: two shifts, one secret, twice the pace. Station three becomes a stove press by Friday, and the canteen starts smelling like an actual future. Gerald is moved beneath the serving hatch, sheeted, where the day shift leaves him a biscuit on principle.
effects: schedule 3 r18_arms_factory_crisis The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: Moving the crated stove press between shifts, the cellar stair gives up a step it had been saving, and you ride the crate to the bottom. The crash brings half the street to the gate asking what the shell works drops in daylight now, and the answer, anvils again, has stopped working. The senior fitter hauls you up, checks your arms, and says the day shift breaks bigger things than that before lunch.
effects: schedule 3 r18_arms_factory_crisis The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Drill everyone in fluent quarterly vocabulary

outcomes.text: By Thursday the whole floor can say throughput-positive warmth vertical without laughing, and visiting managers hear their own language spoken back at native speed and retreat reassured. A stove is now a customer-facing heat solution, a pump is decentralized hydration, and the fuse line's absence is a legacy divestment. Gerald's stencil is upgraded to LEGACY ASSET, and legacy assets, per policy, are nobody's job.
effects: schedule 3 r18_arms_factory_crisis The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

Now the factory owes head office a slide deck about a product strategy that must not survive scrutiny. The Manifest Clerk starts drawing charts and swearing in ledger.

The money is real, the fuses are real, and the senior fitter passes station five each day without looking at it, which is its own kind of looking. By Friday somebody has chalked an arrow on Gerald's sheet pointing at station five, and nobody admits to the handwriting.

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Head office descends on a Tuesday, eleven coats that have never been near machine oil, led by the Vice President of Outcomes. Peace Through Quarterly Growth has noticed that its mourning output is down and its hinge revenue is up, and it would like the numbers to explain themselves. Worse, dispatch has followed the beautiful ledger to its logical end and staged Gerald on the outbound dock as AGRICULTURAL BOILER, SINGLE USE. The truck comes at six.

choices.text: Walk the VP through the beautiful ledger yourself

success.text: You take the Vice President of Outcomes line by line through a ledger tidier than his own company, where every pump descends from an order and every order from a clause with a pedigree. He finds one entry he does not like, boiler, diplomatic, single use, and you explain it is a treaty gift, which is, historically, true. He initials the page, and Gerald becomes the only bomb ever vouched for by the man sent to find him.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: Page forty sticks to page forty one, and the Vice President of Outcomes reads two totals at once that should never have met. You argue the ledger's case for an hour and win on points, but he leaves with a photograph of page forty and the word discrepancy in his mouth. On the dock, Gerald waits for the six o'clock truck like a package with somewhere to be.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Sell the VP a glowing report on his own visit

outcomes.text: You draft the Vice President of Outcomes a site assessment praising his decisive leadership of a turnaround he has not understood, and he pays for it out of a budget line called insight. He presents it upstairs, upstairs raises the kinetic quota at some other factory to match his success, and the envelope in your coat weighs about the same as that sentence. Gerald stays on the dock, boiler, single use, next to a crate of actual boilers who are learning terrible habits from him.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Flood the floor with immaculate boring work

outcomes.text: Every station performs perfection at half speed, a factory impersonating its own training film, and eleven coats watch pumps being born until their eyes water with reassurance. Behind the tour, two fitters wheel Gerald off the dock and into the tooling cage under a manifest that says RETURNED, INSUFFICIENT DIPLOMACY. The Vice President of Outcomes leaves impressed and none the wiser, which for him is one condition, not two.

outcomes.text: The performance holds until a belt snaps on the stove press and takes skin off your forearm in front of the whole tour. The Vice President of Outcomes watches you bleed on a stove and notes that safety culture is an area of opportunity. On the dock, Gerald's truck is delayed, not canceled, and the district talks for days about the morning the coats came.
effects: schedule 4 r18_arms_factory_compromise The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

The Vice President of Outcomes offers terms in the loading bay while the six o'clock truck idles at the gate with Gerald lashed aboard. Half the line returns to kinetic deliverables, nobody asks how the other half got converted, and everyone receives a bonus called flexibility recognition. He calls this generous, and by his arithmetic it is. The truck's manifest still says AGRICULTURAL BOILER, SINGLE USE, and the driver is finishing a sandwich.

choices.text: Swing the gantry crane across the gate, slowly

success.text: You walk the overhead crane down the bay at ceremonial speed and set a crate of stoves in the truck's path with the tenderness of a state funeral. The driver looks at the crate, looks at his watch, and clocks off, because moving it is a different job classification. Gerald comes off the truck in the confusion, and the Vice President of Outcomes learns that the factory's machinery has opinions. The crew is grateful and rattled in equal parts, because cranes are not meant to editorialize.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The crane load swings wide, and you take the corner of a stove crate across the shoulder hard enough to change your relationship with that shoulder. The truck leaves in the confusion, and you chase it on a borrowed bicycle for two miles until the border checkpoint bounces it back, single use boilers lacking an import class. Gerald returns to the factory by teatime, unshipped and smug, while you return by cart, at an angle. The VP logs the incident as equipment enthusiasm and reopens the kinetic question.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Decline the bonus together, politely, in writing

outcomes.text: The whole shift signs one page declining flexibility recognition on the grounds that the flexibility is already theirs and they are not selling it back. The Vice President of Outcomes has spreadsheets for strikes and spreadsheets for demands, and nothing at all for a polite no with forty signatures. He withdraws the terms to seek guidance, and the truck is unloaded pending guidance, which never comes. Gerald spends the evening back in the tooling cage, being leaned on affectionately.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: The VP answers the letter with a mandatory realignment meeting in the unheated bay, two hours standing, agenda: gratitude. You come out of it with a cough that stays a fortnight and a district full of talk that the shell works is refusing money now, which sounds mad even to the sympathetic. The letter worked, in that the terms are dead, but so is everyone's evening.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Accept his terms, then define every word in them

outcomes.text: You accept in full, subject to definitions, and define kinetic deliverable as any product with moving parts, which pumps have, and hinges are, and stoves contain. The VP signs before reading the appendix, because signing before reading is how his career was built. Half the line is now contractually obligated to make pumps, and the other half, per the same clause, may continue making pumps. Gerald is exempted by name as a diplomatic instrument, which he will not let the other inventory forget.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: The VP's traveling lawyer counter-defines kinetic with a citation from the original shell contract, and suddenly you are on record accepting terms that mean whatever he says they mean. The deal stalls in definitions committee, which buys the line three weeks but costs you the appearance of cleverness. The truck stays in the yard, undriven, while both sides argue about what a boiler is.
effects: schedule 5 r18_arms_factory_reversal The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Deep in the company archive, the Compliance Cherub has found the thing nobody was supposed to find, and he brings it to the night shift at two in the morning, shaking. For nine years, for tariff and insurance reasons, Peace Through Quarterly Growth has booked every shell it ever shipped as durable goods, agricultural. There have never, officially, been any bombs. The audit cannot prove the plowshares were ever anything else, because the company got there first, in writing, for money.

choices.text: Pour him tea and show him the true ledger

success.text: You sit the Cherub down in the break room and open the true ledger, the one with real numbers and, in the back, the Toolsetter's list of names from shipment 41, kept in his cap for nine years and copied here so the cap is not the only place. The Cherub reads for a long time, and nobody hurries him. At dawn he signs the audit closed in his own hand, asks to keep his clipboard, and asks, quieter, whether the day shift is hiring. Gerald, present as always, holds the biscuits.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

fail.text: The Cherub takes the true ledger badly at first, and talking him through it costs the whole night and most of your voice, and at one point he tries to resign to you personally, which is not a thing. By morning he is steady, but a watchman saw the break room lit till dawn, and the district now believes the shell works holds seances. You sleep standing up against the hinge bender, which leaves a mark. It was worth it, probably, the way true things are.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Certify continuity with the company's own archive

outcomes.text: You file a continuity certification stating that the factory continues its documented historical product line, durable goods, agricultural, and attach nine years of the company's own manifests as evidence. It is unanswerable, because answering it would require Peace Through Quarterly Growth to declare in writing that it has been arming a continent under a farm tariff. The company chooses the pumps. Gerald is reclassified, per his stamp, as diplomacy handling equipment, and the filing clerk does not blink, because filing clerks have seen things.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

outcomes.text: Your certification runs to forty pages, which is thirty nine more than boring, and an archivist upstairs requests definitions of the word continuity. The request means nothing and can be answered with more paper, but the folder with the factory's name is now thick, and thick folders attract readers. The line holds its breath and keeps making stoves.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

choices.text: Sell the tariff secret to the quiet broker

outcomes.text: The broker pays a year's wages for the shape of the euphemism, because knowing how Peace Through Quarterly Growth books its shipping is worth exactly that to men who move cargo past tariffs. You take the envelopes, and somewhere down the coast a scheme you will never see begins pricing itself accordingly. The Cherub watches you take the money with an expression you last saw in a mirror. Gerald's sheet slips off that night, and nobody rushes to cover him.
effects: schedule 2 r18_arms_factory_legacy The night shift retools the line while nobody counts

text:
ARMS FACTORY

Six months on, the factory smells of solder and soup instead of cordite, and the order board lists pumps, hinges, and stoves in a font nobody had to invent a euphemism for. The audit concluded, in writing, that the factory has always made what it now makes, and Peace Through Quarterly Growth reports the pivot as its own strategic vision. In the break room, defused over one careful weekend by the Toolsetter, Gerald stands holding the kettle, stamp intact: HANDLE WITH DIPLOMACY. The shift does, every morning.

choices.text: Author the quarterly report head office deserves

outcomes.text: You write Peace Through Quarterly Growth a quarterly of unbearable beauty: legacy divestment complete, warmth verticals at capacity, mourning down across all markets and nobody asking why. Head office frames it, hands out bonuses upstairs, and reduces the factory's oversight tier to minimal, on the grounds that success requires no supervision. Gerald appears in the appendix as a long-term diplomatic asset, depreciating gracefully. The kettle boils on schedule.

Head office frames it, hands out bonuses upstairs, and reduces the factory's oversight tier to minimal, on the grounds that success requires no supervision. Gerald appears in the appendix as a long-term diplomatic asset, depreciating gracefully. The kettle boils on schedule.

Your report is so persuasive that head office announces a best-practices delegation to study the miracle factory, arrival Thursday. The line spends three days teaching stoves to look like strategy, and the strain shows in the tea. The delegation leaves converted, in the commercial sense, but the district has now seen two visitations in a season and draws conclusions at volume.

choices.text: Charge a tour fee to see the line the audit blessed

outcomes.text: Word has spread of the factory the audit could not decode, and manufacturers arrive weekly to learn the trick, so you charge at the door and narrate freely, omitting only everything that matters. The fees are handsome and the tours are useless, which is the correct ratio. Gerald is the final stop, kettle steaming on his shoulders, and visitors photograph his stamp believing it is a joke, which suits everyone in the room except possibly Gerald.

choices.text: Take the night wrench full time and train the next shift

success.text: The Toolsetter hands over the night wrench with both hands, the way you pass a thing that has been used for something that mattered. You take on three apprentices, teach them tolerances first and vocabulary second, and add a line to the rota that simply says whoever holds the wrench answers for the line. On quiet mornings the shift takes its tea standing near Gerald, not for any reason anyone states. The kettle sees to the rest.

fail.text: Your first week of training, the hinge bender takes a demonstration too literally and leaves you a scar shaped roughly like a hinge, which the apprentices find funnier than you do. The clatter of your teaching carries, and the district decides the shell works is arming again, and has to be talked down street by street. The rota gets your name on it anyway, in the Toolsetter's hand. Scars, he says, are just tenure.

text:
MINE RESCUE

At six minutes past the whistle, the north shaft of the Bottomless Extraction pit sits down like a tired animal, taking the whole second shift with it. Eleven names stay chalked on the tally board, and the tally-keeper puts her thumb over the rag so nobody wipes them. Head office responds within the hour, which impresses everyone until the response is read aloud: rescue operations may commence upon completion of Form RA-77, Risk Assessment of Proposed Rescue, in triplicate. The regional manager, a canary named Gareth, is brought to the pithead in his executive cage to observe.

choices.text: File the emergency exemption before the ink dries

outcomes.text: You cite subsection nine, the one about imminent loss of company property, and list the crew as company property with a straight face. The clerk stamps it because the wording is technically beautiful. Digging is authorized pending review, and head office, wrong-footed, suspends pit-cottage rents for the duration to look humane in the record. The tally-keeper lets you touch the board, once.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Your exemption is received, numbered, and placed in the queue behind a dispute about ladder width. The clerk is sympathetic in the way of a man reading from a card that says be sympathetic. Above ground the crowd gets louder, and below ground the tapping stays exactly as patient as before, which is somehow worse.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Take the company coin to keep the crowd orderly

outcomes.text: Bottomless Extraction hires you on the spot as Community Calm Facilitator, eight now, six when the crowd goes home. You walk the rope line saying the word procedure in a soothing voice, and it works, which is the upsetting part. Gareth watches you from his cage with the expression of one professional recognizing another.

choices.text: Grab a shovel and sign the rescue rota

outcomes.text: You buy your own gloves, which turns out to be the entry fee, and the tally-keeper writes you onto the rota under volunteers, no pay, all weather. By dark you know eleven names and what each man tapped last. The pit-cottage landlords quietly stop collecting for the week, sensing the weather. Somewhere behind you a clerk records the digging as unauthorized soil relocation, and nobody stops to correct him.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The first rock you pull frees three more with opinions. You leave a knuckle in the rubble and gain a bandage from the widow who runs the tea urn, which formalizes your membership better than any card. The crowd sees the blood and gets loud enough that head office logs a disturbance.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mine_rescue_assembly Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Day two opens with a flip chart. The deputy assessor of foreseeable events, sent up from head office with a laminated agenda, convenes an information session titled Managing Expectations Around the North Shaft Event. He would like to be liked, and has brought pastries. Gareth attends in his capacity as regional manager and does not sing, which the miners' wives notice, because a canary that stops singing is telling you something. The tapping from below has settled into a rhythm the old men translate as: still here, send soup.

choices.text: Sell the pastry concession at the pithead

outcomes.text: You set up a stall between grief and the rope line, and business is excellent, because waiting is hungry work. Head office matches your take with a hospitality grant once they notice you make the crowd look catered rather than desperate. The tea widow stops speaking to you, though she does check your prices twice a day, professionally.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Turn the meeting into a shift roster for the dig

outcomes.text: You stand during questions and read out a dig rota, names and hours, and the room sorts itself around it like filings around a magnet. The deputy assessor watches his meeting become useful and cannot decide whether to object. Gareth hops once along his perch, which the wives take as a yes.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: Half the room follows you out, the other half follows you out slower. In the crush at the door a trestle table takes you across the shins, and the pastries are lost to history. The session is minuted as concluded early due to enthusiasm, and security starts carrying longer torches.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Ask the assessor one question with witnesses present

success.text: You ask, mildly, whether the risk assessment of the rescue also requires a risk assessment, and if so, who assesses that one. The deputy assessor consults his flowchart and finds that it points at itself. To make it stop he grants provisional site access, and the flip chart is quietly turned to a fresh page.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: Your question has three clauses and he survives all of them by writing each one down slowly. By the time he finishes, the meeting has run to its scheduled end, which was the plan, though not yours. The crowd files out with pastries and the sense of having been processed.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mine_rescue_retaliation Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Overnight, Bottomless Extraction padlocks the winch house and posts two loss prevention officers on the lift machinery, citing concern for unauthorized wear. The lift is the only way down that does not involve a shovel and a fortnight. A notice on the door explains that the equipment is being protected for the crew's own eventual use, a sentence the tally-keeper reads aloud twice, then once more at a funeral pace. From his cage, Gareth studies the padlock the way managers study a budget line they intend to lose.

choices.text: Ring the winch house with people who will not move

outcomes.text: By noon the winch house is surrounded by a rota so orderly it embarrasses the padlock: widows on chairs, retired lamp-men with sandwiches, a fiddler who takes requests. The loss prevention officers, trained mainly to prevent losses, cannot identify one. Somebody's aunt feeds them anyway, on the reasoning that they look underpaid, which they are.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The night watch of the sit is thinner, and a scuffle at the padlock costs you an elbow and gains the company a photograph of the scuffle. By morning the notice on the door has a second notice about the first notice. The tapping below misses a beat, then resumes, forgiving you.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Serve papers claiming the lift as rescue equipment

outcomes.text: You file to reclassify the lift machinery as emergency apparatus, which under the safety code cannot be locked against its purpose. The magistrate, whose own uncle was a pit man, signs faster than anyone has ever seen him do anything. The padlock comes off with a ceremony nobody planned and everybody attends.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The company's counsel argues that the lift cannot be rescue equipment because no rescue has been approved for it to equip. The magistrate calls this argument disgusting and legally sound. You leave with costs awarded against no one and the padlock developing a shine of permanence.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Cut the padlock and run the winch yourselves

success.text: The bolt cutters go through the padlock like a rumor through a small town. Two old winch-men run the drum by feel, lamps drop into the dark, and for one hour the pit works exactly as designed, without permission. The rescue council disowns the act in public and feeds everyone involved in private.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

fail.text: The cutters slip and the loss prevention officers arrive mid-swing, batons out, apologetic afterward in writing. You wake behind the tea urn with three ribs filing complaints and the padlock promoted to a chain. Head office approves a budget for additional security, effective immediately, unlike the rescue.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mine_rescue_coalition Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

text:
MINE RESCUE

Gareth resigns from management at a quarter past dawn, exiting his executive cage through a door the tally-keeper swears she did not leave open. He is gone down the ventilation gap before anyone can complete an incident report. Two hours later he returns dusty, alive, and carrying in his claw a button from a second-shift coat, at which point the pithead understands it has a route no map admits to. The neighboring pits send word by boy and by pigeon: whatever is needed, ask, invoice later.

choices.text: Turn the bird's route into a surveyed drawing

outcomes.text: An off-duty clerk from the survey office, moonlighting for tea and vengeance, converts Gareth's comings and goings into a proper sectional drawing with a stamp on it. Documents with stamps cannot be ignored by people who worship stamps; head office schedules a site inspection out of pure reflex. The drawing shows an old gallery running forty yards from the trapped face, and now it officially exists.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

outcomes.text: The drawing is beautiful and the survey office disowns the stamp within the hour, on account of the clerk borrowing it. Head office declares the route hearsay by an unlicensed bird. The gallery goes on existing anyway, quietly, the way facts do while waiting for paperwork to catch up.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.

choices.text: Auction the story of the manager bird to the broadsheets

outcomes.text: The broadsheets pay eight for the tale and six more for a portrait of Gareth beside his abandoned cage, captioned executive walks out. The story runs everywhere, and so does a souvenir stall selling little tin canaries. The rescue council notes that you sold their bird, then notes that the tin canaries fund nothing, then stops taking your rounds at the tea urn.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mine_rescue_crisis Gareth waits at the pithead, still not singing.
