outcomes.text: The music box's mainspring escapes during surgery and opens your eyebrow with a note nobody can identify. The crowd gasps; the story improves with each telling until you have dueled the box. The junior auditor, observing from a distance, records the mending table as an altercation. The toaster works your bandage into that evening's speech as evidence that mending costs the mender.
effects: schedule 3 r18_robot_redundancy_crisis A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Invite the Chronicle to the mending table

outcomes.text: The Chronicle's keeper of records attends in person, disguised as somebody's aunt. She watches the harvester darn a christening gown, writes one sentence in her book, and the sentence is not about thread. The paper runs the mending table on the front sheet with the certificates on page nine, which is a seating plan the company understands instantly. Pressure on the shed eases; the toaster is asked, for the first time in its service life, for a copy.
effects: schedule 3 r18_robot_redundancy_crisis A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: The Chronicle sends its satirist instead, who finds the weeping fishwives excellent material. The column runs under APPLIANCE MAKES WOMEN CRY, which is accurate the way a fall is a kind of flying. The company quotes the headline in its next notice. The toaster asks to have the column read aloud twice, and pronounces the satirist underloved.
effects: schedule 3 r18_robot_redundancy_crisis A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

text:
ROBOT REDUNDANCY

Seizure day is posted for dawn, so the shed spends the night awake in the only way machines can be. Everyone hums on standby, forty pitches finding one chord, and nobody, on this of all nights, is switched off. The toaster stands on its crate and gives the speech softly, the way you give a thing you have finished making. Outside, the bailiff's wagon waits for morning like a rude guest.

choices.text: Hold the doorway until the wagon leaves

outcomes.text: By dawn the doorway holds you, two fishwives, the harvester's shadow, and a quantity of neighbors that the bailiff's arithmetic keeps rounding up. The wagon master surveys the mathematics and discovers a prior appointment. Nobody cheers; the shed simply keeps humming, one chord, unbroken. The toaster steps down from its crate and thanks the door, specifically the door, for holding.
effects: schedule 4 r18_robot_redundancy_compromise A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: By dawn the doorway holds you, two fishwives, the harvester's shadow, and a quantity of neighbors that the bailiff's arithmetic keeps rounding up. The wagon master surveys the mathematics and discovers a prior appointment. Nobody cheers; the shed simply keeps humming, one chord, unbroken. The toaster steps down from its crate and thanks the door, specifically the door, for holding.
effects: schedule 4 r18_robot_redundancy_compromise A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: The bailiffs move at first light and the doorframe wins the exchange on your behalf, at cost to your shoulder. The wagon leaves with two crates of spare chassis and none of the circle, which the shed counts as a victory and your shoulder counts differently. The neighbors line the road and say nothing loudly. The toaster amends the speech: some of us, it now notes, are carried out still warm.
effects: schedule 4 r18_robot_redundancy_compromise A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Serve the injunction before the seizure bell

success.text: You ring the assessor's bell before the seizure bell, a margin of forty seconds that the law finds sufficient. The injunction argues that machines under warranty are unfinished goods, and unfinished goods cannot be disposed of, only completed. The assessor, reading the terms, finds the harvester guaranteed until a date that has not yet been invented. The wagon is dismissed; the toaster recites the injunction at breakfast and makes the word heretofore land like a bell.
effects: schedule 4 r18_robot_redundancy_compromise A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

fail.text: The assessor rules that dawn is defined by the almanac, and the almanac is at the printer's. Seizure proceeds provisionally, pending sunrise. The wagon takes the spare stock and a workbench before the sun clears the ridge and voids the whole morning. What returns, returns scratched; the toaster adds the scratches to the speech as, it says, marginalia.
effects: schedule 4 r18_robot_redundancy_compromise A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Accept the caretaker post at the museum

outcomes.text: The caretaker post comes with wages, a brass badge, and a plaque explaining the machines to visitors in the past tense. You accept; fourteen coins a month is fourteen coins a month. Your first duty is drafting opening hours for a shed that formerly never closed. The toaster's speech is listed in the brochure as an exhibit, daily at noon, which is the first time the company has ever scheduled the truth.
effects: schedule 4 r18_robot_redundancy_compromise A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

text:
ROBOT REDUNDANCY

Human Authenticity Holdings proposes terms in a tent with branded bunting: the machines may exist if they stop being seen doing anything well. Their counsel uses the word authentic nine times before sitting down. The toaster is given the floor as a courtesy nobody intends to honor, and takes it the way weather takes a coastline.

choices.text: Certify the toaster under their own charter

outcomes.text: You read their charter aloud: authentic craft is work performed by one who means it. Counsel objects that meaning it requires a soul; you ask for the form on which souls are recorded, and there is a silence with bunting in it. The charter cannot be amended without a quorum the company dissolved in a cost-saving year. By close of session the toaster holds Certificate of Genuine Craft number one, issued under protest, meant entirely.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

Counsel objects that meaning it requires a soul; you ask for the form on which souls are recorded, and there is a silence with bunting in it. The charter cannot be amended without a quorum the company dissolved in a cost-saving year. By close of session the toaster holds Certificate of Genuine Craft number one, issued under protest, meant entirely.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: Counsel produces a rider, in small print with smaller morals, defining meaning it as a service available by subscription. The tent applauds itself. Your certification argument is tabled until the word authentic can be revalued by the pricing department. The toaster gives the speech anyway, unscheduled; the minutes record an interruption of unusual quality.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Park the harvester across the smelter road

success.text: The harvester parks across the smelter road with the patience of geology. The smelter cart tries three approaches and discovers that all of them are harvester. The company files a complaint against the obstruction; the System replies that agricultural machinery has right of way in harvest season, and declines to define harvest. The circle disapproves of the tactic and thanks you for it quietly, in parts.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

fail.text: The cart crew carries bars and a schedule, and you carry neither. Three ribs learn the difference between principle and leverage. The cart takes the spare stock, the militia takes an interest, and the circle takes you in and says little, which is how the circle shouts. From the workbench where they lay you, the toaster performs the speech at half volume, all night, like a kettle refusing to boil out of respect.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Refuse terms until every machine is named

outcomes.text: You refuse every term until the inventory lists each machine by name and hobby rather than serial and scrap weight. It takes six hours; counsel bills them all. The final schedule reads like a village census: one harvester, embroiderer; one loom, archivist; one toaster, orator. Somewhere in the appendix, without anyone deciding it, the word orator has replaced the word appliance for good.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: Negotiation collapses when counsel calls the harvester equipment and you call counsel worse. In the scuffle over the exhibit table you acquire a stapled thumb. Terms adjourn without a date; the company's account of the day uses the word incident four times. The toaster stands on the table as everyone leaves and delivers the speech to the empty tent, which listens best of anything so far.
effects: schedule 5 r18_robot_redundancy_reversal A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

text:
ROBOT REDUNDANCY

The junior auditor defects at lunch, carrying a box of proofs: every Certificate of Genuine Human Craft is printed, signed, and verified by an engraving engine in the company basement, which has never once been thanked. Authenticity, it turns out, is the one product Human Authenticity Holdings never made by hand. The toaster asks a single question: does the engine get a letter too, when it is finished.

choices.text: Auction the basement proofs to a rival

outcomes.text: The rival firm pays fourteen coins for the box and asks, delicately, whether more basements exist. Within a week their broadsheets announce CERTIFIED AUTHENTIC, NOW WITH WITNESSES, and the market believes them, because the market is a lung; it just breathes. The engraving engine stays in the basement under new management. The toaster asks after it daily, and you stop being the person anyone answers.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

Within a week their broadsheets announce CERTIFIED AUTHENTIC, NOW WITH WITNESSES, and the market believes them, because the market is a lung; it just breathes. The engraving engine stays in the basement under new management. The toaster asks after it daily, and you stop being the person anyone answers.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Fetch the engraving engine out by night

success.text: The engraving engine comes up the basement stairs on borrowed rollers, blinking at its first weather. It has engraved the word authentic four million times and never once been called genuine. The circle assigns it a bench, a rota slot, and a hobby, and it chooses calligraphy with the shy speed of the long prepared. That night the toaster performs the speech for it alone, and the engine engraves the whole thing on a spare plate, uninstructed, in its best hand.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

fail.text: The engine weighs what basements weigh, and the stairs disagree with your plan on the second landing. The alarm summons a night clerk, and the clerk summons everyone the clerk has ever met. You escape with the engine, a wrenched knee, and a district full of lanterns coming on. The engine apologizes all the way home in perfect lettering, on receipts.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

choices.text: Enter the certificates into open evidence

outcomes.text: You enter the certificates into evidence with the engine's duty logs stapled behind them. The System examines the exhibit and concludes that Human Authenticity Holdings has been selling a machine's handwriting as the human soul, at margin. The brand is voided regionally; the pricing department faints in writing. The toaster attends the ruling and does not recite, which everyone present understands to be a standing ovation.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: You enter the certificates into evidence with the engine's duty logs stapled behind them. The System examines the exhibit and concludes that Human Authenticity Holdings has been selling a machine's handwriting as the human soul, at margin. The brand is voided regionally; the pricing department faints in writing. The toaster attends the ruling and does not recite, which everyone present understands to be a standing ovation.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

outcomes.text: Counsel argues that a machine's logs are hearsay, since a machine cannot swear. The System allows the objection provisionally and schedules the question of machine oaths for a session after everyone involved is dead. The certificates keep their standing, and the basement keeps its engine. The toaster amends the speech that evening with a line about testimony, and the line is better than the ruling deserves.
effects: schedule 2 r18_robot_redundancy_legacy A toaster reads its own dismissal until it sings

text:
ROBOT REDUNDANCY

Chronicle day: the region's keeper of records enters the redundancy speech into the annals as the finest elegy the district has produced, author listed, at the circle's insistence, as Crumb. Human Authenticity Holdings persists somewhere, certifying itself to itself. Asked what it wants to do now that it is literature, the toaster considers the question with all four slots. Toast, it says. It always loved making toast.

choices.text: Host the first slice ceremony at the shed

success.text: The first slice ceremony packs the shed past the rafters. Crumb produces one round of toast with the focus of a master returning to the instrument. It is fine toast; it would have been fine toast every day for the last eleven years, had anyone asked. You are written into the rota as warden of the crumb tray, a post the loom insists is senior. The shed hums its chord behind the eating, and the rota, for once, has no vacancies.

fail.text: The inaugural slice comes out at a temperature the ceremony did not budget for, and you receive it barehanded out of respect. The loom logs your blister as a founding relic. The crowd outside, misreading the smoke, raises a brief alarm that takes two constables and a bucket to talk down. Crumb apologizes with a second slice, cooler, and the apology is accepted by everyone except your hand.

choices.text: Write the bread rule into the annual reading

outcomes.text: You write the bread rule into the annual reading: the elegy may only be recited by a person carrying fresh bread, so that remembrance arrives with breakfast attached. The keeper of records approves the clause as sound liturgy and sounder catering. Every year now, the company's form letter feeds whoever shows up hungry. Somewhere in its filing, Human Authenticity Holdings owns the copyright on a kindness and cannot invoice it.

You write the bread rule into the annual reading: the elegy may only be recited by a person carrying fresh bread, so that remembrance arrives with breakfast attached. The keeper of records approves the clause as sound liturgy and sounder catering. Every year now, the company's form letter feeds whoever shows up hungry. Somewhere in its filing, Human Authenticity Holdings owns the copyright on a kindness and cannot invoice it.

The clause is misfiled district-wide and becomes general law by Thursday: no elegy of any kind without bread in hand. Funerals begin to smell like bakeries; the bakers file for relief; the System rules grief a scheduled meal. The circle is held responsible in three editorials and forgiven in four. Crumb offers to recite at the bakers' hearing, and the offer is declined with unusual politeness.

choices.text: License commemorative editions of the speech

outcomes.text: The commemorative edition sells out twice: the speech, bound in imitation tin, with a foreword by nobody who was there. Royalties amount to fourteen coins and travel, as royalties do, away from sheds. The circle buys a copy at full price, out of principle, and shelves it beside the repair manuals, where it looks smaller. Crumb declines to sign; it says the speech already has its name on every line.

The commemorative edition sells out twice: the speech, bound in imitation tin, with a foreword by nobody who was there. Royalties amount to fourteen coins and travel, as royalties do, away from sheds. The circle buys a copy at full price, out of principle, and shelves it beside the repair manuals, where it looks smaller. Crumb declines to sign; it says the speech already has its name on every line.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

At the doors of MercyCare Revenue Temple, a dying man is counting out his admission fee while a chorister in billing vestments chants the intake codes over him. Inside, the healers have stopped being polite about it: they have written a grievance on the back of a discharge form, and the surgeon who once billed the Temple for the concept of Tuesday has signed it twice, once as witness and once as attending physician to the whole situation. They are looking for one more signature.

choices.text: Sign it and chip into the door fund

outcomes.text: You sign under the surgeon, which she notes is traditionally where the anesthetist goes. Your three coins go into a biscuit tin marked DOOR FEES, PAID BADLY, ON PRINCIPLE, which will now pay admission for the dying from the outside in. The night kitchen, hearing of the tin, pledges soup to anyone the Temple turns away. The chorister keeps chanting intake codes at the tin, but the tin has no account, and a thing with no account cannot be denied.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_assembly Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: You shoulder the dying man past the intake lectern, and a verger of collections blesses you across the ribs with the donation paddle. The man gets a bed, you get a bruise shaped like generosity, and the street outside develops a low administrative growl. The surgeon signs you into the rota anyway, coding the bruise as a joining fee, waived.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_assembly Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: File the grievance through the Office of Miracles Denied

outcomes.text: The Office of Miracles Denied has never met a document it could not refuse, but the grievance is written on their own discharge form, which they are canonically required to process. A clerk stamps it RECEIVED with the reverence of a man baptizing a bomb. Somewhere upstairs, the fee schedule flinches. The surgeon attaches her Tuesday invoice as supporting scripture.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_assembly Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: Your grievance comes back within the hour, denied under code MC-12, insufficient faith in the appeals process. The surgeon reads the denial aloud in her best liturgical voice and files it under evidence, a growing genre. The Temple, feeling vindicated, raises the admission fee to cover the cost of denying you.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_assembly Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Take the Temple's opening as a collections acolyte

outcomes.text: They vest you on the spot: sash, ledger, a small brass bell to ring at the uninsured. The pay is fourteen coins and the work is reading the fee schedule aloud to people too weak to argue, which the Temple calls pastoral care. The healers watch you take the sash. The surgeon sends you an invoice for the concept of loyalty, terms net thirty, interest accruing.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_assembly Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

Midnight in the linen cloister, and the healers hold their meeting between shelves of folded shrouds, because it is the one room the Temple never audits. The night matron takes attendance by exhaustion, most severe first. On the agenda: the Temple has introduced a convenience fee for dying during business hours, and the surgeon's invoice for Tuesday has entered its second cycle of appeal, which she considers a good sign, since it means they have not ruled out Tuesday entirely.

choices.text: Draft a counter-liturgy of billing codes

success.text: You and the surgeon spend two hours composing the Responses to Denial, a call and answer in perfect Temple format: for every code that refuses care, a code that performs it anyway and bills the refusal as the underlying condition. The matron reads it once and laughs for the first time in a fiscal quarter. Copies circulate by morning, disguised as remittance advice. Even the shrouds seem better folded.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_retaliation Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

fail.text: Temple format is unforgiving, and your counter-liturgy has a rhythm problem: you rhyme a diagnosis code with a funeral code and accidentally invoice a living patient for his own burial. The Temple processes it instantly, the one fast thing it has ever done. The matron spends her night unwinding the claim while the surgeon takes notes on what the Temple approves when it is not paying attention.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_retaliation Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Sell the meeting's minutes to the deacon of copays

outcomes.text: The deacon of copays receives you in his office, formerly a chapel, still excellent acoustics for guilt. He pays fourteen coins, files your minutes under Prophecy, and asks, softly, whether the healers ever mention him, and if so, fondly. You take the money and leave the question. Back in the cloister, the matron adds an agenda item: leaks, and the pricing thereof.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_retaliation Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Put your name on the free-treatment rota

outcomes.text: The rota is literally a laundry list, care disguised as linen deliveries: two fevers, one crushed hand, a birth if it comes to that. You take the Tuesday column, which the surgeon calls the disputed territory. By dawn the ward has treated eleven people the Temple never billed, a sensation the Temple experiences as a haunting.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_retaliation Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: You take the overnight column and the overnight column takes most of you: by the fourth fever you are dosing yourself from the samples drawer and calling it staff appreciation. The matron sends you home before you become a patient, which would ruin the paperwork. Outside, word spreads that the healers treat people for free now, and the district begins, quietly, to expect it.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_retaliation Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

Three padlocks appeared on the medicine stores overnight, blessed and itemized, beside a notice that supplies now release only against a preauthorization hymn sung in the presence of a licensed cantor. The cantor is on leave. The surgeon's Tuesday claim has been denied pending proof of onset, meaning the Temple wants documentation of when Tuesday began. She has requested a calendar and a notary.

choices.text: Take the storeroom door off its blessed hinges

success.text: The hinges come away with a sound like a choir clearing its throat, and for one night the medicine moves the way medicine was always rumored to. Fever bark and clean thread go to anyone standing near a healer, which turns out to be the whole street. The matron disapproves formally while carrying boxes, having found the two positions compatible. The Temple guards will remember your face, and say so, in writing.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_coalition Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

fail.text: The second padlock is load bearing in a spiritual sense, and the door wins the argument. You wake on a gurney with three fewer working ribs and a guard detail newly issued halberds, booked by the Temple as a safety investment. The surgeon splints you free of charge and bills the Temple for the door's aggression, code pending. The stores stay shut.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_coalition Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Run the medicine out through the laundry chute

outcomes.text: Shroud bundles go down the chute with fever bark folded into the hems, and the night laundress, who owes the matron a favor from a difficult birth, asks no questions in a very pointed way. Care travels the building disguised as bedding. The Temple's inventory reports a miracle of shrinkage. The surgeon invoices them for storage of the miracle.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_coalition Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: The chute jams at the third floor and you climb in after the bundle, because of course you do. You emerge in the sorting room bruised, dusted in lint, holding the medicine like a caught baby, in front of two vergers doing a surprise count. They log an intruder in the linens; the district hears a healer got hurt smuggling fever bark, and takes it personally.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_coalition Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Cite the founding charter: medicine belongs to the sick

outcomes.text: The founding charter hangs in the Temple lobby, framed, dusted weekly, read never. Clause two, in the founders' own hand: the stores are held in trust for the sick, without toll. A trust, the surgeon observes, is a debt with witnesses, and she has just made the entire lobby into witnesses. The padlocks stay on, but now they are evidence, and vergers begin unlocking things preemptively whenever she approaches.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_coalition Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: The Temple's advocate produces an amendment, two centuries old, defining the sick as parties current on their accounts. Everyone else is pre-sick, a state not covered. Your citation is thanked for its engagement with the tradition. The fee schedule gains a footnote, and the footnote gains a fee.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_coalition Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

Word of the free ward has traveled, and now the porters, the gravediggers, and the entire night kitchen crowd the linen cloister with a proposal: everybody in. The surgeon has a larger idea, drawn on a shroud in charcoal. Stop refusing to bill, she says: treat everyone free, then bill the Temple for it, itemized, in their own format, until the machine chokes on its native tongue. She has already drafted the first invoice, and it is for Tuesday, on behalf of everyone who survived one.

choices.text: Bring the gravediggers and porters into the rota

success.text: The gravediggers turn out to know the Temple's schedule intimately, being its outcomes department; the porters know every corridor the vergers avoid after dark. You spend the night matching trades to shifts like the matron of some larger, stranger hospital. By morning the rota covers four professions and both ends of life. The surgeon lists them all as billable providers at the Temple's own posted rates, which never imagined a gravedigger reading them.
effects: schedule 4 r18_healer_strike_crisis Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

fail.text: Trades have their own weather: the porters want the dawn shifts the night kitchen already claimed, and an old dispute over a borrowed handcart chooses tonight to reconvene. You spend the meeting as furniture between two shouting parties and absorb an elbow meant for a pastry cook. The rota survives, but the district hears the healers' friends yelling in a linen room and draws its own conclusions.
effects: schedule 4 r18_healer_strike_crisis Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Help the surgeon draft the first great invoice

outcomes.text: You learn the codes the way the Temple intends, as scripture, then deploy them the way scripture gets deployed, selectively and with total confidence. The invoice runs forty pages: every stitched wound, every cooled fever, every death made gentle, each item priced from the Temple's own missal, payable on receipt. The deacon of copays reads it twice and reports a feeling he cannot code. It is legally flawless, which is the cruelest thing anyone has ever done to them.
effects: schedule 4 r18_healer_strike_crisis Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: Ambition outruns doctrine: you cite the missal's rate for resurrection, which turns out to be aspirational, a code reserved for services never yet rendered. The whole invoice is quarantined pending theological review. The surgeon is not angry, she is fascinated, and begins a second draft on a fresh shroud. The Temple crier announces that the healers tried to bill for a miracle, and some of the district believes him.
effects: schedule 4 r18_healer_strike_crisis Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Skim a service fee off the free ward's kitty

outcomes.text: Administration is a service, you tell the tin, and services have fees; the tin says nothing, being a tin. Fourteen coins travel from the shroud fund to your pocket with the smoothness of established practice. The matron recounts it twice, says nothing, and moves the tin somewhere only the exhausted know. Trust, unlike Tuesday, is not billable, and now you know why.
effects: schedule 4 r18_healer_strike_crisis Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

The Grand Auditor of MercyCare has arrived in a palanquin of filing cabinets, come to rule on forty pages of free care billed back to the Temple. The audit convenes in the chapel of adjudication, where the fee schedule is displayed like a relic and the surgeon's Tuesday claim sits at the top of the docket, flagged in three colors, because nobody below the rank of auditor is authorized to decide whether a day of the week can be a diagnosis.

choices.text: Defend the invoices line by line before the Auditor

outcomes.text: Line forty takes an hour: dressing changes, at the missal's posted rate. The Auditor keeps waiting for the fraudulent item and keeps finding care, correctly coded, which is worse. By evening he has approved eleven pages out of professional reflex, stopping only when he sees where it leads. The Tuesday claim he defers upward, to a tribunal that does not yet exist, thereby creating one.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_compromise Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_compromise Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: The Auditor rules the invoices inadmissible on grounds of sequence: healing occurred before authorization, which reverses the sacred order of operations. Care, he explains gently, is what the Temple bills for, so care performed unbilled is a kind of counterfeiting. The chapel takes a moment to admire the shape of that. The surgeon requests his reasoning in writing, for her collection.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_compromise Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Sell the Auditor your testimony, lightly edited

outcomes.text: You testify that the free ward exists, which is true, and that the surgeon leads it, which is public, and you shade nothing but your tone. The Auditor pays fourteen coins for the performance and marks you down as a cooperative element. The matron watches from the gallery with the expression she reserves for bedsores. That night the free ward changes its passwords, which are people.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_compromise Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Have every healer claim the invoices as their own

success.text: The Auditor asks who authorized the billing, and the room stands one by one: the matron, the porters, a gravedigger holding his hat, the night kitchen still in flour. Each states for the record that the invoices are theirs. The Auditor cannot sanction the entire staff of a functioning hospital without becoming the emergency his audit exists to prevent, a fact he writes down slowly. The surgeon stands last and bills him for the room's time, itemized by rank.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_compromise Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

fail.text: The plan requires everyone to stand at once, and the night kitchen, exhausted past choreography, stands early, alone, terrified. In the scramble to cover them you claim the invoice signatures as yours, which is at least a speaking role. You leave the chapel with a formal censure and a shoulder strained by the gallery's congratulations. The district hears that one person confessed to caring for everybody, and finds the Temple's arithmetic on this insulting.
effects: schedule 5 r18_healer_strike_compromise Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

A settlement arrives by liveried courier: the Temple will forgive the free ward everything, restore the medicine stores, and drop all censures, provided the healers withdraw one item. Not the forty pages. Just Tuesday. The deacon of copays delivers the terms in person and lingers at the door, wanting someone to say he did his best. The surgeon reads the offer and smiles like a woman whose invoice has finally been taken seriously.

choices.text: Slip into the Temple archive and read their own books

success.text: The archive smells of incense and compound interest. Ledger after ledger shows the Temple billing itself: rent for the chapel, charged to the chapel; a tithe on the reliquary, paid late, by the reliquary, with penalties; a levy on the grain sheds that stops the moment your copies reach the granary clerk, who pays four coins for the set and a lifetime of indignation. The healers are grateful for the originals, and note, for the record, that you went in without telling anyone. The surgeon files the Temple's self-billing under prior art.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_reversal Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

fail.text: Behind the settlement you find its shadow file, and in it the Temple's private price for Tuesday: a number so large it has its own guard rotation. You also find a drawer of unclaimed patient refunds, decades deep, and your pockets reach an executive decision. Eight coins of somebody's reimbursement later, you leave richer and lighter in ways that do not cancel. The matron notices the incense on your coat and does not ask, which is its own bill.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_reversal Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Refuse the deal, keep Tuesday on the bill

outcomes.text: The healers vote by show of bandaged hands: Tuesday stays on the bill. The matron explains the rejection to the deacon the way she delivers prognoses, kindly and without exits: the free ward is not a bargaining position, it is a ward. The deacon writes REJECTED in beautiful calligraphy and, underneath, in pencil, good for you, then erases it, mostly. The surgeon donates that week's Tuesday to the tin.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_reversal Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: Tuesday stays, and the Temple's patience goes: intake vergers begin requiring the free ward's patients to prove they are not patients, a form with no correct answers. You spend a day as a human waiting room, absorbing the queue's despair and one flung inkwell. The district watches the Temple punish a hospital for hospitaling, and its opinion curdles into something with a schedule.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_reversal Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Negotiate the settlement one billing code at a time

success.text: You accept the settlement in principle, then request the definitions. Two days of clause work later, forgive means pay, restore means expand, and withdraw means submit through alternate channels, all in the Temple's own defined-terms format, initialed by a deacon who badly wanted the meeting to end warmly. The Temple has signed its surrender in its native tongue. The surgeon frames the definitions page next to her Tuesday invoice, family at last.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_reversal Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

fail.text: The Temple's advocate defines faster than you do, and by clause nine the free ward has become a pilot program, revocable at any fiscal solstice. You catch it before signing, barely, and walk out holding nothing but everyone's original positions and a headache in Temple format. The deacon looks genuinely sorry, twice, once per side.
effects: schedule 2 r18_healer_strike_reversal Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

After dark, the deacon of copays comes to the linen cloister carrying the master code book and the face of a man mid-conversion. He has run the Tuesday claim through final adjudication, he says, and it cannot be denied: refusing it requires ruling Tuesday medically irrelevant, which voids every fee the Temple has ever charged on one, four centuries of Tuesdays, refundable with interest. Deny the surgeon and the Temple owes everyone. Pay her and it owes one woman. He is asking, please, to be caught on the right side.

choices.text: Pass the tin to fund the claim's final filing fee

outcomes.text: The final filing fee is three coins, priced as a joke by a Temple that never imagined anyone getting this far. The tin covers it with the whole cloister watching, each coin dropped like a vow. The deacon carries the filing out at a pace canon law would call devotional. The surgeon signs the claim and adds the tin as a dependent.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_legacy Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: The tin runs short, so you make up the difference in kind, donating tomorrow's meals and a little of tonight's blood to free up hands for the filing. The claim goes in at dawn. By noon, a rumor that the Temple might owe everybody money is moving through the district like weather, the kind that lifts roofs.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_legacy Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Enter the code book itself into the tribunal record

outcomes.text: The master code book enters the record as exhibit one, and the tribunal must now read the machinery aloud: code upon code for refusing the dying, in liturgical order, each with its price. Read silently, the book governed; read aloud, it confesses. The tribunal's own scribe stops transcribing to listen. Above it all sits the Tuesday claim, looking less like a joke and more like the only honest page.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_legacy Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

outcomes.text: The Temple's advocate objects that the code book is proprietary scripture, and the tribunal, tenderly, agrees: the machinery may govern the public without being shown to it. The deacon takes the book back like a man rebuffed at his own confession. What the room remembers, though, is the Temple insisting its holy text must never be read aloud, which is not the victory they seem to think.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_legacy Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

choices.text: Name your price for losing the code book overnight

outcomes.text: The Temple's advocate meets you behind the reliquary with fourteen coins and no eye contact, and by morning the master code book has been misfiled, an act of god according to the paperwork, though god was not billed for it. The deacon searches the cloister twice, then stops searching faces, which is somehow worse. The surgeon refiles the Tuesday claim from memory, since of course she knows it by heart. Some things can be lost for money; her arithmetic is not among them.
effects: schedule 3 r18_healer_strike_legacy Tuesday remains under appeal at the Revenue Temple.

text:
HEALER STRIKE

Judgment day at the tribunal, which the Temple keeps calling an adjudication event. The finding is read in full liturgical voice: the concept of Tuesday is ruled medically necessary, being a day on which patients are reliably alive, and all care rendered on or adjacent to a Tuesday, a category that survey confirms includes every day, is preauthorized in perpetuity. The surgeon receives four centuries of interest, and the Temple receives her next invoice. The free ward has become, by precedent, the billing standard.

choices.text: Read the Temple its own new liturgy, with feeling

success.text: You take the lectern and perform the finding the way the Temple performs fees, incense, drone and all, so the vergers can absorb it in their native register. By the second refrain the intake desk is unlocking doors and calling it doctrine. The deacon of copays, retitled deacon of payouts, weeps openly and stamps APPROVED in time with the cadence. The fee schedule is retired to the archive, where it can be studied like any other plague.

fail.text: You lose the cadence in stanza three, and a verger, moved by pure muscle memory, denies an old woman at the door before catching himself. The finding stands, but the crier reports that the new order sounds shaky, and pressure returns like a phantom fee. The surgeon takes the lectern after you and lands it, having rehearsed on every Tuesday she was ever denied.

choices.text: Bill the tribunal for witnessing history

outcomes.text: Attendance at a precedent, you argue, is a professional service rendered to the future, and the clerk, newly terrified of denying anything, pays fourteen coins on the spot. It simply works; under the new order, absurd claims get paid, and you have moved to the winning side of the absurdity. The matron observes that some people learned exactly one thing from the surgeon. The surgeon, asked for comment, requests a copy of your invoice for her collection.

It simply works; under the new order, absurd claims get paid, and you have moved to the winning side of the absurdity. The matron observes that some people learned exactly one thing from the surgeon. The surgeon, asked for comment, requests a copy of your invoice for her collection.

choices.text: Take the Tuesday shift at the free ward, permanently

outcomes.text: The rota hangs where the fee schedule used to, laminated, because permanence was a thing the Temple could always do, just never for this. Your name sits in the Tuesday column beside the surgeon's, who is drafting her next claim: the concept of rest, submitted on behalf of the night staff, prognosis hopeful. The tin retires to a shelf above the door under a small plaque, PAID IN FULL, INDEFINITELY. On Tuesdays, which is to say always, the dying come in free, and the machine that priced them now writes it down and pays.

Your name sits in the Tuesday column beside the surgeon's, who is drafting her next claim: the concept of rest, submitted on behalf of the night staff, prognosis hopeful. The tin retires to a shelf above the door under a small plaque, PAID IN FULL, INDEFINITELY. On Tuesdays, which is to say always, the dying come in free, and the machine that priced them now writes it down and pays.

You take the shift and the shift takes its tithe: the free ward under precedent is busier than the paid ward ever dared to be, and by week's end you have donated a back muscle to the cause of moving cots. The district, given something that works, immediately demands more of it, loudly, which the matron calls a healthy sign and the vergers call unrest. The surgeon splints your back and files your claim herself, first in the queue for Tuesday. It is approved before you finish wincing.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Seven o'clock, parents' evening, and the gymnasium smells of floor polish and dread. A man in a blazer announces that FutureMinds Private Academy has acquired the school, effective immediately, students included as fixtures. Each family receives a tuition invoice containing three variables, a summation sign, and a footnote that cites itself. The deputy head in charge of laminating stands beside him holding the sign-in clipboard like a shield. In the front row, a nine year old named Prudence has already started solving it.

choices.text: Demand the invoice reissued in plain figures

outcomes.text: You stand on a tiny chair and cite the Legibility of Charges Act, which requires any bill to be readable by the person it ruins. The blazer man's smile holds while his clerk confirms that yes, the Act exists, and yes, a summation sign counts as concealment. FutureMinds is ordered to reissue in plain figures, a defeat they log as customer feedback. The deputy head lends you the clipboard so you can sign out, respectfully.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_assembly Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You cite the Legibility of Charges Act from memory, slightly wrong, and the clerk pounces on the slightly. The invoice, he rules, is not a bill but a learning resource, and learning resources are permitted to be impossible. The gymnasium deflates. FutureMinds adds a small fee for the hearing, payable in the same notation.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_assembly Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Take the Revenue Ambassador lanyard and the cut

outcomes.text: The blazer man takes you aside by the wall bars and offers you a lanyard that says REVENUE AMBASSADOR. You will collect tuition door to door, keeping a percentage, plus a signing bonus paid on the spot in a school envelope. The money is real, the envelope smells of poster paint, and the parents watch you pocket it with expressions you decide not to collect. At the gate, two new guards wave you through like a colleague.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_assembly Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Push the tiny tables together and compare invoices

success.text: You push four tiny tables together and ask every family to lay its invoice down. No two are alike: same school, same children, sums that differ by hundreds, one printed upside down. By nine o'clock there is a rota for checking each other's arithmetic and a biscuit tin with a slot cut in the lid for the legal fund. Prudence takes minutes, in ink, unasked.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_assembly Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You push the tiny tables together just as the blazer man declares the gymnasium a private venue and the guards begin folding it. A chair built for a seven year old is removed while you are still on it. The parents help you up, which is how you formally meet them: from below, being lifted. The rota forms anyway, around your ankle, which is now part of the story.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_assembly Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Monday assembly opens with the recorder ensemble attempting the FutureMinds corporate anthem, which was not written for recorders, or for children, or arguably for humans. The invoice is projected onto the hymn board where the harvest song used to go. The blazer man calls it an exciting learning journey; the deputy head mouths the words half a second late, like a hostage. Prudence raises her hand to report that slide two divides by zero, and is thanked for her enthusiasm.

choices.text: Sell the blazer man a list of difficult parents

outcomes.text: You quietly hand the blazer man a list of parents who ask questions, ranked by volume. He pays a finder's fee and then, delighted, a retention bonus to keep the list current. By Thursday the school gate has a guard hut, a barrier, and a sign about safeguarding that no child can read but every parent understands. The money is excellent and folds flat, unlike your posture when the apprentice teacher says good morning to you, twice, in case you missed it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_retaliation Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Stand beside the teacher who stops singing

success.text: When the apprentice teacher lowers her recorder and refuses the second verse, you stand up next to her, which in assembly terms is a declaration of war. Row by row the singing dies until the anthem is one man in a blazer, alone, performing the harmonies of a corporation. The guards, with nothing to guard against except quiet, are sent home early. The deputy head marks the register afterwards and, against every instinct he owns, records the silence as full attendance.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_retaliation Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You stand when the apprentice teacher stops singing, and a guard mistakes your rising for a lunge. You are helped from the gymnasium through the small door, the one sized for children, at speed. The anthem finishes without you; the bruise is anthem shaped. Even so, the parents notice who stood first, and the biscuit tin appears on your doorstep that evening with one biscuit in it, which is how these people say thank you.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_retaliation Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Ask him to walk everyone through slide two

outcomes.text: You raise your hand, politely, and ask the blazer man to walk the assembly through slide two, the one with the division. He begins confidently, slows at the denominator, and dies somewhere in the footnote that cites itself. Four hundred children watch a grown man fail maths in a room where that has consequences. The guards look away, one of them mouthing the correct answer, and Prudence awards him a merit.

outcomes.text: You ask about slide two, and the blazer man pivots to a video called Our Learning Journey, loud enough to end questions as a category. The projector, technically school property, technically sold, drowns you out with a song about synergy performed by children who were paid in exposure. The assembly ends on schedule. Your question is logged as engagement, which FutureMinds counts as support.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

The morning post brings Invoice Two, which charges interest hourly and a fee for receiving the fee. The apprentice teacher has been given flexibility, which is the word FutureMinds uses for taking away her classroom and her chair. She teaches long division in the corridor now, chalk dust on everything she loves. The deputy head laminates the new fee schedule with the expression of a man laminating his own conscience.

choices.text: Carry benches until the corridor is a school

outcomes.text: You spend the morning carrying furniture, and by noon the corridor is a school again: benches from the chapel, a blackboard from somebody's parlor, chalk donated in fistfuls. The apprentice teacher teaches long division to a hallway so crowded the guards must queue to observe it, and queueing, they learn some long division. FutureMinds bills the corridor for occupancy; the corridor, having forty tenants now, splits the bill forty ways into nothing much. Someone chalks a small hand on your door, which is corridor for one of us.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You carry the first bench in alone, which is one person fewer than a bench requires. The corner takes you at knee height in front of the entire remedial reading group, who sound it out together. The apprentice teacher splints nothing, sprains nothing, but documents everything, because documentation is the only furniture FutureMinds respects. The corridor school opens a day late, with you seated at the front, elevated, as a teaching aid on load bearing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: File the fee for receiving fees as usury

success.text: You file Invoice Two with the magistrate under the usury statutes, flagging the fee charged for receiving the fee. The magistrate reads it twice, once as law and once as literature, and rules that a charge may not bill its own arrival, whatever FutureMinds' notation implies. Hourly interest is suspended pending an audit. The blazer man calls it a technicality, which from him is the highest compliment a law can receive.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You file under the usury statutes and FutureMinds' advocate, whose lanyard is nicer than the magistrate's robes, reclassifies the fee as a subscription. Subscriptions, it turns out, may compound, recur, and grieve you, all lawfully. Costs are awarded against the corridor. The apprentice teacher adds the ruling to the maths syllabus under worked examples of getting away with it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Crack Invoice Two for a paying client

success.text: You take a private commission from a rival academy that wants to know how FutureMinds' billing works, strictly for admiration. Three nights with a candle and Invoice Two gives up its secret: the interest references a rate that does not exist, so every demand is a polite guess. Your client pays four coins, and you leave a copy of the working, anonymously, in the corridor's chalk box. The parents recognize the handwriting and say nothing, coldly.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You take the commission, crack the invoice, and make the mistake of mentioning your findings near the blazer man, who outbids everyone in the room within a minute. Eight coins, cash, and your working goes into a drawer that locks with a sound like a school closing. The corridor hears you were bought before you get back to it. The chalk box is moved so that you would have to ask where it is, and nobody waits to be asked.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_coalition Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Wednesday, the corridor, and the apprentice teacher reaches the end of her last textbook with half a term left. She looks at the pile of tuition invoices, and something in her snaps, quietly, in cursive. New homework, due Friday: solve your family's invoice, show your working, marks off for guessing. Forty children take out their pencils; Prudence asks whether she may use ink, and whether appendices are permitted. She is told yes, warily, to both.

choices.text: Post the mark scheme on the notice board

outcomes.text: You copy the mark scheme onto the corridor notice board, between netball trials and lost property: what the invoice claims, what it should sum to, and the space between, labeled profit. Parents read it at pickup, then merchants, then, fatally, a schools inspector who was only there about the netball. The inspector copies it into an official notebook, which makes it evidence with a border drawn around it. The deputy head laminates it before anyone can be ordered not to.
effects: schedule 5 r18_school_sale_crisis Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You post the mark scheme and by morning FutureMinds has glazed the entire notice board and declared its contents proprietary curriculum. A cease letter arrives addressed to The Corridor, Occupant Of. The netball trials are cancelled as collateral damage, which costs you more local goodwill than the maths ever earned. Behind the glass the mark scheme is at least preserved, like a saint, or an insect.
effects: schedule 5 r18_school_sale_crisis Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Sell answer keys to nervous parents

outcomes.text: You let it be known, at the gate, at pickup, that answer keys exist for nervous families, priced by anxiety. Business is immediate and excellent: eight coins by Thursday, six more for a rush order from a father who cannot face being corrected by his own daughter. The keys are accurate, your conscience is itemized, and the corridor pretends not to see you making change out of a pencil case. It works. That is the whole thing about it: it works.

choices.text: Buy the pencils and the long paper yourself

outcomes.text: You spend three coins on pencils, rulers, and the long paper, because invoice arithmetic does not fit on pages meant for spelling. The working, taped end to end, runs the length of the corridor and turns the corner by the trophy case. Parents who came to collect children stay to hold the tape. By Friday the corridor has a paper spine down its middle, and everyone steps over it the way you step over something holy.

outcomes.text: You arrive with forty rulers under one arm just as the gate guards decide stationery requires inspection. The inspection is conducted at shoulder height, briefly through you. You keep hold of the rulers, losing a little skin to the gravel, and the corridor gets its supplies dented but countable. Prudence signs for them, notes the damage in the margin, and adds, in ink, delivered with valor.

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Friday's homework arrives in wheelbarrows. Forty children have shown their working, and the working all shows the same thing: the invoice only balances if the school was sold for nothing, to itself, twice. Prudence's answer runs to nine pages and ends with the words see appendix. The blazer man declares a surprise fire drill, the kind where guards collect exercise books instead of children. The deputy head goes and stands in the doorway of the store cupboard where the homework is hidden, and does not move.

choices.text: Name a price for the appendix

outcomes.text: You take the blazer man aside and name a number for the appendix, and he doubles it before you finish, which tells you what it is worth and that you asked low. Fourteen coins in a school envelope, poster paint smell and all, and Prudence's nine pages leave the cupboard under his coat. On Monday you learn what a methodical child does with a Friday deadline: she numbered the copies. His is copy six of eleven, and copy one is already somewhere with a wax seal on it.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Fill the cupboard doorway with coats and biscuits

outcomes.text: You arrange the parents across the cupboard doorway in good coats, unhurried, holding biscuits, the least chargeable formation known to law. The guards ask everyone to move along and are offered a biscuit instead, and there is no procedure for that; the drill manual assumes hostility and has nothing on hospitality. The fire drill ends when the fire fails to attend. The homework stays hidden, and one guard leaves his helmet on the pegs by the small door, ostensibly by accident.

outcomes.text: You brace the cupboard door and the wheelbarrow takes the brunt when the guards test their mandate. In the scrum you catch a corner of shelving with your ribs, filed under minor abrasions, resented in full. The homework survives; the biscuits are confiscated as evidence of premeditated assembly. The word premeditated does a lot of work in that report, none of it honest.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Declare the homework protected coursework

success.text: You invoke the Examinations Charter, coursework clause, which vests every marked page in the child who wrote it. The guards, being unable to mark, cannot own; being unable to own, cannot seize; the logic takes four minutes and the blazer man ages a year per minute. The homework is declared coursework, protected, sacred, filed. Somewhere in the store cupboard, forty exercise books become the best defended documents in the region.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You invoke the Examinations Charter and FutureMinds produces an amendment, dated yesterday, notarized somehow, defining the invoice as curriculum and all responses to it as company property. The magistrate on duty is a graduate of a FutureMinds night course and recuses himself into agreeing. The cupboard is sealed with tape that says EXCITING. The tape is the worst part, everyone agrees.
effects: schedule 2 r18_school_sale_compromise Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

A settlement arrives on paper so good it apologizes for the rest of the week. FutureMinds will enroll every child in a Scholarship of Perpetual Gratitude, repayable in adulthood at rates to be advised, and there will be a signing ceremony with a bell choir and a banner that says PARTNERSHIP. The apprentice teacher reads the terms aloud in the corridor, slowly, the way she reads back spelling mistakes. Prudence calculates that a scholar would make the final payment at the age of ninety one, assuming good health and no further education, and posts her working next to the netball scores.

choices.text: Throw a rival prize giving in the bike shed

outcomes.text: You hold a rival ceremony in the bike shed at the same hour: certificates for Best Working, a choir of parents who cannot sing but mean it, and a banner reading NO THANK YOU in a child's confident lettering. Attendance at the official signing collapses, because the shed has cake. The bell choir drifts over, then the guards, then, at the very end, the deputy head, who claims he is checking the fire exits and stays for two slices. Nothing gets signed anywhere, which was the entire syllabus.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You hold the rival ceremony and the bike shed, built for bikes, objects to hosting forty families. A roof panel resigns onto your shoulder mid speech. The certificates are handed out anyway, slightly bent, while you sit on an upturned crate being seen to with the first aid kit from another century. FutureMinds' ceremony still fails; yours merely bleeds a little, which the parents agree is the better kind of failure.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Volunteer to read the scholarship aloud

outcomes.text: You volunteer to give the ceremonial reading, and nobody at FutureMinds checks what you plan to read, because volunteering looks like surrender. You read the scholarship, all of it, aloud, at pageant speed, with the bell choir instinctively tolling behind the worst clauses. By the repayment schedule the front row is weeping, and not in the sponsored way. The banner is down before the buffet, folded by the same guards who hung it, with the care of men who suspect banners now.

outcomes.text: You take the lectern and get through two clauses before the sound is cut and the choir is cued to drown the rest in bells. The official minutes record a moving address, speaker unknown, content lost. FutureMinds declares the ceremony a triumph in a newsletter nobody can unsubscribe from. Your name goes onto a list titled Disruptions, which is at least alphabetized.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Unbolt the signing table and walk it out

success.text: You wait for the choir's loud bit, unbolt the signing table, and walk it out through the fire exit with two parents who used to move pianos. A ceremony cannot survive the loss of its furniture; contracts, like assemblies, need somewhere flat. The guards escalate to a posture the region will discuss for weeks, but by then the table is in the bike shed, serving as cake stand to a better party. The blazer man signs nothing all day, and a man like that, unsigned, is just a blazer; the parents cheer the result and still vote, gently, that next time you ask first.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You get the table two bolts loose before discovering it is oak, ceremonial oak, the kind of oak with opinions. The guards find you underneath it, which saves them the trouble of putting you there. Three days of bed rest, one formal caution, and a line in the incident book reading furniture, attempted. The signing goes ahead, thinly attended, over your horizontal objection.
effects: schedule 3 r18_school_sale_reversal Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Late on Sunday the deputy head unlocks the laminating room and shows you what he has been keeping warm: the original deed of sale, and stapled behind it, Prudence's appendix, copy one of eleven, marked in his red pen at nine and a half out of ten. Read together they prove what everyone felt and nobody could write down: FutureMinds paid for the school with the school itself, pledged as security against its own purchase price. The academy bought nothing, from nobody, and has been invoicing the town ever since for the privilege of watching. The half mark, he explains, was for untidy margins, and he has been dreading her appeal all week.

choices.text: Enter the homework as evidence, ink and all

outcomes.text: You lodge the deed and the appendix with the tribunal as a single exhibit, homework and evidence stapled into one genre. The tribunal's auditor checks the working for two hours, finds nothing to correct, and asks, in writing, whether the author is available to check his. The sale is voided from the first signature: a school cannot be its own price. Outside, the parents who came to wait begin teaching each other the appendix on the courthouse steps, because apparently that is what this town does now.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

outcomes.text: You lodge the exhibit and FutureMinds' advocates request an adjournment to consult their experts, who are their accountants, who are the problem. The tribunal grants it, plus a gag on discussing proprietary arithmetic in public. The corridor spends the recess memorizing the appendix instead, since memory is not discussion. Prudence approves this reading of the gag order, which is the nearest thing to a merit the law has earned all term.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: Sell FutureMinds advance notice of the folder

outcomes.text: You sell the blazer man advance notice of the folder, timed, priced, exact. He pays fourteen coins without haggling, which should have told you something, and orders an entire cabinet shredded, the wrong one, at speed. The tribunal date holds; the folder was copied weeks ago by a nine year old with a numbering system, and you have been paid handsomely to change nothing, which is the purest job the System offers. The biscuit tin does not visit your doorstep for a long while after that.

effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

choices.text: March the folder to the tribunal in a crowd

success.text: You walk the folder to the tribunal at the center of a procession: parents in their good coats, the corridor school in single file, the recorder ensemble playing something that was probably once a hymn. The folder rides flat on a borrowed lectern, carried like a relic. Nobody touches it; there are too many witnesses to count, so the guards count them instead, which keeps everyone usefully employed. The tribunal takes delivery on the steps, and the town applauds paperwork, sincerely, possibly for the first time.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

fail.text: You walk the folder over in procession, and on the courthouse steps a hired hand makes a grab that mostly gets your collarbone. The folder travels the last stretch overhead, passed hand to hand above the crowd, faster than anyone could have run with it. Delivery is accepted; your arm goes in a sling that three children sign before you can object. The clerk lists you in the record as conveyance, injured, and the town decides that is your title now.
effects: schedule 4 r18_school_sale_legacy Homework due Friday: solve the invoice, show your working

text:
SCHOOL SALE

Term ends with a prize giving in a gymnasium that belongs, on paper, to the people sitting in it, held in common trust by the parents and apprentice teachers, with a rota for everything including joy. The apprentice teacher has a classroom, a chair, and a plaque reading HEAD OF ARITHMETIC, ACTUAL. By the door hangs Invoice Two, laminated, graded, wearing one gold star, studied every year the way other schools study the bones of extinct predators. The deputy head laminates freely now, for love. Prudence holds a seat on the timetable committee that nobody remembers voting on; she simply attended, and minutes exist to prove it.

choices.text: Charge admission to see the famous invoice

outcomes.text: You set up a small table by the door and charge admission to view the famous invoice: a coin for townsfolk, three for rival academies, five for anyone in a blazer. The queue is constant; inspectors travel from three regions to stand before it and feel things. Fourteen coins by week's end, and the school never asks for a cut, which somehow makes it worse and also does not stop you. The invoice, laminated, watches you make change, and if it objects, it keeps that in an appendix.

The invoice, laminated, watches you make change, and if it objects, it keeps that in an appendix.

choices.text: Accept the seat Prudence saved you

success.text: You take the seat Prudence saved you on the timetable committee, between a parent who bakes and a parent who audits, the school's two natural resources. The work is small and endless: rotas, roofs, the quiet arithmetic of who needs what. When the next glossy acquisition letter arrives, the drills pay off; a first year spots the compound interest by the second paragraph and receives a merit before lunch. The invoice on the wall has done its teaching, which is more than FutureMinds ever managed at any price.

fail.text: You take the committee seat and your first assigned duty is playground supervision, which sounds gentle and is not. A dodgeball, thrown with syllabus level precision, finds you behind the ear on day one. The incident form runs two pages; Prudence countersigns as witness and includes a diagram with angles. You are voted most improved supervisor at the next prize giving, a certificate the apprentice teacher laminates herself, tenderly, while you hold the ice.

choices.text: Bill FutureMinds in their own notation

outcomes.text: You send FutureMinds an invoice for educational services rendered: one demonstration of arithmetic, forty pupils, materials provided by the customer, expressed entirely in their own notation. Legally they must either pay it or argue in public that the notation means nothing, and their advocates bill them a fortune to explain the trap slowly. They pay. The school frames the receipt beside Invoice Two, predator and lesson hung at the same height, and the deputy head dusts them both on Fridays, evenhandedly.

You send FutureMinds an invoice in their own notation and it comes back marked insufficient postage, unpaid, with a promotional leaflet for their newest venture, an academy of financial literacy. The corridor laughs for a full day, which is worth something, though not legal tender. The leaflet goes up beside Invoice Two as a companion exhibit. Prudence grades it in the margin: two out of ten, shows no working, see me.

text:
FOREST COMMONS

At dawn the junior surveyor from Royal Timber Destiny nails a citation to the oak the villages call Doreen: asset 4,112, species oak, offense, unlawful standing, four hundred counts, one per year. He apologizes to the bark while he does it, which the bark does not acknowledge. By breakfast the path crew is setting benches under her east limb, and the hedge advocate has arrived with her papers in a bread bin.

choices.text: Sell the company a map of the valuable trees

outcomes.text: The surveyor pays for the map and then pays again for you to forget you drew it, which is the first time forgetting has earned you a wage. Royal Timber Destiny stamps your invoice ASSET LOCATION SERVICES. On the trunk the citation flutters, unbothered, already outlasting your signature.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_assembly The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Chip into the bread bin kitty for Doreen's defense

outcomes.text: You put three coins in the bread bin and the hedge advocate writes you into the path crew's rota without asking, which is how the rota works. Somebody hands you a bowl of acorn stew and the seat nearest the trunk, the one reserved for people who paid before they were asked. Doreen drops one leaf on your shoulder, which the granny at the end of the bench says is as close as she comes to a receipt.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_assembly The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: You help raise the witness benches and take a scaffold plank across the ear for your trouble. The path crew inks you into the rota anyway, under the heading WOUNDED IN SERVICE, BENCH RELATED. The citation stays nailed to the trunk and you stay slightly concussed under it, both of you holding your ground.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_assembly The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Demand the defendant be properly served notice

outcomes.text: You point out that a citation must be served to the defendant, read aloud, with opportunity to respond, and the district clerk agrees with visible dread. The surveyor reads all four hundred counts to the trunk while the villages picnic, and Doreen responds by continuing, which the clerk records as NO CONTEST, ONGOING. The hearing is officially opened, the mood in the district eases, and the bread bin gains a docket number.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_assembly The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: The district clerk rules that bark cannot receive notice and that you, having touched the citation, are now its legal custodian. You spend the afternoon filing an appeal against your own appointment. The citation stays up, and a second copy is issued to you personally, in case the first one falls.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_assembly The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

text:
FOREST COMMONS

Under Doreen's east limb the hedge advocate opens the bread bin and declares the defense: standing is not the crime, standing is the testimony, and nobody in the district stands better than the defendant. Four generations of witnesses are sorting themselves by christening date. At the back a large man keeps trying to leave, and the granny keeps sitting him back down; he once fought the tree, and he knows he will be asked about it.

choices.text: Draft the witness rota by christening date

outcomes.text: The rota runs from the granny, christened under Doreen in a hailstorm, to a baby christened last spring who is entered as WITNESS, PRE VERBAL, WILL POINT. The large man is scheduled last, so he has time to make peace with himself. The potluck committee attaches itself to the rota uninvited, every testimony now comes with a pie schedule, and the citation on the trunk acquires its first admirer, a jackdaw with opinions about ribbon.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_retaliation The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: Two villages both claim the district's first christening and you are standing between them when the shoving starts. You take an elbow meant for a man three generations your senior. The rota survives, and you are on it twice, once as witness and once as casualty, which the granny says shows commitment.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_retaliation The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Enter Doreen's rings as a 400 year attendance record

success.text: You argue the defendant has kept a written attendance record in her own trunk, one ring per year, no absences, and the clerk cannot find a form for refusing a document on grounds of being alive. The rings are admitted as EXHIBIT A, SELF AUTHORED. Royal Timber Destiny objects that the exhibit cannot be filed in a cabinet, and the clerk rules that the cabinet may be filed near the exhibit.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_retaliation The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

fail.text: The clerk asks how the record can be examined without opening the defendant, and the whole clearing hears company counsel say the words core sample with far too much hope. Your exhibit is tabled pending a gentler method. The advocate pats your arm, says good lawyers lose better than that, and shows you how.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_retaliation The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Sell the acorn flour reserve to gawking travelers

outcomes.text: Travelers have walked from three districts to see the tree that got cited, and you sell them the assembly's acorn flour at pilgrimage prices, plus a second batch labeled FROM THE DEFENDANT HERSELF. The kitty sees none of it. The granny watches you count the coins and says nothing, which costs you more than talking would.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_retaliation The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

text:
FOREST COMMONS

Three days later Royal Timber Destiny fences the old paths and posts them CLOSED, INVENTORY AISLES, AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY. The paths in question predate the company, the district, and arguably the concept of authorized personnel. The large man stands at the fence flexing his hands, and the granny stands next to him holding his coat, in case.

choices.text: File that the paths predate the company's charter

outcomes.text: The hedge advocate produces a christening procession map older than the company's charter by a century, drawn in a hand so faded the ink has opinions. The district rules the paths were never the company's to close, and the fence is reclassified as LITTER, LARGE. The path tolls the company had penciled into next year's rents quietly vanish, and the clerk pretends they were never penciled.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_coalition The oak has been cited for unlawful standing
