outcomes.text: Company counsel argues the map shows paths, not permission, and the clerk, exhausted, schedules the question for a hearing at a date described as eventual. The fence stays and the district grinds its teeth. Someone paints AISLE FOUR on the granny's favorite shortcut, which she takes personally.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_coalition The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Help the big man pull the fence out by moonlight

success.text: The fence comes up in polite sections, which you stack outside the company depot with a note reading RETURNED, UNUSED. The large man handles the posts like a man settling an old score with lumber in general, and the tolls the company meant to charge on the paths die of embarrassment. The path crew, which had a filing ready and a speech to go with it, is diplomatically furious with you both.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_coalition The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

fail.text: The third post is set in company concrete and does not care about your shoulder, your footing, or your plan. You wake up under the fence with the large man apologizing, mostly to the fence. Compliance officers sketch the scene for a pamphlet about outside agitators, and your name is spelled wrong in it, twice.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_coalition The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Walk every old path daily so they stay in use

outcomes.text: The path crew builds a walking rota and the district's oldest law does the rest: a path walked daily is a path, whatever the signage says. Grannies promenade through the inventory aisles at dawn with baskets, on legal advice, and pay their respects to the citation on the way past, as one does to a relative doing well. The company's enforcement budget is spent guarding fences from women who wish them good morning.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_coalition The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: You take the dusk shift alone and turn an ankle in a rut the company dug for a survey stake. You finish the walk anyway, because an interrupted walk does not count and the rota is strict. The path stays legally in use, and you stay legally limping.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_coalition The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

text:
FOREST COMMONS

The river arrives for the hearing a week early, as rivers do, and must be accommodated: nature has standing in this district, and the defense intends to call all of it. The swamp has sent a deposition written in moss, the hill has sent regrets, and Doreen has sent nothing, because Doreen will be present, as always. The hedge advocate stares at the seating plan and asks how one chairs a river.

choices.text: Sell Royal Timber Destiny a list of ringleaders

outcomes.text: The company pays for a list of the defense's key people, then pays again when you pad it with three invented troublemakers and one woman who died before the company existed. Somewhere a clerk opens a file on her anyway, marked DECEASED, PRESUMED DANGEROUS. The bread bin gets thinner, and so does everyone's patience with strangers who take notes.
effects: schedule 2 r18_forest_commons_crisis The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Solve the seating plan for a river and a swamp

success.text: You dig the river a channel along the north side of the clearing so it can attend without flooding the witness benches, and it settles in with the sound of a satisfied audience. The moss deposition is stationed in the shade with a boy assigned to water it. Within the month two other districts copy your seating plan under the heading PRECEDENT, DAMP.
effects: schedule 2 r18_forest_commons_crisis The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

fail.text: The channel is a hand's width too shallow and the river attends enthusiastically anyway, through the benches, briefly through you. The witnesses relocate uphill with reasonable grace. Attendance is nonetheless recorded as full, and the river is noted in the minutes as APOLOGETIC, STILL PRESENT.
effects: schedule 2 r18_forest_commons_crisis The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Get the swamp's moss deposition admitted as evidence

outcomes.text: You argue the deposition is signed, since the moss grew in the swamp's own hand, and the clerk, who has already admitted tree rings, has no ground left to stand on. The moss is entered as EXHIBIT B and keeps growing, which counsel objects to as amending testimony after filing. The clerk rules that living evidence may supplement itself, a precedent the whole forest hears about by nightfall.
effects: schedule 2 r18_forest_commons_crisis The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: Company counsel demands the moss be dried for the permanent record, and the clerk, cornered by his own manual, agrees. The boy assigned to water it is inconsolable until the swamp sends word that there is plenty more where that came from. The defense loses an exhibit and gains a grudge with excellent drainage.

text:
FOREST COMMONS

Trial day, and the courtroom has failed its one job: Doreen does not fit in it, so the court convenes under her instead, which the defense counts as a home fixture. Then company counsel rises and invokes the emergency harvest clause: pending assets may be liquidated before verdict if the company certifies urgency. The saw wagon is already on the road, certified urgent.

choices.text: Run the witnesses in relay so court cannot adjourn

success.text: The rota holds: christening by christening, proposal by proposal, the villages testify in an unbroken chain, and a court in session cannot be harvested around, even by the urgent. At dusk the large man takes the stand and confesses to the whole district that he fought the defendant in his youth and lost on points. He demonstrates the grip; Doreen declines, again, to demonstrate hers; the saw wagon waits at the fence line all night with nothing legal to do.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_compromise The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

fail.text: The relay holds until midnight, when the torch you are carrying between witnesses spends some time on your hand. You finish the handoff, because an interrupted relay is an adjourned court. By dawn the chain is intact, you are bandaged, and the saw wagon has not moved an inch it can prove.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_compromise The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Cite their manual: assets under citation are frozen

outcomes.text: You read from the Royal Timber Destiny compliance manual, page 212: an asset under active citation may not be modified, moved, or milled until the citation resolves. Their citation, their manual, their tree, untouchable by their own hand. Counsel asks for a recess to consult the manual and is told the manual is now evidence, and the saw wagon is sent home with a receipt.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_compromise The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: Counsel produces a newer edition of the manual, with page 212 revised to say ask your regional director. The clerk admits both editions and schedules a hearing to determine which manual is the real manual. The saw wagon idles at the fence line, burning company money, which is not a victory but does feel like one.

choices.text: Provision the saw wagon crew at urgency prices

outcomes.text: The saw crew was certified urgent without being certified fed, so you sell them supper at triple rate and breakfast at quadruple, citing urgency. Their foreman pays from a company purse and signs your receipt MISCELLANEOUS COMPLIANCE. The villages watch you feed the enemy well, and the seat nearest the trunk is somehow no longer yours.
effects: schedule 3 r18_forest_commons_compromise The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

text:
FOREST COMMONS

A settlement arrives by liveried courier: Royal Timber Destiny will withdraw the citation, with prejudice, if the villages accept the inventory of everything else, every path, every coppice, every stump with prospects. The hedge advocate reads it twice and puts it in the bread bin face down. The granny asks what with prejudice means and is told it means they want to stop being laughed at.

choices.text: Counter: keep Doreen on their books forever, unfellable

success.text: You propose the company keep the defendant listed as an appreciating asset that may never be liquidated, gaining value ring by ring on their own ledger until the end of ledgers. Their accountants fall in love, because an asset that only appreciates is the most beautiful thing anyone has ever offered them. The citation stays active by design, freezing the saws forever under page 212, and counsel signs before anyone upstairs does the math on what the villages just got for free.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_reversal The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

fail.text: The accountants like it; the regional director does not, on the grounds that it sounds clever, and clever has been beating him for weeks. The counter is refused with a stiffness that suggests a nerve. The settlement talks cool, the district holds its breath, and the courier stops accepting tea.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_reversal The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Audit the company inventory ledger yourself

success.text: You take the district's auditing stipend and open the company ledger, and it is a work of fiction in a lovely hand: a hill listed as standing timber, a heron counted twice, forty trees that are one tree's reflection in the mill pond. Your report is read to open court and open laughter. The path crew is grateful, and also notes in the rota margin that you spent the week in the company's offices, eating the company's biscuits.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_reversal The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

fail.text: You find the fictions, the reflected forest, the twice counted heron, and a company man finds you finding them. He buys your notes at a price that suggests they were worth more, and the ledger stays a rumor with excellent penmanship. In open court the defense argues against a document nobody can produce, while you count your coins away from the benches.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_reversal The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Put the settlement to a potluck vote of the villages

outcomes.text: The vote is held over four long tables, one per generation, and the settlement is read aloud between courses so nobody decides on an empty stomach. It fails unanimously; even the baby votes no by pointing at the correct tree this time, to sustained applause. The advocate returns the papers to the courier with a pie for the road, because a no can be a no and still come with pie.
effects: schedule 4 r18_forest_commons_reversal The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: The vote is not the problem; the tables are. You are carrying the fourth generation's table uphill when it wins. The settlement still fails unanimously from your position under the tabletop, and the granny counts your muffled no aloud so it enters the record.

text:
FOREST COMMONS

Under oath, with his spyglass entered as evidence, the junior surveyor cracks: he never touched a single tree, counted the whole forest from a tower at quota speed, and knew the ledger was fiction the day he signed it. Then the real confession: he cited Doreen on purpose. His quota demanded one enforcement action, and he chose the only defendant in the district that could not possibly lose.

choices.text: Buy company timber claims cheap as the scrip crashes

outcomes.text: The clearing empties of company clerks the way a tower empties in a fire, and you buy their timber claims for less than the paper's weight in bread. By evening you are technically a timber magnate; by the weekend you have resold everything to men in the capital who have not heard yet. The granny asks what you own now, and you answer, honestly, nothing that stands.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_legacy The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Take the fired surveyor in before nightfall

outcomes.text: He is dismissed by courier before he is even off the stand, so you spend your own coin on boots, a coat, and a bed at the granny's, who has decided he reminds her of a nephew. It turns out a man who counted a forest wrong from a tower is desperate to count something right from the inside, and by first frost he has recounted the granaries the company muddled and found the villages a winter they did not know they owned. He cries a little into the acorn stew, which everyone politely attributes to the stew.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_legacy The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: You reach him just as two former colleagues arrive to discuss the company's disappointment, and the discussion includes your ribs. The large man arrives second, and the discussion ends. The surveyor sleeps in the granny's back room, and you sleep sitting up, breathing carefully, featured prominently in everyone's prayers.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_legacy The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

choices.text: Move that Doreen's standing be ruled lawful forever

outcomes.text: The judge rules from beneath the defendant, which everyone agrees is fitting: standing is found lawful, retroactively, perpetually, and in the judge's words, self evidently. The company inventory is voided and a fresh survey ordered, to be conducted by persons who climb, a phrase the path crew hears as a job posting. The old paths reopen in law as well as in mud, and the district's larders breathe easier with the woods back on the villages' side of the fence.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_legacy The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

The company inventory is voided and a fresh survey ordered, to be conducted by persons who climb, a phrase the path crew hears as a job posting. The old paths reopen in law as well as in mud, and the district's larders breathe easier with the woods back on the villages' side of the fence.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_legacy The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

outcomes.text: The judge rules narrowly: this citation is void, this tree may stand, and the wider question of forests is deferred to a commission with no meeting date. The advocate calls it a win the size of one tree. The company's remaining clerks take the deferral as permission to keep the ledger warm, and the stacked fence posts wait by the depot to learn whether they were litter after all.
effects: schedule 5 r18_forest_commons_legacy The oak has been cited for unlawful standing

text:
FOREST COMMONS

A year on, the verdict is fading in its file and Doreen is not: she has added a ring, her four hundred and first, annotated by nobody. The citation hangs on her trunk in weatherproof glass, the forest's first and only trophy, and christenings have resumed beneath it on schedule. The new survey is underway, conducted by people who climb, one of whom used to own a spyglass and now refuses to count anything he has not personally touched.

choices.text: Join the climbing survey as warden of the old paths

outcomes.text: You are sworn in with sap on your hands, the only oath the path crew recognizes, and given a stretch of paths to walk and a ledger to keep honestly. Your first entry reads: one oak, present, magnificent, defendant retired. The granny attends the swearing in, the surveyor checks your arithmetic, and the large man teaches you the grip, purely, he says, for historical purposes.

You are sworn in with sap on your hands, the only oath the path crew recognizes, and given a stretch of paths to walk and a ledger to keep honestly. Your first entry reads: one oak, present, magnificent, defendant retired. The granny attends the swearing in, the surveyor checks your arithmetic, and the large man teaches you the grip, purely, he says, for historical purposes.

Your first climb goes beautifully until the descent, which the crew scores generously as a landing. You are entered in the rota as WARDEN, AIRBORNE BRIEFLY, and assigned the low paths while your ankle reconsiders. The ledger you keep from a bench under Doreen is, everyone agrees, the most accurate in the district.

choices.text: Write the plaque to hang beside the framed citation

success.text: You draft it in one line: SERVED FOUR HUNDRED COUNTS OF STANDING, PLEADED NOTHING, STANDING STILL. The advocate reads it, laughs once, the way she does when a filing is airtight, and has it carved. Visiting clerks from three districts copy the wording into their notebooks, and somewhere in the capital a compliance manual quietly gains a chapter on what citations are for.

fail.text: Your draft runs to four paragraphs and uses the word jurisprudence twice, and the granny falls asleep against the trunk during the reading, which is the district's harshest form of edit. The frame hangs with no plaque while you revise, so visiting clerks invent their own explanations, and the version with the ghost is the one that travels.

choices.text: Charge pilgrims a coin to view the famous trophy

outcomes.text: Pilgrims come steadily now, and you set up at the fence line with a coin box and a speech about the tree that beat a company, admission voluntary, refusal awkward. It works; it simply works, all season. Doreen does not mind, the kitty never sees a copper, and four hundred years from now the rings will not record you either.

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

At first light a company barge noses into the reed beds and a crew in dry boots installs a safety mascot: a plywood frog, belly up, eyes crossed out, wearing a little hard hat. The sign beneath it reads ACCIDENTS HAPPEN, WHICH IS WHY WE APOLOGIZE IN ADVANCE. The reed warden stands in the shallows with a petition on waterproof paper and a look that could cure timber. Somewhere below, the swamp squelches once, meaningfully.

choices.text: Wade in and gather signatures with the warden

success.text: You spend the morning thigh deep, holding the clipboard while the warden holds the neighbors. By noon the petition has forty names, three webbed, one just a damp circle from the swamp itself, which the warden counts twice on principle. The camp decides you are one of theirs and issues you waders with somebody else's name scratched out. Above the barge, the plywood frog watches everything with its crossed out eyes and says nothing, which is the most anyone has ever gotten out of a safety mascot.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_assembly A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: You step off the path to reach a holdout household and the swamp, testing your commitment, accepts your left boot as a deposit. You come back barefoot, leech adjacent, and short one page of signatures, which is now bedding for something. The neighbors are agitated, mostly on your behalf, partly about the barge. The warden issues you waders anyway; the camp keeps its promises even to people who negotiate with mud.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_assembly A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: File a spawning ground objection before the ink dries

outcomes.text: The clerk at the district office reads your objection twice, because the first time he assumed it was a joke and the second time he assumed it was a good one. Spawning pools, it turns out, have standing; somebody granted it in an old wet century and nobody ever dared take it back. Continental's schedule pauses with a sound like a barge swallowing. The plywood frog stays on its pole, already the only thing on site with nothing to be ashamed of.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_assembly A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: The clerk rejects your objection on the grounds that the frogs did not sign it, and frogs, he notes, have signatures on file from a prior case. You return to the pools to collect them and learn that frog signatures are seasonal. Continental's lawyers cite your rejected paperwork as proof of due process, which is a sentence they keep in stock. The pressure on the marsh goes up a notch, and the plywood frog gets a second, larger sign.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_assembly A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Sell the surveyor your depth chart of the pools

outcomes.text: The company surveyor pays eight up front and six on delivery, and shakes your hand with the gratitude of a man who no longer has to get wet. Your chart is good; it is, unfortunately, why the trench line now runs straight and confident through the deepest spawning pool. The camp watches you count coins in the launch and does not say anything, which is worse. The plywood frog on its pole appears, if anything, slightly more dead.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_assembly A dead frog becomes load bearing

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

Three nights later the drying shed hosts the camp's regular meeting, tea urn hissing, mascot in attendance because someone carried it in out of the rain and now it feels rude to put it back. The reed warden proposes the unthinkable: keep it. The apology officer from Continental has already sent a note asking politely for the company's frog to be returned in its original condition, dead. The spokesfrog, up from the pools for the evening, examines the mascot at length and pronounces it a fair likeness of his uncle.

choices.text: Adopt the frog, name him, swear him in

outcomes.text: He is named Herbert by a show of hands, and the swamp, consulted through the floorboards, burps assent. The warden knits him a scarf; the spokesfrog appoints him honorary pool safety officer, a title Herbert accepts with characteristic stillness. Continental's note is answered in the camp's best handwriting: the frog has taken up residence and sends his regards. It is very hard, the apology officer discovers, to draft a stern reply to that.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_retaliation A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: The naming goes long, as namings do, and by the time Herbert is Herbert the camp has also voted him a scarf, a feast day, and a pension, none of which exist. Continental's lawyers respond by dawn with a cease and desist regarding unauthorized affection toward company property. The district reads the phrase unauthorized affection out loud in the market and laughs for a week, but the paperwork is real and the pressure climbs. Herbert, newly sworn in, already has a legal history.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_retaliation A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Charge a copper at the door for Herbert's swearing in

outcomes.text: The shed fills twice over; people will pay to watch a dead frog take office, this is simply known now. You collect eight coppers in the tin and six more from a Continental clerk who attends in a false mustache and takes notes. The camp finds the door charge cold, given the occasion, and the tin does not make it into the kitty. Herbert presides over his own ticketed inauguration, scarf slightly askew, more beloved and more merchandised by the hour.

choices.text: Carry Herbert house to house so the parish can meet him

success.text: You walk the plank roads all day with Herbert on your shoulder, and door after door opens to a dead frog in a scarf the way doors never opened to a petition. Old men salute him; children explain him to their parents, correctly. By dusk half the parish has touched his hard hat for luck, and the other half is planning to. The spokesfrog follows at a distance, professionally satisfied, like a manager watching a trainee exceed the forecast.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_retaliation A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: On the fourth plank road Herbert and you both go through a rotten board, and you protect him with your body, which the parish witnesses and will never forget. Your ankle swells; his scarf stays dry, priorities being what they are. The neighborhood works itself up about the state of the roads, the state of the swamp, and the near loss of Herbert, in ascending order. The camp inducts you into the rota with honors and a poultice.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_retaliation A dead frog becomes load bearing

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

On collection day a Continental crew arrives with a velvet lined crate and a work order for the retrieval of one safety mascot, deceased. Survey stakes have sprouted overnight in the reed beds like confident orange mushrooms. The apology officer stands at the front with a prepared statement of regret for an action that has not happened yet, which is the department's specialty. All night the swamp has been rehearsing opinions, and it releases one now, low and long, from somewhere under the barge.

choices.text: Ask the crate crew what happens to retrieved mascots

success.text: They tell you, because nobody has ever asked: retrieved mascots go to a warehouse of discontinued regret outside the depot, shelf after shelf of crossed out eyes. One of them shows you a sketch of the shelf reserved for Herbert and gets a tremor in his voice doing it. They pay you four coppers to sign a witness form, decide as a crew that they are not paid enough to crate a frog with a name, and leave the velvet box on the dock. The camp is unsettled that you talked to the enemy at all, but the enemy walked away, so quietly.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: They pay you eight coppers for what the work order calls local liaison, and you liaise, mostly by holding the crate lid. Word travels before you are done: the camp hears you were on the velvet end of the operation. The crew fails to take Herbert, because the shed is locked and the swamp has moved the dock, but Continental logs local resistance and requisitions an escort for next time. You keep the coppers; the coppers keep the story.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Hide Herbert in the eel smoker until the crate leaves

outcomes.text: Herbert spends the retrieval in the cold half of the eel smoker, guarded by the spokesfrog, who has arranged, purely as a favor, for forty live frogs to sunbathe on the velvet crate. The crew cannot bring themselves to move a frog in order to take a frog, which is the entire trap. The apology officer reads the statement of regret to an empty pole and receives a small ovation from the reeds. Herbert emerges an hour later faintly smoked and considerably more distinguished.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: The smoker's cold half turns out to be the warm half; you get Herbert out unharmed at the cost of your eyebrows and a burn you will describe as a work injury forever. The crew leaves empty handed but files a report about smoke of suspicious origin, and the district simmers along with the eels. The camp treats your burn with marsh honey and admiration. Herbert smells permanently of eel now, which the parish decides is his cologne.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Demand the crew produce a removal permit for a resident

success.text: You argue that Herbert, having an address, a title, and a scarf, is a resident, and residents cannot be retrieved, only evicted, which requires a hearing. The crew foreman checks the work order, finds the word retrieval doing too much work, and stands his people down out of respect for paperwork he does not have. The apology officer looks briefly, treacherously relieved. The stakes stay in the reeds, but the crate goes home empty, riding light.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: The magistrate rules that Herbert is not a resident but an installation, and installations belong to whoever installed them. It is a clean ruling and a bad afternoon. The camp appeals on the grounds that Herbert has a feast day, which is not a legal argument yet, and the pressure on the marsh ratchets up. Herbert remains in the shed under what the warden calls protective affection.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_coalition A dead frog becomes load bearing

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

By the new moon Herbert has outgrown the parish: two neighboring counties have written asking to borrow him, and a third has simply printed him on a beer label and hoped. The camp plans a touring procession, pools to market to chapel green, with the spokesfrog drilling his people for the send off. The swamp contributes fog at tasteful moments. The apology officer has begun attending Herbert events in a plain coat, in what everyone politely pretends is secrecy.

choices.text: Put three coppers in the kitty for Herbert's wagon

outcomes.text: The wagon that carries Herbert out of the marsh is bought with small coins and painted by whoever was holding a brush, and your three coppers bought the axle, which the warden announces publicly, to your lasting credit. Three counties turn out along the roads; innkeepers argue over who hosted him first; a bridge town declares a half holiday with full pay, and the millers actually honor it. The spokesfrog rides in front, reviewing the crowds like a general who has finally been issued a parade. Herbert returns muddier, scarfed in three new scarves, and famous.
effects: schedule 3 r18_swamp_pipeline_crisis A dead frog becomes load bearing

effects: schedule 3 r18_swamp_pipeline_crisis A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: Your coppers are still in your hand when the axle cracks on the chapel hill, so the kitty never sees them; what the tour gets instead is your shoulder under the wagon bed for the length of the hymn, which is long, this being a parish that enjoys a crisis. Herbert does not so much as wobble, and the crowd credits him for the miracle, correctly identifying you as the mechanism. Your back files an objection that will be heard for a fortnight. Word of the near disaster travels farther than the tour does.
effects: schedule 3 r18_swamp_pipeline_crisis A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Book Herbert a viewing box at the harvest fair

outcomes.text: Herbert presides over the fair from a bunting draped box between the cheese tent and the magistrate, outranking at least one of them. Continental has a promotional stall at the same fair, staffed by two clerks and a banner about progress, and by noon the clerks have drifted over to have their portraits sketched with Herbert like everybody else. The district paper runs the sketch. There is no caption that helps Continental, and the editor tries several.

outcomes.text: The fair committee, after dark negotiations with a brewery, seats Herbert directly under the Continental banner, and the resulting portrait looks unfortunately like an endorsement. The camp spends a week explaining that Herbert endorses nothing, he is dead, that is his entire rhetorical position. Continental's stall hands out pastries shaped like him before anyone can stop it. The pastries are, and this is the worst part, excellent.
effects: schedule 3 r18_swamp_pipeline_crisis A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: License Herbert's likeness to a pie stall chain

outcomes.text: The pie men pay eight for the face and six for the scarf, cash in a napkin, and by week's end there is a Herbert pie in every market within a day's cart ride: pastry frog, blackcurrant interior, eyes piped shut. The camp is livid; the kitty saw none of it and the warden refers to you for a while as the estate of a friend. But the pies travel where processions cannot, and strangers who never saw a marsh now feel strongly about a frog. Greed, this once, does the tour's job for it, and eats well besides.
effects: schedule 3 r18_swamp_pipeline_crisis A dead frog becomes load bearing

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

Quarter's end lands like a dropped crate: Continental announces the trench will cross the spawning pools this week, and, in the same circular, that the safety mascot program has been discontinued for cost reasons. Discontinued, the apology officer confirms miserably, means collected and pulped. The spokesfrog activates the evacuation rota he has drilled all season, buckets numbered, tadpoles manifested, nobody hopping out of turn. Under everything the swamp has gone quiet in the way of a courtroom before the verdict.

choices.text: Petition for a stay of digging until the spawn hatches

success.text: The magistrate, who has a Herbert pennant in his kitchen he will not discuss, grants a stay of digging until the last egg hatches, citing the old wet century precedent with visible pleasure. Continental's counsel objects that eggs cannot be parties to a case and is handed the file where frogs already are. The trench crews stand down to half pay and spend the week fishing, which the swamp permits, within reason. It is not victory; it is a fortnight, but a fortnight is a hatching.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_compromise A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: The magistrate grants the stay; the regional bench, reached by fast courier and slow money, ungrants it by evening. The camp learns the ruling from a Continental notice nailed to Herbert's empty pole, which is the sort of touch the company considers efficient and everyone else considers a confession. Pressure settles over the marsh like weather. The spokesfrog, unbothered, moves the rota up a day; he has never once trusted a courier.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_compromise A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Sell Continental your map of the firm ground

outcomes.text: The route engineer pays eight for the map and six for your silence, and compliments your draftsmanship, which stings precisely because it is deserved. Firm ground means faster digging; faster digging means the trench reaches the pools before the stay, the spawn, or the shame. The camp does not confront you; the warden just reassigns your rota slot to the tea urn, effective forever. In the evacuation buckets the tadpoles ride to safety past your handwriting.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_compromise A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Take a numbered bucket and haul spawn till dark

outcomes.text: You haul buckets in the spokesfrog's convoy from noon to lanternlight, and he corrects your carrying posture twice, professionally, the way you would for a colleague you intend to keep. Every clutch is out of the cut line by dark, logged, and settled in the nursery pools with a receipt. The apology officer, discovered helping under a borrowed hat, is issued a bucket and told the hat fools no one. That night the swamp speaks for an hour, and the old hands say it is reading out the names of everyone who carried.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_compromise A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: On the last run your boot finds the one soft spot the spokesfrog's map calls DO NOT, and you go down holding the bucket up, which is the correct order of operations. The spawn arrives unshaken; your ribs file a minority report. The camp hauls you out, wrings you out, and logs your bucket complete, because it was. The district takes the dunking of a rescuer as one insult past bearing, and the reed beds bristle with new visitors asking what Continental plans to be sorry for next.
effects: schedule 4 r18_swamp_pipeline_compromise A dead frog becomes load bearing

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

Midweek Continental offers terms: the trench proceeds, but the company will fund a memorial viewing platform from which future generations may view where things used to spawn. The camp's counteroffer is architectural: a shrine to Herbert, sited by remarkable coincidence on the exact center line of the route, dedication scheduled for whenever the company is watching. Even the fog turns out for the planning meeting. The apology officer reads the memorial platform proposal aloud and cannot finish it.

choices.text: Sink the shrine pilings on the route tonight, unasked

success.text: You and two like minded diggers sink four pilings by moonlight, and by dawn there is a platform on the center line with Herbert's plinth already mortared in. Continental cannot touch it without touching him, and everyone on both payrolls knows it. The warden is furious that nobody asked her, which you accept, because her fury comes with breakfast. The route now has a shrine problem, and shrine problems, unlike frog problems, appear on maps.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_reversal A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: The second piling goes through a bog lid and takes you with it, and you spend a cold hour being talked out of the ground by the swamp, which holds you the way a creditor holds collateral. Continental's night patrol fishes you out, writes you up, and doubles the watch on the route. The warden's fury arrives without breakfast this time. Three ribs and your standing crack in the same night; the pilings, at least, stand.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_reversal A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Keep vigil at the shrine site with the frogs

outcomes.text: The vigil is quiet and enormously well catered, the camp having long since learned that a cause with soup outlasts a cause without. Frogs sit the night in shifts, per the spokesfrog's rota, throats glowing like small lamps down the center line. Continental's watchmen, sent to observe, are fed by the third hour and humming by the fifth. Nothing is built and nothing is dug, and the dawn dig is rescheduled by somebody who never signed the order.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_reversal A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: A cold snap moves in around midnight and you give your blanket to the brazier boy, which is correct and costs you. By dawn you are croaking worse than the vigil's official croakers and the fever takes two days of your week. The district hears the company let neighbors freeze on their own marsh, which is not precisely what happened, but the story has better circulation than you do. The frogs held the line all night; the spokesfrog notes, for the record, that his people were never cold.
effects: schedule 5 r18_swamp_pipeline_reversal A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Invite the apology officer to dedicate the shrine

outcomes.text: The invitation is addressed to the officer personally, not the department, which turns out to be the load bearing courtesy. The officer arrives in the plain coat, reads a dedication that begins as boilerplate and ends as the first sincere apology in the department's history, and lays a wreath of reeds at Herbert's little boots. There are witnesses from three counties and one deeply confused Continental stenographer. The company cannot disavow its own apology; disavowing apologies is the one product it does not make.

outcomes.text: The department, smelling ceremony, sends the officer with a supervisor, a banner, and a speech about partnership between pipe and pond. The crowd hears the word partnership and begins, gently, to laugh, and the laughter is the kind that gets into upholstery and cannot be aired out. The officer stands at the plinth mouthing an apology that was not in the script. Continental logs the event as community outreach, successful, and the pressure rises with the fog.

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

Two hours past midnight the apology officer rows into camp with a lantern, a resignation letter in one pocket in case, and Continental's master safety manual in the other. Appendix F, read aloud in the drying shed: the safety mascot may not be removed, defaced, endangered, or exposed to hazard while hazardous conditions persist, and a pipeline trench is the manual's own first example of a hazardous condition. Herbert cannot be decommissioned on an active site, and the shrine now sits on the most active site in three counties. The swamp, hearing this through the floor, produces a sound the old hands identify as laughing.

choices.text: Row the officer back before the office notices

success.text: You row the long dark loop with the officer in the stern, resignation letter unposted, courage restocked. Halfway home the officer confesses to having chosen the mascot's design personally, years ago, off a memo titled RELATABLE HAZARDS, and you agree the frog turned out well despite everyone's intentions. The fog covers the return so thoroughly that even the swamp loses track of the boat and has to be told at breakfast. By nine the officer is at a desk, yawning, in a building that does not yet know it has already lost.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_legacy A dead frog becomes load bearing

fail.text: The fog, moody after being left off the vigil rota, quits halfway across, and a Continental watchman clocks the boat, the lantern, and your extremely memorable rowing form. The officer talks you both out of it by presenting the trip as proactive community listening, a phrase so plausible it will later be made mandatory. You take a chill that settles into your shoulders for a week. The manual, crucially, is already copied in three hands and one careful frog print.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_legacy A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Read appendix F into the district record at noon

outcomes.text: The clerk enters appendix F into the record while the Continental agent present objects that the manual is confidential, and the clerk asks, mildly, whether the safety of the district is confidential, and the agent sits down forever. Once it is in the record it is citable, and once it is citable every landholder on the route cites it by supper. The trench cannot lawfully approach its own mascot. Somewhere a regional engineer begins, quietly, to draw a curve.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_legacy A dead frog becomes load bearing

outcomes.text: Continental's counsel arrives with an injunction and the manual is sealed as proprietary literature, pending review by a judge who summers, it emerges, at a lodge the company built. The district watches its own safety rules get repossessed, which is agitating in a lasting way. But sealed is not gone: the parish now knows appendix F the way it knows a hymn, and the injunction covers paper. Nobody has ever managed to enjoin a hymn.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_legacy A dead frog becomes load bearing

choices.text: Ransom the manual to head office for a finder's fee

outcomes.text: Head office pays eight for the manual and six for the rowboat's silence, and a courier in company grey carries appendix F away to be revised somewhere dry. Revision takes lawyers, and lawyers take quarters, and the camp made four copies before you ever touched it, one of which is laminated and lives under the shrine. So the money is real and the betrayal is mostly ornamental, which everyone understands except the warden, who understands it perfectly and simply files it. Guards double at the depot to protect a secret three counties can recite.
effects: schedule 2 r18_swamp_pipeline_legacy A dead frog becomes load bearing

text:
SWAMP PIPELINE

On survey morning the new route stakes go in: a long respectful curve around the shrine, the pools, and, because the safety zone is measured from Herbert outward, most of the marsh. The quarterly report will call it a community centered realignment; the swamp will call it, at night, at length, winning. The apology department has been assigned the dedication plaque, and the apology officer arrives with three drafts and, for the first time in departmental history, no apology to make. The spokesfrog reviews the curve, pronounces the gradient acceptable, and stands his rota down to reserve status.

choices.text: Correct the plaque's drafts until they tell the truth

outcomes.text: Draft one thanks Continental for its vision; you strike vision and write frog. By draft three the plaque reads: THIS MARSH IS PROTECTED BY GERALD, WHO DIED, IN A MANNER OF SPEAKING, SO THAT NOTHING ELSE HAD TO, and the officer signs it on behalf of a department that has finally produced a sentence it can stand behind. The company masons carve it because carving it is cheaper than explaining why not. Visitors read it, laugh, go quiet, and put a coin in the kitty, in that order, every time.

Draft one thanks Continental for its vision; you strike vision and write frog. By draft three the plaque reads: THIS MARSH IS PROTECTED BY GERALD, WHO DIED, IN A MANNER OF SPEAKING, SO THAT NOTHING ELSE HAD TO, and the officer signs it on behalf of a department that has finally produced a sentence it can stand behind. The company masons carve it because carving it is cheaper than explaining why not. Visitors read it, laugh, go quiet, and put a coin in the kitty, in that order, every time.

Your final edit is accurate, which legal flags as the problem, and the installed plaque thanks all stakeholders for their journey. The parish fixes it within a week: someone with a nail and good handwriting adds GERALD LIVES HERE underneath, and the addition outlasts three cleanings. The officer files your original draft in the department archive under futures. The pressure of the unsaid hangs around the plinth, but the curve holds, and the curve was the point.

choices.text: Sell commemorative mud from the protected pools

outcomes.text: You jar the mud in eights and sixes, SACRED SPAWNING GROUNDS on every lid, and pilgrims buy it faster than the swamp makes it, which the swamp finds flattering and unsustainable. The kitty sees nothing; the warden sees everything; your name migrates to the rota column labeled seasonal. Still, every sold jar is a household somewhere keeping marsh on the mantel, feeling proprietary about a swamp it has never smelled. Herbert's economy, like Herbert, refuses to die.

Still, every sold jar is a household somewhere keeping marsh on the mantel, feeling proprietary about a swamp it has never smelled. Herbert's economy, like Herbert, refuses to die.

choices.text: Accept the keeper's post on the shrine rota

success.text: The warden offers you the keeper's post in front of everyone, which is how offers are made when refusal is meant to be possible and pride is meant to be public. You accept; the spokesfrog presents you with a laminated map of every soft spot in the marsh, annotated DO NOT, his highest honor. Your duties are to sweep the plinth, mind the kitty, and read complaints to the swamp on the first of the month, which it prefers aloud. Herbert watches the pipeline curve around his marsh with crossed out eyes, load bearing at last, beloved past all removal.

fail.text: You accept the post and, showing the map to a pilgrim on your first patrol, step into the one soft spot added since printing. The swamp lets you sink to the exact depth of a lesson and not a thumb further, then hands you back, initiated. The pilgrims are thrilled; word spreads that the keeper walks into the marsh and the marsh returns him, which does the shrine's reputation no harm and yours only a little. You sweep the plinth wet, mind the kitty damp, and hold the post all the same: keeper of Herbert, waders and all.

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

At dawn the licensing officer nails a notice to the observatory door: the night sky is now the exclusive product of Celestial Data Exclusives, effective retroactively. Stars may be viewed through licensed apertures only, and a comet called Wilmot is booked for next spring as the premium viewing event of the century, tickets on sale now. The lens grinder reads the notice twice, then puts the kettle on, which on this hill counts as a formal response. The kettle boy asks whether the moon is included and is told the moon is a separate bundle.

choices.text: File an objection to the ownership of up

outcomes.text: The registrar accepts the objection, largely because nobody has ever tried to register a direction before. The sky is provisionally ruled a view rather than a product, pending appeal, and the queue for appealing against up is currently eleven years. The licensing officer takes a number anyway. The kettle boy adds your name to the tea rota, which is how the hill says thank you.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_assembly The comet came early and free, out of spite

The sky is provisionally ruled a view rather than a product, pending appeal, and the queue for appealing against up is currently eleven years. The licensing officer takes a number anyway. The kettle boy adds your name to the tea rota, which is how the hill says thank you.

outcomes.text: The registrar finds that Celestial Data Exclusives filed first, and that first is the entire law on Tuesdays. The notice on the door acquires a second notice confirming the first. Wilmot, eight months out and gaining, is listed in the ruling as an exhibit, which is the closest a comet has ever come to a subpoena.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_assembly The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Sign on as a licensed aperture inspector

outcomes.text: The company pays handsomely for someone local who already knows where all the telescopes are. You walk the valley measuring your neighbors' windows for unlicensed sky exposure and are paid by the pane. The lens grinder says nothing, which takes her a remarkably long time. Wilmot's brochure now carries your signature under the words verified dark.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_assembly The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Put money in the kitty for the big lens

outcomes.text: You pay for the new eyepiece and are handed the logbook to hold, which turns out to be a rank. The lens grinder shows you a margin note from forty years back predicting Wilmot to within a week, written in pencil, because ink was for certainties. Nobody on this hill has ever paid to see a star, and the tea is free too, provided you wash your own mug.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_assembly The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The crate with the counterweight goes up the mountain the honest way, over your foot at one point. The lens grinder signs you into the logbook while you bleed respectfully into a sock. Membership here costs three coppers or one toe, and the toe is considered the more distinguished entrance.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_assembly The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

Three nights of borrowed lenses have produced a heresy: Wilmot is early. Comets keep schedules the way rivers keep appointments, but this one is eight weeks ahead of the brochure and accelerating, which the lens grinder says comets do not do for money. The hillside gathers at the chalkboard to decide who tells whom, and whether the premium viewing window of the century is about to be stood up by its own headliner.

choices.text: Sell the corrected course to the company

outcomes.text: Celestial Data Exclusives pays in advance and in cash, the two dialects it speaks fluently. The gala is rescheduled, the tickets reprinted, and Wilmot is issued a formal amendment, which it does not sign. The hillside works out whose handwriting was on the corrected tables, and the mood up there turns from tea to tools.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_retaliation The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Check the arithmetic with every eye on the hill

outcomes.text: The goat herder spots the error nobody wanted to find, the kettle boy reruns the tables, and the number survives everyone, which is the only way a number gets to be true up here. Wilmot is confirmed early by seven independent squints. The lens grinder enters it in the logbook under weather, since the sky is not currently permitted to be astronomy.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_retaliation The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The all night checking session claims your eyesight for two days, chalk by candlelight being its own dark bargain. The number holds, though, and Wilmot is officially early in the logbook. The hill decides you did the going blind so the goat herder would not have to, which is its idea of a promotion.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_retaliation The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Publish the correction as a free forecast

success.text: The corrected arrival runs on the almanac printer's front page, filed under weather, where predictions are still legally allowed to be free. The company's lawyers spend a week discovering that nobody has ever successfully sued a forecast. Wilmot, unbothered by the litigation review, gains another day on the brochure.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_retaliation The comet came early and free, out of spite

fail.text: The almanac printer loses his nerve halfway through the run, and the forecast comes out stating that Wilmot will arrive both early and on schedule, in bold. The company cites the contradiction as proof that only licensed predictions can be trusted. The hill spends the week explaining the difference between a misprint and a business model, to limited uptake.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_retaliation The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

The company's answer arrives on forty wagons: floodlight towers, ringed around the hill, to wash the stars out of the free sky. If the public will not pay for darkness, runs the reasoning, the public can have all the light it wants. The licensing officer reads a statement about protecting the integrity of the viewing product while, behind him, Wilmot becomes visible to the naked eye a full season ahead of its ticketed debut.

choices.text: Feed the night watch from a shared pot

outcomes.text: Soup goes up the hill in relays and the watch stays warm enough to keep watching. The floodlights hum at the mountain all night and the mountain declines to be lit, being large, as mountains go. Above the glare line, Wilmot is now bright enough to be seen reflected in the soup.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: Soup goes up the hill in relays and the watch stays warm enough to keep watching. The floodlights hum at the mountain all night and the mountain declines to be lit, being large, as mountains go. Above the glare line, Wilmot is now bright enough to be seen reflected in the soup.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: You carry the stockpot up in full dark to keep the flame from the patrols, and reward yourself with one moderately boiled hand. The watch eats, and the watch keeps watching. Wilmot clears the glare before the throbbing stops, which the kettle boy logs as the comet expressing sympathy.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Report the towers as unpermitted weather

outcomes.text: The district inspector finds that forty towers of artificial daylight constitute a modification of night, and night, it turns out, is protected as a public utility under an old ordinance about lamplighters. The towers are ordered dark by nine. On the first dark evening Wilmot shows a tail, and the fine print of the sky has never looked better.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The district inspector finds that forty towers of artificial daylight constitute a modification of night, and night, it turns out, is protected as a public utility under an old ordinance about lamplighters. outcomes.text: The inspector agrees the towers are outrageous, then notices the observatory's own chimney lacks a permit, and a fine is a fine. The floodlights stay lit pending review. Wilmot outshines them anyway within the fortnight, out of what the lens grinder records in the logbook as spite.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Cut the floodlight cables at the junction

success.text: The hill goes dark in one beautiful clunk and the stars come back on like a debt repaid. The company posts guards on the ruined junction, the guards look up, and the guards are lost to astronomy within the hour. The lens grinder disapproves of sabotage on principle, and logs the sky quality as excellent on the same page.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

fail.text: The junction box answers your crowbar with the full voltage of the company's opinion. You wake up downhill with singed sleeves and a citation for unlicensed interaction with light. The company hires actual soldiers to guard its lamps, and the lens grinder's silence follows you around for a week.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_coalition The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

Word of the early comet travels the valley faster than the company's retraction of it. The almanac printer arrives with a wagon of unsold star charts, the goat herders offer the high pasture with the good horizon, and three villages send delegates to ask what a paywall looks like from underneath. Meanwhile Celestial Data Exclusives begins invoicing houses whose windows face upward, a levy it calls exposure rent. Wilmot can now be seen at dusk, before the sky is even properly open for business.

choices.text: Ask what counts as a window under the levy

outcomes.text: You request the levy's definition of a window and receive a silence lasting four days. It then emerges that the wording covers any aperture admitting sky, which includes chimneys, wells, nostrils, and the company's own skylights, whose exposure rent exceeds its quarterly revenue. The levy is withdrawn quietly, the way a hand comes back from a hot stove. Wilmot appears in the withdrawal notice as an aggravating factor.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mountain_observatory_crisis The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: You request the levy's definition of a window and receive a silence lasting four days. It then emerges that the wording covers any aperture admitting sky, which includes chimneys, wells, nostrils, and the company's own skylights, whose exposure rent exceeds its quarterly revenue. The levy is withdrawn quietly, the way a hand comes back from a hot stove. Wilmot appears in the withdrawal notice as an aggravating factor.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mountain_observatory_crisis The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The clerk hears the question and rules, defensively, that everything is a window. The levy triples in scope while the appeal is drafted, and for one grim week the valley is billed for looking. Nobody stops looking, because Wilmot is up there getting brighter about it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mountain_observatory_crisis The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Take the contract to assess exposure rents

outcomes.text: Somebody will measure the windows, reasons the company, and somebody might as well be paid your rates. You walk the valley with a brass gauge and a receipt book, pricing everyone's view of Wilmot by the square foot. The comet stands fifteen degrees up and does not appear in your ledger, there being no column for the sky cooperating with the defendants.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mountain_observatory_crisis The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Host the three villages at one long table

success.text: The potluck runs to midnight and the seating plan does more work than any charter could. By pudding, the goat herders have pledged the pasture, the printer has pledged free charts, and every delegate has looked through the big lens once, which is the entire argument. Wilmot obliges with its tail out, and three villages walk home owing each other favors, the strongest currency this valley mints.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mountain_observatory_crisis The comet came early and free, out of spite

fail.text: The long table collapses at the exact moment of agreement, which everyone but your shin considers worth it. The pasture is pledged anyway, and so are the charts. The lens grinder notes that furniture fails at the rate wonder increases, and that Wilmot was very high tonight.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mountain_observatory_crisis The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

Six weeks before its ticketed debut, Wilmot arrives. It crests the ridge on a Tuesday, tail fanned, visible from every doorstep, free of charge, in flagrant breach of its own booking. The gala pavilion stands half built at the summit, hosting an emergency meeting on whether a comet can be trespassing. The licensing officer serves the sky with a cease and desist by reading it aloud, upward, into the wind.

choices.text: Scalp premium tickets to a free comet

outcomes.text: The gala tickets are worthless, which is the best possible moment to buy them. You acquire the lot for pennies and resell them to collectors as souvenirs of the century's most stood up appointment, and the company takes its cut without meeting your eye. Overhead, Wilmot burns through the exact sky it was contracted to arrive in later, unpaid, unstoppable, and unaware of you entirely.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The gala tickets are worthless, which is the best possible moment to buy them. You acquire the lot for pennies and resell them to collectors as souvenirs of the century's most stood up appointment, and the company takes its cut without meeting your eye. Overhead, Wilmot burns through the exact sky it was contracted to arrive in later, unpaid, unstoppable, and unaware of you entirely.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Get every doorstep facing uphill tonight

success.text: You knock, and the knock travels. At full dark the whole town is out at once, valley to summit, faces up, the kettle abandoned on its hook for the first time in the logbook's memory. Nobody speaks for a long minute, and the minute is not billed. The lens grinder stands beside the big lens and does not look through it, because some things are for the naked eye, and Wilmot, early, enormous, and free, is one of them.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

fail.text: You run the whole town's stairs in one evening and your ankle files a minority report. The doorsteps fill anyway, and the sky does the rest without needing you upright. From flat on your back on the cold hill you have, by general agreement, the best seat in the valley.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Move to declare the comet a member of the public

outcomes.text: The magistrate hears the petition outdoors, directly beneath the comet, which is unusual pressure for a Tuesday court. She rules that Wilmot, being visible, unticketed, and here, meets every test of the public the statute contains, and that the sky is therefore attending itself free of charge. The cease and desist is entered into the record as a letter to no one.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The magistrate hears the petition outdoors, directly beneath the comet, which is unusual pressure for a Tuesday court. She rules that Wilmot, being visible, unticketed, and here, meets every test of the public the statute contains, and that the sky is therefore attending itself free of charge. The cease and desist is entered into the record as a letter to no one.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The magistrate declines to rule, on the grounds that the comet has not been properly served, and nobody can name a bailiff with the reach. The company declares victory in a pamphlet. Wilmot remains up, bright, and unserved, and the pamphlet finds its best use shading eyes for viewing.
effects: schedule 5 r18_mountain_observatory_compromise The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

With Wilmot blazing free over its half built pavilion, Celestial Data Exclusives unveils a generous new model: the basic sky remains available at no cost, while premium sky, which is the same sky described more confidently, requires a subscription. The licensing officer carries the offer up the hill in a leather folder and stays to look through the big lens, claiming it is for audit purposes. The lens grinder pours him tea, which he does not know is a verdict.

choices.text: Turn down premium sky as one hill, politely

outcomes.text: The refusal is unanimous and comes with scones, which makes it difficult to report as hostility. The hill's counteroffer is the existing arrangement: the sky for everyone, forever, no tiers. The licensing officer writes hostile crowd, crosses it out, writes good scones, and looks through the lens once more before starting down. Wilmot is in his eyes the whole way off the mountain.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The refusal is unanimous and comes with scones, which makes it difficult to report as hostility. The hill's counteroffer is the existing arrangement: the sky for everyone, forever, no tiers. The licensing officer writes hostile crowd, crosses it out, writes good scones, and looks through the lens once more before starting down. Wilmot is in his eyes the whole way off the mountain.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The vote to refuse is unanimous except for a wasp that gets you during the show of hands. The refusal stands, swollen thumb and all. The lens grinder records the hill's position as unchanged and the wasp's as opportunistic, and beneath both entries, Wilmot's, which is directly overhead and improving.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Ask which tier the comet itself is on

success.text: The question goes up the company's chain and comes back down wearing a truth: Wilmot was never migrated to the premium catalog, having arrived before the paperwork. The comet of the century is, by the company's own records, basic sky. The pricing model does not survive its own catalog, and the kettle boy has the exchange framed.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

fail.text: The question is ruled hostile, and your name goes on a register of persons asking questions with intent. The premium tier limps along on paper for another month. Wilmot, a basic object by the company's own filing, keeps outperforming it nightly, free, to full houses.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Read the sky license all the way through

success.text: You read every clause, including the ones set in type meant to discourage survivors, and there it is: clause ninety, in which the licensee guarantees delivery of listed events within the premium window. You publish the finding as a pamphlet, priced at cost plus a little, and the little adds up; the hill wishes you had given it away, but buys it anyway. Somewhere in the company's offices, a lawyer reads clause ninety and requests the afternoon off.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

fail.text: You find clause ninety, and the company finds you first, offering a consultancy to keep the finding in a drawer. The drawer pays better than the truth this month. The hill notices the new coat, and the lens grinder starts pouring your tea without asking about your day, which is a demotion. Wilmot is not consulted, and shines on regardless.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mountain_observatory_reversal The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

Clause ninety detonates in the district court like a slow, well dressed firework. The exclusive license, finally read all the way through by someone with tea and patience, did not merely permit Celestial Data Exclusives to sell the comet: it obligated the company to deliver it, on the booked date, to paying subscribers. Wilmot came early, free, to everyone, so the company stands in breach of its own paywall, and every premium ticket is a refund wearing a disguise. The licensing officer arrives on the hill without his folder for the first time, and accepts tea openly.

choices.text: Enter the breach claim for every ticket holder

outcomes.text: The court finds the company in breach of the sky and orders refunds with interest, the interest running from the moment Wilmot crossed the brochure. Precedent is set: the heavens cannot be sold by anyone who cannot also make them show up. The registrar files the ruling under contracts, pauses, and files a copy under weather. The lens grinder has it read aloud on the hill twice, once for accuracy and once for pleasure.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_legacy The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The court finds the company in breach of the sky and orders refunds with interest, the interest running from the moment Wilmot crossed the brochure. Precedent is set: the heavens cannot be sold by anyone who cannot also make them show up. The registrar files the ruling under contracts, pauses, and files a copy under weather. The lens grinder has it read aloud on the hill twice, once for accuracy and once for pleasure.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_legacy The comet came early and free, out of spite

outcomes.text: The claim is thrown out on a technicality involving the width of the margins, which the company's lawyers measured personally. The refunds stall for a season and the valley grumbles on the courthouse steps. The town keeps looking up regardless, since Wilmot is still free, still early, and unlike the court, in session nightly.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_legacy The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Consult for the company on comet proof wording

outcomes.text: The company needs its next contract drafted by someone who understands exactly how it lost, and you have the misfortune of qualifying. You write force of nature clauses wide enough to excuse the company from the sky entirely, and the pay is superb and arrives in a plain envelope. On the hill, your mug migrates to the back of the shelf, behind the cracked one. Wilmot alone does not think less of you, being incapable.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_legacy The comet came early and free, out of spite

choices.text: Front the postage for a thousand refund claims

outcomes.text: You pay the postage and the valley's claims go out in one glorious sack. Refunds come back in dribbles, then in floods, each coin accompanied by a printed apology the company was ordered to include, and the apologies are read aloud at the potluck to sustained applause. Wilmot's tail is by now generally agreed to be pointing at the company's offices, a finding the lens grinder rules unscientific and lets stand.

outcomes.text: The sack of claims outweighs your ambition on the courthouse steps and takes you down with it. A clerk helps you up, stamps everything, and asks whether the comet is really visible from the bridge. You take her to the bridge. It is.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mountain_observatory_legacy The comet came early and free, out of spite

text:
MOUNTAIN OBSERVATORY

By autumn the paywall is a memory with paperwork. Celestial Data Exclusives has pivoted to manufacturing blackout curtains, sold under the slogan your sky, when you want it, and is doing modestly well among people who sleep past dawn. The observatory kettle has been mended twice and formally promoted to an instrument. On the hill, the lens grinder opens the town records to a fresh page, because a departing comet deserves the correct entry.

choices.text: Take the regional franchise for the curtains

outcomes.text: Somebody in this valley was always going to end up rich off the sky, and the last surviving way to do it is curtains. You sell darkness by the yard to the same houses the company once billed for light, and the margins are honestly beautiful. The lens grinder buys one pair, for the darkroom, and pays full price, which stings worse than a discount would have. Wilmot sets over your warehouse without comment.

choices.text: Take the logbook watch for the next forty years

outcomes.text: The lens grinder hands over the logbook the way other places hand over crowns. Your first duty is the comet's leaving entry, your second is the tea, and the order of those duties is settled law. The last line of Wilmot's page goes down in ink, because it is a certainty now: arrival early, conduct exemplary, next visit expected, admission free.

The last line of Wilmot's page goes down in ink, because it is a certainty now: arrival early, conduct exemplary, next visit expected, admission free.

You accept the logbook at the top of the ladder, and the ladder objects to the ceremony. From the ground, holding the book aloft and your ribs together, you complete the oath. The hill agrees the fall counts as your first observation from a new angle, and enters it on Wilmot's page under related phenomena.

choices.text: Enter the early arrival as a personal favor

success.text: The town records now state, in the permanent hand reserved for floods and jubilees: Comet Wilmot, booked by others, arrived early for us, admission waived, favor recorded, to be returned when possible. The registrar objects that a comet cannot receive a favor, is invited to look up, and withdraws the objection on the spot. Somewhere past the cold planets, Wilmot recedes with the town's debt in its tail, the first celestial body ever entered in a ledger as a friend.

fail.text: The registrar rejects the wording as sentimental and files the arrival under unscheduled illumination. The town simply keeps the real entry in the observatory logbook, where the registrar's jurisdiction has never once reached. Wilmot leaves as it came, early, unlicensed, and owed a favor by everyone under it, whatever the filing says.

text:
DEBT JUBILEE

Eleven at night, and the barkeep is dusting the tab wall, four generations of chalk debts nobody alive remembers drinking. A brass calculator sits on the bar beside a cheese sandwich, and it has just printed a slip demanding the sandwich post collateral. The deacon of arrears arrives at closing time with word from Saint Compound Interest: the inherited tabs come due at the next full ledger, principal plus everything since. The tavern debt circle looks at you the way people look at someone who can read.

choices.text: Copy the tab wall into the deacon's ledger, at his rates

outcomes.text: The deacon pays eight on the spot and six when you finish, and never once checks your spelling, which tells you plenty about how the totals got this way. You copy four generations of chalk into his portable ledger while the calculator watches, printing a small slip that says NOTED. The circle does not say anything. They just stop saving you a stool near the fire.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Pool every receipt and memory the circle can produce

success.text: You push three tables together and the circle empties its pockets: receipts, promissory scraps, a betrothal contract with a bar tab in the margin. By midnight you can prove at least a third of the wall was paid twice, once in coin and once in a pig. The barkeep chalks your name in the corner reserved for people the house trusts with the books. The calculator prints a slip asking whether the pig posted collateral.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

fail.text: The pooling goes well until two families discover their great-grandfathers are both recorded buying the same round, and the argument re-enacts a fistfight from ninety years ago with you in the historic middle. You take an elbow meant for a man who died before your mother was born. Still, once the swelling settles, the circle counts you as one of theirs. Inherited debts, inherited bruises.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

choices.text: Demand the original instrument be read aloud, in full

outcomes.text: The deacon unrolls the original instrument and gets four words in before discovering the founding entry is water damaged, beer damaged, and possibly bitten. He is required by his own procedures to read only what is legible, which comes to roughly a preposition and half a date. The room takes minutes. The calculator prints a slip that says INCONCLUSIVE, which from a collections instrument is nearly an apology.
effects: schedule 2 r18_debt_jubilee_assembly The tab wall remembers a round you never drank

outcomes.text: The deacon reads beautifully, from memory, in the cadence of a man who has buried this parish in installments. It turns out the instrument survives in triplicate at the shrine, certified, laminated, and cross-referenced against baptismal records. You have accomplished a dramatic reading of your neighbors' obligations. The calculator adds a line item for candle depreciation during the performance.
