choices.text: Stand between the Smudge and the blame

success.text: The inquiry convenes at the type cases with the Night Editor demanding the culprit's name, and the crew answers with a rota: every hand present claims the error, in writing, in order of seniority. You sign third. A house cannot dismiss a walkout it already has, and an error with forty authors is not an error, it is an edition. The Smudge, guilty and glowing, files type worse than ever, and she has started checking his cases the way you proof a good paragraph: expecting treasure.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_legacy Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

fail.text: You step forward before the crew can set its rota and claim the error alone, which is brave arithmetic: one name divides worse than forty. The Night Editor's questions come with a slammed case drawer, and your fingers are in its jurisdiction when it arrives. The crew hauls you out before the bar from the building does, and smuggles you a case of your favorite type as severance, which is how printers cry. Outside, the street has already chalked the new line on three walls, so the inquiry is looking for the author of a proverb.
effects: schedule 2 r18_strike_press_legacy Two papers, one press, and the ink is choosing

text:
STRIKE PRESS

Six weeks on, the press hall keeps civil hours and two mastheads. The Correction prints at dusk under its new motto, INK TAKES NO ORDERS, with a line beneath in small caps: BIASED, AND SAYS SO. The Concern prints at dawn, still neutral, still reviewing its rival weekly, still reviewed in return; the original manuscript of the editorial hangs framed by the door, labeled FICTION, FIRST EDITION. Visitors ask which paper tells the truth, and the pie woman, who now holds the refreshment concession, says both, alternately, and sells them the pair.

choices.text: Sell the framed original to a private collector

outcomes.text: A gentleman arrives with gloves and a draft on the Owners' Circle bank, seeking the manuscript of INK TAKES NO SIDES for a private gallery of steadying documents. You lift the frame off its nail at night and hang a fair copy nobody examines, since nobody reads a relic, they visit it. The original travels to a locked room where important men take turns agreeing with it, and your purse acquires gravity. The fake hangs on, beloved, doing the original's job better than the original ever did, which you privately consider a review.

choices.text: Take the empty chair at the Correction's stone

success.text: The crew offers you a permanent place at the stone, which comes with a rota, a share of the kitty, and the obligation to argue about layout forever. You sign the articles under the motto, and the backwards compositor shakes your hand mirrorwise, which from her is a citation. Your first solo page carries the weekly review of the Concern, judged fair by everyone it wounds. At dusk the presses turn, taking, as the masthead says, no orders, and the sound is a wage in itself.

fail.text: Your first solo forme, carried in front of the whole crew, catches the stone's edge and takes your thumbnail as commission. The ceremony continues around the swearing, printers regarding a first blood forme the way sailors regard weather: expected, noted, logged. The page resets in twenty minutes and prints beautifully, your thumb throbbing under the motto like an apprentice metronome. She tells you the stone takes a toll from everyone it plans to keep, and hands you the next forme without asking.

choices.text: Accept the Concern's request for a guest editorial

outcomes.text: The Night Editor, in the flattest voice a man can survive, invites a guest editorial from the opposition, neutrality having a vacancy. You submit one sentence, ink believes whatever the hand does, set it in the house's largest type yourself, and sign it THE COMPOSING ROOM. It runs unedited, becomes the Concern's most reprinted page of the decade, and gets pinned up in press halls the trade over. The Night Editor tells the review desk it proves the Concern prints all views, and for once both papers report his statement the same way.

You submit six paragraphs where one would carry, because the target is large and your aim is proud. The Night Editor edits it neutrally, which is to say fatally, and runs the remains beside a note thanking the opposition for its contribution to balance. She reads your original aloud at the stone so the crew can hear where it was good, then spikes it on the house spike, which now hangs by the door as a trophy. You learn what he learned years ago: nothing neutralizes like an editor.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

A brass plate goes up overnight: the Office of Pure Lineage, hours nine to four, closed for purity audits. Inside, the Deputy Registrar of Fractions unveils an abacus with beads of dark and pale wood, built to count ancestry in sixteenths. Form L-1, Declaration of Lineage, is due from every household by the new moon, and the sentence describing what happens after the deadline is the only one printed in plain language. The schoolteacher who invents cousins finds you first: the refusal network needs hands that can hold a pen wrong.

choices.text: Take the office's coin as a licensed enumerator

outcomes.text: They pay in advance, which established offices never do, and this one is trying very hard to seem established. You count doors, the abacus counts you counting, and the Deputy Registrar signs your receipt with both hands, like a christening. The coin is real. The list you started is also real, and it does not stay in your handwriting.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_assembly Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Buy ink and join the refusal network

outcomes.text: You put three coins in the paper kitty and receive a pen with a bent nib, community property. By morning your household owns an ancestry with four spellings and a grandmother of purely disputed origin. The abacus receives your Form L-1 and is held upside down over it for a while, which is also how the Deputy Registrar approaches most problems.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_assembly Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The patrol on the corner finds blank forms in your coat and objects with a truncheon, briefly and by the book. You are released with a receipt for your bruise, injury class two, no follow-up. The forms were blank, which is not yet a crime, though the office has one drafted.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_assembly Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: File an objection to the form itself

outcomes.text: You cite the Statute of Legible Intent: no declaration may be compelled on a form whose questions contradict each other, and question nine asks for the maiden name of your bloodline. The office suspends the deadline pending redrafting, which is what the office calls thinking. The abacus waits with its beads at zero, its best position.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_assembly Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The clerk of objections reads your objection, stamps it OBJECTION NOTED, and files it in the drawer where noting happens instead of anything else. The deadline stands. On your way out the abacus clicks once, a single bead, as if a fraction of you had already been decided.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_assembly Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

Candlelight in the schoolhouse after hours, forty adults practicing bad handwriting. The schoolteacher who invents cousins runs the board: tonight's lesson is ancestors, how to have too many, and how to spell each one differently on every line. Down the hill the office has hired a second clerk to decipher the district, then a third clerk to decipher the second. The abacus sits idle, waiting for one certified input, like a groom stood up at the altar of arithmetic.

choices.text: Copy cousins into every household ledger

outcomes.text: You work the rows with the bent nib, granting each family a genealogy shaped like bramble. By dawn the district is one enormous cousin, related to itself in ways no fraction survives. At the office the third clerk requests a fourth, and the abacus is dusted daily but never, technically, used.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_retaliation Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The night patrol takes exception to a lit schoolhouse and clears it with regulation elbows; you catch one and keep the ledger dry under your coat. Your bruise is entered nowhere, which is the point of you. The lesson resumes at a different address, one the office's alphabet cannot pronounce.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_retaliation Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Draft one ancestry that contains all others

success.text: You compose a founding grandmother from whom, by the office's own tables, every household in the district descends, including, by marriage, the Deputy Registrar. He verifies it three times because the abacus keeps arriving at a district that is one sixteenth itself. The finding is sealed as an anomaly, and sealed things, by regulation, cannot be acted upon.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_retaliation Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

fail.text: Your founding grandmother has a flaw: you gave her a birthday, and birthdays can be checked. The office voids forty forms on the strength of one candle in a church registry and posts a notice about integrity. The notice contains a spelling error, but nobody is in a position to enjoy it.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_retaliation Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Sell the office your premium slow-drying ink

outcomes.text: The office buys eleven bottles at your invented price, because purity requires supplies, supplies require invoices, and invoices are the one lineage they can actually trace. Your ink dries slow enough to smear under a decisive thumb, though you did not exactly promise otherwise. You are paid, praised, and entered into the vendor registry, which is a registry, which you try not to think about.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_retaliation Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

Wagons arrive on a Tuesday with the audit division, six men, one velvet cushion, and on the cushion the abacus, traveling first class. Doors are chalked with fractions, and households below thirteen sixteenths receive an invitation printed in the passive voice: attendance will occur. Bags are packed quietly on the hill that night. The Deputy Registrar walks the lane behind the cushion, nodding at houses like a man being loved back.

choices.text: Demand the auditors audit their own credentials

outcomes.text: You invoke the Integrity of Instruments Act, which requires every examiner to be examined and every seal to bear a seal. Four of the six auditors turn out to be provisionally certified by a stamp that expired at the border, and provisional men cannot chalk doors. The sweep adjourns to go verify itself in triplicate, and the abacus rides home at walking pace, counting nothing.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_coalition Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The audit division produces credentials faster than you can question them, laminated, cross-stamped, notarized against doubt. Your challenge is logged as obstruction, category polite. The chalk fractions stay on the doors, and the rain, which is not affiliated with anyone, refuses to come.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_coalition Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Stop the ledger wagon on the dark mile

success.text: The wagon meets a rope, the rope meets an axle, and forty pounds of certified paper meet the river, which accepts all declarations without checking. The auditors save the cushion first, instinctively, and stand holding it in the shallows while their evidence learns to swim. Nobody is followed home. The office announces that the ledgers are recovering.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_coalition Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

fail.text: The escort was doubled overnight, and you learn this at rib height. You leave the dark mile with three cracked reasons to lie still, and the wagon leaves with its paper dry. In the morning the office adds a line item for escort hazard pay, so at least somebody profited.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_coalition Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Walk ahead of the chalk and empty the houses

outcomes.text: You move door to door one street ahead of the audit, and by the time the fractions arrive, the households below thirteen sixteenths are away visiting relatives the census insists they do not have. The auditors chalk empty rooms, and the abacus counts them beautifully: zero, zero, zero, its finest work to date.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_coalition Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The last house takes persuading and the audit takes shortcuts, and the two of you meet on the back stairs. A truncheon settles the scheduling dispute. The family is out the pantry window with their papers, which you count as a victory from the floor, one, one, one.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_coalition Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

Below the sorting room after midnight, the night clerk deals misfiled forms like cards to people who were never here. The office has issued the Integrity Order: every instrument of enumeration must itself be enumerated, every stamp registered, every seal sealed. The abacus receives an inventory tag, asset one, and a file of its own, which is empty. The schoolteacher who invents cousins reads the Order twice and starts smiling at the wall.

choices.text: Charge the office rent for dead storage

outcomes.text: You lease the office your driest cellar for their overflow ledgers, priced by the pound of paper, payable monthly, mold not warranted. The Deputy Registrar signs gratefully, relieved that someone finally understands the burden of custody. The ledgers sleep under your floorboards, technically in your possession, an arrangement nobody upstairs has thought all the way through.
effects: schedule 4 r18_border_census_crisis Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Bring the night clerk in from the cold

success.text: Over the safe room's one candle the night clerk confesses a decade of devoted misfiling: forms received, stamped, and posted into a drawer that opens on both sides. You give the drawer a rota and the clerk a hot meal, the first thing the office never invoiced. From tonight the registry loses weight on a schedule.
effects: schedule 4 r18_border_census_crisis Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

fail.text: The night clerk is followed, not well, but enough, and the safe room spends an hour pretending to be a cellar full of turnips while boots go by overhead. Somebody's elbow finds your eye in the dark and everyone apologizes to the turnips. The clerk gets out clean. The candle does not.
effects: schedule 4 r18_border_census_crisis Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Help the office word its Integrity Order

outcomes.text: You volunteer for the drafting committee, where your one amendment passes on a voice vote because it sounds strict: no instrument may certify what it cannot itself produce. The Deputy Registrar reads it aloud twice, moved by its purity. The abacus's empty file sits in the cabinet, waiting for that sentence the way a lock waits for a key.
effects: schedule 4 r18_border_census_crisis Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: Your amendment reads as strict to everyone except the office lawyer, who trims one clause for elegance and accidentally leaves the abacus exempt. Instruments of founding grade, it now says, are presumed pure. Presumed is a load-bearing word, and you will have to come back for it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_border_census_crisis Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

Quota week opens with a rehearsal: the office practices its roundup using empty carts, to get the turning radius right. Ration books are impounded for cross-checking against Form L-1, which means dinner is now a document. On the hill the lamps burn late and nobody sings. The abacus works overtime, beads clacking past midnight, and even the clerks have started flinching at the sound, the way animals flinch at weather before it happens.

choices.text: Run a pantry the paperwork cannot see

success.text: Bread moves by rota through back gardens, unindexed, unweighed, warm. Households whose ration books are in custody eat exactly as well as households in good standing, which ruins the office's incentive structure and several of its charts. The abacus is not consulted, since bread resists sixteenths, being divisible only by hunger.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_compromise Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

fail.text: A crate slips on a garden wall in the dark and you break its fall with the arm you like. The bread survives. Deliveries continue on schedule, minus one carrier, plus one sling, and the rota adjusts around you the way water does, without a meeting.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_compromise Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Sue to unimpound the ration books

outcomes.text: You argue that a ration book is a contract for bread, and that the office, in seizing them, has assumed the baker's obligations, including delivery. The court agrees with visible pleasure. The office spends quota week delivering loaves to households it had intended to cart away, and the abacus is briefly repurposed to count flour, its happiest assignment on record.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_compromise Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The court finds for the office on procedural grounds: hunger, lacking a filing number, has no standing. You are billed for the office's costs, itemized, including cushion transport. Outside the courtroom the empty carts practice another turn.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_compromise Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Sell the office candles for the night shift

outcomes.text: Quota week runs on light, and you have light, at a price that would embarrass a lighthouse. The office pays without blinking, since the alternative is counting in the dark, and purity is nothing without visibility. Your candles burn while the beads clack, and you tell yourself the wax is neutral, which the wax, being wax, neither confirms nor denies.
effects: schedule 5 r18_border_census_compromise Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

A gentler poster appears, pastel, with a dove: the Amnesty Window, register voluntarily this fortnight and receive a stamped exemption from consequences to be specified later. The Deputy Registrar calls it mercy and appears to mean it, which is worse. In the asset cabinet the abacus's file now has one line filled in, condition excellent, and one line blank, provenance. The night clerk taps the blank line twice and looks at you.

choices.text: Read the exemption aloud at the window, slowly

success.text: You stand in the queue and recite the fine print at speaking pace, which nobody had heard before, since it was set in type the size of doubt. Consequences to be specified later empties the line faster than rain. The office closes the window early, citing weather, and the poster's dove is retired to the drawer of discontinued mercies.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_reversal Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

fail.text: An office reader is deployed against you, trained to recite the same words warmly, and warmth wins the queue back by suppertime. Eleven households register at the window. You memorize eleven names, not for the office's reasons, and walk home the long way, slowly.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_reversal Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Take the office's fee to appraise their abacus

success.text: You accept the valuation contract, since somebody will, and spend an afternoon alone with asset one. The beads are eleven different woods, the frame is a twelfth, the carver signed nothing, and the grain runs in directions that argue. You write value considerable, origin undocumented, and keep a copy of that phrase for personal use. The network hears you took office coin and says little, loudly.
effects: schedule 2 r18_border_census_reversal Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Walk the queue and talk people home

outcomes.text: You work the amnesty line from the back, one household at a time, carrying the schoolteacher's arithmetic: an exemption from a consequence is a receipt for the consequence. The queue thins from behind, which the office cannot see from the window. That evening the rota adds four new houses and one very good soup.

outcomes.text: The queue marshal decides your conversation is loitering and makes his case with a boot. You lose a tooth's worth of dignity and keep the argument. Three households leave the line anyway, stepping around you politely, which you choose to count as carried.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

Form L-0 arrives at the office through its own post: request for enumeration of the instrument of enumeration, citing the Integrity Order, first amendment, no instrument may certify what it cannot itself produce. The schoolteacher who invents cousins wears her good coat to the hearing. The Deputy Registrar of Fractions sets the abacus on the bench with both hands and asks it, formally and aloud, for sixteen sixteenths of itself. The room goes very quiet, and the beads do not move.

choices.text: Sell seats and transcripts to the hearing

outcomes.text: You paper the district with tickets to the enumeration of asset one, front rows priced beyond reason and sold out by noon. The office objects to commerce at a solemnity, then requests six complimentary seats. Whatever happens on that bench, you have already turned a profit on it, which makes yours the only counting in the building today that will succeed.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_legacy Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Fill the gallery with the cousins you invented

outcomes.text: The benches load with bramble genealogy, forty households of mutually assured relation, and every time the Registrar seeks a witness to the abacus's origin, a cousin volunteers a different forest. The instrument cannot produce its fractions; the gallery can produce infinite ones. The registry fails closed at fourteen minutes past two, and the room does not cheer. It exhales.

outcomes.text: Marshals thin the gallery at the door, and your shoulder learns the doorframe. You watch the hearing through the gap, standing all afternoon, and it is enough. When the beads hang silent you are the first one outside with the news, walking fast on a numb arm, and the hill has its lamps lit before the office finds its own stairs.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_legacy Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

choices.text: Move that an uncertified count voids backward

outcomes.text: You rise on a point of consequence: if the instrument is unproduced, its every prior count is unfounded, and the registry is a building full of confident blank paper. The office lawyer checks the amendment for an exit and finds your handwriting all over the absence of one. Fail closed is entered into the record in the passive voice, the office's native tense, and this time it happens to them.
effects: schedule 3 r18_border_census_legacy Somewhere a form is being misfiled on your behalf.

outcomes.text: The bench grants your motion in principle and refers it in practice to a committee of the office, which is like referring the fox to the henhouse's suggestion box. Still, the abacus stands unproduced, and unproduced it stays. The registry survives the afternoon on paper only, and paper, you have learned, is where things go to be lost.

text:
BORDER CENSUS

Frost on the brass plate the morning they unscrew it, four turns a corner, no ceremony. The registry is sealed in its own vault pending certification by a certified instrument, of which, by its own order, there can now never be one. The ledgers wait in the dark, forever received, never read. The Deputy Registrar of Fractions files a final requisition, one abacus, sentimental value, denied, and goes home to whatever a man is without a denominator.

choices.text: Carry the abacus up to the schoolhouse

outcomes.text: It takes two of you, one for the frame and one for the beads' feelings. The schoolteacher hangs it low on the wall where children can reach, and by afternoon it is counting apples, wrens, and days until spring, whole numbers only, each belonging to whoever is counting. Asset one has retired into arithmetic. The rota that fed a district keeps meeting anyway, with your name at the top of the soup ledger, the one list this year you are glad to be on.

It takes two of you, one for the frame and one for the beads' feelings. The schoolteacher hangs it low on the wall where children can reach, and by afternoon it is counting apples, wrens, and days until spring, whole numbers only, each belonging to whoever is counting. Asset one has retired into arithmetic. The rota that fed a district keeps meeting anyway, with your name at the top of the soup ledger, the one list this year you are glad to be on.

The frame is oak and the hill is ice, and you keep the beads dry at some cost to your tailbone. The children have it counting apples by afternoon regardless. Every bead slides easy as gossip now; whatever the abacus could never do for the office, it does all day for anyone, and your bruise is, on balance, the cheapest line item of the whole census.

choices.text: Write the registry's obituary in office style

success.text: You compose the closing notice in flawless passive voice: errors occurred, instruments went unproduced, closure has been achieved. The regional authority accepts it verbatim, relieved to be told what happened by someone who was there. Filed beside it, at your insistence, is Form L-0, blank copies available free at the schoolhouse, in case any office anywhere ever gets ideas about sixteenths again.

fail.text: Your obituary is judged too warm for the file, and a functionary rewrites it into fog. The vault is sealed either way. Years from now an archivist will find your first draft folded behind the fog, read the line about the beads refusing, and understand the whole thing in one sitting, which is more than the office ever managed.

choices.text: Auction the office furniture to the district

outcomes.text: The velvet cushion goes for a week's wages to a woman who wants it for her dog, and the stamp collection starts a bidding war between two printers with histories. You take a commission off the top of every lot, cash, no provenance asked, in honor of the deceased. The vault keeps the ledgers and you keep the proceeds, and of the two of you, you are the one who can spend them.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

Nine in the morning at the demobilization office, and the queue is four hundred professionals long. The treaty was signed at dawn; by breakfast, EverWar Solutions had issued a statement calling peace an aggressive market condition. At the counter, a sword lies on the form tray, filing for hostile unemployment. The benefits clerk reads its claim twice and does not laugh, which the sword appreciates.

choices.text: Keep the queue fed and standing until every form is in

success.text: You fetch water, hold places, and teach the siege captain that a queue is a formation that wins by staying put. By dusk everyone has a number, including the sword, which receives a case file and its first ever piece of paper that is not an invoice. The clerk stamps it HOSTILE UNEMPLOYMENT, PENDING, and the whole line watches like it is a coronation. Somebody has to see this through. Apparently you.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mercenary_peace_assembly A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

fail.text: A scuffle starts over queue positions; old habits, professional footwork. You catch an elbow that was aimed at nobody in particular and everybody specifically. The line reforms, ashamed of itself, and the sword's claim goes in anyway, stamped PENDING with a note about the applicant's temperament. Your eye will be several colors by morning.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mercenary_peace_assembly A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: File the demobilization forms exactly as the System intends

outcomes.text: You discover the forms were written for farmers who fight, not fighters who might farm, and you annotate accordingly. The clerk accepts your corrections with the gratitude of a person who did not write the form and has apologized for it daily. The sword's claim is docketed as precedent pending: no weapon has ever filed before, but no rule says one cannot. EverWar's lawyer, watching from the door, writes something down and leaves.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mercenary_peace_assembly A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

Four hundred claims stall on a technicality with a marketing budget. The sword's file is stamped INCOMPLETE, which it takes personally, insofar as a sword takes things.

choices.text: Take EverWar's re-enlistment bonus and sign whatever it says

The clerk files the addendum. It is technically evidence.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

The drill hall has been rebooked as a retraining fair, and the chalkboard is teaching translation: siegecraft is logistics, sapping is demolition with permits, sentry duty is security, campaign mapping is cartography, and feeding a company is catering. The sapper copies the word permits three times like it might detonate. In the corner, the benefits clerk has set up a folding table for claims, and the sword lies on it with its case number tied to the hilt, waiting to be seen.

choices.text: Translate every war skill into a trade on one chalkboard

outcomes.text: You write it plainly: two hundred dinners a night is a supply line, and the siege captain has run longer ones under fire. She reads the board, sits down, and quietly writes a menu for the first time in thirty years of feeding people she was also commanding. Three kitchens, a survey office, and a quarry sign people on the spot. The sword's case number gets read aloud with the rest of the placements, still PENDING, and nobody laughs at it twice.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_retaliation A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

outcomes.text: Your chalkboard is a success right up until EverWar's account manager photographs it for a brochure titled Transferable Lethality: Invest in Readiness. The fair's mood curdles; half the hall suspects the other half of being the brochure. Placements happen anyway, but the clerk covers the sword's case number with her hand when the camera passes, on instinct.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_retaliation A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

Somewhere in a records office a filing drawer shuts a little harder than necessary. The sword, listed at number four hundred one, is the only applicant EverWar declines to poach, on the grounds that it has developed opinions.

choices.text: Pair every old soldier with a trade that fits their hands

success.text: You do it one pair of hands at a time: the sapper to the quarry, where things are meant to come down, and the siege captain to the wake-house kitchen, where two hundred mourners eat on schedule. It is slower than a chalkboard and it holds better, the way a knot holds better than a nail. The clerk pins the sword's claim to the top of her stack, because precedent should watch how this is done.

The sword's file gains a footnote: NOT THE WORST TEMPERAMENT ON RECORD.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

EverWar Solutions files a formal objection to the sword's claim, arguing the applicant left service voluntarily by declining to be swung. Posters go up overnight: PEACE IS A SUPPLY SHORTAGE, with a tasteful woodcut of an empty scabbard. The benefits clerk is served with a subpoena and a fruit basket, in that order. The siege captain reads the objection twice and says it is the best drafted thing EverWar has ever aimed at anyone.

choices.text: Audit EverWar's readiness invoices line by line

success.text: You find it in the third ledger: EverWar bills the treaty office a retainer for standing ready to resume hostilities, which means peace is their best contract and they are lobbying against their own invoice. You sell the observation to a broadsheet for four coins, and it runs under the headline WAR FIRM PAID TO WAIT. The peace people wish you had brought it to them first, and say so. But the damage to EverWar is public and load bearing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_coalition A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

fail.text: EverWar's account manager finds you in the ledger room, mid-audit, and names a consulting fee for closing the book gently: eight coins, and a card in case you find any other books. The posters double by morning, and the clerk's second fruit basket arrives with a note that says only STILL READY. You tell yourself somebody braver would have found the ledger eventually. The ledger had been waiting years for you specifically.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_coalition A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: Walk the subpoenaed clerk to the hearing and pack the gallery

outcomes.text: Forty demobilized professionals attend in pressed civilian clothes, sitting in perfect ranks, doing nothing, which turns out to be the single most unnerving thing forty mercenaries can do. The clerk testifies that the sword's claim is complete, correct, and unswung. EverWar's advocate objects to the gallery's posture and is overruled. The fruit baskets stop.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_coalition A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

outcomes.text: The gallery holds until an EverWar bailiff decides your row is standing too calmly and clears it with a staff. The hearing is adjourned to a smaller room with fewer witnesses, which is how EverWar prefers its rooms.

choices.text: File a counterclaim: the war ended, the contract did not

success.text: Your counterclaim is short and it lands: EverWar cannot argue the sword resigned from a war EverWar insists is ongoing, and cannot bill for readiness against a peace it calls fictional. Pick one, the tribunal says, in the polite tense reserved for people about to lose money. EverWar requests a recess to consult its own invoices. The sword's file is stamped OBJECTION HELD IN ABEYANCE, its best stamp yet.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

Two hundred covers a night at the wake-house kitchen, and the siege captain runs it like a campaign that finally deserves her: bread at dawn, soup by noon, grief fed on schedule. Half her brigade is former payroll of EverWar Solutions and the other half was once their target list, and the rota does not record which. The sword's hearing is set for Thursday. It has requested, through the clerk, something to wear.

choices.text: Put your own coin in the kitty and take the worst shift

outcomes.text: Three coins into the kitty and you take the pot-wash watch, which in any kitchen is the trench. Beside you, the sapper scrubs cauldrons and hums something she used to hum before setting charges, slower now, in a better key. By Thursday the kitchen has fed a wake for both sides of the same old battle at adjoining tables. Nobody plans this. The seating plan does.

choices.text: Cater the sword's hearing and invite both sides to eat

EverWar's advocate accepts a bowl before recognizing the strategy, and is visibly diminished by having enjoyed it. The hearing runs eleven minutes and is taken under advisement, which is tribunal for a full stomach and an open mind. The sword attends in plain oiled leather, which the clerk lists as business dress.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

Word arrives of a border incident: two shepherds, one fence, and an EverWar observer who filed the report before the shouting finished. By noon the benefits office is ordered to freeze every demobilization claim pending a Review of Hostilities, and the clerk stands in front of her filing drawers like a garrison of one. The sword's file is at the top of the frozen stack. EverWar's account manager is already at the treaty office, offering to handle the emergency at last year's rates.

choices.text: Demand the incident report before the review can breathe

success.text: You get the report unsealed, and it is a masterpiece of prewriting: the observer logged the incident forty minutes before the fence broke, in ink that had time to dry. The Review of Hostilities dies of embarrassment in a single afternoon. The freeze lifts. The clerk reopens her drawers one at a time, gently, the way you wake someone you like.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mercenary_peace_compromise A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

fail.text: The report stays sealed on grounds of commercial confidence, a phrase doing more work than any soldier ever did. The freeze holds for the season. In the frozen stack, four hundred claims wait, and the clerk takes to reading the case numbers aloud at closing time so they stay real. The sword's number is last, and she says it like a name.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mercenary_peace_compromise A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: Bill the emergency yourself: hire out as a border assessor

outcomes.text: You reach the border first and assess it: one fence, mendable, two shepherds, mortified. EverWar pays you fourteen coins for the assessment and files it under corroboration, having removed the word mendable. Your name is now in a report that keeps a freeze on four hundred claims. The coins spend fine. Most coins do.

choices.text: Mend the fence with both shepherds before anyone else arrives

outcomes.text: You and the sapper mend the fence in an afternoon, and the shepherds sign a joint statement drafted on the back of a feed invoice: no hostilities, one argument, resolved by lunch. It travels faster than EverWar's report because it is shorter and has a joke in it. The Review of Hostilities is left reviewing a fence that is visibly fine. The freeze thaws from the bottom of the stack up, and the clerk starts with the oldest claims, as is right.

outcomes.text: The fence fights back; wire, tension, and forty years of rust have their own foreign policy. You bleed on the treaty's behalf and the mend holds, but an EverWar observer sketches you from the ridge, and the broadsheet version has you dismantling a border defense. The shepherds' statement calls this manure, in the professional vocabulary of shepherds. Unrest anyway. Ink beats wire, some seasons.
effects: schedule 2 r18_mercenary_peace_compromise A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

Thursday's hearing is postponed again, and attention turns to the weapons depots, which EverWar Solutions now proposes to manage in trust for future demand. The demobilized professionals have a different proposal involving crowbars and an inventory list. The clerk has begun accepting claims from the depot in advance: three halberds, a ballista, and a drum of quarrel bolts have all expressed interest in the sword's precedent. The armory doors stand open like a question.

choices.text: Strip the depot to plowshares before the trust papers land

success.text: One long night of professional demolition, permitless, old school: blades to the smithy, powder to the quarry, siege parts to a bridge that needed them. By dawn the depot inventory reads like a hardware catalogue and EverWar's trust has nothing to hold in trust. The peace people are furious at the method and quietly relieved at the result, and will not say the second part aloud. The sapper keeps one fuse coil, for sentimental reasons she declines to have.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

fail.text: The night goes wrong at the powder store: a floor gives, a stack shifts, and you are dug out by the sapper with three ribs' worth of regret. Worse, the accident is exactly the pretext EverWar's trust needed, and armed custodians arrive within the week to protect the inventory from the people it used to belong to. The clerk logs your injury as industrial, which is the truest thing anyone writes all month.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: Convert the depot to winter stores, shelf by shelf

outcomes.text: Rack by rack, the depot learns a second trade: grain where the pikes stood, seed in the quarrel drums, root vegetables in a vault built to survive a siege and therefore ideal. The halberds are shelved, pending, beside their claim forms. EverWar's surveyor arrives to find four hundred people stocking a granary and no crime to photograph. The wake-house kitchen calls it their long pantry.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

outcomes.text: Rack by rack, the depot learns a second trade: grain where the pikes stood, seed in the quarrel drums, root vegetables in a vault built to survive a siege and therefore ideal. The halberds are shelved, pending, beside their claim forms. EverWar's surveyor arrives to find four hundred people stocking a granary and no crime to photograph. The wake-house kitchen calls it their long pantry.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

outcomes.text: The conversion is going well until an EverWar custodian decides a sack of barley is contraband under the trust, and the argument becomes a shove, and the shove becomes your bad shoulder meeting a grain shelf. The barley stays; you count that as the campaign's objective, held. The clerk adds a barley sack to the claims ledger as a witness. Nobody stops her anymore.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: Offer EverWar the depot's rats at market valuation

outcomes.text: Your counterproposal values the depot as EverWar's own filings do: depreciated stock, hazardous, infested, a liability generously transferable for one coin. Their surveyor confirms every word, since the alternative is admitting their valuations lie. EverWar declines to hold a certified liability in trust; their withdrawal letter is one sentence long. The clerk frames it above the folding table, next to the sword's case number.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

outcomes.text: EverWar accepts the valuation, buys the liability for a coin, and announces a restoration levy on the region to make the depot safe, payable to EverWar. You have been outplayed at your own paperwork, an experience the siege captain says builds character in the specific way that losing does. The halberds' claims are transferred to new management. New management objects to their temperament.
effects: schedule 3 r18_mercenary_peace_reversal A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

The approval arrives on a Tuesday, unannounced, the way real verdicts do: HOSTILE UNEMPLOYMENT, GRANTED, the sword's case number at the top and a seal at the bottom. Then the second page: the ruling defines an eligible claimant as any instrument whose employment was hostilities, and the clerk reads it twice, sits down, and laughs once, briefly, like a hinge. Every weapon in the armory is now eligible. EverWar Solutions files an emergency appeal against the concept.

choices.text: Help the clerk process the armory's claims before the appeal

success.text: Four hundred professionals who once carried the applicants now file for them, and it goes quick, because everyone already knows the make, age, and service history of the thing that kept them alive. The sapper vouches for a mattock. The siege captain signs for a trebuchet arm she once cursed through a whole winter. By the appeal date the precedent is not a case but a census, and nobody appeals a census and wins.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_legacy A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

fail.text: The filing sprint runs all night, and at some point a pallet of pending pole-arms comes down on your foot, which the clerk logs, with apologies, as a workplace incident involving claimants. You limp; the stack does not. By morning the drawer marked GRANTED outweighs the drawer marked PENDING for the first time in the office's history. The clerk oils that drawer's runners. It sees more use now.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_legacy A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: Argue the appeal: readiness billed is employment owed

outcomes.text: Your argument is one page: EverWar billed the treaty for keeping instruments ready, therefore the instruments were employed, therefore their unemployment is real, and the invoice is the proof. EverWar must abandon either the appeal or the retainer, and their advocate stands silent long enough that the tribunal fines them for wasting its morning. The retainer dies. The appeal dies with it. The clerk stamps twelve claims GRANTED before lunch, to celebrate.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_legacy A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

outcomes.text: The appeal court grants EverWar a stay, on the reasoning that the definition of employment is too important to settle while money depends on it. The clerk keeps processing anyway, marking each file GRANTED, STAYED, which she pronounces as one word, like a breed of dog. The sword's approval survives, grandfathered. The armory waits, eligible and furious.
effects: schedule 4 r18_mercenary_peace_legacy A sword files for hostile unemployment. Claim pending.

choices.text: Broker weapon claims for a cut, ten percent of eligibility

outcomes.text: You set up a folding table beside the clerk's and offer expedited filing at a broker's rate, and the desperate pay it, because desperate is what a rate is for. Fourteen coins by dusk. The clerk moves her table away from yours without a word, which from her is a verdict. The sword, GRANTED, exercises its new standing by declining to be represented.

text:
MERCENARY PEACE

By autumn the armory sign has been repainted: REGIONAL PLACEMENT CENTER, EDGED AND HAFTED TRADES, WALK-INS WELCOME. Inside, the clerk matches instruments to work: pikes to hop poles, the ballista to a bridge crew, halberds to a ceremonial rota they pretend to resent. EverWar Solutions has restructured into a risk consultancy that issues quarterly warnings about the instability of peace, which nobody buys but everybody reads aloud at the kitchen for the laugh. On the rolls, one line is famous: SWORD, EMPLOYED, BREAD.

choices.text: Read EverWar's instability forecast at the harvest supper

outcomes.text: The siege captain reads the forecast between courses in her briefing voice, and two hundred people hear the phrase sustained outbreaks of calm delivered deadpan over soup. Laughter is a poor weapon and an excellent solvent; by the last course the forecast has been folded into a hat for the smallest child present. EverWar's regional office closes in the spring, citing market conditions. The market conditions are that everyone eats together now.

Laughter is a poor weapon and an excellent solvent; by the last course the forecast has been folded into a hat for the smallest child present. EverWar's regional office closes in the spring, citing market conditions. The market conditions are that everyone eats together now.

The reading goes fine until an EverWar stringer in the audience reports the supper as a gathering of demobilized combatants mocking regional security, and the broadsheet prints it straight. A magistrate attends the next supper in an official capacity, eats two helpings, and files nothing, but the shadow of the report stays on the season. The kitchen adds the stringer to the pot-wash rota anyway. Nobody stays a stringer through a full pot-wash shift.

choices.text: License the famous sword's story to EverWar's rebranding

outcomes.text: EverWar pays fourteen coins for the rights to the sword's story, and their new brochure, Beating Swords Into Market Share, uses its silhouette on the cover. The sword does not read brochures; it slices bread for two hundred mourners a night with professional accuracy and no comment. But the clerk sees the brochure, and the line she draws through your name on the kitchen rota is thin, straight, and permanent.

But the clerk sees the brochure, and the line she draws through your name on the kitchen rota is thin, straight, and permanent.

choices.text: Take the empty station beside the sword at the bread bench

success.text: You learn the bread watch: dawn loaves, wake orders, two hundred covers and rising, and the sword beside you keeping perfect slices with the patience of a professional who has retrained once and does not intend to again. The clerk visits on inspection days and stays past inspection, on account of the soup. On the rolls you are both listed the same way now, EMPLOYED, and the sword's file carries a final stamp: PRECEDENT, WORKING. Peace has survived its investors. The bread has to be in by six.

fail.text: Your first week at the bread bench costs you a fingertip's worth of skin, because the sword sets a pace and honors no learning curve. The siege captain binds the cut and moves you to soup, where ambition does less damage. You come back to the bench anyway. The sword, they say around the kitchen, works well with the persistent, and by the rolls' next revision you are its listed apprentice, which no form supports and no clerk objects to.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

At nine sharp the Under-Herald of Vacancies unlocks the throne room and bows to an empty chair. The crown sits on its cushion beside a sign reading POSITION AVAILABLE, ENQUIRE WITHIN. The Crown Continuity Fund has sent a junior actuary with projections showing that an unoccupied throne depresses adjacent asset values by four percent. The crown, per its bearer Osbert, has agreed to take applications, and it has a rubric.

choices.text: Insist the vacancy be posted per charter

outcomes.text: You find the relevant clause: royal vacancies must be cried by herald in every square, including the squares the palace does not like. The Under-Herald performs all forty proclamations with full posture, and by noon the whole city knows the throne is hiring. The actuary objects that public postings attract the public. The crown warms slightly on its cushion, which Osbert records as enthusiasm.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_assembly A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You cite the charter from memory and get the clause number wrong, which in this palace is a form of blasphemy. The Under-Herald corrects you at proclamation volume in front of the actuary. The vacancy is posted anyway, but under the heading MISCELLANEOUS, between a lost glove and a found glove. The crown declines to comment through two layers of velvet.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_assembly A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Sell the Fund a list of pliable candidates

outcomes.text: The actuary needs a local consultant to identify candidates with, as the projections put it, favorable head volume and low opinion count. You provide the six most obedient people you know and invoice for eight coins. The Fund pays a retention bonus of six more without being asked, which is how you learn to worry. Around the squares, people notice the throne is being shopped before it was posted, and they mention it with their arms crossed.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_assembly A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Chip in for the picnic hamper at the gates

outcomes.text: The commoners' constitutional picnic convenes on blankets outside the palace gates, and your three coins buy the good mustard. Between sandwiches, the hamper warden reads the vacancy notice aloud, and the picnic votes by raised crusts to send the crown a fruit basket and no candidate. Osbert carries the basket in with ceremony. The crown, he reports, considers it the strongest application received so far, precisely because it is not one.

outcomes.text: You arrive with coins but the kitty is closed, so you take the heavy end of the trestle instead and set it down on your own foot. The picnic patches you with a napkin and a slice of cake, and the hamper warden declares you provisionally family. The gate guards order the blankets moved along and are handed plates, which they hold uncertainly. From its cushion the crown observes everything, and Osbert notes it has begun tilting toward the picnic, which in a crown is either interest or a loose cushion.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_assembly A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

The first interview panel convenes at the palace gates: one trestle table, three chairs, and the crown on its cushion at the head. Osbert has prepared a tarnish chart for scoring. Nineteen dukes queue in full regalia beside the picnic, which is also queuing, but for lemonade. The rubric has four criteria, and the fourth is sealed.

choices.text: Sell places at the front of the queue

outcomes.text: Dukes will pay anything to be humiliated earlier. You move a marquess up eleven places for eight coins and a viscount above him for six, and the queue becomes an auction with heraldry. Two dukes hire personal halberdiers to hold their positions overnight, and the actuary writes queue liquidity in his ledger with approval. The crown interviews them in your order and rejects them in its own, which takes considerably less time.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_retaliation A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Feed the queue from the picnic hamper

outcomes.text: The picnic decides that even a duke goes to pieces on an empty stomach, and you carry sandwiches down the line. Fed candidates stop scheming and start chatting, and by afternoon the queue has a shade rota and a lemonade kitty of its own. Osbert reports that the crown scored the sandwich distribution against the sealed criterion, and that this is the highest mark awarded in four centuries. Nobody is told what the criterion is.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_retaliation A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You take the heavy end of the tea urn and a duke's ceremonial elbow takes you in the eye. The picnic sits you down and applies cake. The queue absorbs the lesson that whoever holds the food holds the initiative, which agitates several earls into leaving loudly. The crown, per Osbert, marks the entire afternoon as evidence.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_retaliation A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Ask the crown who wrote its rubric

success.text: The crown answers through Osbert: it did, one criterion per century, each one after a king it regrets. Criterion one followed the tall one, two the loud one, three the one with the fleet. You ask what the sealed fourth followed, and Osbert goes pale and says, all of them. The queue thins by a third on the spot, which the Under-Herald records as the rubric working as designed.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_retaliation A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

fail.text: You ask the question with your hands in your pockets, and the panel takes posture very seriously. The Under-Herald rules the enquiry ambitious and enters your name on the candidate list as a precaution. You explain that you do not want the throne, which is when Osbert looks up, because per the rubric that is the strongest statement anyone has made all day. You are now shortlisted against your will.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_retaliation A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

Overnight the Fund unveils its candidate: a professional monarch with laminated references, three previous kingdoms, all of them, he says, satisfied. He arrives at the gates in a rented procession with a coronation tent already assembled. The Under-Herald objects that interviews are not finished, and the actuary produces a schedule showing that markets prefer certainty by Thursday. The crown asks, through Osbert, for the references.

choices.text: Check his references over sandwiches

outcomes.text: The picnic writes to all three previous kingdoms through the hamper warden's cousins, who are faster than the post and considerably more thorough. Two kingdoms turn out to be registered to the Fund at the same address as its stationery. The third replies with a single sentence: please do not send him back. The replies are read aloud between courses, and the crown tarnishes so hard that Osbert has to fetch a second cloth.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_coalition A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You bicycle to the nearest of the three kingdoms to check the reference in person, and you return with a signed statement and a dog bite. The statement says the candidate reigned for one month and licensed the weather. The picnic patches your leg while the gates fill with people asking about weather licensing, loudly. It is not a calm afternoon, but it is an informative one.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_coalition A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Demand every criterion be applied to him

outcomes.text: The charter is plain: every candidate faces every criterion, and none may be skipped for scheduling reasons, market related or otherwise. The crown interviews him for six minutes. Osbert reads the finding: criteria one through three passed with distinction, criterion four failed at the door, because the candidate wanted the job so badly he had brought his own anthem. The tent is refunded, and the picnic applauds with sandwiches raised.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_coalition A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You demand full process, and the Under-Herald agrees so completely that he schedules three days of preliminary regalia inspection first. The city spends the delay believing the Fund's man has already won, and his portrait appears on mugs. The crown notes for the record, through Osbert, that mugs are not a criterion. The mood at the gates goes sour anyway.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_coalition A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Block the coronation tent at the gates

success.text: You plant yourself in the tent's doorway while the picnic spreads blankets across the entrance behind you, which by picnic custom makes it a dining room, and nobody crowns anyone in a dining room. The rented procession refuses to trample lunch and files back down the hill. The professional monarch calls this an outrage and is offered coleslaw. The picnic minutes record the win under improvisation rather than method, which on a blanket is a demotion, but the crown watches from its cushion with what Osbert scores as its first recorded expression.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_coalition A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

fail.text: You go through the tent flap and meet a ceremonial halberd that turns out to have a working end. The picnic drags you out by the ankles and applies cake to the wrong injury first. The tent stands one more day, guarded now, which makes the palace look like it is being besieged by its own coronation. Even the actuary concedes the optics are poor, and he says it in yield.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_coalition A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

On the fourth Sunday the crown attends the picnic, officially for site assessment, unofficially because it asked. Osbert carries it down at the head of a procession of one. The picnic makes room on the constitution blanket between the hamper and the mustard, and the Under-Herald follows with a folding chair he does not use, out of respect. The actuary attends as well, with a clipboard, to determine whether lunch is a competing product.

choices.text: Seat the crown per the picnic seating plan

outcomes.text: The seating plan puts the crown below the salt but beside the hamper warden, which is the position of honor a picnic actually has. From there it watches the picnic settle a boundary dispute, fund a roof repair from the kitty, and adjourn a feud, all before pudding, on the budget of one hamper. Osbert's notes for the afternoon run to nine pages. The last line, he admits, is in the crown's own tarnish, and it reads: better rubric.
effects: schedule 2 r18_royal_succession_crisis A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You consult the seating plan upside down and place the crown at the head of the blanket, which a picnic does not have. The picnic goes formal, elbows in, nobody laughing, and the sandwiches proceed in silence like a state funeral for bread. The hamper warden corrects the placement after one course, but the damage is filed. Osbert reports the crown spent the meal mortified, which he scores as promising, kings never were.
effects: schedule 2 r18_royal_succession_crisis A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Sell commemorative programmes of the visit

outcomes.text: You letterpress a souvenir programme, CROWN AT PICNIC, ONE DAY ONLY, and sell out two printings at four coins each. The actuary buys the plates from you for six more and reissues them with the Fund's seal on the cover, which is how history acquires a shareholder. The picnic is too polite to say anything and says it loudly. The crown appears on page two, miscaptioned as ceremonial produce.
effects: schedule 2 r18_royal_succession_crisis A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Teach the palace staff the picnic rota

success.text: Half the palace staff attend on their day off, aprons still on, and you walk them through the rota: who carries, who pours, who watches the weather, how a hamper decides. The under-cooks grasp it before the footmen do, because kitchens already work this way, and by evening the palace pantry has a kitty of its own. Osbert signs the rota last, on behalf of the cushion, and after a pause, the crown.
effects: schedule 2 r18_royal_succession_crisis A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

fail.text: The head footman takes the rota as a chain of command and appoints himself to the top of it, and you spend an hour un-explaining. In the scuffle over the tea urn you take a splash of it, which is how you learn palace tea is served at declaration temperature. The under-cooks apologize with burn salve and a pastry. The rota survives, but the footman is now telling the gates he runs the picnic, which the gates find hilarious and repeat.
effects: schedule 2 r18_royal_succession_crisis A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

The Interim Diadem arrives on Thursday in a case with its own footman: a lightweight sovereignty product in brushed alloy, described by its prospectus as offering superior yield and no opinions. The actuary announces that the old crown will be retired to a vault where its value can mature in the dark, and Osbert with it, on a pension of thanks. Then the Under-Herald, gray to the collar, finds the flaw and reads it aloud: the vacancy notice covers all instruments of sovereignty, which makes the Diadem a candidate. Candidates must be interviewed. The interviewer of record is the crown.

choices.text: Hire on as the Diadem's cushion bearer

outcomes.text: The Diadem requires a bearer for its interview, and the Fund pays eight coins for local hands and six more for local silence. You carry the challenger in behind Osbert, who does not look at you, and the two cushions pass in the corridor like duelists. The Diadem weighs nothing, which its brochure lists as an advantage and everyone else identifies as the problem. Outside the gates, word spreads of who carried it, and your name is said with a fork pointed at it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_compromise A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Stand with Osbert against his retirement

success.text: You gather the picnic, the under-cooks, and the gate guards, who by now hold plates comfortably, and everyone attends the retirement hearing as interested parties, with sandwiches for the room. The actuary looks up from his projections into forty chewing faces and adjourns the pension of thanks indefinitely. Osbert keeps the cushion. The crown, he reports, has asked that the fruit basket people be thanked by name, and there are no names, and it finds that correct.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_compromise A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

fail.text: You rise at the hearing to speak for Osbert and the ceremonial door swings shut on your hand, because palace doors are counterweighted for dignity, not attendance. Osbert dresses your fingers with cushion braid, which he says has waited forty years to be useful. The hearing is postponed in the confusion, which is a kind of winning. Outside, the gates crowd learns the Fund tried to pension a cushion bearer, and begins asking what a Fund is for, in tones.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_compromise A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: File the Diadem's candidacy by the book

outcomes.text: You file the Diadem's application on its behalf, complete and correct, which the Fund cannot object to without conceding its product is not sovereign. The interview lasts four minutes. Osbert reads the old crown's finding: the candidate is punctual, lightweight, and hollow, criteria one and two passed, three not attempted, four failed by prospectus, since wanting the job is printed on its literature. The Diadem is returned to its case, the case to its crate, and the crate ships with a reference letter the Fund does not frame.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_compromise A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You file the application with the fee unpaid, having assumed alloy candidates were exempt, and the Under-Herald must rule the interview void after it has already happened. The finding is sealed, the Diadem remains technically pending, and the Fund instructs the mugs printer to add TECHNICALLY PENDING under the portrait. The crown, through Osbert, spends the evening facing the wall.
effects: schedule 3 r18_royal_succession_compromise A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

Two hundred and thirteen candidates have failed the rubric, and the charter provides that when the list is exhausted, everyone present at the reading is deemed to have applied. The reading is held at the picnic. You are present. Osbert lays the tarnish chart on the constitution blanket, and the Under-Herald announces your name with the resignation of a man reading his own handwriting.

choices.text: Cater the whole shortlist's interviews

outcomes.text: If everyone at the picnic is now a candidate, then the candidates control the catering, and you help the hamper warden scale lunch to constitutional proportions. Interviews proceed between courses, and every villager who sits before the crown answers the same way: no thank you, and would it like anything from the hamper. Osbert's chart runs out of room in the one column he has never needed, the column for candidates it likes. Food has never polled higher at a succession.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_reversal A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You carry the second hamper up the hill, and your back files an objection on the final step. The picnic lays you flat on the constitution blanket, which technically makes you the closest thing to a seated monarch the kingdom has had in months, and several people bow satirically. The interviews run long and the crowd runs restless. The crown, per Osbert, requests that the candidate on the blanket be permitted to finish lying down.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_reversal A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Answer the sealed criterion honestly

success.text: Your interview lasts one question. Osbert unseals criterion four and reads it aloud for the first time in four centuries: WHY. You say you have no answer, you only came for the picnic, and Osbert transcribes it, and the crown warms one full degree, its highest recorded mark. The Under-Herald weeps quietly into his sleeve, which is lined for it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_reversal A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

fail.text: The interview has one question, and you attempt to give it three answers. The crown notes, through Osbert, that answering WHY at length is itself an answer to WHY, and scores it accordingly. You are ruled overqualified in the wrong direction, which places you in the company of every king since the rubric began. The picnic consoles you with the last of the good mustard.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_reversal A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Take the full candidate experience yourself

success.text: You sit the entire process: measurement, posture review, the tarnish chart explained at last. Candidates receive a hospitality honorarium of four coins, unclaimed for years because candidates were rich. You publish your notes on the procedure at the gates, and attendance at the next Sunday picnic doubles, half of them just to see the chart. The picnic mutters that you interviewed for a throne, however educationally, and serves you last for a week.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_reversal A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

fail.text: Halfway through your interview the actuary leans in with a sponsorship: eight coins to describe yourself as the continuity candidate, no obligations, just the phrase. You take it, and by supper the phrase hangs on a banner over the gates, above your face. The picnic serves you bread with nothing on it, which in picnic law is a formal document. The crown, Osbert reports, has scored you under a criterion it invented this afternoon.
effects: schedule 4 r18_royal_succession_reversal A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

At dawn on proclamation day the panel convenes to hear the crown's decision, and the actuary has brought champagne with the Fund's seal on the cork. Osbert stands, unfolds four centuries of notes, and reads the finding in full. Every candidate who applied is disqualified by criterion four, wanting it. Every candidate who did not apply is disqualified by not applying. The position is therefore not filled, and the crown moves that it remain so indefinitely, citing its own reference: four hundred years of unfortunate hires, all of them its.

choices.text: Draft the vacancy into constitutional form

outcomes.text: You sit with the Under-Herald and translate the finding into charter language: the realm shall keep its crown, and the charter says keep, not wear, so a hamper qualifies as keeping. One clause follows another until the last one falls out on its own: no monarch, no royal rents, and the collectors are sent home carrying a form that apologizes for four centuries of arithmetic. The Under-Herald seals it with his good wax. The actuary asks what yield an apology bears and is handed one.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_legacy A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You draft with such thoroughness that you accidentally define the vacancy as an office, and offices, per the charter, must be filled. The Under-Herald catches the loop at the last line and has to un-ratify the afternoon in public, in front of the mugs printer. The Fund calls the confusion a market opportunity. The crown requests, through Osbert, that future drafting be done nearer the hamper, where the thinking is better.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_legacy A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Auction souvenirs of the reign that never was

outcomes.text: You corner the memorabilia of a coronation that never happened, which turns out to be a category collectors go mad for, and the tent receipts alone fetch eight coins. The Fund quietly pays six more for the champagne cork with its seal on it, to keep it out of a museum. Prices for relics of nothing climb until people who wanted a keepsake of their own history start saying so in the tone that precedes thrown produce. On the blanket, your seat is moved downwind of the mustard without a word.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_legacy A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

choices.text: Chip in for the crown's hamper cushion

outcomes.text: The crown requires lodgings suited to its new duties, and the picnic takes up a kitty for a cushion cut to fit the hamper's left corner. Your three coins buy the horsehair. Osbert delivers the crown at teatime and announces his own retirement in the same breath, effective never, because the hamper, he notes, still needs a bearer on Sundays. The crown settles into the corner with what he officially scores as relief.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_legacy A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

outcomes.text: You carry the hamper up for the installment ceremony and take the palace steps too proudly, and the top step has opinions. You arrive at the blanket with the hamper intact and both knees filed under expenses. The gates crowd, watching the crown move house into a picnic basket, understands the finding all at once and gets loud about it in a celebratory direction. The Under-Herald rules the noise constitutional.
effects: schedule 5 r18_royal_succession_legacy A throne seeks an occupant. The crown has questions.

text:
ROYAL SUCCESSION

One year into the vacancy, the kingdom observes Stability Day, and the Royal Gazette prints the official figures: no wars, no favorites, no decrees, the most stable reign on record, occupant, none. The crown resides in the hamper's left corner and holds the constitution blanket down when the wind argues. The Fund has rebranded the vacancy as its own product, Sovereign Zero, and the actuary stands at the gates handing out prospectuses for a throne it does not have. Osbert bears the hamper on Sundays.

choices.text: License Stability Day merchandise yourself

outcomes.text: You register the phrase OCCUPANT, NONE before the Fund thinks to, and license it to the mugs printer for eight coins against royalties. The Fund, unable to sell the vacancy without your phrase, pays six for a sublicense, and you are now collecting rent on the absence of a king. The actuary shakes your hand with genuine feeling, one professional to another. At the picnic they have begun calling you the occupant, and it is not affectionate.
