choices.text: Take a Sunday turn bearing the hamper

outcomes.text: Osbert trains you in the walk, the pause, and the setting down, which is the whole of bearing and takes a lifetime. On your first Sunday you carry the hamper to the far meadow, and the crown rides in its corner like a retired admiral inspecting a harbor it no longer commands and likes better this way. The rota adds your name in ink. Osbert says the cushion approves, and then, correcting himself for the record, that he does.

Osbert trains you in the walk, the pause, and the setting down, which is the whole of bearing and takes a lifetime. On your first Sunday you carry the hamper to the far meadow, and the crown rides in its corner like a retired admiral inspecting a harbor it no longer commands and likes better this way. The rota adds your name in ink. Osbert says the cushion approves, and then, correcting himself for the record, that he does.

Your first bearing ends in a rabbit hole and a full somersault of state, hamper airborne, crown describing an arc the Gazette will call unauthorized flight. You catch it with your face, which Osbert scores as correct form under the circumstances. The far meadow is briefly certain the monarchy has fallen, then reassured that it merely bounced. Your name goes on the rota anyway, first under incidents, then under Sundays.

choices.text: Cite the crown's finding against the Fund

success.text: The actuary announces at the gates that Sovereign Zero will pay dividends, and you ask, per the finding, who signs them, since wanting the throne's yield is criterion four in a suit. He checks his own prospectus and discovers the rubric quoted in the risk disclosures, where his clerks pasted it for length. The Fund is disqualified from its own product by its own literature. The crown, consulted in the hamper, tarnishes once, which Osbert translates, after forty years and one good one, as laughter.

fail.text: You quote the finding from memory at full volume, and you misquote it, and the actuary, of all people, corrects you from the authorized text, which the Fund has printed and is selling. The gates crowd boos the accuracy. The Under-Herald must publicly certify that the Fund's edition is faithful, and the Fund has that on the cover by morning. The crown keeps the blanket down through the whole embarrassment, which Osbert notes is the job now, and it is done well.

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

Tuesday, soup night, and a procession arrives at the shared sanctuary in the vestments of graded bowls. The Archdeacon of Cutlery unrolls a writ declaring the holy spoon of Saint Opal of the Second Helping to be class one liturgical, forbidden to touch food. The soup warden, mid ladle, points out that Saint Opal is chiefly famous for seconds. The Archdeacon has the writ notarized against the doorframe while ninety strangers wait for dinner.

choices.text: Take the Synod's fee to inventory the cutlery

outcomes.text: The Synod pays handsomely and in advance, which is how you learn they expected resistance. You count forty one spoons and write holy beside the one in the velvet box, as instructed. The soup warden watches you work with the expression of someone updating a guest list. The Archdeacon calls your penmanship devout, which is the most anyone has ever paid you for the word forty.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_assembly Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: The Synod pays handsomely and in advance, which is how you learn they expected resistance. You count forty one spoons and write holy beside the one in the velvet box, as instructed. The soup warden watches you work with the expression of someone updating a guest list. The Archdeacon calls your penmanship devout, which is the most anyone has ever paid you for the word forty.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_assembly Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Keep the line moving while the writ is read

success.text: You take the second ladle and the line does not so much as slow. The Archdeacon reads eleven clauses to the backs of people eating; the writ has no provision for being ignored politely. By the last bowl the soup warden has taught you the sanctuary's one rule, which is that the pot decides when it is empty. You are handed a dish towel, which is how membership works here.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_assembly Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: You take the second ladle with confidence and the cauldron's handle takes offense. The burn is minor; the noise you make during clause six is not, and the line receives it as commentary. The Archdeacon adds a codicil about disorder near relics while the soup warden bandages your hand with liturgical calm. She gives you a dish towel anyway; apparently that is how joining goes in this kitchen.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_assembly Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Ask the writ to name the spoon it governs

outcomes.text: You ask the writ to identify the spoon it claims. It says the holy spoon, singular, and you point out that the sanctuary drawer holds nine spoons of identical pattern, donated in grief and in bulk. Jurisdiction over an unspecified spoon is jurisdiction over no spoon, a point the Archdeacon concedes with the face of a man swallowing a bone. The procession withdraws to commission a description, and dinner continues at the pace of victory.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_assembly Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You ask the writ to identify the spoon it claims, and unfortunately it does: appendix, engraving, a wobble in the handle recorded by three witnesses. The Archdeacon recites the wobble from memory, tenderly. The writ is upheld pending appeal and the velvet box is sealed with the Synod's lesser wax, the kind they use when they are winning.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_assembly Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

Two suppers face each other across the sanctuary yard. The Synod's Correct Supper has place cards, a herald, and a menu approved in committee; the congregation's potluck has food. The Archdeacon blesses an empty tureen at length while the Synod's novice auditor, third row, watches the potluck's bread the way sailors watch land. The holy spoon sits in its sealed box on the Correct Supper's altar table, jurisdiction pending.

choices.text: Carry the potluck to the fence line, dish by dish

success.text: You walk the beans, the bread, and the pot itself to the fence, close enough that the smell crosses the property line without permission. Attendance at the Correct Supper drops in order of rank, lowest first, which is also the order of hunger. The novice auditor arrives holding an empty approved plate and the soup warden fills it without checking its doctrine. By dark there is one supper with two names, and the sealed box has no one left to guard it but the Archdeacon, dining alone and correctly.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_retaliation Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: You walk the pot to the fence line and the fence, which is the Synod's, wins on home ground; a plank folds and you go down among the beans. The Correct Supper takes it as a portent and the potluck takes it as grounds to raise the fence question generally, loudly. The novice auditor helps you up before remembering whose ledger he carries, and records the plank as a casualty of doctrine. Supper happens twice that night, angrier in both directions.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_retaliation Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Seat the Synod at the potluck by strict precedence

outcomes.text: You draft a seating plan honoring every rank the Synod recognizes, which obliges them to attend in order to occupy it; an unclaimed seat of honor is, by their own manual, a scandal. The Archdeacon processes across the yard to defend his chair and finds soup already in front of it. Precedence forbids him to leave before the lowest rank finishes, and the lowest rank is on thirds. The two suppers end as one supper with an argument for a centerpiece, which is the traditional arrangement.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_retaliation Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You draft the seating plan but rank the Vergers above the Deacons of Bowl, a heresy you had no way of respecting and no excuse for missing, per the manual you had not read. The Correct Supper takes the insult as proof the potluck cannot be trusted with hierarchy, which was never the potluck's ambition. The Archdeacon returns the plan annotated in red wax. The novice auditor keeps your draft, folded small, the way people keep dangerous ideas.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_retaliation Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Cater the Correct Supper at schism prices

outcomes.text: The Synod's kitchen has correct utensils and no cooks, so your invoice reads what the market for doctrine will bear, which is plenty. You serve eleven courses to nine people while the herald announces each dish's classification. The potluck watches you carry approved soup past them at a margin and reaches its own conclusions about direct methods. The Archdeacon tips you for reverence, and the coin has a spoon on it.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_retaliation Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

The relic audit begins at dawn with a knock performed in triplicate. The novice auditor unpacks calipers, wax, and the Registry of Utensil Rank; every holy object in the shared sanctuary must be produced, weighed, and matched to its description. The sealed box has come home from the altar table, and the Archdeacon means to open it under full rite, prove the spoon's wobble, and settle jurisdiction by inventory. The soup warden goes on peeling carrots at audit velocity, which is the same as her usual velocity.

choices.text: File the sanctuary's relics as kitchenware in use

success.text: You produce the sanctuary's ledger, in which every relic is filed under equipment, working, with dates of last use. The Registry has no procedure for auditing a holy object currently employed; sanctity in use may not be interrupted, per a rule written to protect long sermons. The novice auditor stamps deferred nineteen times with visible relief. The sealed box alone stays auditable, because the one thing the Synod insists is holiest is the one thing nobody is allowed to use.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_coalition Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: You produce the sanctuary's ledger and the Archdeacon produces a newer one, the Synod's, in which the sanctuary itself is filed as a reliquary, contents included. Auditing a building from inside is procedurally elegant and personally humiliating; you are counted, described, and found ordinary. The audit adjourns to schedule the box's opening under full rite, quorum required. The soup warden's carrots are assessed as votive, and she peels them anyway.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_coalition Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Read the Registry while the auditor eats

success.text: The novice auditor cannot audit and chew at once, so you turn the Registry's pages for him and find the taxonomy's load bearing sentence: a spoon's class is determined by its function at the time of inspection. You copy it out twice and sell one copy to a curious deacon for pocket silver, since doctrine in your handwriting is technically yours. The congregation is unsettled to find you billing for scripture, but the sentence itself goes home with the soup warden, who reads it to the pot. Function at the time of inspection; she says it like a forecast.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_coalition Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: The novice auditor cannot audit and chew at once, and while he chews you find the Registry's appendix of bounties: the Synod pays for provenance, and you are holding the sanctuary's donation records in your other hand. The Archdeacon buys them at a price that embarrasses you for less time than you expected. The congregation learns who described the nine identical spoons in writing, and dinner is served to you slightly cooler thereafter.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_coalition Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Feed the audit until it loses count

outcomes.text: Audits run on stomachs, and you keep the auditor's full: bread at the third relic, stew at the ninth, a heel of cheese for the candlesticks. Counting slows to the speed of gratitude. By afternoon the novice auditor has recorded the stockpot as one vessel, contents various, blessed by use, a phrase found nowhere in the Registry and now entered in it. The sealed box is left for a future session, on account of there being pie.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_coalition Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You carry the stew to the audit table with the confidence of a career waiter, and the tureen states its temperature through the towel. Two relics receive an anointing of broth, which is either a scandal or an upgrade depending on which supper you attend. The novice auditor drafts an incident wax and cries a little, for the relics or the stew, unclear. The audit adjourns overnight and the sealed box goes back on the shelf between the flour and the good bowl, which will matter later.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_coalition Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

Word of the schism travels along gravy routes, and by Friday four neighboring kitchens have sent envoys to the shared sanctuary: two bakers, a fish widow, and a monk who left the Synod over the fork question and does not discuss it. The Archdeacon has invited the same four to a Symposium on Correct Feeding, catered by nobody. The question at every table is which supper to lend their pots to. The sealed box has begun receiving pilgrims, who are directed to the soup line by signage.

choices.text: Sell the pilgrim traffic to the Synod as attendance

outcomes.text: The Synod counts heads for legitimacy the way kitchens count them for portions, and you offer them the pilgrim ledger at a devotional markup. The Archdeacon publishes the figures as Correct Supper attendance, footnoted honestly and read dishonestly. The pilgrims keep arriving, keep bowing to the box, keep eating with the congregation, and keep being counted by you twice. It is the most reverent bookkeeping in the region, and it pays like it.
effects: schedule 3 r18_gentle_schism_crisis Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Start the kitty for a second pot with your own coin

outcomes.text: You start the kitty with your own coin, loudly, which is the only way a kitty starts. The fish widow matches it, the bakers match it in flour, and the monk contributes a pot he describes only as liberated. Two pots means the fence line supper becomes a standing arrangement with a rota, and the rota fills before the ink dries. The sealed box looks on from the shelf, increasingly a mascot.
effects: schedule 3 r18_gentle_schism_crisis Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You start the kitty and carry the new pot home yourself to save cartage, a decision your spine files under grievances. The pot arrives; you arrive slightly after it, at an angle. The fish widow splints your finger with a whitefish rib and the rota gains a line about lifting in pairs, written while looking at you. The second supper stands, and you sit.
effects: schedule 3 r18_gentle_schism_crisis Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Make the Symposium define feeding, in writing

outcomes.text: The Symposium on Correct Feeding cannot refuse a request for definitions; definitions are its whole harvest. Three sessions produce the finding that feeding is the transfer of nourishment to the hungry, a sentence the congregation has been performing nightly without benefit of committee. You have the finding sealed and posted on the sanctuary door, above the soup schedule, as endorsement. The Archdeacon objects that it was descriptive, not permissive, and the fish widow asks him to define the difference over dinner, which he attends.
effects: schedule 3 r18_gentle_schism_crisis Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: The Symposium accepts your request and refers it to a subcommittee on the word the, which is where questions go to be raised as its own. The envoys wait a week, then lend their pots to whoever is actually cooking, which was decidable without you. The Archdeacon thanks you publicly for your faith in process, the cruelest sentence available to him. The sealed box gathers dust at exactly the rate of the docket.
effects: schedule 3 r18_gentle_schism_crisis Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

Harvest festival puts both suppers in the same hall by ancient booking error: one long table, two clergies. The Archdeacon rises to bless the meal in full vestment; the soup warden rises at the same moment out of habit, holding a ladle like a crozier. Two blessings begin over one soup, in different keys. Ninety hungry guests stand with heads bowed and patience audibly cooling, and the sealed box has been placed at the table's center by a helpful pilgrim, where both blessings must pass through it.

choices.text: Serve the soup while both blessings continue

outcomes.text: You take the third option, which is a full bowl moving from hand to hand while both blessings continue overhead. By the second verse every guest is eating under a canopy of competing sanctity, warm either way. The Archdeacon finishes, opens his eyes to a fed hall, and cannot locate the error in it; the soup warden finishes and starts collecting bowls. The blessings are ruled concurrent, the soup is ruled excellent, and the sealed box at the table's center is ruled, for now, a centerpiece.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_compromise Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You take the third option at speed, and speed is how a full tureen finds an untied bootlace. The crash lands between verses like a rival liturgy. Both blessings stop; ninety heads lift; you are on the floor holding one intact bowl aloft, which reads as either an offering or a defense. Supper survives on the second pot, the argument survives on the first, and you will be finding barley in your hem all week.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_compromise Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Rule on which blessing has standing, by the booking

success.text: You produce the hall's booking ledger and read it as law, since everyone else is reading it as grievance. The booking names the meal itself, not either clergy; the hall is rented to supper, and supper has therefore convened both blessings as its guests. Guests may speak in the order they finish, a standard no one can object to aloud. The Archdeacon shortens his liturgy for the first recorded time, the soup warden says grace in eleven words, and the soup goes out hot on a technicality.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_compromise Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: You produce the hall's booking ledger, and the booking, on inspection, is in the Synod's name after all: their clerk books yearly, in advance, in wax. Precedence goes to the Archdeacon, who blesses at commemorative length while the soup performs its slow doctrinal cooling. The congregation eats lukewarm and files out hot; the pilgrims take notes. The sealed box at the table's center is the only party that stayed neutral, and it gets the credit.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_compromise Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Charge the pilgrims for seats at the schism

outcomes.text: Word has spread that the two blessings may collide tonight, and the pilgrims regard doctrine the way other people regard weather: worth traveling for. You sell standing room by the window at a price you invent per pilgrim. The blessings do collide, gorgeously, and your gallery gasps on cue while the guests just want supper. The Archdeacon later cites the attendance as revival; the soup warden cites it as a fire risk; your purse cites it as a very good festival.
effects: schedule 4 r18_gentle_schism_compromise Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

Negotiators meet in the vestry over the question of custody: the sealed box to alternate weekly between altar and sanctuary shelf, escorted each way in procession. The Archdeacon arrives with a draft schedule in four colors of wax; the soup warden arrives with the actual shelf measurements, which the draft has not consulted. The novice auditor sits between them as recording clerk, having by now eaten at the sanctuary eleven times in a professional capacity. Every clause is agreed except the last, which is who holds the box while the schedule is being signed.

choices.text: Give custody of the box to the schedule itself

outcomes.text: You propose that the box rest, during signing and thereafter in all disputes, in the custody of the document: whoever is currently executing the schedule holds the spoon, and the schedule is always being executed by someone. Jurisdiction becomes a relay, which is the only shape both sides can carry. The Archdeacon admires the clause the way one admires a rival's vestments, sincerely and against his will. The novice auditor copies it out three times, once for each party and once, folded small, for himself.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You propose custody by document, and the document, once signed, develops opinions: clause nine can be read to give the box to the hall porter on alternating feast days, and the porter, once told, will not waive it. Both clergies unite for one afternoon in resenting the porter, which is progress of a kind you did not intend. The schedule goes back for redrafting in plainer wax. The box remains on the shelf between the flour and the good bowl, undisturbed and, though nobody checks, unusually light.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Walk the first escort yourself, staff in hand

success.text: You lead the first custody procession across the yard, staff borrowed, expression rented from a statue. A Correct Supper steward tries to improve the route mid march and finds your staff describing an arc of pure administration. The procession arrives intact and on schedule, and both clergies quietly note that the escort answered to the schedule, not to either of them, which unsettles everyone into better behavior. The congregation thinks you enjoyed the staff slightly too much, and the congregation is right.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: You lead the first procession and the route, on the Archdeacon's advice, goes past the Correct Supper for veneration, unannounced. The potluck reads the detour as capture and comes over the fence; the stewards read the potluck as invasion and close ranks around a box that weighs less than the argument about it. You end the afternoon underneath three stewards and one baker, all apologizing in order of rank. Custody survives; your ribs file a minority report; the Synod hires two more stewards with halberds, for the dignity.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Cook the negotiation's meals on neutral ground

outcomes.text: You set a small table in the vestry doorway, halfway between jurisdictions, and feed the negotiation from it without comment. It is difficult to draft hostile clauses across a shared dish of dumplings; the wax cools slower than the food, and the clauses soften accordingly. The schedule is signed by supper, the last clause resolved by the soup warden holding the box while everyone else held bowls. The novice auditor asks, off the record, whether there will be dumplings at future custody events. There will.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You set the neutral table, and the vestry brazier, unattended for one clause too long, sets a corner of the draft schedule alight; you beat it out with your sleeve and your sleeve keeps a souvenir. Four colors of wax reflow into one color, which no one can agree is anybody's. Redrafting adds a week, and the week adds grievances, itemized. The novice auditor's minutes for the day read, in full: fire, small; progress, smaller; dumplings, excellent.
effects: schedule 5 r18_gentle_schism_reversal Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

The Rite of Consultation convenes at last: full quorum, four candles, the sealed box on a cushion, doctrine requiring that in disputes of jurisdiction the spoon itself be examined. The Archdeacon breaks the Synod's greater wax with tongs reserved for the purpose. The box is empty. It has, the dust suggests, been empty since before anyone present held office, and ninety witnesses watch the Archdeacon look into the velvet the way men look into wells.

choices.text: Sell replica spoons to the stunned pilgrims

outcomes.text: A crisis of provenance is a seller's market, and you have nine identical spoons' worth of pattern to work from. The replicas go for pilgrim prices, each with a wax tag reading in the tradition of Saint Opal, a phrase your conscience and a notary both accept. The Synod, needing something to guard, buys four and posts stewards over them, which restores everyone's sense of order at the going rate. Somewhere in the kitchen a pot goes on simmering, unaware it has become the most important room in the province.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_legacy Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Move the whole rite to the kitchen, candles and all

success.text: You suggest, gently, that if the spoon must be examined, the rite should go where spoons are. Quorum, candles, cushion, and all process to the kitchen, where the soup warden is stirring the evening pot with a long spoon that has a wobble in the handle, recorded by three witnesses, one of whom faints. Doctrine is consulted where it stands: a spoon's class is determined by its function at the time of inspection, and at the time of inspection it is feeding ninety strangers. The Archdeacon watches the holy spoon of Saint Opal execute its office and says, at last, that jurisdiction appears to follow use. The stewards are sent home, and the halberds are hung up as trivets.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_legacy Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

fail.text: You suggest the rite adjourn to the kitchen, and the procession takes the servants' stair, which was built for servants with narrower candles. Hot wax, your knuckles, a brief liturgical pileup. The rite arrives disordered and finds the soup warden stirring with the spoon anyway, wobble and all, at which point the fainting starts on its own schedule. Jurisdiction follows use; the finding is unanimous; your hand is bandaged with the cushion's fringe, the most ceremonial first aid available.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_legacy Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

choices.text: Read the taxonomy over the stockpot, aloud

outcomes.text: You fetch the Registry of Utensil Rank and read its load bearing sentence over the pot like a verdict: class is function at the time of inspection. The spoon is inspected mid stir; the function is mercy at a rolling boil; the classification writes itself, working spoon, kitchen, in service. The Archdeacon, hoist by his own appendix, signs the finding with the good wax, because if the Synod cannot be right it will at least be procedurally impeccable. The extra stewards are reassigned to carrying trays, where the halberds prove useless and are checked at the door.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_legacy Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

outcomes.text: You fetch the Registry and read the sentence aloud, and a Synod canonist stands to argue that stirring is arguably veneration, in which case function at inspection is worship and the old class holds. The kitchen becomes a seminar. The soup, which does not attend seminars, begins to catch, and the soup warden pulls the holy spoon free of doctrine to save the bottom of the pot, settling by demonstration what the canonist would not settle by argument. The finding is postponed, the pot is scraped, and everyone eats a little of the burnt layer as penance for jurisprudence.
effects: schedule 2 r18_gentle_schism_legacy Two blessings, one soup, and a spoon with opinions

text:
GENTLE SCHISM

Months on, the shared sanctuary serves supper under an arrangement nobody remembers voting for: the holy spoon lives in the stockpot, doctrine says so, and the doctrine hangs framed above the drawer with the nine identical spoons that once confused a writ. The Archdeacon attends on alternate Tuesdays as a guest, blesses briefly, and has learned to hold a bowl. The former novice auditor has petitioned to transfer his vows to the kitchen, where he keeps a small registry of who has eaten, titled the Roll of the Fed. The Synod's amended taxonomy now contains one new class, working relic, with a single entry.

choices.text: Take the ladle rota's hardest shift, permanently

success.text: You take the dawn shift, the one that starts the stock and ends social lives, and after a month the rota quietly lists you as keeper of the pot, a title with no vestments and considerable jurisdiction. The catch arrives with the amended taxonomy: a working relic must work, so supper is now doctrinally mandatory, daily, forever, and the congregation has acquired the one thing it never wanted, a liturgy. The soup warden shrugs and says every kitchen gets religion eventually, if it keeps feeding people. The former auditor enters you in the Roll of the Fed under both columns.

fail.text: You take the dawn shift and dawn, in winter, takes payment in knuckles; the stock scalds you on day nine and the rota adds your name to the injury margin, which has become its own small honor roll. Supper goes out anyway, because the amended taxonomy is merciless: a working relic must work, daily, which means so must everyone. You are keeper of the pot with one hand in a mitten. The Archdeacon, on his Tuesday, carries trays without being asked, and is not thanked, which he has learned to read as belonging.

choices.text: File the stockpot as the spoon's official reliquary

outcomes.text: You draft the instrument yourself: the stockpot is designated the reliquary of Saint Opal's spoon, and a reliquary, per the Synod's own manual, must be kept full and attended by the faithful. The Synod ratifies it before noticing it has just made soup canonically continuous. Pilgrims now venerate by peeling, the manual being clear that a reliquary's attendants may not stand idle. The Archdeacon calls it the only relic in the province that feeds its witnesses, and files the phrase, uncredited, in next year's sermons.

You draft the reliquary instrument, and the Synod's canonists, handed one last document, find their footing: a reliquary requires a feast day, a feast day requires their calendar, and their calendar requires fees. The congregation now owes the Synod an annual observance with processional escort, invoiced. The soup warden pays it in soup, which the invoice did not think to forbid. The schism ends gently, as advertised, but the paperwork will outlive the pot.

choices.text: License Saint Opal's name to every kitchen that asks

outcomes.text: Every kitchen in four parishes wants the name that beat a Synod, and you hold, by accident and notary, the transcription rights. The licenses sell briskly: Saint Opal's Chophouse, Saint Opal's Pies, one tavern of doubtful mercy. The congregation declines to sue on the grounds that suing is not feeding anyone, which you count, privately, as permission. The original spoon goes on stirring, unlicensed and unbothered, and the Roll of the Fed gets longer every night without charging admission.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

Third bell past midnight, and the fever has the west lane going door by door, polite as a census. Pestilence Opportunity Partners bought the clinic stores on Tuesday and is releasing medicine 'as demand matures,' which is investor language for never. On the doorstep stands a vulture in a mustard waistcoat, Mr. Moult, regional bereavement associate, offering funeral futures at a preneed discount. Inside, the night matron is running three sickrooms on two hours of sleep and a rota written on a pillowcase.

choices.text: Chip into the medicine kitty and take a bedside shift

outcomes.text: You put three coins in the biscuit tin and your name on the pillowcase, which is how the bedside crews do contracts. The night matron assigns you the widow at the crossroads, hours two through four, cold flannels and lying cheerfully about the weather. Mr. Moult watches you sign and marks the address 'deferred interest' in his ledger. The tin does not thank you, but the rota now knows your name, which is worse and better.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_assembly The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: You take the four o'clock shift with no training and catch the edge of the fever by dawn, a light dose, the kind that files a claim on you and settles later. The lane hears you coughing and concludes the crews are down another pair of hands, which does nothing for the general mood. The night matron logs you as 'walking wounded, still counts.' Mr. Moult offers you a policy at the associate rate, out of respect.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_assembly The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Cite the plague-price statute at the clinic doors

outcomes.text: You find it in the municipal code, an ordinance from the last outbreak: medicine held during a declared fever must be priced at the counter, not the exchange. The clerk at the clinic reads it twice, goes pale in a compliance sort of way, and unlocks one shelf pending review. One shelf is eleven families breathing easier. Mr. Moult crosses eleven addresses out of his book and calls it market softening.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_assembly The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: The statute exists, but Pestilence Opportunity Partners filed for an exemption in advance, the way you would pack an umbrella. Their counsel explains that the fever has not been 'declared,' merely 'observed,' and observation carries no pricing obligations. You have taught the lane that the law is a door that opens from the inside. Mr. Moult offers his card in sympathy; the waistcoat has a pocket just for those.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_assembly The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Sell Mr. Moult a list of the freshly bedridden

outcomes.text: He pays eight for the list and six more for the annotations, because 'coughing, third day' is actionable intelligence in his line. The waistcoat produces the coins from an inner pocket that seems to hold the whole economy. Somewhere behind you the biscuit tin rattles without you in it. Business, you tell yourself, is only weather with a signature.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_assembly The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

Someone has put six kettles on one stove, which is how the bedside crews convene at three in the morning: broth for the sick, tea for the sitters, and one pot of something medicinal for the matron. The agenda, chalked on a skillet, reads STORES, SHIFTS, THE BIRD. Mr. Moult has attended uninvited and sits in the corner taking minutes nobody asked for, waistcoat buttoned to the throat out of professional courtesy. The broth courier, sixteen and owed soup debts across half the district, wants to know why medicine has a ticker price and broth does not.

choices.text: Publish the crews' recovery ledger to spook the market

success.text: You post the pillowcase arithmetic on the market board: forty-one fevers taken on by the crews, thirty-five recoveries, six pending, none priced. Funeral futures dip on the news, because nothing ruins a bereavement product like people getting better in public. Two herbalists and a gravedigger ask to be included in next week's figures. Mr. Moult records the number, and for one unguarded second the waistcoat looks tight.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_retaliation The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

fail.text: Your arithmetic is honest but your handwriting is not, and the board reads six recoveries, thirty-five pending. Funeral futures rally by lunch and Pestilence Opportunity Partners sends the crews a fruit basket, which everyone agrees is the most sinister object ever to enter the kitchen. The broth courier eats the pears anyway; debts are debts. Mr. Moult underlines something and looks briefly, professionally moved.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_retaliation The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Broker POP a seat at the meeting for a finder's fee

outcomes.text: Pestilence Opportunity Partners pays eight for the introduction and six for the seating chart, since knowing who sits near the matron is worth a premium. Their observer arrives with a branded kettle nobody touches. The crews notice the new face, then notice you not noticing it, and the tin's little lid shuts with feeling. Mr. Moult, outflanked at his own trade, gives you a look that in vulture circles counts as respect.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_retaliation The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Take over the worst shift so the matron can sleep

outcomes.text: You take the hour nobody names, the one between the last kettle and the first bell, and the matron sleeps in a chair with the authority of a monument. The widow at the crossroads teaches you her card game; the rules change whenever she is losing, which she says is also how medicine is priced now. By dawn the rota lists you as regular, underlined. Mr. Moult, leaving, holds the door for you, then seems alarmed at his own wing.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_retaliation The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: You take the dead hour and the dead hour takes a little back, a cough you will be carrying for a week. Word gets round that even the new pair of hands is rattling, and the lane's mood curdles from worried to lively. The matron sleeps through it, which everyone agrees was the objective. On his way out Mr. Moult leaves a pamphlet titled Planning Ahead, folded, apologetically, into thirds.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_retaliation The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

The repossession men arrive at breakfast with paperwork describing the biscuit tin as an unlicensed financial institution and the kettles as clinic assets, wrongly liberated. Pestilence Opportunity Partners regrets the inconvenience and has enclosed a coupon. Mr. Moult stands behind them holding his hat, wearing the new company pin on the waistcoat like a small wound. The widow at the crossroads is sitting on the tin, and has stated her terms, which are none.

choices.text: Form a wall of tired people around the tin

success.text: Fourteen exhausted sitters stand shoulder to shoulder in dressing gowns, and it turns out repossession men have no protocol for a wall that coughs. They withdraw to seek clarification, which is the professional word for retreat. The lane feels dangerous now in the way a kettle feels dangerous, quietly, at a rolling boil. The matron docks you a point on principle; walls, she says, are just shifts you cannot schedule.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

fail.text: The wall holds until it does not, and you personally learn what a writ feels like when applied at speed. Three cracked ribs are logged as 'friction during recovery of assets,' which is also what the fever is calling itself these days. The men take one kettle and leave the tin out of embarrassment. Mr. Moult finds your hat in the gutter afterward, brushes it, and returns it without eye contact, and his pin is upside down for the rest of the day.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Spirit the sickroom supplies out through back doors

outcomes.text: By the time the men finish their inventory, the lane has performed a miracle of redistribution: cots in attics, kettles in coal sheds, the tin under a christening gown where no bailiff dares. The paperwork is left describing a kitchen that no longer exists, and paperwork hates that. Neighbors who never spoke now hold each other's contraband, which is a friendship with structure. Mr. Moult inventories an empty room and writes 'assets pending' with what might be relief.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: You carry a cot over four fences in the dark and the fourth fence wins on points. The supplies scatter to safety but so does the news, and the lane spends the day discussing raids in the tone people reserve for weather and war. The matron paints your scrapes with something that stings on purpose. Mr. Moult, consulting his actuarial tables, revises your life expectancy downward and, unprompted, apologizes.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Demand the warrant name each spoon it seizes

outcomes.text: You invoke the inventory rule: seizures during a fever must itemize, and itemizing a kitchen takes three clerks four days. The repossession men wilt at the arithmetic and reschedule indefinitely, pending a spoon census. The lane learns that boredom, correctly aimed, is a civic weapon. Mr. Moult submits his uninvited minutes as evidence, then seems surprised at whose side they help.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: The itemization gambit works until Pestilence Opportunity Partners hires a fourth clerk, which for them is pocket change and for the lane is a siege. Every spoon now has a docket number and a hearing date. The matron's opinion of the law was low and has found a basement. Still, the tin gains a certain notoriety: a biscuit tin with legal representation commands respect.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_coalition The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

Rain on market day, and under the last dry awning an unlikely huddle: the herbalists, two gravediggers, the broth courier with her ledger of soup debts, and the matron, off shift and therefore twice as dangerous. Between them they hold most of what the fever needs and none of what the market respects. Mr. Moult circles the square twice before landing at the edge of the awning, technically outside, hat dripping. His sales are down; people keep offering him soup instead of signatures, and he has begun accepting.

choices.text: Merge the soup ledger with the sickbed rota

success.text: The courier's ledger and the matron's pillowcase are reconciled at one table, and it emerges the lane already runs on a single currency, favors, at a fixed rate of one good turn. Willowbark for the diggers' backs, broth for the herbalists' apprentices, sitters for everyone: settled by supper. Nobody calls the arrangement anything; naming it would attract fees. Mr. Moult asks, quietly, whether the ledger takes new accounts, and is told to bring a dish next time.
effects: schedule 4 r18_plague_market_crisis The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

fail.text: Reconciling two ledgers by candlelight costs you the night and most of your voice, and dawn finds three soup debts double entered and one gravedigger owed broth from two directions. The dispute spills into the square at volume, and the lane enjoys it more than is strictly helpful. Still, the pillowcase and the ledger now live in the same drawer, which is the institutional version of a marriage. Mr. Moult offers to audit, and everyone laughs, and he was not joking.
effects: schedule 4 r18_plague_market_crisis The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Rebrand the crews as a preferred provider network

outcomes.text: You draft letterhead: The Lane Bedside Concern, established whenever, motto 'Present Before Payment.' Institutions cannot attack letterhead; it would look like competition, and competition has rules. The clinic clerk, cornered by formatting, begins forwarding the crews their overflow cases officially. Mr. Moult requests a brochure for his files and then does not file it; it stays in the waistcoat, over the heart pocket, for no stated reason.
effects: schedule 4 r18_plague_market_crisis The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: Your letterhead impresses everyone except the licensing office, which rules that a concern with a motto is a business, and a business owes registration fees backdated to 'established whenever.' The fine is small but the precedent is a nuisance with a future. The matron folds your brochure into a fan and uses it on the fever ward, so the design was not wasted. Mr. Moult calls the ruling 'the market defending itself,' and for once he does not sound proud of it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_plague_market_crisis The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Quote POP a price for the huddle's supply routes

outcomes.text: Eight coins for the herbalists' delivery times, six more for the gravediggers' schedule, and Pestilence Opportunity Partners thanks you for 'mapping the informal sector.' The awning huddle feels the chill a week later, when their routes acquire tolls. The courier's ledger opens to your page and something is written there in the smallest handwriting she owns. Mr. Moult watches the transaction with the strange grief of a professional watching an amateur turn out gifted.
effects: schedule 4 r18_plague_market_crisis The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

Peak fever week, and the clinic posts its price list behind glass with a velvet rope, as though scarcity were an exhibit. Pestilence Opportunity Partners has begun selling 'priority breathing windows,' which are appointments, against the stores it still holds. In the kitchen the matron says the unsayable over the six kettles: if medicine insists on being a market, the lane will be a buyer with manners, and the tin has been saving. Mr. Moult, present in a personal capacity, mentions that willowbark futures are, at this hour, curiously cheap.

choices.text: Register the biscuit tin as a purchasing house

outcomes.text: The registrar, a man sustained entirely by stamps, finds a category left over from the grain riots: household purchasing house, fever provision, fees waived upon declaration of need. The tin becomes a legal person with better standing than most people in it. As a licensed buyer it can bid at the medicine exchange, where Pestilence Opportunity Partners must now say 'esteemed counterparty' to a biscuit tin. Mr. Moult witnesses the signature and lists his occupation, after a pause, as 'transitional.'
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_compromise The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: The registrar finds the category, then finds the counter category: POP's counsel objects that a tin cannot hold intent, and the hearing is set for a date the fever does not observe. The lane learns the exchange has a door policy and the door has a lawyer. The matron says the law is a queue that sells its own places, which gets chalked on the skillet under the agenda. The tin bids anyway, unofficially, through a gravedigger with an honest face.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_compromise The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Front-run the tin's willowbark order yourself

outcomes.text: You buy an hour before the tin does, and when the kitty's big order moves the price, your little stake ripens beautifully: eight on the turn, six on the resale. It is the cleanest money you have ever made and the room it was made in is full of coughing. The tin's order fills at the worse price, short by one crate. Mr. Moult, who taught you the term front-running on the walk over, looks like a bird who has taught a fish to drown.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_compromise The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Pool every soup debt into one enormous bid

success.text: The courier calls in the whole ledger at once, and it turns out the lane has been sitting on a fortune denominated in favors. Converted to coin, soup debts and sitting hours buy the tin its crate of willowbark and a second crate for spite. The exchange records its first purchase settled partly in broth, a precedent nobody knows how to price. Mr. Moult buys a small share in the tin 'for diversification,' and buttons the receipt into the waistcoat next to the brochure.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_compromise The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

fail.text: The great bid is assembled by dawn and you are the one who carries the coin box across town at a jog, which the fever notices. The order lands, the price jumps, and half the lane hears 'the tin is bidding' as 'the medicine is gone,' so the square spends a day at a simmer. The willowbark comes anyway, a day late, carried by two gravediggers like a coffin going the wrong way, to cheers. You sleep for eleven hours and are fined one biscuit by the tin, as policy.
effects: schedule 5 r18_plague_market_compromise The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

Mr. Moult delivers the envelope at noon, holding it at wing's length like something between a summons and a fish. Inside, Pestilence Opportunity Partners proposes a partnership: the crews provide the bedsides, POP provides the invoices, and the arrangement is to be called Community Care Forward. The terms are printed small enough to constitute a dare. Moult reads them aloud in the kitchen because it is his job, and his voice does the thing a structure does just before engineers are consulted.

choices.text: Take POP's coin to annotate their own fine print

success.text: You hire yourself to POP as a 'community legibility consultant' and annotate the contract honestly, four coins a page. Your marginalia leak, as marginalia do, and the lane reads the deal with your notes attached: clause nine, the beds become collateral; clause twelve, grief incurs interest. The offer dies of publicity and the crews are left standing, though they note whose payroll the pen was on. Mr. Moult files your annotations under 'precedent' and shakes your hand with one wing, formally.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_reversal The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

fail.text: You take the fee in full, eight coins in an envelope with a logo on the flap, and your notes come back 'streamlined,' your doubts converted into bullet points praising flexibility. Fourteen households sign Community Care Forward on the strength of language wearing your fingerprints. The kitchen does not shout at you; it simply reroutes the rota around your name, which is louder. Mr. Moult returns your original draft in an unmarked envelope, the only unbranded object POP's office ever produced.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_reversal The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Send the contract back signed only with the rota

outcomes.text: The crews return the contract unsigned, wrapped in the pillowcase rota, which now runs to four pillowcases and reads like a town. The message requires no annotation: here is what a bedside costs, nothing, and here is the waiting list to pay it. Two clinic nurses defect to the kitchen that week, bringing a thermometer and gossip of empty POP wards. Mr. Moult copies the pillowcases into his ledger, and for the first time the entries have no premium column.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_reversal The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: The refusal is unanimous but the fever does not respect drama; the night you spend drafting it is a night short staffed, and you cover the gap yourself at cost. Word of the spurned deal reaches the square before breakfast and the square adds its own dialogue, most of it flammable. POP issues a statement regretting the lane's 'appetite for risk.' The matron reads it aloud in a funny voice, which is how the kitchen performs triage on despair.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_reversal The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Accept the branding, reject everything under it

success.text: You sign the name and nothing else: Community Care Forward now legally denotes six kettles, four pillowcases, and a tin. POP discovers it has spent a season advertising its rival, at cost, in two fonts. Their counsel demands the name back and is invited to value it; goodwill, it turns out, is the one asset they cannot repossess. Mr. Moult begins wearing the crews' brochure openly in the waistcoat's breast pocket, like a flower.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_reversal The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

fail.text: The name-only gambit hits a snag: accepting any clause reopens the whole instrument, per a rule that only exists when needed. Suddenly the kettles are 'brand assets under discussion' and a man comes to photograph the tin. The lane spends the week talking like lawyers, which everyone blames on the fever's choice of carrier. Mr. Moult testifies the tin predates the brand, citing his own minutes, and the company pin comes off the waistcoat that evening, into a drawer, without ceremony.
effects: schedule 2 r18_plague_market_reversal The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

Two hours before the exchange opens, Mr. Moult stands in the kitchen doorway out of uniform, wing on the doorframe, holding his entire client ledger like a resignation. Recovery rates have crested, funeral futures have gone to pennies, and Pestilence Opportunity Partners, it turns out, sold breathing windows against stores it had already quietly pledged elsewhere, twice. The medicine that actually exists is mostly willowbark, in crates, under a christening gown. The tin, in other words, is the market now, and the market is being asked, politely, at three in the morning, what kind it intends to be.

choices.text: Fix the tin's medicine price at cost plus nothing

outcomes.text: The tin votes with its lid: cost plus nothing, need before queue, and three of your own coins go in to cover the first week's gap. The exchange calls it predatory generosity and files a complaint about unfair kindness, which the registrar frames. Willowbark moves through the lane the way rumors used to. Mr. Moult asks for a job on the delivery side; he knows every address, he says, from before, and would like to knock on them differently.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_legacy The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: Cost plus nothing works until the queue does not, and you spend a day as the human waiting list, absorbing opinions at the door. Somebody's cousin jumps the line, somebody's elbow makes an argument, and you catch it just under the eye. The price holds; your face is described in the minutes as 'a casualty of clarity.' Mr. Moult brings you a cold spoon for the swelling, borrowed from POP's kitchen, which he no longer sees any reason to knock at.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_legacy The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Hand Moult's ledger to the fraud registrar intact

outcomes.text: The ledger shows POP selling the same crates three times, a financial instrument known in the registrar's office as theft with stationery. Warrants issue at the speed warrants go when the defendant's donations lapse. The clinic stores revert to the counter, priced by the old ordinance, and the shelf by the door gets its lock removed in front of witnesses. Mr. Moult testifies for two days in a borrowed scarf, having declared the waistcoat 'evidence, sentimental.'
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_legacy The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

outcomes.text: The registrar accepts the ledger, then recuses himself, then his replacement recuses, and the file enters the building's long intestine. POP's counsel calls the ledger 'the fiction of a disgruntled bird.' The lane keeps its willowbark and its opinion of institutions, both under the christening gown. Mr. Moult, disgruntled and a bird, starts saying so on doorsteps, free of charge, to excellent reviews.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_legacy The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

choices.text: Scalp the tin's crates to out-of-town buyers

outcomes.text: A courier from the next district pays eight over the odds for one crate, and a second buyer, hearing there was a first, pays six more on principle. The lane's shelf runs one crate lighter through the worst of the week, and you find you have stopped looking at doors. It is, technically, exactly what POP did, at a scale small enough to feel personal. Mr. Moult, who left a whole career to stop watching this precise transaction, watches it again from the doorway, older.
effects: schedule 3 r18_plague_market_legacy The vulture in the waistcoat has your address.

text:
PLAGUE MARKET

First frost since the fever broke, and the kitchen has shrunk back to two kettles, which is what victory looks like: less. The tin sits on the shelf with its charter tucked inside, semi retired, consulted on matters of principle and biscuits. On the coat peg by the door hangs a mustard waistcoat, donated, laundered, the company pin melted down into a spare button. Mr. Moult does his rounds in an apron now, keeping a condolence rota nobody pays him for, and the matron wears the waistcoat on cold vigils because, she says, the pockets are full of useful.

choices.text: Write the fever's history as an annual report

success.text: You publish it in POP's own format: assets, six kettles; liabilities, none surviving; outlook, breathing. The registrar shelves it beside the municipal code, where it is technically citable, and one clerk already has. Visitors from three districts copy the pillowcase system, fees waived upon declaration of need. Mr. Moult contributes an appendix titled What The Waistcoat Held, itemized, and the last line reads 'nothing, latterly, by choice.'

fail.text: Your report is a success everywhere except the exchange, which cites it as evidence the lane 'operates an unregulated model of care' and opens a consultation. Consultations are how institutions sigh. The kitchen answers the questionnaire in broth stained unanimity and frames the receipt. Mr. Moult attends the hearing in the apron and is asked where the waistcoat is; 'working,' he says, and declines to elaborate.

choices.text: License the pillowcase method to distant landlords

outcomes.text: A syndicate of landlords three valleys over pays eight for the manual and six for your testimonial, planning to run bedside rotas as a leaseable amenity, care included, rent adjusted. You are briefly the only person ever to sell the recipe for a free thing twice. The matron hears of it and assigns you the four o'clock shift for a month, wordlessly, which in her currency is both a fine and forgiveness. Mr. Moult notes the sale on the old ledger's last page, under a column he has relabeled 'tuition.'

choices.text: Take the keeping of the tin when the matron asks

outcomes.text: The matron hands over the tin with both hands, which is the entire ceremony, and the rota lists you henceforth as keeper: of biscuits, of the charter, of the hour nobody names. Your first ruling waives a widow's kitty dues against sitting hours already served, and the precedent holds, and her rent envelope is lighter for it. The kettles come to a boil in what sounds like agreement. On cold vigils the waistcoat goes out on whichever shoulders drew the worst shift, and it has never once come back with empty pockets.

You accept the tin and drop it, publicly, on your foot, and the lane's last fever nerves come out as an argument about succession, at volume, with pointing. The matron restores order by standing up. You limp through your first week as keeper, and the limp becomes part of the office, the way robes are. Mr. Moult fits the tin with a leather strap cut from his old sample case, and calls the modification 'estate planning.'

text:
FLOOD HOUSING

Tuesday, low tide, and the Deputy Registrar of Tides rows between the masts nailing invoices to them. The floodplain went under three weeks ago, the tenants moved onto boats, and DryLand Portfolio Group has responded by billing everyone raft rent plus a water occupancy surcharge. The postman in waders delivers the invoices anyway, apologizing, because it is still technically his route. Stapled to each invoice is a laminated document in which DryLand claims ownership of the tide itself, twice daily, both directions.

choices.text: Lodge a protest at the Registry before the ink dries

outcomes.text: The Registry clerk reads DryLand's tide deed twice, then a third time with his glasses off, hoping it will improve. It does not. He stamps your protest PENDING WITH ENTHUSIASM, a category he invents on the spot, and the raft invoices are suspended while somebody senior decides whether water can be freehold. The tenant boats fly your protest from a clothesline, like a flag that knows paperwork.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_assembly The landlord has invoiced the tide

The tenant boats fly your protest from a clothesline, like a flag that knows paperwork.

outcomes.text: The Registry clerk agrees the tide deed is absurd, which is not the same as illegal. Absurd has a filing fee; illegal requires a hearing date, and the next one is in February. Your protest enters the queue behind a man contesting ownership of a hill that is currently underwater, which nobody takes as a good sign.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_assembly The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Take DryLand's coin to deliver the raft invoices

outcomes.text: DryLand pays eight up front and six on completion for a local face to hand out the raft invoices, and you have a dry pocket for the first time in three weeks. The postman in waders lets you take half his route without comment, which is somehow worse than comment. On the coal barge, a grandmother signs for her invoice, reads the tide deed stapled behind it, and tells you the water has been coming to this floodplain far longer than anyone has been charging it admission.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_assembly The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Rope your boat into the tenant line and refuse to pay

success.text: By evening there are forty boats roped gunwale to gunwale and one shared decision, which is that nobody pays rent on a rescue. The tenant boats vote you onto the night watch, which mostly involves tea and counting masts. The Deputy Registrar rows past with the tide deed under his arm and pretends not to see the flag; the flag is under no such obligation.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_assembly The landlord has invoiced the tide

fail.text: The knot holds; your footing does not, and you go into the water between two hulls with the dignity of a dropped kettle. The tenant boats haul you out, wring you, and rope you in anyway, on the grounds that anyone that committed should be watched closely. On the far piling, the laminated tide deed stays perfectly dry, which everyone agrees is the most offensive thing about it.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_assembly The landlord has invoiced the tide

text:
FLOOD HOUSING

Wednesday's meeting hangs between two masts: agenda pegged to the clothesline, minutes taken on a biscuit tin. Overnight the tide deed grew an appendix defining premises as wherever the tenant floats, which means the rent now follows you around like weather. Half the meeting is an argument about where the street is now, conducted entirely by pointing at water. The postman in waders keeps his route map in oilcloth, next to his heart, like a man who knows what documents are becoming.

choices.text: Sell the mooring chart to DryLand's man

outcomes.text: The Deputy Registrar pays eight for the chart and six for the names, filing both inside the tide deed, which accepts them the way a wet boot accepts a foot. Within a day the invoices find every boat on the first try, and the meeting starts checking the clothesline for listeners. You are not suspected, exactly. You are merely served your own invoice with your position accurate to the yard, as a courtesy.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_retaliation The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Chair the meeting and build the raft rota

success.text: You get the shouting down to two shouters and the rota up to forty names: who tows, who bails, who minds the chicken raft when the wind turns. It is not glamorous, but neither is drowning, and the meeting votes to treat both accordingly. When the tide deed's new appendix is read aloud, the biscuit tin records the response as general laughter, sustained.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_retaliation The landlord has invoiced the tide

fail.text: The meeting agrees on the rota at the exact moment the boom you are leaning on does not. You take it just above the ear and finish the vote sitting down, seeing two of everyone, which at least doubles the attendance. The rota carries; you are on it twice, once as chair and once as cautionary tale.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_retaliation The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Declare the fleet legally at sea, beyond the registry

outcomes.text: If premises are wherever the tenant floats, you announce, then the tenants have floated clean out of DryLand's registry district and into open water, where the tide deed has no more standing than a duck. A junior clerk confirms with visible joy that the district boundary was drawn at the old shoreline, which is now forty feet behind everybody. The deed spends the rest of the week being rowed around in search of jurisdiction.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_retaliation The landlord has invoiced the tide

outcomes.text: The Registry rules that a district follows its rateable value, and the rateable value is currently floating, so the district is wherever DryLand says it is. The tide deed acquires a footnote thanking you by role, if not by name, for the clarification. The meeting takes it well, meaning nobody throws you in, meaning the day still counts as a success.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_retaliation The landlord has invoiced the tide

text:
FLOOD HOUSING

Dawn, and the company gondola is towing away rescue rafts for nonpayment, six of them, roped nose to tail like ducklings in receivership. The Deputy Registrar reads the repossession rider from the tide deed without meeting anyone's eye, which is difficult in a neighborhood that is entirely eyes. The grandmother on the coal barge watches him through opera glasses and writes something down.

choices.text: Row out and take the rafts back before breakfast

outcomes.text: Fourteen boats form a slow, polite wall between the gondola and the channel, and physics does the rest, since a gondola cannot argue with a coal barge and should not try. The rafts come home to applause and reassigned moorings. The Deputy Registrar notes an obstruction in the tide deed's margin, and the margin, running out of room, notes nothing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_flood_housing_coalition The landlord has invoiced the tide

outcomes.text: You get the tow rope off the last raft just as the gondola surges, and the rope removes you from your boat in the manner of a magician removing a tablecloth. Cold water, brief flight, applause from the chicken raft. The rafts are recovered; your left side files a complaint that will take a week to settle.
effects: schedule 4 r18_flood_housing_coalition The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Serve notice that rescue gear cannot be collateral

success.text: You cite the emergency equipment provision, an old one, written after some previous water got ambitious, and the Registry agrees before lunch: a rescue raft is no more repossessable than a rope thrown to a drowning man. The rafts are returned with a receipt apiece. The tide deed's repossession rider is struck through in red, and the deed holds itself differently after that, like a dog that has met a larger dog.
effects: schedule 4 r18_flood_housing_coalition The landlord has invoiced the tide

fail.text: The Registry finds the rafts stopped being emergency equipment the moment DryLand leased them back to the emergency. You ask how equipment can be rented to the disaster it was built for, and the clerk points, with real sympathy, at the tide deed's appendix, where the disaster is listed as an amenity. The floodplain now officially features water views.
effects: schedule 4 r18_flood_housing_coalition The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Trace where the tide deed actually came from

success.text: Two afternoons in the archive turn up the goods: the tide deed was drafted in a hurry, notarized by a clerk who had already been let go, and backdated to a Sunday, when the Registry does not open. You sell the story to the gazette for four coins, and the gazette prints the deed's Sunday problem above the fold. The tenant boats enjoy the article but note who got paid for it, and where you were during the rowing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_flood_housing_coalition The landlord has invoiced the tide

fail.text: You find the Sunday backdating, and so does DryLand's man, who finds you finding it. He offers eight coins for the archive slip and your sudden loss of interest, and the coins are dry and immediate in a way vindication never is. The gazette prints the crossword instead; the tide deed keeps its Sunday to itself.
effects: schedule 4 r18_flood_housing_coalition The landlord has invoiced the tide

text:
FLOOD HOUSING

By Friday the neighborhood has found its shape: clotheslines between masts, a pantry on the baker's barge, the chicken raft holding its own diplomatically. What it has not found is a way past DryLand's toll chain, strung across the only channel to market, priced per hull, per direction, per the tide deed. The postman in waders unfolds his oilcloth map of the drowned streets and observes that in his professional experience, the mail has never once cared about a chain.

choices.text: Redraw the delivery route around the toll chain

outcomes.text: The postman's map shows a back lane, three gardens, and an alley, all under four feet of water, all public right of way, all wide enough for a flat hull. A chain across the mail is a crime with actual teeth, so the route cannot be tolled by anyone. The bread comes through dry, the tide deed's toll schedule is left billing an empty channel, and the postman is stood a supper he attempts, and fails, to decline.
effects: schedule 5 r18_flood_housing_crisis The landlord has invoiced the tide

outcomes.text: The back lane works until DryLand registers the drowned gardens as private moorings, at a speed suggesting the paperwork was already filled in. The tide deed gains a schedule of gardens; the gazette gains a cartoon of a rose bush wearing a rent book. The bread now arrives damp and indignant.
effects: schedule 5 r18_flood_housing_crisis The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Run your own ferry under the chain, coin a head

outcomes.text: Your skiff fits under the toll chain with an inch to spare, and an inch is a business model. Eight coins by noon, six more by dusk, and DryLand sends a note proposing you formalize the arrangement as a licensed sub toll, which you pocket along with everything else. The pantry barge stops saving you the heel of the loaf, which is not a fine, but functions as one.
effects: schedule 5 r18_flood_housing_crisis The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Stock the pantry barge from your own pocket

outcomes.text: Three coins buys flour, salt, and a wheel of cheese with structural integrity, and the pantry barge logs it in the biscuit tin under promises kept. The rota adds a supper shift; the chicken raft contributes eggs with the air of an aristocrat honoring a debt. When the toll chain comes up, the meeting decides a fed neighborhood can outlast a hungry ledger, and writes that down as policy.
effects: schedule 5 r18_flood_housing_crisis The landlord has invoiced the tide

outcomes.text: You misjudge the flour sack's transfer between hulls, and the flour beats you across the gap by arriving as a cloud. You arrive as ballast, briefly submerged, wearing enough paste to be classed as bakery. The pantry barge accepts the surviving cheese with honors; your shoulder enters a long negotiation with itself.
effects: schedule 5 r18_flood_housing_crisis The landlord has invoiced the tide

text:
FLOOD HOUSING

Spring tide, and DryLand serves the whole floodplain at once: eviction from the premises, the premises being wherever each tenant floats, effective wherever you go. Service requires the tide deed to be read aloud in full, so the Deputy Registrar stands up in the gondola at first light and begins, and it is longer than anyone feared; by clause forty he has learned things about his employer that his face is failing to keep private. The water rises evenly beneath everyone, landlord and tenant alike, uninvoiced.

choices.text: Cash DryLand's first leaver bonus while it is fat

outcomes.text: Fourteen coins for the first tenant to accept relocation, and you are the first tenant, signing on the gondola's gunwale while the reading pauses respectfully. The relocation turns out to be a berth in a dry dock two districts over, beside a tannery, but coins do not smell of tannery, whatever the neighbors say. The reading resumes behind you, and you find you can still hear clause forty from a remarkable distance.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_compromise The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Lash every boat into one raft, one premises, one eviction

outcomes.text: Forty hulls roped into a single premises means a single eviction, and a single eviction must be served on every occupant at once, and the occupants keep moving between decks carrying soup. The Deputy Registrar cannot complete service before the tide turns and takes the gondola with it, mid clause. The tide deed goes home unread past clause fifty, and the raft holds a dance to celebrate the largest address in the district.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_compromise The landlord has invoiced the tide

outcomes.text: The lashing holds; the dance is where it goes wrong, forty hulls agreeing on one rhythm except yours. You drop between fenders and are retrieved by the supper shift with a boat hook and moderate ceremony. The eviction fails anyway, service incomplete, and your bruises are entered in the biscuit tin as expenses incurred by the premises.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_compromise The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Demand the deed be read within the premises it names

success.text: The tide deed names the premises as wherever the tenant floats, so you request, respectfully, that service occur there. The Deputy Registrar looks at the water, then at the deed, then at the water, and declines to stand on either. Service is voided for want of a floor, and the Registry logs the phrase with what can only be called relish.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_compromise The landlord has invoiced the tide

fail.text: The Registry rules that reading from a gondola counts as reading within any premises made of water, which is, on reflection, the tide deed's whole trick. The eviction stands served; worse, the gondola is now legally part of everyone's home, and its mooring fee appears on the next invoice, split forty ways. The grandmother on the coal barge says she has been evicted by better documents than this, and produces, worryingly, a list.
effects: schedule 2 r18_flood_housing_compromise The landlord has invoiced the tide

text:
FLOOD HOUSING

Saturday, on the high ground, DryLand offers terms in the shelters it built and bills for: half rent on the rescue rafts, amnesty on arrears, in exchange for the boats signing one line acknowledging the tide deed as valid. The shelters smell of new tar and old percentages. The Deputy Registrar lays the deed on the trestle table like a caterer presenting something that is still alive.

choices.text: Refuse as one fleet and walk back down the hill

outcomes.text: Forty tenants stand, thank DryLand for the smell of tar, and file out in rota order, which takes long enough to constitute a statement. Half rent on a rescue is still rent on a rescue, the biscuit tin records, and the hill learns that the boats can climb it and leave it. On the trestle table the tide deed remains unsigned and looks, for the first time, slightly alone.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_reversal The landlord has invoiced the tide

outcomes.text: The walkout goes to plan until the duckboards do not, and you leave the shelter horizontally, taking a tea urn with you as a witness. DryLand's man offers a hand up and an accident waiver in the same motion, and you accept only the hand, which takes more concentration than it should. The fleet walks; you are carried, with the urn, both of you dented, neither of you signed.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_reversal The landlord has invoiced the tide

choices.text: Sign in principle, then attach your own definitions page

outcomes.text: You sign directly beneath a fresh schedule defining valid as read aloud in full at every future proceeding, and rent as a sum owed only where dry land is provided. DryLand's counsel finds your schedule on Monday, upside down in the file where you left it, load bearing. The tide deed now cannot be invoked without being recited, and nobody who survived clause forty wants that; the deed has become its own deterrent.
effects: schedule 3 r18_flood_housing_reversal The landlord has invoiced the tide
