outcomes.text: The charter also contains an appendix, and the appendix defines miners as atmosphere consumers, class two. The company's counsel thanks you on the record for locating the clause, which head office had lost. The deduction stands, now with citations, and the repossession agent adds a fresh sheet to his clipboard, because precision is contagious.

choices.text: Hire on as the agent's local guide, cash up front

A memo circulates praising the cooperative spirit of the rock, naming no names, meaning yours.

text:
ASTEROID MINE

The mess hall fits ninety if nobody exhales with ambition, and tonight it holds ninety three. The agenda is the deduction sheet; the mood is arithmetic. In the back row the repossession agent attends in a professional capacity, rereading his manual, and raises one hand with a procedural question: step six says recover the asset and depart the premises, and he would like to know, for completeness, what the occupant is expected to do next. The room goes quiet the way vacuum is quiet, which is to say completely and without malice.

choices.text: Put the agent's own question to the company, in writing

success.text: The letter asks Meteor Wealth Extraction to specify the occupant's next step, in numbered form, suitable for framing. Legal cannot answer it, because every honest answer is a confession with a word count. The System marks the query well formed, the comptroller requests an extension, and the meeting votes to widen the tent: drillers, cooks, and one repossession agent, admitted as an interested party in the most literal sense.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_retaliation The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

fail.text: The company replies with a glossary in which occupant is defined as residual contents, see contents, residual. Legal considers the matter closed, and the System, which grades on formatting, accepts the definition provisionally. The repossession agent reads the glossary twice and starts carrying his manual the way other men carry an unpaid bill. The meeting widens the tent anyway, mostly out of spite.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_retaliation The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Sell the comptroller tonight's sign-in sheet

outcomes.text: Eight for the sheet, six for the seating chart, and the comptroller of atmosphere pays without haggling, which should have been the warning. A week later two enforcement officers arrive with fresh boots and standing instructions, and the repossession agent acquires a partner he did not request and does not like. The back row where he sits gets a supervisor. You get a receipt, which is more than the meeting got.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_retaliation The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Start the tether rule: nobody works vacuum alone

outcomes.text: The rota goes up next to the menu: every surface shift is two names on one line, and the second name is not optional. Within a week it is simply how the rock works, and even the repossession agent finds himself pencilled in as somebody's second, an error nobody hurries to fix. He asks what the second person is for; the newest miner tells him: for the part of the job the manual leaves out.

outcomes.text: The rota goes up, and the first week eats you alive: you cover three second shifts for people the company scheduled apart on purpose. You come in off the last one with a thin-air headache the size of a moon, large as these things go. The corridor grumbling rises; the rota stays; Vesta calls that a fair exchange rate.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_retaliation The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

text:
ASTEROID MINE

Overnight the comptroller converts every airlock to exact change. The notice calls it demand-responsive egress pricing; the miners call it a door with a toll and a grudge. Peak breathing hours now carry a surcharge, and the repossession agent's stack of notices doubles, because arrears compound faster when leaving work costs money. He asks his second procedural question of the month, quietly, to nobody: the manual's final step assumes the premises has a door that opens for free.

choices.text: Fuse the coin mechanisms with cooled slag

success.text: Slag sets in whatever shape it hates you least, and by shift's end six airlocks are permanently generous. The company, unable to admit its doors were beaten by a byproduct, quietly retires the surcharge and rebrands the free operation as a loyalty benefit. Vesta does not celebrate: an airlock that cannot lock is a door that cannot promise anything, and she says so at volume, which from her is one decibel. The repossession agent inspects the slag with what can only be called respect.
effects: schedule 3 r18_asteroid_mine_coalition The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

fail.text: The third mechanism shorts and cycles the lock with you half in it, and the difference between half in and in turns out to be three days in the infirmary. The company photographs the damage for a poster about sabotage and orders enforcement helmets with chin straps, which is how you know they mean it. Vesta visits, says nothing, and leaves rations, which from her is a speech.
effects: schedule 3 r18_asteroid_mine_coalition The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: March the long surface route in pairs, tethered

outcomes.text: Ninety miners take the outside walk in linked pairs, helmet lamps in a slow procession the company cameras cannot make look like anything but what it is. No ore moves through a tolled door for two days, and on the third the surcharge dies of loneliness. The repossession agent watches the lamps from the office window and writes in his manual's margin: occupants, plural, appear to be the working unit. Air rent drops; nobody thanks the door.
effects: schedule 3 r18_asteroid_mine_coalition The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

outcomes.text: The outside walk is longer than the map admits, and you finish it with frost in one boot and a lungful of bottom-of-the-tank. The company waits you out for a day, then two; the surcharge holds; the corridor talk gets teeth. What survives is the pairing itself: nobody walked alone, which the local writes down as the week's only profit.
effects: schedule 3 r18_asteroid_mine_coalition The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: File the airlocks as toll booths, not emergency exits

outcomes.text: The System's safety schedule is clear that an emergency exit may not carry a fare during the emergency, and vacuum, you argue, is an emergency with excellent attendance. The inspector rules for the miners before lunch and invoices the company for his shuttle. Air rent comes down with the surcharge, and the repossession agent files a personal query in the ruling's margin: does repossession count as an emergency for the occupant. The inspector marks it out of scope, which is not the same as no.
effects: schedule 3 r18_asteroid_mine_coalition The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

outcomes.text: The inspector agrees the exits must be free and rules, on the same page, that entries are a separate category with a separate tariff. It now costs double to come in from a shift, which the company calls balance. The System files your complaint under partially successful, a phrase that does a lot of walking. The whole rock spends the week angrier and poorer, in that order.

text:
ASTEROID MINE

Delegates from three neighboring rocks arrive for the potluck carrying casseroles and grievances in roughly equal weight. The smelter crew is billed for shade; the shuttle pilots are billed for the distance between stops; everyone agrees the tariffs are getting creative. In the corner the repossession agent reads head office's answer to his question about the occupant: a form titled Disposition of Contents, and the contents, he is beginning to suspect, are people. He folds it into his manual at step six, like a bookmark, or a wound.

choices.text: Open the tether rota to all three rocks

success.text: Any suit, any rock, any tether: the pledge takes four minutes to draft and one potluck to ratify, and the casserole dishes go home full of other kitchens' cooking. Within a month a smelter hand pulls a shuttle pilot out of a slow leak neither company would have billed itself to prevent. The repossession agent copies the pledge into his manual opposite Disposition of Contents, so the two pages have to look at each other.
effects: schedule 4 r18_asteroid_mine_crisis The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

fail.text: The pledge holds but the freight does not: the first cross-rock tether run leaves you hauling spare suits through a cargo bay with the gravity set to ambitious. Your back files a grievance of its own. Two comptrollers exchange stern memos about unauthorized generosity, and the corridor mood curdles from festive to determined, which is at least adjacent.
effects: schedule 4 r18_asteroid_mine_crisis The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Invoice the company for every exhale, per its tariff

outcomes.text: If oxygen out of the pipes is billable, carbon dioxide into the hydroponics bay is a delivery, and the potluck drafts an invoice at the company's own per-liter rate. Accounting checks the math three times and finds it disgustingly sound. Credits post to ninety accounts; the comptroller develops a brief allergy to the word tariff; the repossession agent audits the invoice for free, and finding it correct troubles him more than any sabotage has.
effects: schedule 4 r18_asteroid_mine_crisis The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

outcomes.text: Accounting accepts the invoice with enthusiasm, then applies an inbound-gas processing fee that exceeds it by one coin per person exactly. The exactness is the message. The potluck reprices its opinion of cleverness, and the System notes that both sides now agree air is money, a consensus it finds efficient.
effects: schedule 4 r18_asteroid_mine_crisis The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Run the agent's notices out to the rocks, hazard rate

outcomes.text: Eight for the run, six for the hazard, and the shuttle pilot who flies you there does not speak on the way back, which is its own kind of invoice. The notices land on three rocks like slow meteors. The kitty that was saving toward a joint suit reserve counts what walked out the airlock in your bag, and Vesta adjusts the seating plan without comment: you are near the door now.

text:
ASTEROID MINE

A second repossession agent steps off the shuttle carrying exactly one notice, and the first agent reads his own name on it the way a man finds a typo on a headstone. Head office, it turns out, has been deducting his air, his boots, and the distance he travels to repossess; his suit is four payments behind. The recovery is scheduled for the cargo airlock at noon, per the manual, step six. Vacuum is already there; vacuum is always early.

choices.text: Assert the occupant's lien: the suit is currently full

outcomes.text: You file it in an hour: possession of an occupied suit vests in the occupant for the duration of the vacuum, which out here is the duration of everything. The System clerk stamps it with something close to pleasure, and the precedent lands on every rock in the belt by supper: no recovery while the asset breathes. The second agent departs with an empty clipboard, and the first agent reads the ruling four times, lips moving, like a man learning the language he was written in.
effects: schedule 5 r18_asteroid_mine_compromise The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

The System clerk stamps it with something close to pleasure, and the precedent lands on every rock in the belt by supper: no recovery while the asset breathes. The second agent departs with an empty clipboard, and the first agent reads the ruling four times, lips moving, like a man learning the language he was written in.

outcomes.text: The clerk grants a stay, but the company refiles overnight, reclassifying every suit as worn scaffolding, occupancy incidental. The stay holds for the agent; the language holds for nobody. The rock spends the week learning that a right arriving with a footnote is a loan. The first agent keeps the stay folded against his chest, where the payments used to be.

choices.text: Assist the second agent at the standard rate

outcomes.text: Eight for the assist, six for discretion, and the work is easy because the miners get him out themselves: the rota produces a spare suit in under a minute, and the second agent recovers an empty shell, which his manual accepts as a complete transaction. Head office logs you as a reliable local contractor, a phrase that follows a person. At supper the first agent, alive in borrowed sleeves, thanks the room and does not look at you, which is the most expensive part of the fee.

choices.text: Cycle him inside and get a spare suit on him first

success.text: The rota produces a spare suit in forty seconds, a record, and he is inside before noon finishes arriving. The second agent recovers an empty suit and leaves satisfied; the manual never asks what the asset was wearing. In the mess hall the first agent breathes borrowed air and asks, professionally, what a second person costs; Vesta quotes him the rate: one second person, payable whenever somebody else is short one.

fail.text: The spare rack is empty at the worst minute, so you hand him your backup bottle and run your shift on fumes and arithmetic. He lives; you finish the day grey at the edges and furious at doors in general. The corridor is loud about whose fault the empty rack was, and the loudness is a kind of inventory. The second agent leaves with a suit and without applause.
effects: schedule 5 r18_asteroid_mine_compromise The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

text:
ASTEROID MINE

Settlement day: the comptroller of atmosphere arrives with a projector and a tone of forgiveness. Oxygen becomes free, effective immediately; in exchange there is a modest new fee for carbon dioxide removal, which the newest miner points out is the same air billed on the way out instead of the way in. By the airlock, the former repossession agent, jobless and wearing a loaner with somebody else's name on the chest, has begun arranging the spare suits so the walk to any of them is short. He says he is keeping busy; he keeps busy at the exact spot where he nearly stopped being anything.

choices.text: Audit the tariff table with him, line by line

success.text: You and the former agent take the table apart like a suit due for service, and find eleven errors in the company's favor and, to everyone's embarrassment, two in the miners'. The comptroller pays a correction fee of four, because accurate books are the one god head office fears. The System commends the audit; the corridor wonders aloud whose side an honest ledger is on, and does not fully like the answer.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_reversal The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

fail.text: The comptroller offers eight to certify the table as reviewed, and the word reviewed is doing exactly as much work as you let it. You sign; the stub prints; stubs are public documents on a rock this small. The fee posts under miscellaneous cooperation, and the mess hall's silence when you sit down is itemized too.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_reversal The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Counter: air free both directions, the rack stays

outcomes.text: The counterproposal is two lines, and Vesta reads it standing: air is free in both directions, and the suit rack by the airlock is a safety fixture, non-billable, non-negotiable. The comptroller, low on projector slides, concedes both. The former agent learns his rack has been written into the terms and rereads the clause with his mouth open, a man discovering that keeping busy became binding while he was busy.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_reversal The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

outcomes.text: Talks collapse for a day, and the company runs the scrubbers to rule out of pique, so everyone breathes yesterday's air with a headache attached. You take a double watch at the rack because the former agent will not leave it, and someone has to make him eat. The deal lands the next morning, worse by one comma, and the comma is load-bearing.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_reversal The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Reroute the CO2 fee to hydroponics as a crop input

success.text: Carbon dioxide, you argue, is not waste but feedstock, and hydroponics should be buying it like any other input at fair market rate. Accounting agrees with the horror of the trapped. The removal fee inverts into a small credit per exhale, and for one pay period the miners are technically farmers, which the pantry celebrates with actual tomatoes. The former agent laughs for the first time on record; the sound startles the rack.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_reversal The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

fail.text: Hydroponics counters that if exhaling is a sale, then the lamp pretending to be sunlight is also a sale, and bills the rock for photosynthesis services rendered. The System admires the symmetry and lets both charges stand while it thinks. Everyone breathes carefully for a week, which is not what breathing is for.
effects: schedule 2 r18_asteroid_mine_reversal The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

text:
ASTEROID MINE

At dawn the office printer produces forty one notices, and the forty first is addressed to Meteor Wealth Extraction. The former agent's license, it turns out, remains valid until surrendered, and head office never asked for it back; a clerical oversight, like most history. By his manual's own arithmetic the company is three payments behind on what it owes this rock: the exhale credits, the correction fees, and one suit, occupied at the time of billing. Step six, he notes, does not say who may not perform it.

choices.text: Bond his notice with the kitty, three coins each

outcomes.text: A repossession bond is posted in small coins from ninety pockets, which the System finds irregular and the manual finds acceptable, and the manual outranks taste. The former agent executes the first recovery of his new career: the company's reserve oxygen stock, recovered into the common store, per procedure, with a receipt. The pantry shelves have never looked so legal. He signs the paperwork occupant, first class.

outcomes.text: The bond posts, but the company contests the count, and the hearing is set for a date the company owns most of. In the meantime enforcement doubles the corridor patrols, and somebody's elbow finds you at the rack, officially by accident. The notice survives; the week does not, entirely. Vesta banks the bond receipt next to the rota, two promises in one drawer.
effects: schedule 3 r18_asteroid_mine_legacy The rock gets billed for breathing; the vacuum takes notes

choices.text: Lodge his notice with the System before the shift bell

outcomes.text: The clerk receives a repossession notice against a company with the joy of a man handed a rare stamp. Everything is in order, which out here is the highest compliment and the rarest. The company settles by noon in credits and pantry stock rather than let the precedent breathe, and the former agent's paperwork is displayed briefly in the office window, the way other rocks display trophies.

outcomes.text: The company counter-files within the hour, and the System, facing two immaculate notices, annuls both on the grounds of symmetry. Legitimacy takes the day off. The corridor spends the evening explaining to itself how a perfect filing can lose, and the former agent rereads his manual for the clause where fairness was, and finds a margin.

choices.text: Testify his license lapsed, at head office rates

outcomes.text: Eight before the deposition, six after, wired without a signature anywhere near the sum. Your testimony is confident, notarized, and irrelevant: the notice stands on the bond, not the license, a detail head office's counsel missed and yours did not exist to catch. The company pays twice, once to you and once to the rock, and files you under assets, reliable, which is a drawer that opens from the outside.

text:
ASTEROID MINE

Forty one suits hang by the cargo airlock, each a short walk from everyone, and the tally board above them reads: suits forty one, occupants forty one, discrepancies not tolerated. The former repossession agent is called the airlock steward now, and his old manual sits on the rack shelf with step six crossed out and rewritten: recover the asset, return it filled. The company still owns the rock; the door, in every sense that matters at noon, belongs to whoever is nearest. Vacuum remains outside, punctual, unimpressed, and no longer anyone's collections department.

choices.text: Write the steward's tally into the shift charter

success.text: The clause is one sentence: no shift begins until the board balances. The System ratifies it without comment, which from the System is a standing ovation. Inspectors from two other rocks arrive to copy the wording, and the steward explains the whole apparatus in four minutes, then spends an hour on step six, because that is the part people travel for.

fail.text: The System ratifies the clause in triplicate and requires the tally kept in triplicate too, so the steward now balances three boards that must agree with each other before anyone works. He does it daily, muttering, and the muttering is the closest thing the rock has to a hymn. The clause holds; the paperwork breeds; the company bills nobody for any of it, having learned which invoices come back.

choices.text: Sell the whole story to a mining journal, exclusive

outcomes.text: Eight on signature, six on publication, and the journal retitles it The Airlock Incident: A Cautionary Tale for Operators. Two competing companies purchase reprints to study which procedures failed them, and their conclusions involve stronger doors rather than smaller bills. The steward is not named; you are, in the byline; the rota spells your name correctly on the seating plan and puts it one seat further from the rack.

choices.text: Take the second chair at the airlock, on the rota

outcomes.text: The second chair is not ceremonial: it is the seat of the person who goes out when the tether goes taut. Vesta calls it settling your account; the steward calls it staffing; the newest miner calls you at all hours, and you go. The rate for a second person turns out to be exactly what she quoted, and the rock pays it forward on schedule, for as long as the shift chart runs.

Your first week in the chair, a driller's tether parts, and you haul him back with boot gravity and both arms, which the infirmary describes as enthusiastic. Ribs knit; the board balanced by supper. The corridor debates loudly whether the chair should be safer, and the steward closes the debate by pointing at the tally: forty one and forty one. He logs the incident as discrepancy, zero, eventually.

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

Dawn at the landing meadow: the ambassador has arrived, presented its credentials, and served them. The credentials are a tureen of grey consomme, faintly luminous, at the exact temperature of a held breath. The Protocol Preservation Office wants it transcribed onto Form D-9, which has no field for flavor and firmly believes it should not need one. The translators' welcome circle wants a spoon. Both parties are staring at you.

choices.text: File the soup as a diplomatic document

outcomes.text: The registry clerk, to her lasting credit, stamps the air above the tureen and assigns the consomme an accession number. It becomes the first document in the archive with a legally mandated serving temperature, and two guards are detailed to keep it from being read by flies. The ambassador emits a small satisfied steam. The Office objects, but an objection to a filed document must itself be filed, and the queue is long.
effects: schedule 2 r18_alien_embassy_assembly An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

outcomes.text: The clerk asks for page numbers. You indicate the soup. She indicates Form D-9, which requires the communication's text, margins, and signature, and offers no guidance on croutons. The tureen is refused entry and sits on the meadow developing a skin, which may or may not be a postscript. The Office logs your attempt as a formatting error, and the neighborhood hears about it by lunch.
effects: schedule 2 r18_alien_embassy_assembly An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Charge onlookers a coin per look at the tureen

outcomes.text: You rope off the tureen and charge admission, two coins for a look, six for a sniff. The crowd is enormous, the takings are excellent, and a broadsheet man pays extra for the exclusive right to call it a menace. The ambassador reheats itself slightly, which you choose to read as amusement. The welcome circle chooses to read it differently.

choices.text: Take up a spoon with the welcome circle

success.text: The ladle warden, an old dockside cook with opinions about thyme, hands you a spoon and the circle tastes in silence. Nobody understands a word, but everybody agrees the salt is behaving like punctuation and that something in the aftertaste is politely waiting. You are counted into the circle's rota on the spot. The Office notes that tasting a document constitutes tampering, and is ignored.
effects: schedule 2 r18_alien_embassy_assembly An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: You take the first spoonful too fast. The consomme is a formal greeting in the honorific register, which turns out to be carbonated, and it greets your sinuses at length. You come round on the grass with the ladle warden counting your pulse and the crowd taking sides. The circle signs you up anyway, on the grounds that you were the first to be spoken to directly.
effects: schedule 2 r18_alien_embassy_assembly An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

Three nights later the welcome circle's kitchen is a grammar lesson at a rolling boil. Bowls line the translation table in what everyone hopes is sentence order: broth for tense, seasoning for syntax, temperature for tone. The ambassador waits at the head of the table with the patience of something that has crossed a distance it declines to specify. Tonight the circle attempts its first reply, and the junior archivist from the Protocol Preservation Office has arrived to observe, armed with Form D-9 and a defensive expression.

choices.text: Bottle the practice broths and sell them dear

outcomes.text: The failed drafts sell wonderfully. Half the region wants to sip an unfinished sentence, and a wine merchant pays six extra for the one that means, as near as anyone can tell, regrettably, Tuesday. The circle finds its rough drafts gone and its shelf bare. The ambassador says nothing, at some length, in a chilled course.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_retaliation An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Cook the reply shoulder to shoulder

success.text: The circle cooks until the windows fog, and at midnight the ambassador tastes the reply and answers with a small clear broth passed once around the table. The ladle warden takes a spoonful and goes still. It says it has come a very long way, she reports, and that it is glad the water here is good. For a moment the kitchen is quiet in a way that has nothing to do with procedure. Then the junior archivist asks how to spell it, and everyone is rescued.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_retaliation An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: The reply scalds. Somewhere between the cardamom and the tone, the pot conveys an insult regarding the ambassador's mother, or possibly its moon, and the rebuttal takes the skin off your thumb. The neighborhood hears the crash of a retracted sentence and assumes the worst. The circle bandages you and adjusts the seasoning, because that is what revision is.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_retaliation An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Teach the junior archivist to file flavor

outcomes.text: You sit her down with a spoon and Form D-9 and show her the trick: describe each course in the margin as if reporting weather. She writes warm front, apologetic, ginger rising, and finds to her horror that it is accurate. The form goes in with the margins doing all the work, and the Office accepts it because the boxes are technically full. The archivist takes a second helping, strictly for accuracy.

outcomes.text: The margins overflow onto a second sheet, and a second sheet requires Form D-9b, which does not exist. The junior archivist submits it anyway and is summoned home to explain herself. The Office declares the dinner unminuted and therefore, administratively, still happening; nobody is permitted to leave the table until further notice. The ambassador ladles out fourths, delighted.

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

The impound notice is nailed to the kitchen door at the height regulations require and nobody can read. The Protocol Preservation Office has seized the translation table's stockpot as unlicensed correspondence and sealed it in the records annex, where it is being stored, the notice says, at an archival temperature. The ambassador's response is on the doorstep: a bowl of broth gone deliberately, eloquently cold. The junior archivist, back in the neighborhood and avoiding eye contact, says the Registrar of First Impressions signed the order personally.

choices.text: Ring the records annex with a potluck

outcomes.text: By evening there are forty pots on the annex steps and the guards have been fed twice, which complicates their loyalties. The ambassador circulates with a ladle, translating itself bowl by bowl, and the crowd's mood settles from angry to well fed, which is harder to disperse. The Registrar watches from a window while the smell gets into the filing. Nobody has broken a single rule, which is the most annoying thing anyone has done to the Office all year.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_coalition An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

outcomes.text: The potluck is a success until the chowder faction and the clear broth faction discover each other. You catch an elbow in the crush by the bread table, and the guards report an unlawful gathering of soups. Still, the annex clerks eat at their windows, looking down at the pots the way exiles look at coastlines. The circle counts that as progress and your rib as overhead.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_coalition An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Contest the seizure in the ambassador's grammar

success.text: You argue that impounded correspondence must be receipted to its author in the author's own language, as the charter says, and the charter, drafted in a more confident century, does say that. The Office is therefore ordered to serve notice in soup. Its attempt, boiled from Form D-9 itself, is ruled illegible on grounds of taste. The stockpot comes home under escort, steaming with what everyone agrees is the ambassador laughing.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_coalition An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: The tribunal accepts your premise and then rules that the ambassador, having no fixed mailing address, cannot legally be a sender, and that its soup is therefore ownerless weather. The stockpot stays sealed. Worse, the ruling implies the entire embassy has been raining, in a legal sense, since it landed. The Office begins drafting an umbrella policy.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_coalition An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Study the cold broth and publish the findings

success.text: You spend two nights with a thermometer and a notebook and establish that the ambassador's cold is not an absence of heat but a tense: the pluperfect, roughly, of welcome. The paper sells out its first printing among scholars and cooks alike, and a small honorarium finds you. The garrison, which had been pricing the ambassador as a threat, reads it and quietly downgrades the file to guest, disgruntled. The circle wishes you had asked first, but frames a copy anyway.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_coalition An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: Your notes are good, which is the problem. A procurement man from the garrison buys the lot before the ink dries, and by week's end there is a memorandum on weaponized tone circulating with your diagrams in it. The pay is handsome and the feeling is not. The ambassador serves you something at neutral temperature, which from it is a raised eyebrow.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_coalition An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

On the fifth week the ambassador begins hosting. It has borrowed the granary hall, the long benches, and every stockpot the neighborhood will admit to owning, and it sets a table for anyone who arrives hungry, which around here is a form of genius. Conversation is served in courses: soup that asks after your family, soup that remembers your answer from last week. The Registrar of First Impressions now dines alone at the Office, on principle, and the principle does not appear to nourish.

choices.text: Draft a seating plan the Office cannot refuse

outcomes.text: You seat the Registrar's deputies at the head of the table, which Form D-9 defines as the position nearest the door, and the door is where the soup comes in, so they are served first and outrank no one. Precedence is satisfied, appetite is satisfied, and the hall discovers that a fed official is a quiet one. The ambassador adds a course that tastes like a chair being pulled out for you. Attendance from the Office doubles by the following week.
effects: schedule 5 r18_alien_embassy_crisis An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

outcomes.text: Your seating plan works flawlessly until two deputies claim the same chair and cite different editions of the protocol. The resulting appeal freezes the entire table: legally, nobody is seated, so dinner cannot begin. The ambassador serves everyone standing, which is ruled a picnic and therefore outside the Office's remit. It is a good picnic, but the paperwork now describes the embassy as intermittent.
effects: schedule 5 r18_alien_embassy_crisis An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Auction the seats nearest the ladle

outcomes.text: You quietly auction the six seats nearest the ladle and the bidding is ferocious. A grain factor pays eight for the corner spot and a mill owner six more to keep a rival at the far end, by the bread. The hall notices the new geography of the table and goes a little quiet. The ambassador serves everyone the same soup anyway, but the seating chart has learned something about the neighborhood, and so has the Office.

choices.text: Put your own coin into the common pot

outcomes.text: You put three coins into the kitty for a cauldron with a bottom thicker than the Office's patience, and half the hall matches you before the hat gets around. The new pot holds a whole paragraph at a rolling simmer. The ambassador, presented with it, produces a course that several people afterward describe as being thanked in their own mother's cooking. The rota for washing up fills itself for a month.

The ambassador sends a poultice course, warm at the exact temperature of an apology, and the granary hall now owns both a dented cauldron and a story it will never stop telling. Your shoulder disagrees with the weather for a while.

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

At noon the Registrar voids first contact. The ruling is elegant: no communication has been filed on Form D-9, therefore no communication has occurred, therefore the ambassador is an undocumented phenomenon and must be treated as weather until it can prove it has spoken. The granary hall goes quiet, and across the region every pot the ambassador has touched cools at once, politely and completely, like a held tongue. The junior archivist arrives with the ruling in one hand and her resignation, unsubmitted, in the other.

choices.text: Sell keepsake bowls of the final broth

outcomes.text: You bottle the last warm batch and sell it as the end of an era, two coins the ounce, souvenir spoon extra. Collectors, mourners, and one man who just likes soup clear the stock by dusk. The Office buys a case for the evidence room and pays in crisp notes. It is excellent business, and it lends the ruling a certain retail permanence that the hall will remember you arranged.

choices.text: Keep the ambassador's table laid regardless

outcomes.text: The hall votes with its aprons: if contact has not occurred, then this is not a dinner, and nobody needs a form to not have dinner together. Every evening the benches fill and a bowl is set at the head of the table, steaming at the ambassador's favorite tense. On the fourth night the bowl is empty by morning. Not filed, not minuted, not deniable: eaten.

outcomes.text: The Office sends inspectors to confirm that no dinner is occurring, and you are cited for obstructing an absence. In the scuffle over the ladle you take the handle end above the eyebrow. The hall keeps cooking, louder. The inspectors' report concedes that whatever is not happening smells remarkable.

choices.text: Turn the weather ruling on the Registrar

success.text: You read the ruling twice and then the charter once, which is once more than the Registrar did. If the ambassador is weather, the Office's own meteorological annex must log every occurrence: time, duration, composition, and, where notable, taste. The Registrar has accidentally ordered his own clerks to eat soup professionally and write down what it says. The annex fills with tasting notes within the week, each one stamped, dated, and legally binding.
effects: schedule 2 r18_alien_embassy_compromise An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: The tribunal agrees the ambassador is weather and then, warming to the theme, issues a small craft advisory against it. Pots must now fly warning flags whenever the ambassador is simmering. The hall complies with bunting, which reads as festive rather than chastened, but the ruling stands and the pressure does not drop.

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

The summit convenes over an empty table, which both sides agree is neutral. The Office proposes an amended Form D-9 with a single new field, TASTE, ten characters maximum; the welcome circle proposes that the Office sit down and eat like anybody's cousins. Outside, a crowd calling itself the Plain Fare League is chanting that soup should mind its borders. The ambassador, between the two drafts, quietly begins to cook, on the theory that nobody has ever signed anything on an empty stomach.

choices.text: Serve both delegations from the same pot

outcomes.text: You carry one pot down both sides of the table, and procedure cannot survive the sight of the Registrar's deputies and the ladle warden blowing on the same course. The TASTE field is extended to a full page by the fish course and to an appendix by the pudding one. Somebody's clerk laughs at something the ambassador says, then looks around to see whether that was allowed. It was.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_reversal An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

outcomes.text: The pot is heavy and the aisle is long, and a deputy's ceremonial chair takes you across the shin at full stride. You finish the pour on one leg out of sheer principle. Talks stall over whether your limp constitutes pressure on the delegates. The League outside claims credit, inaccurately, for the chair.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_reversal An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Answer the ten character field in kind

outcomes.text: You take the amended form and fill the field with the only word that fits: WELCOME, in the ambassador's own reduction glaze, brushed on with a pastry tool. Filed, the word sits in the record smelling faintly of home cooking, and clerks keep finding excuses to fetch that folder. The League's pamphlets look thin next to a form you can taste. The Office rules the entry legible, which for the Office is a love letter.

outcomes.text: Your entry runs to eleven characters, and the Office's rejection is instant and serene. The eleventh character was the ambassador's honorific, which cannot be omitted without insult, so the form now insults either the guest or the format, and both factions want it framed. The summit adjourns to consider the alphabet. The League calls this a victory for plain fare, which tells you what their pamphlets are like.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_reversal An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: Hold the door against the Plain Fare League

success.text: You plant yourself in the doorway with a carving fork you have no intention of explaining. The League's front rank sizes you up, then the smell of the summit's third course drifts out and does most of the work; a mob is only a queue that has not eaten. Half of them end up at the long benches by dark. The Office marks you down as an unlicensed doorman, which is the closest it comes to gratitude.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_reversal An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: The League's second rank includes a blacksmith with opinions and a bench leg. You wake up under the coats with three ribs filing complaints and the constables in the hall, counting heads for a report that recommends more constables. The summit survives, barely, behind a locked door. The ambassador sends broth to your bedside twice a day, at the temperature of someone sitting with you.
effects: schedule 3 r18_alien_embassy_reversal An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

In the deep archive the junior archivist finds the Office's founding instrument, filed under D, first of its line. Form D-1 is a recipe. It is written in a firm old hand, headed WELCOME OF STRANGERS, and it lists a broth, a seating order beginning with whoever traveled farthest, and the note: serves twelve, footnotes to follow. Every form since has been an abbreviation of an abbreviation of a dinner. She brings it to you before she brings it to the Registrar, because some documents need witnesses more than they need filing.

choices.text: Enter Form D-1 into the living record

outcomes.text: You file the recipe as controlling precedent, and the Registrar reads his own founding document three times looking for a way out. There is none: the Office exists to preserve protocol, and the protocol is dinner. The garrison's file on the ambassador is reclassified from threat assessment to guest list. The Registrar is heard asking, in a small voice, what a footnote to a broth might look like.

outcomes.text: The Registrar rules Form D-1 an unratified draft, on the grounds that the footnotes it promises never arrived, and a document awaiting footnotes for two centuries is technically still in progress. The archive now contains an open matter older than the Office itself. Pressure climbs while everyone consults counsel about whether a recipe can lapse. The junior archivist starts writing the footnotes herself, out of spite, which is how most scholarship begins.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_legacy An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

choices.text: License facsimiles of Form D-1 to printers

outcomes.text: The printers pay eight up front and six on delivery, and by market day every kitchen in the region has WELCOME OF STRANGERS pinned over the hearth in decorative type. The original's whereabouts get vague in the retelling, which suits certain buyers. A colonel purchases the exclusive on the seating order, farthest traveled first, and begins applying it to parades. History sells briskly, and it was not entirely yours to sell.

choices.text: Cook the founding recipe as written

success.text: The circle cooks Form D-1 exactly as written, and the ambassador, tasting it, does something no one has seen it do: it recognizes the accent. Whoever wrote D-1 had eaten with the ambassador's people before, long enough ago that the archive forgot and the recipe did not. The welcome circle turns out to be the second of its kind, and the seat for whoever traveled farthest was built for exactly this guest. The kitchen is very loud about it, and the loudness has gravy in it.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_legacy An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

fail.text: Two centuries change a spice trade. The pepper D-1 calls for is extinct, and the nearest substitute has ambitions; the first taste puts you on the floor and lifts the ambassador's lid clean off. When the smoke clears the ambassador tastes it anyway, corrects the proportions gently from memory, and finishes the pot. The neighborhood argues for a week about what it saw. Your tongue takes the same week off.
effects: schedule 4 r18_alien_embassy_legacy An ambassador lands and serves its opening statement

text:
ALIEN EMBASSY

The treaty is ratified at the table, course by course. It takes the form of a recipe with footnotes, the footnotes Form D-1 was owed for two centuries, and its signature page is a ledger of everyone who has ever tasted it, which grows at every sitting and can therefore never be closed. The ambassador hosts weekly in the granary hall now, remembered fondly by people who could not point to its planet with both hands and a map. The Registrar attends. He brings his own spoon, which the Office has begun issuing as standard equipment.

choices.text: Open a chartered treaty house, admission six

outcomes.text: Your treaty house serves the ratified recipe at six the bowl and the trade is roaring; diplomats expense it, tourists queue for it, and the crockery has a crest. The granary hall next door still serves it free, but you have better chairs and a man at the door. The wealth concentrates the way soup does not. The ambassador dines with you once, correctly, and thereafter is always previously engaged.

Your treaty house serves the ratified recipe at six the bowl and the trade is roaring; diplomats expense it, tourists queue for it, and the crockery has a crest. The granary hall next door still serves it free, but you have better chairs and a man at the door. The wealth concentrates the way soup does not. The ambassador dines with you once, correctly, and thereafter is always previously engaged.

choices.text: Take the ladle for a standing turn

success.text: You take a standing turn in the rota, and in time the footnotes start coming to you: a widow's correction to the salt, a bargeman's note on what the broth should say in flood season. The ladle warden calls you her relief, which from her is a knighthood. The treaty stays alive the only way a recipe can, by being argued with weekly. When the ambassador finally names a favorite version, it names the one with the most handwriting on it.

fail.text: Your first solo service, you set the treaty course down proudly and the trivet gives way. Ratification, that night, proceeds at a mop's pace, and your burned hand signs nothing for a fortnight. The hall votes you back onto the rota before the floor is dry. Around here, dropping the treaty and staying is considered a stronger signature than most.

choices.text: Retire Form D-9 with full honors

outcomes.text: You propose the form be retired the way old ships are: with ceremony, and usefully. Form D-9 is withdrawn from service, boiled, and found, as the archive long suspected, to make a serviceable stock; its successor is a single sheet reading STATE THE DISH, CITE YOUR FOOTNOTES, and the Office enforces it with the zeal it once spent on margins. The Registrar personally stamps the last blank D-9 and eats the soup it becomes. He files the bowl, empty, as a first impression preserved.

You propose the form be retired the way old ships are: with ceremony, and usefully. Form D-9 is withdrawn from service, boiled, and found, as the archive long suspected, to make a serviceable stock; its successor is a single sheet reading STATE THE DISH, CITE YOUR FOOTNOTES, and the Office enforces it with the zeal it once spent on margins. The Registrar personally stamps the last blank D-9 and eats the soup it becomes. He files the bowl, empty, as a first impression preserved.

The retirement ceremony goes long, as ceremonies will, and somewhere in the speeches the Office discovers that a form cannot be withdrawn without a form for withdrawing forms, which must be printed on the stock being withdrawn. The last Form D-9 is therefore used to abolish itself, which the archive rules a paradox and stores in a locked drawer, from which it faintly smells of celery. The successor sheet circulates anyway. Around the Office this is called progress, quietly, after hours.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

Tuesday's post brings the league eleven invoices for unauthorized resemblance, itemized by feature. Original Persons Incorporated has patented the donor's face, and everyone wearing it owes back rent from birth. The copy who takes notes reads her invoice twice, which the company would bill as two uses of the eyes. At the tribunal a crate arrives marked FRAGILE WITNESS; inside is a mirror, subpoenaed by the company as hostile.

choices.text: Take company scrip to serve the resemblance invoices

outcomes.text: The company pays eight up front and six more when you stop apologizing at the doors. You serve eleven invoices to eleven identical frowns, which is efficient, provided you do not think about it. The mirror waits in its crate at the tribunal, listed on the docket as hostile, though nobody has asked it anything yet.
effects: schedule 3 r18_clone_personhood_assembly A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Chip into the kitty for the counter-filing fee

outcomes.text: You put three coins in the kitty and the reading room finds the rest between the cushions of shared furniture. The counter-petition is filed under the heading PERSONS, ASSORTED, which the clerk accepts because there is no form for the alternative. The commons adopts the case as a matter of record, and the copy who takes notes writes your name on the roster, then checks it against nothing, since you are, as far as anyone can prove, the only one.

choices.text: File to have resemblance struck as a billable act

outcomes.text: The magistrate reads the invoice, then the patent, then the invoice again. She notes that the patent covers a face but the bill charges for wearing one, and wearing, she observes, is not manufacture. The motion survives. The company answers by uncrating the mirror early, on the theory that it has seen everything and respects no one.

outcomes.text: You cite precedent from a twin dispute two centuries old, which turns out to have been settled by duel. The magistrate declines to reopen that option. The counsel smiles with a face he presumably licensed, and the crate marked FRAGILE WITNESS is wheeled to a better parking spot near the bench.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

Eight members of the league rehearse testimony in the commons reading room, and all eight remember the same first snow. The tribunal wants one witness per memory, which works badly when a childhood has been issued in duplicate. In the witness box the mirror sits under a dust cloth, pending oath, and the bailiff dusts around it the way one dusts around a superior. The counsel with the embossed briefcase has proposed a personhood test, details to follow.

choices.text: Assign each copy one memory to carry into the box

outcomes.text: The rota takes an evening: one copy gets the first snow, one gets the dog that followed everyone home, one gets the argument about the dog. Each walks into rehearsal owning a memory outright for the first time. The mirror's cloth slips an inch during the applause, and the bailiff replaces it with what can only be called respect.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_retaliation A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

outcomes.text: The rota collapses over who gets the first snow, since all eight were, in a sense, there first. A chair is involved. You catch an elbow meant for someone identical, and the dust cloth over the mirror does not move, which feels pointed.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_retaliation A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Demand the company define a person before testing for one

success.text: You file a request that the test be published in advance, together with the pass mark and the name of anyone who has ever passed it. The counsel's office responds in triplicate: the definition is proprietary, the pass mark is confidential, and the list of passers is under seal. The magistrate reads all three answers aloud slowly, which is its own kind of verdict.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_retaliation A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

fail.text: You ask the counsel to define a person and he cites the patent, which defines a face, a gait, and a way of standing in queues. You point out that this describes furniture with anxiety. The gallery laughs; the magistrate does not; the request is denied as frivolous, though she keeps a copy.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_retaliation A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Sell the company your notes on who testifies to what

outcomes.text: The counsel pays eight for the rota and six for your annotations, which he calls colour. By morning the company has objected to every witness by name, which is impressive, since most of them have not chosen names yet. In the box the mirror's cloth has been straightened by a careful hand, the way evidence is straightened by someone who plans to rely on it.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

At dawn the company posts revocation notices on doors: licenses void, features due back by Friday. Two recovery agents in matching coats wait outside the commons with a form titled SURRENDER OF LIKENESS and no instructions for how one would. Overnight, a junior from the counsel's office was caught in the courtroom rehearsing answers at the mirror; the mirror, being what it is, coached him right back.

choices.text: Move to enjoin the repossession of installed faces

outcomes.text: The magistrate asks the agents to demonstrate the procedure for repossessing a face without also taking the person it is attached to. They confer with their form, which is silent on the matter. The injunction is granted pending an answer, which the magistrate notes could take a lifetime, and she says lifetime while looking at the counsel. The mirror is formally logged as tamper-evident, since anyone who tampers with it appears in it.

outcomes.text: Your motion cites the sanctity of the body, the company cites the sanctity of the licensing schedule, and the schedule has more recent signatures. The injunction fails on paperwork. The agents celebrate outside at a respectful volume, and one of them checks his reflection in the courthouse window, then apologizes to it, which suggests morale problems.
effects: schedule 5 r18_clone_personhood_coalition A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Stand between the recovery agents and the doors

success.text: You plant yourself in the doorway beside a dozen residents wearing the donor's face, which makes eyewitness paperwork a nightmare the agents decline to start. Their form has no box for CROWD, IDENTICAL. They withdraw pending clarification, which is agent for never. Inside, the copy who takes notes writes down the whole morning, including the part where you smiled.
effects: schedule 5 r18_clone_personhood_coalition A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

fail.text: The agents turn out to be licensed for reasonable recovery force, a phrase whose definition is also proprietary. You lose the doorway and a rib's worth of comfort, and the company hires four more coats by lunch. In the courtroom the night guard reports that the mirror has started facing the wall, and nobody on the payroll will admit to turning it.
effects: schedule 5 r18_clone_personhood_coalition A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

Word spreads anyway: the company is charging people for their own faces, and the street has opinions the agents now have to walk through.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

The donor himself arrives at the commons carrying a casserole and thirty years of royalties he never saw. He has read the invoices and would like it noted that the company is billing eleven people for a face he was never paid for wearing first. The league holds a potluck to mark the occasion, and every copy brings the same casserole, because everyone learned the recipe from the same childhood, which he alone actually attended.

choices.text: Sell the story of the donor's defection to the broadsheets

outcomes.text: The broadsheets pay eight for the story and six for the sketch, which they run over the caption ORIGINAL SIN. The potluck is mobbed by subscription agents and nobody gets seconds. The counsel enters the article into evidence as proof the copies are newsworthy, which is not the same as personhood but bills at the same rate.

choices.text: Seat the donor and his copies at one long table

success.text: The seating plan takes an hour: the donor at the middle, copies outward in order of decanting, guests wherever the casserole density allows. Eleven identical dishes turn out to have eleven different faults, which the donor declares the strongest evidence yet filed. Leftovers go home with half the district. The mirror, wheeled in for security reasons nobody can explain, reflects the whole table at once and shows nobody twice.

effects: schedule 2 r18_clone_personhood_crisis A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

outcomes.text: The magistrate accepts the exhibits over the counsel's objection that dinner cannot be sworn in. One casserole is salted with grief, one is burnt with ambition, one contains an experimental raisin. Identical origin, the filing argues, eleven outcomes: personhood demonstrated by seasoning. The tribunal adjourns to deliberate, taking forks.

The gallery watches eleven dishes carried into evidence storage, and the mirror at the back of the room reflects eleven expressions of loss it declines to rank.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

Testing day opens with the counsel unveiling the Continuity of Memory Examination and closing it four minutes later, when the donor cannot state where he left his keys. Uniqueness of taste falls next: donor and copies all order the same terrible tea, independently, with the same apology. The company has hired tribunal bailiffs in new boots for a verdict it has begun to doubt, and the mirror now has its own chair, which nobody remembers authorizing.

choices.text: Take every test together and share the failing grade

success.text: Twelve people sit one examination and fail it identically, which under the company's own scoring makes them either one person or none, and the counsel cannot afford either. The bailiffs, watching a room full of citizens calmly failing to prove they exist, remove their new boots at the door out of something like reverence. The results are framed and hung in the commons under the title PASS.
effects: schedule 3 r18_clone_personhood_compromise A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

fail.text: The joint examination descends into a dispute over whether sharing an eraser constitutes continuity of mind. A bailiff separates two identical candidates and pulls a muscle deciding which one to hold. You take a knock in the shuffle. The counsel declares the session void, which is unfortunate for him, since void sessions cannot be billed.
effects: schedule 3 r18_clone_personhood_compromise A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Move that any test must first be passed by its author

outcomes.text: The magistrate adopts the motion with visible enjoyment. The counsel sits his own examination and cannot prove continuity of memory before coffee, uniqueness of taste in socks, or independent thought in the presence of his employer. His personhood is not questioned; the magistrate merely notes that under company standards it has never been established. The bailiffs are reassigned to guarding the results.

New examinations are announced, harder ones, and the mirror's chair is moved further from the bench, allegedly for glare, allegedly.

choices.text: Sell the bailiffs a floor plan of the commons

outcomes.text: Eight for the corridors, six for the note about which stair creaks. The bailiffs drill entering the reading room until they can do it in the dark, which they then do, at night, for practice, terrifying a study group. The company calls this preparedness. The study group calls it the reason the kitty now buys locks.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

The company offers terms: copies may register as Licensed Persons, second class, at a preferential rate, faces to remain company property but generously leased back. The counsel calls it a bridge. The copy who takes notes reads the schedule of fees all the way to the end, where being oneself renews annually, and puts her pen down for the first time anyone can remember.

choices.text: Offer the mirror the same settlement and minute its answer

success.text: You ask, on the record, whether the mirror would accept second-class status as a Licensed Reflector. The mirror declines to answer, which the clerk must minute as the witness showed the counsel his own face. The gallery understands at once, the way one understands weather. The settlement's dignity does not survive the minuting, and the counsel withdraws the schedule for revision.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_reversal A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

fail.text: The counsel objects that furniture cannot receive offers, and you reply that neither, under his schedule, can his clients, which is the point. The magistrate rules the stunt out of order, though she rules it slowly, like someone finishing a dessert. The offer stands another week, and the mirror is served with a warning for contempt, which it reflects straight back at the server.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_reversal A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Audit the identity records for the original license

success.text: You spend a night in the records hall and find the founding entry: the donor's likeness was registered by a clerk who never met him, from a sketch, in the rain. There is no signature. There has never been a signature. The commons pays a finder's fee and grumbles about your invoice, but half the district's resemblance rents lapse at dawn for want of an original anything.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_reversal A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

fail.text: The records hall is unlocked, which should have been the clue. The company's archivist finds you at the founding ledger and offers eight for the page number and your silence. You take it. The page is gone by morning, and the rents go up to cover what the invoice calls archival security.
effects: schedule 4 r18_clone_personhood_reversal A mirror takes the stand and declines to take sides

choices.text: Refuse any personhood that comes in classes

outcomes.text: The league votes no in under a minute and spends the evening on the commons steps, doing nothing billable. The copy who takes notes sits by the donor and tells him, quietly, that she learned to be a person by watching him through the hedge: how he drank his tea, how he waved at dogs before deciding to. He says she got the tea wrong. She says she improved it. They watch the light go, one sunset each.

outcomes.text: The refusal is unanimous but the leaving is not: a company man photographs the vote from a window, and you turn your ankle chasing him off the fire stairs. The photographs come out as eleven identical acts of defiance, which the broadsheets run as one person having a very thorough evening. The counsel bills for the misprint.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

On the last morning the company calls its hostile witness, and the bailiffs carry the mirror to the stand with the cloth folded like a flag. The counsel asks it to identify the original in the room. The mirror shows him the counsel, then the magistrate, then the gallery, one face at a time, each of them original for exactly as long as they stand there. It has, it would testify if it testified, always shown whoever is in front of it, and it declines to begin editorializing now.

choices.text: Hawk viewing slots in front of the sworn mirror

outcomes.text: Eight from the queue outside, six from a broadsheet man who wants an exclusive angle on his own face. People pay to stand before a sworn witness and be shown, under oath, themselves. It is the most honest business in the district and somehow still yours. The bailiffs break up the queue at dusk, citing fire regulations and, unofficially, envy.

choices.text: Stand the whole gallery before the witness in turn

outcomes.text: You put three coins toward keeping the hall open late, and the whole district files past the stand: donors, copies, bailiffs off shift, the archivist, one apologetic recovery agent still holding his form. The mirror shows each of them one person, no footnotes. The clerk gives up minuting individual appearances and writes PERSONS, ASSORTED, CONFIRMED. Somewhere near the back the counsel takes his turn, and does not object.

The magistrate clears the hall, though she is seen afterward standing before the mirror alone, checking, presumably, the precedent.

choices.text: Ask the witness to identify the copy in the room

outcomes.text: The counsel objects and is overruled on the grounds that his own question is merely being returned to him, and returns are not new arguments. The mirror shows the room no duplicates and no originals, only attendance. The magistrate lets the silence run to the length of a ruling. The founding ledger is opened beside the stand, and its unsigned first page is reflected, briefly, next to the counsel's face, which is the closest the mirror ever comes to comment.

outcomes.text: The counsel objects that the question calls for speculation from glass. The magistrate allows the objection and then, curious, asks the question herself, at which the counsel objects again, this time to his own success. The tangle takes all afternoon. The ruling is deferred a day, and the mirror spends it facing the window, showing the courtroom the sky, which is large, as these things go.

text:
CLONE PERSONHOOD

The ruling runs one page: personhood is found to be non-exclusive, prior art being everyone. License fees for resemblance are refunded in full to anybody who ever paid to be themselves, with interest at the rate of one apology. Original Persons Incorporated is renamed by the clerk's office to Persons, its remaining assets one mirror, which declines to be an asset. The copy who takes notes signs the new register first, in a name she chose herself: Marigold.

choices.text: Run the refund queue so nobody is paid twice by mistake

outcomes.text: The queue takes three days and the rota takes care of itself, which is how you know the district has learned something. Refunds go out to donors, copies, and one man who paid preemptively in case he ever resembled anyone. The mirror is mounted in the commons hall over a small plaque: WITNESS, RETIRED, STILL PRACTICING. People nod to it on the way in, and it shows them somebody worth nodding to.

The refund queue is orderly until two identical claimants present the same receipt with equal sincerity, and the ensuing debate about which of them paid it becomes, briefly, load-bearing for the whole doorway. You are in the doorway. The clerk resolves it by paying both and minuting the loss as the cost of precedent.

choices.text: Draft the new register so it can never be licensed

success.text: You write the register's charter in one sentence: entry is free, exit is death, and resemblance is a compliment. The magistrate signs it and adds, unprompted, a second sentence: no future clause may charge rent on a face. Marigold files it in the commons between the casserole exhibits and the framed failing grades, which is where the district keeps its constitution now.

fail.text: Your draft charter runs forty pages of safeguards, and the clerk's office, trained by the old company, finds a fee schedule implied in the margins. It takes a week of amendments to make the register say nothing billable. The final version is one line long, and the forty pages are hung beside the mirror as a warning, which the mirror faithfully shows to anyone caught drafting.

choices.text: Buy the refund-processing contract at a public tender

outcomes.text: You bid low, staff it with idle recovery agents, and clear eight on the contract and six on the coats you resell. Every refund arrives with a small deduction labeled handling, which is legal, and a smaller one labeled handling the handling, which is tradition. Marigold pays it, notes it, and starts a ledger of her own. The mirror, they say, shows you a little sooner than it used to.

text:
MEMORY MARKET

Nine in the morning and a clerk from Nostalgia Capital is nailing a rate board to the fountain: first kisses four coins, snow days two, the smell of a grandmother's kitchen one and falling. Beside him a broker in a coat of many pockets takes bets that embarrassment will get cheaper, reasoning that once a fund owns your worst moment nobody can afford shame retail. From the vault doorway the archivist of unbought memories watches the queue form and asks whether you are busy this week.

choices.text: Help the archivist hide the vault index

success.text: You spend the morning moving the index out of plain sight, drawer by drawer, while the archivist explains the filing system: joy by smell, grief by weight, regret alphabetical because there is so much of it. The broker watches you carry the last drawer and touches his ear, which she says is his tell for respect. By noon you are on the vault rota, under a new sign facing the fountain: WE PAY NOTHING AND YOU KEEP EVERYTHING.
effects: schedule 4 r18_memory_market_assembly Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

fail.text: The clerk objects to the removal of inventory, the queue objects to the clerk, and in the resulting scrum you catch an elbow meant for the fountain. The archivist pulls you out by the collar and pronounces you hired, on the grounds that anyone who bleeds for an index deserves a drawer. The broker shorts your dignity on the spot and, insultingly, profits.
effects: schedule 4 r18_memory_market_assembly Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

choices.text: Report the rate board as an unlicensed exchange

outcomes.text: You observe to the clerk that the fountain is municipal, which makes his rate board an unlicensed financial exchange operating in a public bath. The System agrees with a speed suggesting it had been waiting for someone to ask, and the board comes down pending a permit, which requires a hearing, which requires a clerk who is on sabbatical indefinitely. The archivist files your argument under vinegar, her drawer for things that sharpen with age.
effects: schedule 4 r18_memory_market_assembly Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

outcomes.text: The clerk produces a permit, a countersigned permit, and a permit for producing permits, all in laminate. Worse, your objection is entered into the record as evidence of local market interest, which Nostalgia Capital cites by teatime in a circular about untapped demand. The rate for first kisses drops half a coin, and the circular thanks you by role if not by name.
effects: schedule 4 r18_memory_market_assembly Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

choices.text: Sell the summer you were nine

outcomes.text: The clerk pays eight coins for the pond, the dog, and the long light, and the broker pays six more for the embarrassing part with the pond, which he shorts before the ink dries, tipping his ear at you like a colleague. The archivist watches you count the coins and says nothing, which takes her a while. Walking home you can remember being happy that year and cannot remember why, which is a strange thing to learn about yourself at a discount.
effects: schedule 4 r18_memory_market_assembly Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

text:
MEMORY MARKET

Midnight in the recording vault, and the stainwright is brewing a pot of tea nobody will drink. The archivist has gathered the keepers of unbought memories around a single question: if Nostalgia Capital wants a golden age, why on earth sell them a real one. On the table sit blank diaries, a town map with a better summer sketched over it, and the first draft of the Marmalade Summer, a year so good that nobody remembers it yet.

choices.text: Punch up the golden age until it sells itself

outcomes.text: You give the Marmalade Summer a harvest so generous the granaries had to sleep in shifts, a regatta decided by a swan, and a bread recipe recorded in loving detail because auditors trust specifics. The bakers try the recipe for research purposes and it works, which is how the fake year starts improving the real one, loaves first. The stainwright ages your pages with the good tea and declares them plausibly ruined.
effects: schedule 5 r18_memory_market_retaliation Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

outcomes.text: Your draft is too good: three keepers weep over the swan and one old man insists he was actually there, which is the correct effect on the wrong audience. A visiting pot mender overhears the rehearsal and leaves town quickly, and by week's end Nostalgia Capital has heard a rumor it was always meant to hear, just sooner than planned. The stainwright doubles her tea order and stops sleeping.
effects: schedule 5 r18_memory_market_retaliation Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

choices.text: Sell the broker advance word of the vintage

outcomes.text: You find the broker at the dockside counting other people's regrets and tell him a golden age is about to surface, provenance immaculate. He pays eight for the tip and six for exclusivity, ear untouched, which means he either believes you or has stopped respecting you enough to lie. That night he builds a position in nostalgia futures the size of a modest cathedral, and whatever happens next, you are in it.
effects: schedule 5 r18_memory_market_retaliation Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

choices.text: Teach the town its new memories door to door

success.text: You go street by street with the list of things everyone remembers now, and the town takes to it like choir practice: the ferryman polishes his account of the swan, the bakers rehearse the smell. By Thursday two neighbors are arguing over whose grandmother invented the recipe, which is when you know the summer has taken. The archivist calls it the first honest lying this town has ever managed together.
effects: schedule 5 r18_memory_market_retaliation Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

fail.text: Most doors open to the idea, but one householder resents being told what she remembers and demonstrates, with a broom, what she remembers about trespassers. You retreat with a lump and her signature, since she liked the summer itself, just not the delivery. The stainwright logs your injury as production expenses, under tea.
effects: schedule 5 r18_memory_market_retaliation Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

text:
MEMORY MARKET

The appraiser arrives on the noon coach with a scale for weighing wonder and a licensed sniffer trained to tell old paper from paper that has merely been through eleven pots of tea. Nostalgia Capital does not buy stories, he announces at the vault door, it buys provenance. Behind him the broker lounges against the coach, openly shorting the appraiser's confidence, which everyone finds informative.

choices.text: Get the appraiser drunk and read his manifest

success.text: Four coins change hands, technically a consultancy fee for local color, and by the third bottle the appraiser is explaining his craft: the sniffer only ever checks the stains, never the ink. You also learn the firm resells a childhood at nine times cost once it is framed, which the appraiser calls the appreciation of appreciation. The keepers are cool with you for drinking with the enemy, but you walked out holding the lock and the key both.
effects: schedule 2 r18_memory_market_coalition Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

fail.text: The evening goes so well the appraiser retains you as resident authenticator, eight coins, ribbon included, effective immediately. This makes you professionally responsible for sniffing out forgeries you personally commissioned, a conflict of interest so pure it belongs in a museum. The town glares at the ribbon, the keepers glare at you, and the broker opens a book on how long you last.
effects: schedule 2 r18_memory_market_coalition Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.

choices.text: Drill the whole town on the swan story

outcomes.text: By evening the town can recite the Marmalade Summer down to the weather, complete with the small contradictions the stainwright wrote in on purpose, since perfect agreement is what liars have. The bakers feed the rehearsal queue between takes, and the appraiser interviews forty residents and finds their accounts inconsistent in exactly the way truth is. He wires his firm two words, provenance holds, and the sniffer gets the afternoon off.
effects: schedule 2 r18_memory_market_coalition Nostalgia Capital pays cash for childhoods. Ask why.
