choices.text: Stay the night and cut a gear that lasts

success.text: The Hinge Whisperer files a new gear out of an old plow blade, muttering that steel does not observe birthdays. By dawn the mill turns, the town smells of flour, and the baker pays the commons in loaves, which is the exchange rate everyone secretly prefers. The miller nails the sheared tin gear above his door as a warning to its relatives. In the wagon, the patched warranty has smoothed itself out and taken to rustling in time with the millwheel.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_retaliation The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

fail.text: The millwheel starts while your hand is still confirming a bearing, and you leave a little skin in the works as a tip. The mill runs, though, and the town cheers loudly enough to be heard three fields over, which the Saints' circuit rider writes down as a disturbance. The Hinge Whisperer wraps your knuckles and says the mill is sorry, and the worst part is you believe her. The patched warranty rustles all night, unsettled, like a sleeper who heard his name.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_retaliation The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: File the new gear as an act of devotion

outcomes.text: You find the clause in the Saints' own missal exempting acts of devotion from licensing, and you file the gear as one, with witnesses. The circuit rider reads it twice, finds the definition of devotion regrettably broad, and blesses the mill through gritted teeth. The town eats bread that is technically sacred, and nobody complains about the technicality. The patched warranty receives a second patch and, the Apprentice swears, purrs.

outcomes.text: The circuit rider rules that devotion requires a collection plate, and the mill cannot be devout because it has never tithed. Your paperwork comes back annotated in red, which is the Saints' love language. The gear stays in, because paperwork travels slower than flour, but the town goes on a list. The patched warranty spends the evening lying face down.

text:
UNIVERSAL REPAIR

Recall wagons arrive at the next crossroads flying the Saints' pennant, crewed by the Retrieval Choir, who sing while they confiscate. Their writ authorizes the recovery of all mended objects as evidence of heresy, and they break a farmer's fence getting through it, which nobody recalls. The Hinge Whisperer watches them stack rescued kettles like prisoners of war. In the Apprentice's coat, the patched warranty has begun underlining its own exclusions, apparently in shame.

choices.text: Move the mended things one house ahead of the Choir

outcomes.text: The village runs its mended goods along a rota, each kettle sleeping in a different kitchen, one house ahead of the Choir all afternoon. The Choir confiscates a great deal of honestly broken rubbish and departs singing, none the wiser and considerably heavier. A grandmother lends the commons her cellar and her opinion of the Saints, both extensive. The patched warranty rides at the front of the rota in a bread basket, rustling directions.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_coalition The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

outcomes.text: You are carrying a mended clock over a back fence when the Choir's descant spots you, and the fence, next on the list for repairs, gives out under you both. You sprain something proving the clock is fine. The clock survives, the village hides it in a christening basket, and the Choir writes the whole hamlet up for rhythmic noncompliance. The patched warranty flattens itself over the clock's paperwork like a small shield, which changes nothing and means everything.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_coalition The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Audit the recall writ for expired seals

success.text: The writ's seal, you discover, was certified by a stamp whose own license lapsed in spring, audibly, before witnesses who remember the click. A writ served under a dead seal is not recovery, it is theft, and you say so at recitation volume. The Choir returns three wagonloads of kettles with the stiff courtesy of an institution reloading. The crossroads toasts the Hinge Whisperer, who points out she only fixed the fence.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_coalition The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

fail.text: The Choir's cantor produces a rider stating that seals of the Saints do not expire, they ascend. You argue with a man holding a hymnal of precedents and lose on volume alone. The wagons keep the kettles, the fence stays broken, and the crossroads learns a new verse about obedience. The patched warranty underlines the word ascend so hard it goes through the page.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_coalition The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Look under the recall wagon's tarpaulin

success.text: Under the tarpaulin you find the recall wagon's own service plate, four years out of coverage, axle weeping rust, a heresy on wheels by its owners' arithmetic. You mention this to the cantor quietly, and the Choir pays you four coins for discretion and departs at a pace that favors the axle. Word gets around anyway, because you sold discretion, not silence. The Hinge Whisperer allows that the axle deserved better employers.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_coalition The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

fail.text: The cantor catches you elbow deep in their axle and, instead of outrage, offers a consultancy. You spend an hour explaining how tinkers hide kettles, in general terms, hypothetically, for eight coins. The Choir's next sweep is unpleasantly well informed, and two villages over a militia gets a manual with your fingerprints in the margins. The patched warranty will not lie flat in your pocket for a week.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_coalition The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

text:
UNIVERSAL REPAIR

At the boarding house the boiler died in autumn, and the landlord has been charging a seasonal warmth surcharge for the memory of heat. The boiler's warranty is eternal but void, a combination the Saints sell at a premium: it expired the day it was signed, mid-signature, with a click the landlord had notarized. Tenants queue at the one warm pipe with ration mugs. The patched warranty has started writing in its own margins, small neat clauses, and the Apprentice With Pockets has stopped pretending it does not.

choices.text: Read the boiler's warranty aloud in the parlor

outcomes.text: You read the void warranty aloud in the parlor, all of it, in the voice reserved for wills, and the landlord's tenants hear the clause defining heat as a structural fixture, inseparable from rent already paid. The surcharge dies mid-defense with a click of its own, which everyone politely does not laugh at. The boiler is opened for lawful maintenance of a fixture by breakfast. In the margins, the patched warranty copies down the fixture clause with what can only be called appetite.
effects: schedule 3 r18_universal_repair_crisis The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

outcomes.text: You are two appendices in when the landlord's cousin, a licensed adjuster, objects that reading a void instrument aloud constitutes necromancy in the first degree. The parlor divides between the cold and the technical. The surcharge survives on a procedural vote of one cousin. The patched warranty writes necromancy in its margin and adds a question mark, the most alive punctuation you have ever seen.
effects: schedule 3 r18_universal_repair_crisis The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Rent out standing room at the warm pipe

outcomes.text: You are the one who thought to charge by the quarter hour, and the queue pays, because a warm quarter hour is the only luxury on the menu. Fourteen coins by nightfall, and the landlord shakes your hand as one professional to another, which should feel worse than it does. The Hinge Whisperer fixes the boiler two days later anyway, collapsing your market. The patched warranty declines to record your clause, the first thing it has ever refused.
effects: schedule 3 r18_universal_repair_crisis The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Put three coins in the kitty for boiler parts

outcomes.text: The kitty fills with coins, buttons, and one heirloom thimble, and the parts wagon takes it all at face value because the Hinge Whisperer vouches. The boiler comes apart rusty and goes together singing, and by evening the whole house gathers in the parlor, because warmth, it turns out, was the landlord's only crowd control. Rent day arrives to find the tenants comparing ledgers over cocoa. The patched warranty adds the boiler to its margins as kin, coverage continuing until further notice.

outcomes.text: The boiler's relief valve, sold separately and never sold, lets go while you are tightening the jacket, and steam signs its name on your forearm. The parts hold, the house warms, and you get the first bath from the tank you were scalded filling, which is either justice or interest. The landlord bills the commons for the water. The patched warranty writes his name in its margin under a heading it will not show anyone.
effects: schedule 3 r18_universal_repair_crisis The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

text:
UNIVERSAL REPAIR

At the toll bridge the Saints are waiting with an injunction the size of a tabard. The repair manuals are declared apocrypha, the wagon is impounded as a reliquary of error, and the Hinge Whisperer is cited for unauthorized resurrection of an expired instrument, meaning the patched warranty, which the Deacon of Shrinkwrap holds up with tongs. Its expiry line, everyone can see, is now blank. The Deacon calls this forgery. The warranty rustles, and it does not sound like denial.

choices.text: Sell the Saints a map to the manual copies

outcomes.text: There are nine copies of the manuals between here and the mountains, and you know six, and the Deacon pays landmark by landmark from the EVANGELISM purse, freshly restocked. Fourteen coins and a signed indulgence excusing you from all future repairs. The Saints burn what they find, then discover that tinkers memorize faster than paper. Nobody chalks a wrench on your sleeve again, but the coins do spend.
effects: schedule 4 r18_universal_repair_compromise The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Deal the manuals out page by page to pockets

outcomes.text: The manuals come apart at the stitching, which the Hinge Whisperer loosened years ago for exactly this weather, and every traveler at the bridge takes one page. A chapter on valves crosses the toll in a fiddle case; the index leaves in a baby's sock. The Saints impound a wagonload of empty bindings and declare victory over furniture. When the pages reunite at the next stop, three of them come back annotated with improvements.
effects: schedule 4 r18_universal_repair_compromise The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

outcomes.text: The handoff works until a gust takes the lubrication chapter into the river, and you go in after it, because that is the chapter that stops axles from screaming. You come up coughing with page one hundred and nine in your teeth. The bridge crowd applauds, the Saints cite the applause, and the toll keeper waives your crossing out of respect. The pages dry on the tollhouse rail like laundry that knows things.
effects: schedule 4 r18_universal_repair_compromise The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Argue an expired instrument has no standing

success.text: You put it to the Deacon plainly: either the warranty is dead, in which case it is nobody's evidence and less anyone's forgery, or it is alive, in which case its coverage resumed and the arrears are the Saints' problem. The Deacon opens his mouth, and the warranty clicks, once, in the tone of a document closing an argument rather than a life. Three clerks resign on the spot, citing the appendix on miracles. The wagon comes off the impound list before the injunction's ink is dry enough to matter.
effects: schedule 4 r18_universal_repair_compromise The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

fail.text: The Deacon rules that instruments of the Saints occupy a third state, neither living nor expired, called pending, which pays no claims and answers no questions. Pending, it turns out, is also where your objection goes. The wagon stays impounded a week, and the Hinge Whisperer holds her repairs from a borrowed stool. The warranty spends the week with its blank expiry line facing the Deacon, which is the paper equivalent of eye contact.
effects: schedule 4 r18_universal_repair_compromise The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

text:
UNIVERSAL REPAIR

Parley convenes at a crossroads chapel with the organ wheezing through the negotiations like a witness. The Saints offer a settlement: repair becomes legal if every repair is sold back to them and reissued as a renewal, at renewal prices, under renewal coverage that expires, audibly, on schedule. The Hinge Whisperer reads the offer twice. The patched warranty sits on the table between the parties, and both sides keep glancing at it the way you glance at a dog that has learned latches.

choices.text: Answer that the rota fixes things regardless

outcomes.text: The Hinge Whisperer declines the offer on behalf of nobody, since the commons has no head to negotiate with, only hands, and the hands are busy. She lists the week's repairs while the Saints' notary hunts for the signature line on a rota. Word of the refusal travels faster than the wagons and arrives everywhere as encouragement. The patched warranty adds the chapel roof to its margins, unprompted, and the roof does stop leaking, which nobody present is prepared to discuss.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_reversal The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

outcomes.text: Declining goes smoothly until a novice tries to confiscate the warranty as a negotiating asset, and you are the one who catches his wrist. In the scuffle you take a candlestick above the eye, the warranty is saved, and the parley adjourns on grounds of bleeding. The Saints call it an assault on procedure. The crossroads calls it the best chapel service in years, and attendance at the next one triples.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_reversal The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Mend the organ mid-hymn, in front of everyone

outcomes.text: You slip behind the pipes during the processional and free the sticking valve with a filed penny, so the closing hymn arrives at full voice for the first time in a decade. The Saints cannot condemn a repair their own liturgy is currently standing on. The cantor, to his horror, weeps. The offer is quietly withdrawn for redrafting, and the patched warranty transcribes the hymn's page number into its margins, for precedent.

outcomes.text: You slip behind the pipes during the processional and free the sticking valve with a filed penny, so the closing hymn arrives at full voice for the first time in a decade. The Saints cannot condemn a repair their own liturgy is currently standing on. The cantor, to his horror, weeps. outcomes.text: The valve fixes easily, but the bellows, offended at being skipped, folds mid-hymn, and the organ deflates in front of the entire negotiation like the punchline it is. The Saints declare the collapse divine comment on unlicensed hands. Redrafting proceeds with the organ's wheeze written into the minutes. The Hinge Whisperer mends the bellows that night, off the record, because she cannot sleep in a building where something is straining.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_reversal The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Take the impounded wagon back by night

success.text: The impound yard's lock was made by the Saints' own contractor and is therefore due to fail this year anyway; you merely attend the appointment. The wagon rolls out on greased axles past a watchman paid in repaired boots and selective vision. By morning the commons is a county away, and the injunction is enforcing itself against an empty shed. The Saints' bulletin calls it theft; every kitchen that has met the wagon calls it a homecoming.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_reversal The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

fail.text: The watchman is new, unbribed, and armed with the crozier stapler, which turns out to be less liturgical than advertised. You take three staples and a fall from the fence, and come away gripping the wagon's brass bell instead of the wagon. The Saints double the guard and post a homily about violence. The bell rides your belt from then on, ringing for a wagon still in custody.
effects: schedule 5 r18_universal_repair_reversal The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

text:
UNIVERSAL REPAIR

By lamplight the Apprentice With Pockets finally empties the coat, and among the screws and the dead warranties lies a receipt older than the Saints' pennant: the founder's reliquary, built to order by a traveling tinker, mended twice since, all parts guaranteed for the life of the church. The ink matches the patched warranty's ink exactly, and the two documents rustle at each other like relatives. The entire doctrine of holy replacement, it turns out, rides in a repaired object under perpetual coverage. The Hinge Whisperer laughs for the first time all season, and it sounds like a hinge, and she hates that.

choices.text: Enter the receipt into the public record

outcomes.text: You file the receipt at the county registry with a request that it be read at open session, and the clerk, spotting the words perpetual coverage, reads it twice at no extra charge. The Saints cannot dispute the instrument without unseating their own reliquary, and cannot honor it without licensing every tinker who ever matched that ink. The registry crowd is silent, then extremely not. Somewhere a bulletin gets redrafted so fast it smudges.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_legacy The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

outcomes.text: The registry accepts your filing, then loses it, then finds it reclassified under decorative documents, a shelf that legally does not exist. The clerk is apologetic in the way of a man with a mortgage. Rumor does the job the registry would not, but rumor pays no claims. The Deacon of Shrinkwrap denies everything twice, which the crossroads notes is once more than necessary.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_legacy The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Ransom the receipt back to the Deacon

outcomes.text: The Deacon meets you behind the tithe barn with the EVANGELISM purse and does not haggle, which tells you the price was too low. Fourteen coins for the founder's receipt, and he burns it on the spot with the face of a man burying a parent in a rented suit. What he does not know is what the Apprentice knows: paper remembers, and there are rubbings. The patched warranty carries a copy in its margins now, ink matched, notarized by rain.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_legacy The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

choices.text: Have the receipt read aloud at every stop

success.text: The commons adds the receipt to the evening rounds, read aloud after supper at every stop, in the flat voice of inventory, which makes it funnier. Villages that never cared about doctrine care enormously about hypocrisy with a date on it. The Saints' collection plates report a drought. The patched warranty and the receipt travel clipped together now, and the Apprentice swears the pair rustle in harmony at the good parts.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_legacy The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

fail.text: At the third reading a zealous novice makes a grab for the receipt, and you catch a boot in the shin defending literature. The reading finishes anyway, louder. The novice is escorted out by two grandmothers with a combined age of a treaty. The receipt gains a boot shaped crease that the Apprentice flattens nightly, gently, the way you press a fever.
effects: schedule 2 r18_universal_repair_legacy The tinkers are coming, and the Saints hear clicking.

text:
UNIVERSAL REPAIR

Mid-procession, on the feast of the Founding Purchase, the reliquary breaks. It goes with a click heard across the square, the largest warranty in the world expiring in front of its congregation, mid-benediction, mid-clause. No licensed shop will touch it: their licenses exclude sacred objects, and the coverage died with the founder, or so the Saints have always preached. The only repairman in range is the Hinge Whisperer, who looks at the Deacon of Shrinkwrap, says nothing at all, and unrolls her tools. She fixes it for free, out of warranty, on purpose, without a word.

choices.text: Sell relics of the repair to the pilgrims

outcomes.text: You bottle the filings, sell splinters of the old bracket as second class relics, and offer witnesses a signed certificate of having been present, fourteen coins the lot before the benediction resumes. The Saints cannot object without admitting the repair mattered. The Hinge Whisperer packs her roll and does not look at your stall. The patched warranty leaves a blank in its margins where your name would fit, and somehow the blank is worse.

The Saints cannot object without admitting the repair mattered. The Hinge Whisperer packs her roll and does not look at your stall. The patched warranty leaves a blank in its margins where your name would fit, and somehow the blank is worse.

choices.text: Set up a standing bench where the wagon stops

success.text: The commons plants a repair bench at the crossroads, tools chained to nothing, staffed by whoever is passing and knows which end of a file bites. The Hinge Whisperer teaches mornings; the Apprentice teaches pockets. The Saints' bulletin denounces the bench weekly, which functions as advertising. The patched warranty pastes itself to the bench's post, expiry line reading UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE in its own hand, and takes up coverage of everything within earshot.

fail.text: The bench goes up, and on the second morning you catch a novice sawing at its leg, and take the saw away by the wrong end. The leg is mended by lunchtime, by the novice, under supervision, badly, then again, better. He comes back the next week without the saw. The patched warranty adds him to the margins under apprentices, provisional, and the Apprentice With Pockets pretends not to be pleased.

choices.text: Ask the Saints to certify repair as a sacrament

outcomes.text: You submit the argument in the Saints' own format: the reliquary now runs on an unlicensed miracle, so either the miracle is void, which is blasphemy, or repair is sacred, which is a filing matter. The doctrinal office chooses the filing matter. Mending becomes a minor sacrament, taxable, scheduled, thoroughly theirs on paper, and performed at the bench exactly as before. The Hinge Whisperer observes that sacrament is just the liturgical word for maintenance, and goes back to work.

The doctrinal office accepts your petition and assigns it a queue position in the low four figures, appealable annually. Pending, again, the third state. The bench keeps working while the paperwork ages like a cheese nobody ordered. Once a year a clerk writes to confirm you still exist, and the patched warranty answers on your behalf, in its own hand, and the clerk has stopped asking how.

text:
THE COMMON FUTURE

Work still exists, but nobody can buy another person's hunger. The councils are noisy, the trains are late, and every official chair can be recalled by the people forced to look at it.

choices.text: Take a seat you can be recalled from

outcomes.text: You stop wandering and stand for the water committee, where you are outvoted twice in your first week by people you helped free. It is the best defeat of your life. The trains stay late; the reasons are published; nobody can buy another person's hunger, including yours.
effects: finish Helped make ordinary life belong to ordinary people.

choices.text: Keep walking. Late trains cannot be trusted

outcomes.text: You leave between meetings, before anyone can put you on an agenda. Behind you the councils argue on, noisily, permanently, which is the sound a world makes when nobody owns the microphone. You catch the late train out. It apologizes, in writing, to everyone.

text:
ANOTHER ATTEMPT

The old revolution failed. This one has read its minutes, distrusted its heroes, and packed lunch. Nobody promises permanence. Everyone promises to keep watch over power, including their own.

choices.text: Stay and keep the minutes honest

outcomes.text: You take the job nobody celebrates: recording secretary of the new world. Every promise gets a page, every hero gets a footnote, and the lunch rota is enforced with revolutionary discipline. The first attempt died of forgetting. This one has you, and you write everything down.
effects: finish Joined the revolution that expected to be corrected.

choices.text: Keep walking. Watch the watchers from the road

outcomes.text: You go, taking a packed lunch they press on you at the gate. Somewhere behind you the new world distrusts its heroes on schedule, which is the kindest thing it could do for them. You check back in a year like an auditor of hope. The books balance, barely. Barely is the point.

text:
THE BROAD FRONT HOLDS

The fascists are defeated by an alliance that immediately resumes arguing about taxes, banners, and soup viscosity. The arguments are exhausting. The absence of mass graves is not.

choices.text: Stay and take a side on soup viscosity

outcomes.text: You stop wandering and enter the great soup debate on the side of thickness, which costs you two friendships and wins you a ladle of honor. The arguments run past midnight in a hall with no graves outside it. You lose most votes and sleep well anyway. That is the whole treaty, and it holds.
effects: finish Helped a quarrelsome coalition stop the boots.

choices.text: Keep walking before the banner subcommittee finds you

outcomes.text: You slip out during the eleventh banner revision, past the guard post that is now a cloakroom. The alliance argues you out of sight, healthy as an orchard full of wasps. Some victories are best enjoyed from one town over, where the shouting sounds like weather and the weather is fine.

text:
DEMOBILIZED STARS

The warships become ferries, hospitals, and one aggressively overarmed public library. Admirals complain that peace lacks promotion pathways. Nobody is required to solve that problem.

choices.text: Stay and staff the overarmed library

outcomes.text: You stop wandering and take a post at the library, where the reference desk can still achieve orbit and chooses not to daily. Retired gunners reshelve poetry with terrifying precision. The admirals visit to complain about promotion pathways and leave with library cards. Peace, it turns out, has late fees, and you collect them.
effects: finish Helped turn the fleet toward home.

choices.text: Keep walking. Space is mostly exits

outcomes.text: You book passage on a ferry that used to be a dreadnought and still flinches at fireworks. The stars go by demobilized, off duty, unranked. Nobody scrambles anything as you pass. You had forgotten what a sky sounds like when it is not a theater, and it sounds like nothing, gorgeously.

text:
THE WATERS RETURN

The rivers clear slowly, which is how rivers do almost everything. The companies call restoration unrealistic until the fish return without seeking investor approval.

choices.text: Stay and learn the slow work of rivers

outcomes.text: You stop wandering at the weir, where the restoration crew teaches you the river's pace: nothing dramatic, nothing twice, everything eventually. The fish return without a press release. You learn to fish badly and report honestly, which locally counts as wisdom. The water clears in your lifetime, which it did not owe you.
effects: finish Stayed long enough to see the river remember itself.

choices.text: Keep walking upstream, past where the maps give up

outcomes.text: You follow the river up past its last dam and its first argument, to where it is too young to have ever been owned. It is loud up there, and unlicensed, and entirely itself. You drink from it like a shareholder of nothing. Downstream the companies call the recovery unrealistic, and the river keeps unrealistically arriving.

text:
ABUNDANCE WITHOUT OWNERS

The machines can feed everyone. This time, nobody invents an artificial shortage to preserve character. Humanity keeps three landlords in a museum where they explain scarcity to baffled children.

choices.text: Stay and docent at the museum of scarcity

outcomes.text: You stop wandering and take the docent post, guiding children past the three landlords, who explain deposits to an audience that has never paid one. The children ask devastating questions. The landlords age visibly between tours. Outside, the machines feed everyone, and the only queue in town is for the exhibit.
effects: finish Helped abundance escape its owners.

choices.text: Keep walking. Free bread travels well

outcomes.text: You go, provisioned by machines that do not ask for your account number, past silos with their doors formally removed. Abundance follows you at a polite distance the whole way out. In every town the same absence greets you: no artificial shortage, no character built by hunger. Character, it turns out, survives being fed.

text:
THE NAIVE POWER

Kindness did not save everyone. It saved enough people to build clinics, kitchens, rescue ships, and another chance. The cynics remain technically correct and completely outnumbered at dinner.

choices.text: Stay for dinner with the outnumbered cynics

outcomes.text: You stop wandering and take the seat next to the cynics, who are served first out of a kindness they insist is naive, twice, with gravy. The clinics hum, the kitchens sing off key, the rescue ships come home mostly full. Being technically correct keeps the cynics warm at night. So do the blankets somebody knitted them anyway.
effects: finish Kept trying the naive thing until it became infrastructure.

choices.text: Keep walking. Kindness needs couriers

outcomes.text: You leave with your pack full of other people's cooking and a list of towns that could use a hand. The naive power travels light: it is mostly showing up, mostly staying, mostly soup. The cynics wave you off and predict your failure fondly. You will disappoint them somewhere new by spring.
